### Matt Wilson — 2017-03-08T13:56:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greenwave-electric-filters-nikola-tesla-noises.19185/post-209861>

Hi guys, I'm sure you guys know more about electricity than I do... I recently moved into a house in Costa Rica and have plugged in three Greenwave Dirty Electric Filters. One in the bedroom, bathroom, and kitchen...

I've woken up twice in the middle of the night know to ENORMOUS electrical sounds... think: Nikola Tesla experiments!

We don't lose power (although I do have a surge protector on the entire house)... It sounds like this: [Electricity Surge (HD sound effect) - YouTube](https://www.google.com/url?sa=t&rct=j&q=&esrc=s&source=web&cd=2&cad=rja&uact=8&ved=0ahUKEwiJ-bSM68bSAhXINiYKHZNtAcAQtwIIHzAB&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.youtube.com%2Fwatch%3Fv%3Dzkh5RvUjukg&usg=AFQjCNESD84cwoqLuK1aBAsKr5yXJAfzzg)

Any clue what this? I've never heard anything like this in my life.

I'm just looking to learn more about my home and electricity for my own health and wellbeing.

Thanks!

### Matt Wilson — 2017-03-08T16:17:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greenwave-electric-filters-nikola-tesla-noises.19185/post-209879>

Thanks for the video on pitfalls of EMF filters. Part 8 didn't give tons of info on noises (mine are shocking loud!) but I will tell you that the wiring in Costa Rica is never correct. I have a ground tester that came with my grounding mats, and found out that only two or three of my outlets in the entire house are grounded properly. There is a good chance this is part of the problem.

Interested to see if anyone else has evidence!

if you sink 1"dia copper rod, 8-10 feet log, into damp ground.
Just make sure there are no electric wires burred in or near.

### Matt Wilson — 2017-03-08T16:35:16+0200

### JanSz — 2017-03-08T16:51:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greenwave-electric-filters-nikola-tesla-noises.19185/post-209888>

Ok, in USA, I live in the house build in 1960.
Decent neighborhood, good looking house, decent looking wires (on a casual look).
In my case all or most wires have ground. Black, white and ground. For three-ways, black, white, red, ground.
Most ground wires are connected, so if you push a three prong plug it connects to the ground wire.
My problem was that the main box was connected to 1/4" copper wire barely sunken in dry dirt.
So my house (electrically) was rather similar to flying airplane.
Floating ground.

Your mileage may vary.
..

# Thread: Take a stab at these labz
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-a-stab-at-these-labz.19186/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 22
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matt Wilson — 2017-03-08T14:11:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-a-stab-at-these-labz.19186/post-209863>

Hey Kruseaders... I’m an athletic 31, 5’10” and 150 lbs and need some help deciphering my WellnessFX Comprehensive labs…

My lifestyle is pretty damn strict, but I am on the computer 5+ hours per day (grounded) and have 15 employees, so there is some stress…. I eat very keto; eggs, fish, avocados, and sauerkraut at most meals. I strength train 3x/week, meditate 30-60min daily, drink R/O, tan my @$$ at 7am every morning, wear my blueblockers, and haven't taken a warm shower in over a year...

**I’m a concerned about my Thyroid and hormone panel…** T3, Free T3, and IGF1 are a little low, although Testosterone comes in at 1191 ng/dl, Androgen at 520 ug/dL, and SHBG at 60 nmo/L (all high!) Also notable when talking about Thyroid is my Total Iron Binding Capacity is 229 ug/dL (low).

My theory is that low body fat (less than 8%), combined with intermittent fasting, long hours, and lots of travel, has pointed me in the direction of hypothyroidism.... My high levels of testosterone aren't converting into Free Testosterone all that well, and my LargeHDL is low. _I'm guessing not enough carbs and calories has put my body into "you're starving mode" instead of "make babies and relax mode"..._

This was deduced from listening to [Ben Greenfield decipher his own labs](https://bengreenfieldfitness.com/2014/05/most-comprehensive-blood-test/) + [Elle Russ’s Paleo Thyroid Solution](https://bengreenfieldfitness.com/2017/01/the-paleo-thyroid-solution-book-with-elle-russ/), but maybe I’m wrong…

I know this drawing of blood is just a snapshot of this a moment in my life, but any insights on this would be appreciated! Attached are my labs.

### kris90 — 2017-03-09T20:41:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/take-a-stab-at-these-labz.19186/post-209966>

I think you've identified the problem. Your body is in starvation mode.

I actually just recently discovered this myself after battling hypogonadism for several years in addition to having high TSH levels. I think ketosis is meant for mammals who are carrying a lot of fat. Trying to do the ketosis/cold adaptation when you are already lean seems to never end well atleast in my experience. IMO, this superhuman fat burning/heat generating ability of the cold adapted pathway can't work its magic in absence of excess body fat. Works wonders for diabetics or others with metabolic syndrome, but not lean, ripped guys.

I think aside from light, glucose status is one of the driving factors that determine whether we are in "rest/digest" or "fight/flight". If you don't have much bodyfat, you need a constant supply of glucose, and exogenous is preferential. Why? Because there's too little triglycerides in adipose tissue to provide glucose substrate for the body, so bodily tissue (i.e. muscle, bone) gets broken down for glucose through the action of cortisol.

Since I stopped counting calories, and focused on eating as much quality food as possible with a macronutrient ratio somewhere around 40% Fat/40% Carbs/20% Protein. I'm eating a ton of sugar from fruits, honey, cane sugar, chocolate, ice cream, and my fats are mostly saturated from animal sources, butter and coconut oil. I've limited my PUFA intake to below 10g per day, I've been feeling like a million bux after just 2 short weeks.

Nice article on IGF-1. (Have to read the rest of it).
My 2c
I would argue (only a little) about GH--->cancer connection.
My 78yo friend, (near) of end stages of prostate cancer.
Unchecked his testosterone raises to 1000ng/dl.
End stage is when cancer cells are able to make its own testosterone,
he is not there yet, but had metastasis that was gamma knife radiated.
While trying lots of hacks, it was easy for him to try GH (his son uses it)
Immediately he saw sharp raise in PSA. So hi stopped GH.
Me. 77yo. 10 years ago had a surgery in my left thigh.
Liposarcoma.
After surgery had yearly doctors examinations (just by his touch).
Now told to come in 3 years.
Tried GH, see no effect with small doses.
Unfortunately, thanks to **Obama Care** I have lost a doctor who was willing to prescribe it to me.
My current observation:
Yes, GH should not be used in some cancers,
but
it is too valuable tool,
to not make serious evaluation about its use.
**There is a chance,
that similarly to testosterone
in the right situation
its use may prevent development of disease.**
\===================================================
@[Matt Wilson](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19871/)
You have some items that are not usually seen.
Natural very high Total Testosterone
Natural very high DHEAs
Natural very low GH
Natural very high SHBG
Likely you have high Androstenedione since DHEAs-->DHEA-->Androstenedione-->Estrone

You are very lean, little fat.
Aromatase enzyme that is mostly in the fat, converts
Testosterone--->Estradiol (E2=30pg/mL ok)
Androstenedione>Estrone
Presence of estrogens gives raise to SHBG
presence of testosterone lovers SHBG
since
you have little fat, likely your estrogens are low and then I have to wonder why your SHBG is high, it does not look like it is from excessive estrogens. Something else. SHBG is made in the liver (and little bit in testicles)
But check
estrone
Total Estrogens

**Testosterone doesn't convert well to Free T (seen in my labs)
**
**Wrong conclusion.**
Or at least not how it works.
\===================================
Available testosterone is divided into three parts.

1\. Strongly bound testosterone (attached to SHBG)
2\. Weakly bound testosterone (attached to Albumin)
3\. Remaining testosterone, (Free Testosterone)

When testosterone activity is considered it is assumed that it is due to
(Free plus weakly bound) testosterone

[https://www.labcorp.com/test-menu/35486/testosterone-free-and-weakly-bound](https://www.labcorp.com/test-menu/35486/testosterone-free-and-weakly-bound)
Testosterone, Free and Weakly Bound

Free and weakly bound testosterone (FWBT),
also referred to as bioavailable testosterone,
is thought to reflect an individual's biologically active,
circulating testosterone.
FWBT includes free testosterone and testosterone that is bound to albumin.
FWBT does not include sex hormone binding globulin-bound testosterone.
The SHBG-bound fraction is biologically inactive because of the high binding affinity of SHBG for testosterone.
The rapid dissociation of “weakly bound” testosterone from albumin results in the availability of essentially all albumin-bound testosterone for steroid-receptor interaction.1
\-----------------

IIRC, to disassociate testosterone from SHBG it takes 30-40 minutes
(could use better info)
\=================================================

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-e-and-oxldl.19187/post-209886>

Attached to my latest labs, there was a lab suggestion of high dose Vit E and betacarotenes in order to reduce my sky high OxLDL.

In this post @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) says that exogenous Vit E should be avoided. [which vit E supplement is based on olive oil](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/which-vit-e-supplement-is-based-on-olive-oil.18252/#post-199313)

Is vitamin E a friend or foe in my case?

### CoolSwede — 2018-03-01T11:01:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vitamin-e-and-oxldl.19187/post-236026>

Ok, so here I am almost one year later, asking the same question since my latest labs once again came back sky high after a trend of decreasing values. 2095 ug/l in a reference range of 20-170 makes me uneasy, to say the least...

All conventional wisdom says "throw in antioxidants" so it is tempting to biohack Vit E and betacarotenes. But at the same time, it feels like hoping a fuel additive in a Ferrari (or rather Volvo) tank would magically fix a blown piston...

### Miguel Mo — 2017-03-08T23:15:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/livestreaming-biohacking-experiment.19188/post-209921>

Hey everyone!
My name is Miguel Antonio Moya,
For the next three months (or longer) I will be doing live experimentation on myself in the form of a 24/7 non stop livestream. The purpose of the project (among others) is to show real-time effects of different health practices, so that consumers dont have to rely on conjecture or literary information.

Some things me and my team have been considering include daily yoga, exercise routines, or daily use of holistic medicines. (I suffer from poor memory, intrusive thoughts, lethargy, GI disorders related to parasites and candid and more)

I was thinking that there might be a chance that someone here could offer me some feedback or ideas for this project. Since biohacking is still kind of a fringe topic, I'd love to expose the potentials of this technology. What would you like to have me test out?

thanks for reading and feel free to ask me any questions for clarification.
Im looking forward to hearing back from you, I know you'll have something inspiring to say!

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-09T11:28:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nijas-journal-depression-fatigue-obesity.19189/post-209934>

Exercise \*\*\*\*\*\*\*\* I started doing HIIT 20 min everyday.

**_As well as any beneficial effects of exercise, it is a cause of inflammation, if you are already inflamed and you don't feel refreshed after 10 hours sleep, its unlikely you are entering the regeneration phase of sleep, maybe consider swapping this for another gentle barefoot walk in the sunshine. Collect energy in the form of photons and electrons rather than spending energy you don't have with_** _**HIIT?**_

Mediation mindfulness . I recently started mindfulness meditation 4 day a week.

**_Are you meditating outside, grounded and under natural light?_**

Intermittent fasting 16:8.

_**I think intermittent fasting is a natural progression as health improves. If your circadian rhythm is out of whack, maybe consider eating 3 times a day, with breakfast within half hour of waking, and don't eat your last meal after sunset, or if sunset is early at your latitude, don't eat within 4 hours of going to bed. It will help reset your body clock. How much seafood are you eating? Think of food as a source of electrons and photons, as you are able to capture, store and use them from other sources (such as grounding and sunlight), and your body clock is working properly, and sleep has improved, the need for food diminishes, and you should find that IFing becomes a natural progression. If you IF when you are already struggling, and are trying to compensate for missing nutrients with supplements that may not reach where they are supposed to go, or interfere with signalling, it can waste a lot of time and money (expensive wee or poo syndrome ) and - particularly as a female- could make you worse.**_

Please suggest if I ould do something else and any other tools which ould give me better result.

Can I do CT with putting ice on my eyes and face in a bowl ? If I use this vest and have ie pak for 2--30 min will this be CT?

_**There are cheaper ways than a coolfatburner.  I would suggest that before you make any decisions about expensive purchases, that you work on doing enough of the free stuff to lift the brain fog. You then have a better chance of making better decisions.**_
**_Other cheapies to look at:
Have you hacked your bedroom - how dark is it when you go to bed? How cool is it? Is there any sources of nnemf from clocks, gadgets etc? Assuming yours is switched off at night, what wifi signals can you pick up from neighbours?

What's your water intake like?

Light Water Magnetism ....Sleep .... Take the free or inexpensive baby steps first
Best of luck_**

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-10T09:41:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nijas-journal-depression-fatigue-obesity.19189/post-210024>

> [nija said:](/goto/post?id=210022)
>
> I have noticed one things sine few days .. I have severe itchy runny eyes almost 3/4 of the day. It is very annoying .
> Is it something alergy ?

It could be a response to the sudden change of light exposure, that settles down, but be careful of any sungazing routine. I look directly towards the sun at dawn and at dusk, but to the side of the sun between times. Also I make sure I blink (I made a mistake with that one ) .......It will help keep the eye surface wet and cool, and I think my eyelids are a built in pulse machine .....  If you switch your meditation routine to outside, facing the sun (or to the side if its reached a powerful angle or intensity) with eyes closed, you are still getting the sun through the closed lids into the bloodstream in the eyelids, and the deeper frequencies and NIR can still get through to the eyeball. At the start I find myself moving my head and neck as if I was scanning the sky with my eyes closed, then shrugging my shoulders and taking some deeper (photon) breaths, I find it really relaxing at the start and I get into the meditative state quicker. So by switching that one routine to outside I have an extra photon harvesting session, through the eyes, exposed skin and my lungs. If I take my plate of food outside and eat it there, I'm adding another short grounding and photon harvesting session. And its all free .......and I do love a photonic bargain!

### nija — 2017-03-10T20:30:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nijas-journal-depression-fatigue-obesity.19189/post-210064>

@[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) gave reference of this

[http://hillbillyhannah.blogspot.com/2012/08/answers-mthfr-and-pai-1.html](http://hillbillyhannah.blogspot.com/2012/08/answers-mthfr-and-pai-1.html)

**Sunday, August 26, 2012**
**Answers - MTHFR and PAI-1**

Well, we finally have some answers about what likely happened to Jacob. My recent bloodwork revealed I have two blood clotting disorders. I am heterozygous for MTHFR and **PAI-1 4g/4g**. These are caused by genetic mutations, and heterozygous means I have one copy of each muation which means I inherited one copy from either my mom or dad. I know it's kinda confusing. Both of these are definitely associated with adverse pregnancy outcomes, such as reoccurent misccariage, stillbirth, placental abruption, pre-eclampsia, etc.

While there were no obvious clots in the umblical cord or my placenta, these conditions can cause microclots that aren't necessarily seen by visual inspection. This likely explains the small areas of blood loss on my placenta From what I understand, the MTHFR makes me more prone to clots, and the PAI means I don't break clots down well once they form. There is no cure for either one, but they are treatable. In a subsequent pregnancy, I will take baby asprin and most likely lovenox injections, which is a blood thinner similar to heperin. I read somewhere that the lovenox does not cross the placenta but allows proper bloodflow to the placenta. The asprin, apparently, does cross the placenta and keeps blood clots from forming in the baby. I will also need extra folate and specific B vitamins to treat the MTHFR. I will be on this and possibly even baby asprin for the rest of my life. Here is more info about MTHFR. It probably does a better job explaining it than I can.

\*\*\*\*\*\*

What is MTHFR?

MTHFR - (Methylene-tetra-hydro-folate-reductase) is an enzyme found in the cells of our body. It is needed to metabolize (break down) homocysteine, an amino acid found in the proteins you eat. Elevated homocysteine levels have been associated with fetal neural tube defects (i.e., spinal Bifida) and miscarriage. An elevated homocysteine level can also indicate a increased risk factor for blood clots, arteriosclerosis (hardening of arteries) and strokes in both men and women. The metabolism of homocysteine depends on several enzymes, one of which is MTHFR, along with B vitamins and folic acid. In other words, B vitamins, folic acid and MTHFR are necessary to keep homocysteine levels within normal limits.

Sometimes your MTHFR has a genetic defect. These defects are fairly common and its estimated by some studies that up to 60% of the population have one MTHFR mutation or another.

There are two common genetic MTHFR mutations. If you have one mutation only, you are heterozygous. It is reported that nearly half the population is MTHFR heterozygous. - it is nothing abnormal. If you have two copies of the same mutation, you are homozygous. Homozygotes can have elevated homocysteine levels in maternal blood and amniotic fluid. If you have one copy each of the two different mutations, you are compound heterozygotes. Compound heterozygotes have the same treatment and risk level as homozygotes. If you have neither mutation, you are negative and don't need to worry about MTHFR.

There is NO CURE for MTHFR mutation. If you have the mutation, that's the way your body is made. You can, however, lessen any risk factors you have by taking B vitamins, Folic acid and baby aspirin in doses prescribed by your doctor. By taking these agents, you can lower your homocysteine levels and therefor your risk for the conditions above.

MTHFR mutations are hereditary. Families with significant early cardiovascular disease or recurrent pregnancy loss might benefit from clinical investigation.

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

I"ve learned the MTHFR is also linked to many other health issues not related to pregnancy. So, if you're a nerd like me and want to read more about it this website has alot of info. [http://mthfr.net/](http://mthfr.net/). There are specific guidlines on the proper forms of folate and B Vitamins, people with mtfhr need. They are not created equal.

As for the PAI, I've had a hard time finding good information on it in layman's terms. The information below is the easiest to understand that I've come across, and it's still not a leisurely read

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

**First of all, what is PAI-1?** The best way to explain PAI-1 is to describe what it does. The blood clotting system is made up of many different factors, some which cause a clot to form and others which cause the clot to breakdown, and these coexist in a very delicate balance. When the blood clotting pathways are activated, fibrinogen, a soluble plasma glycoprotein, is polymerized to form fibrin which is then cross-linked by the action of factor XIII to form the ‘clot’. Under normal circumstances, as soon as a clot forms, it begins to be broken down by other plasma factors. Specifically, a substance called tissue plasminogen activator (tPA) converts an inactive plasma protein, plasminogen, to the active substance, plasmin, which then plays a critical role in the breakdown of fibrin (fibrinolysis), thereby ‘dissolving’ the clot. (Interestingly, and perhaps germane to our discussion at some later point, plasmin also plays important roles in ovulation, cell migration, and epithelial cell differentiation).

PAI-1 fits into this balance as the primary _inhibitor_ of tPA and other ‘plasminogen activators’ in the blood. To put its role in perspective, by inhibiting tPA, PAI-1 prevents the activation of plasminogen, thereby controlling the rate and extent of fibrin degradation (clot break down, or fibrinolysis) that occurs. Overactivity of PAI-1 will therefore lead to a tendency to form (or maintain) clots and an underactivity will result in an increased risk for bleeding. (With regard to other known functions of plasmin mentioned above, overactivity of PAI-1 might then also impair ovulation, cell migration, and epithelial cell differentiation).

Control of PAI-1 production is complex, but it is at least partly determined on a genetic basis. Certain polymorphisms of **PAI-1, 4G/4G** and 4G/5G, are associated with increased blood concentrations of PAI-1. Elevated PAI-1 levels have been correlated with risk for both arterial and venous thromboembolic conditions (e.g., deep venous thrombosis, pulmonary embolism, and stroke) and atherosclerotic disease (.eg., coronary and carotid artery disease), especially if other genetic (e.g., factor V Leiden; prothrombin G20210A; MTHFR polymorphisms; protein C, protein S, and antithrombin III deficiencies) or inherited (e.g., antiphospholipid antibodies; lupus anticoagulant; anti-beta-1-glycoprotein) [thrombophilias](http://www.healthline.com/blogs/pregnancy_childbirth/2007/04/recurrent-early-pregnancy-loss-7.html) are also present. Individuals with insulin resistance syndromes and diabetes mellitus frequently have elevated PAI-1 levels. Obesity and hyperlipidemia are also associated with elevated PAI-1 and under certain circumstances, weight reduction and/or reduction in cholesterol and triglycerides, can lead to reduction in PAI-1 levels.

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

**This website has much more infomation on PAI and how it relates to pregnancy.** Again, this isn't pleasure reading, but it is fairly understandable [http://www.healthline.com/health-bl...or-1-pai-1-role-adverse-pregnancy-outcome-2-l](http://www.healthline.com/health-blogs/fruit-womb/plasminogen-activator-inhibitor-1-pai-1-role-adverse-pregnancy-outcome-2-l).

I'm sorry this post is so full of information, but I put it up here so my family and friends will better understand and also for any other women who are effected by these issues.

Ok, so how do I feel about all this? The day I received the news was a bit overwhelming. I was thankful to have an answer, true facts to work with, but also kinda discouraged. I mean who wants to find out there is something else wrong with you. Now, that I've had about a week to think it over and to read more about these disorders I am feeling more hopeful. I've read about and heard about many women with blood clotting issues that have successful pregnancies. I try to remind myself that I was able to carry Jacob for 6 months with no added supplementation or medication, so I should be able to carry full-term the next time around. I sooooo wish I knew about all this a year ago because I know my sweet boy would still be alive if I had known....if only....if only....if only.

It probably seems like I've done alot of research, but I haven't really. I am careful about how much I focus on it. That's another reason it's taken over a week to share this. I must be wise and prepare my body the best I can for another pregnancy, but somedays being pregnant again is the farthest thing from my mind. How do you grieve one baby and prepare for another? It's tough, and it's not how it's supposed to be.

I must be wise and prepare my body the best I can for another pregnancy, but some days being pregnant again is the farthest thing from my mind. How do you grieve one baby and prepare for another? It's tough, and it's not how it's supposed to be."_

> [nija said:](/goto/post?id=210225)
>
> I am planning to take selenium and lugol iodine

I'm not saying I'm right, and I'm not in your shoes .....

I was saddened by your story of losing Jacob, and in your shoes if I wanted to get better and/or be healthy enough to conceive and have a healthy pregnancy, these wouldn't be a priority. (I had 2 early miscarriages before having children at 37 and one month off being 40. I wish I'd known then some of the things I know now, especially for DS2). My aim would be to get my circadian rhythm right, stop my sleep from sucking, focus on photons and electrons and eat a diet that supports not only getting healthier, but the _signalling_ that tells my body that the environment is right to support not only conception, but pregnancy, childbirth and breastfeeding. Animals mate at a certain time in the light cycle in _anticipation_ of abundance to come .... In winter and hibernation they live of, and provide their young, with abundance they have stored as fat (in human terms I think of it as leptin sensitivity allowing release of fat during darkness/cold). If I was IFing when I needed nutrients or food electrons, whilst my circadian rhythm and sleep sucked, it would be a stress signal to my body. I don't see how taking a supplement would help switch that signal off. Eating 3 meals a day in line with and to help reset the circadian rhythm, making at least one DHA and iodine rich (and which comes in its natural package with selenium etc), is a signal that comes in a package that my body recognises and knows what to do with _if_ my cells aren't too screwed up from nnemf, blue light, dehydration, circadian rhythm problems etc. I think its also an abundance signal that is missing with a supplement. With a better circadian rhythm comes better sleep - again a good signal. My advice is look at your body as a whole, put in place what matters most, _experience_ the difference it makes in how it makes you look, think and feel. If it were me, I'd give it 3 months, learning as much as I can in the meantime, (especially about mitochondrial heteroplasmy), knowing that I will be in a better position to make better decisions about if, and what to supplement. If I was on them, and made an informed choice to keep on with them, I wouldn't start fiddling with changing them this early in my journey, I'd run the risk of not _experiencing_ the true value of other natural changes I had made, it might sidetrack me away from what really matters, I wouldn't know what supplements were working and what weren't, what effects they were having on feedback loops I didn't yet understand, or how much of what I was taking was just finishing up down the toilet, without doing its job.

Anyway, my thoughts are with you, just offering a different perspective.

To use body fat for fuel your insulin must be low.
Insulin is low when it is in low single digit numbers.
2-3 is ok.
To get to such a low insulin numbers you have to eat infrequently.
After a while 16 or more hours from last meal does that job.
That is called IF. Intermediate Fasting.
Faster way to loose weight is to eat once a day, or EOD or less often.
You can also stop eating for longer period of times.

In many religions there are recommended periods of fasting.
This days people eat without regards to religious recommendations.

Eastern Orthodox Catholics have 200 days in a year when they observe eating restrictions.
There is a lots of Eastern Orthodox Catholics in Mediterranean area.
Mistakenly Mediterranean type of eating got a fame as to be healthy for weight watchers.
The mistake is that it is not about type of food but rather about infrequent eating.

my first thought is mold. the only way to know for sure if it's mold or lyme is a special MRI called NeuroQuant. You'd be amazed how many people are infected with mold. Mold includes all yeast, fungus, spores and more...... Mold is the link to a large number of diseases - parkinson's, cancer, and so on. Nearly all of the food you eat is contaminated with mold. From cereal, nuts, wine to you name it.

Mold is found in nearly all medications. The antidepressant medication you're on is loaded with mold and fluoride. Or other meds will feed mold such as Progesterone. Something nearly all women are put on at some point. The list of meds is beyond shocking.

Mold will fry your leptin levels along nearly all other hormones in some fashion. Just a thought. You can test your home, car, and work with a test you can order yourself called ERMI. May need help reading the results.

> You can also stop eating for longer period of times.
> In many religions there are recommended periods of fasting.
> This days people eat without regards to religious recommendations.
> Eastern Orthodox Catholics have 200 days in a year when they observe eating restrictions.
> There is a lots of Eastern Orthodox Catholics in Mediterranean area.

>
> I am wearing hat to protect my eyes and I do sun-gazing 30 min before sunset or 30 min after sunrise .
> Please suggest what can I do to loose weight ?

I have a different perspective to Jan about losing weight. From mine, I say give it time. The more screwed up your metabolism when you start, the longer it takes to start. When I understood the principles of Jack's teaching at the start of my journey I knew that from my starting point, and at my age, that I wasn't going to heal immediately. I don't believe losing weight _healthily_ can start until the circadian rhythm is working well on an ongoing basis, reading both the signals from the body at that moment in time, and from the seasonal signals, predicting future abundance or lack of it. A mammal's reproductive cycle is geared to the seasonal light cycle.

Anyone can lose weight by not eating at all, or IFing, or following a religious fasting pattern originating from a particular latitude, or however they do it. But the question is - should they? If the circadian cycle is healthy, leptin receptors are operating properly, and its done in line with the seasonal food cycles, the results might be good, particularly for a male. But does that make it right for a female? Done when the brain's leptin receptors can't get the signal, that same regime is the signalling equivalent of a famine, and - like any mammal - your hormonal system will respond accordingly. Females can't outsmart their fat cells ....Nature has built female fat cells differently because females are what carry the next generation....They are operating on the environment they have experienced in the past season, on the information they are getting in the moment, and from the light cycle in their particular latitude, can forecast future abundance that will enable them to put on any weight necessary to carry them - and any offspring - through a winter. Wherever mammals live on the planet, they need the signals from their environment to create the right physiology for reproduction. In a high quantum yield (say nearer the equator), an animal will not need to store fat in the same way as one nearer the poles.

There is a maximum number of births a female mammal can have during their reproductive lifetime, assuming the environmental conditions are right to reproduce every season. For males its different, which is why I think men's fat cells operate differently. In evolutionary terms in a long term famine, male mammals don't have a female's fat system, so are more likely to die first. But of those that survive, one male can father offspring with a lot of females.....

My advice is make friends with your fat cells ..... and give them the environmental signals available at your latitude. As your circadian rhythm and leptin sensitivity improves, I think you will find that body composition improves gradually over time, even if the scale doesn't move. (More muscle? Less fat?) Your leptin receptors will get the message that actually you are holding too much fat for this time of year at your latitude, and your fat cells will respond accordingly. Just a different perspective ....

11\. Make getting well your focus. Looking at things you cannot do yet will take energy away from your core job which is to fix yourself.

The mechanism of resonance transfer of energy between unlike molecules, without (or, with only partially) overlapping absorption bands, was analyzed in 1948 by the German physicist Theodor Förster. Chlorophyll a and b work together because they have an overlapped absorption spectra. The same thing is true for the respiratory proteins. People do not see nature's design because they are ignorant of light's effects on living systems. The picture below shows you that chlorophyll a absorbs most in the purple and red light regions of the visible spectrum. This is UV and IR light. Chlorophyll b is shifted to the right in the UVA and blue range and into green and yellow.

The absorbed quantum (exciton) belongs, at first, to one pigment molecule only. However, in the process of vibrational energy dissipation occurs via internal conversion in the excited electronic state of the "donor" pigment (say Chl b), states are reached that are in resonance with certain (strongly vibrating: think 100 Hz in mitochondria) states of the "acceptor" chlorophyll a. This resonance is what makes energy transfer possible. So when the 100 Hz vibration is absent electron transfer suffers. This lowers the amount of ATP that can be made from light and cells seek other sources of electrons from foods. This is the quantum basis of obesity from a quantum perspective. It is 180 degrees in opposition to the energy excess idea of over eating over energized. Obesity is an energy deficit disease of mitochondria and not a food excess. This is why healthcare today struggles to help people who are overweight lose weight. Buying diet foods made by man makes this harder but it does make for a great long term business with predictable cash flow.

> [nija said:](/goto/post?id=212992)
>
> Levothyroxin 100 mg 20 day before test .. not regular as well . I forgot many days

To do valid testing you have to be on steady dose for about a month.
Other than that, Levothyroxin=T4 often does not work.
The thyroid hormone that is active is FreeT3.
When you take T4, there is assumption made that you will be able to convert T4---->T3
Many peoples cannot do this conversion.
That is why it is better to use NTH(Natural Thyroid Hormone).
NTH contains T3 and T4.
T3 will always be useful. With T4 there still may be some questions.

You have to start somewhere.
When you would start using NTH as I suggested above, do not use T4 with it.
Chew the pills before swallowing.
Take it soon after you get of the bed, about same time each day.

//

### Scompy — 2017-05-07T05:23:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nijas-journal-depression-fatigue-obesity.19189/post-213568>

> [nija said:](/goto/post?id=210374)
>
> @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/)
>
> I have hard time ordering any of the test recommended by you .I spoke to my primary care and she said those test are not necessary .
> Then I took an appointment with an another doctor and she said I should go to some specialist to order those test .
> I paid the consultation fee and still she did not agree to order the test .
> I am really not sure how do I do it.

Hi Nija,
The best tool in my fat-burning toolbox has been intermittent fasting with x2 big meals per day. I start my day with about 40g+ of protein...usually something like 4 pastured eggs with some seafood. Then I have a very late lunch with another large meal... I typically go from 2pm to 7am the next morning for a fast. Some women that have tried this have bad results for whatever individual reasons they have found. Some have required more acclimation to a longer fast.

I'll generally get 5 days of intermittent fasting in a week. At my age of 47, if I don't do at least 4 days of week of fasting, it's very hard for me to shed any fat I want gone. Like many of us, I was once 280 pounds, and I'm 180 today, but I have been doing some biohacks to increase my bodyfat at the moment... I should be at 160 pound optimally and was that way last year. I always include an avocado in my last meal of the day to keep my potassium levels optimal during the 18 hour fasting time. The avocado helps to promote a bit more of a fat-burn about 12+ hours into the fast. I also try to minimize sugars at the last meal of the day so I don't get hungry later in the day and it also helps to promote fat-burning after insulin baselines.

I also incorporate a bit of lighter, anaerobic exercise with leg lifts, dumbbells, squats, etc. I like to do it in the evening around 4 to 7pm a few hours after that last meal at 2pm... this helps insulin get baselined enough in case the last meal pushed it up (like too much protein). I've found even 20 grams of protein at that last meal can trigger my hunger depending on the added carbs (not enough fiber to slow down the GI response) at the meal. Plus in the evening before bed, the lack of fuel in the stomach really helps me to get a solid sleep with blue-blocking. The lack of energy promotes some CT in the evening naturally because there's less thermogenesis in the body from the missing food, and often do face-plunges or splashes if I don't' take a cold shower...

Intermittent fasting is my best tool over several weeks to really target fat from the body from what I've found personally. I've also weaved in ketosis for about 7 years now. The synergy also works extremely well, but often too good if I push the blood ketones too high. So I've found that in the spring/summer/fall, I like to do more intermittent fasting with some carbs as they come into season, then push keto all the way in the winter and limit the fasting. However, if I'm looking to lose more bodyfat, I know I have both tools in my toolbox I can do at the same time for a powerful 1+2 combo...

### caroline — 2017-05-07T05:59:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nijas-journal-depression-fatigue-obesity.19189/post-213572>

Thanks Scompy for telling us how you manage to keep the extra pounds away. It seems so much harder for women to figure this out ....at least it is for me.

So many women here are struggling with getting their weight down and then keeping it down. Maybe we want the extra pounds gone now?
Men generally seem to shed extra weight pretty fast on the leptin RX ......women - not so much altho our bulk does go down.

Scompy - what are your thoughts on a BPC?

I find if I have a BPC in the morning and then one at 12:30 and then lunch at 3:30 - 4:00pm I do pretty well. I do think that I have to watch the fat intake with the BPC - I think that messes me up.

It is such a fine line ......I don't want too much fat, I don't want too much protein, I do better with no fruit \[I love fruit\] and some veg don't agree with me - like broccoli etc.

and then ..... throw in for good measure .....all the yummy cooking I do for my partner! He just naturally uses food for fuel. He is very active and his body works so well so he needs a really good meal for fuel ....then he digests it - works hard ...and then needs more fuel!

He cut out sugar and starting eating way better meals .... and has lost 10K. He is eating a lot more good fats and often his weight drops by a couple of K. - and then I worry he is losing too much. Tony can even have the odd cookie ....he does spend a lot of the day outside and does stretches on the wet grass. Tony is a walking advert for Jack and the three legged stool!

I hope other women here will share their experiences ......... the good, the bad and the ugly so we can all learn from each other.

I've watched a lot of Stephanie "The Business" Person on youtube for many years. She's 49 years old and has a body of a 20 year old from the ketosis she's been doing for the past 10 years. She's also had terrible injuries in the past with her knees so she does mostly upperbody work. Stephanie also has a lot of advice for women.

She's more of a year-long advocate of ketosis, but she also gets outside a great deal and knows exactly what to eat and what to avoid to spike insulin. She has her circadian rhythm DOWN, and has advocated (in many past videos) that people will stall trying to lose fat and get ripped if they miss that element. She puts Chronobiology first in her routine or it bumps her out of being ketotic. I may have to weave in some ketosis now that my hack is done to get back down to 160 by summer, but then I'll still eat lower sugar, lower GI foods when they come into season.

### Sue-UK — 2017-05-07T14:51:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nijas-journal-depression-fatigue-obesity.19189/post-213588>

I was looking back at the original leptin RX blogs this morning, and one thing that struck me was that since they were originally written the nnemf and blue light problem has escalated much further .... and women are naturally more environmentally sensitive. I think women tying in eliminating as much as they can and mitigating more could make the difference between success or failure, both in terms of weight control, and prior to menopause, getting the environment right to be able to conceive and have healthy children if they choose to. I think the quiz in Reality 5 can be a game changer for helping women using the leptin RX . But from one of the blogs: "IFing is worthless in LR states". Add in a bad environment and the result is .......? Already stressed cells and a starvation signal? Worse for women because they are already more environmentally sensitive than men?

Also if only 1/3 of the electrons come from food, then if I can't get the 2/3 from the natural environment for any reason, missing or reducing the 1/3 doesn't do me any favours. For instance if I had to work all day in a nnemf hell and IF'd, for me as a woman it would be a recipe for a disaster. Yes I could IF and lose weight faster, but at what cost? What if I was also leptin resistant? Being post menopausal makes it more difficult to lose weight, but whatever the short term gain in terms of weight loss, what would 10 years of permanent ketosis do to my mitochondria in the long term when I live in an area with definite seasons? I'm nearly 60, and I'm not interested in aiming for a body of a woman 30 years younger, that's façade stuff ... My brain and heart mitochondria are more important, if weight loss takes longer so be it.

I'll take optimal over physique anyday now. But it just so happens, I haven't gained any post menopausal fat, but about 10 lbs of lean muscle and I contribute that to my love affair with Nature and being so faithful and trusting in it....as I said before, I surrendered my heart to nature and as a result, remarkable healing has taken place. It's a beautiful relationship!

Intermittent fasting has worked well for me here. One, possibly two meals per day. My large one about 9 or 10 am and just something small in the early afternoon, if at all. I eat 90% of the time connected to Nature too. I took that advice and ran with it!

### JanSz — 2017-05-09T00:34:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nijas-journal-depression-fatigue-obesity.19189/post-213697>

> [nija said:](/goto/post?id=213684)
>
> yesterday I have spent 4 hours walking in sea beach .I have felt amazing and with lot of energy .
> It was very warm and felt so much rejuvenated . I would like to know -- what is the best time to be in when UV index is low or high .
> I try to in sun morning sunrise when UV index is less than 3 . I am california , San francisco(CA 94404) where UV index is 6 already by 9 -10 am .
> Please suggest the timing for light for better effetive

If your time on the sun is limited,
best time is two hours before and two hours after solar noon.

The thing is about solar noon.
I summer, when we have Daylight Saving Time (DST)
clocks are moved one hour ahead (of solar noon)
So, when we are under DST the best time is 11AM-3PM
other times 10AM-2PM

...........................
Important.
Skin must be hardened to accept prolonged sun exposure.
When having skin that have not seen sun in ages,
one must take one whole month of incremental increases to build skin resistance to sun.

.

### Sue-UK — 2017-05-09T09:56:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nijas-journal-depression-fatigue-obesity.19189/post-213725>

> [nija said:](/goto/post?id=213684)
>
> yesterday I have spent 4 hours walking in sea beach .I have felt amazing and with lot of energy .
> It was very warm and felt so much rejuvenated . I would like to know -- what is the best time to be in when UV index is low or high .
> I try to in sun morning sunrise when UV index is less than 3 . I am california , San francisco(CA 94404) where UV index is 6 already by 9 -10 am .
> Please suggest the timing for light for better effetive

I think of the sun both as a clock, and a battery recharger .... For the clock function to keep my circadian rhythm on time it helps me to get out in the light at regular times of the day, even if its only for a few minutes, and dressed. My eyes need the changing signals. I want my brain to go through its sunrise, its midmorning, its noon (all crescendoing frequencies), then the decrescending frequencies of mid afternoon, sunset ... and then its darkness night time signalling. If I occasionally miss the sunrise because I've slept in, where I am the UV doesn't come in until later, and I'm still going through the rest of the day cycle, but the ideal for me is regularly resetting my clock during the day, preferably starting at sunrise, because that has the best chance of switching the right hormones on and off at the right time. My brain isn't just looking for frequency, its _change_ of frequency. Even though your UV comes in early, at sunrise it still gets the VIS+IR signal to help optimise hormones. What I'm aiming to avoid is later UV turning hormones off that haven't been turned on properly by the VIS+IR signal ....  So if I was working indoors all day, having seen the sunrise, I would also try and have a few minutes outside at regular intervals (coffee break, lunch etc) for the sun clock to give me a circadian update, telling me what's _changed_ .... There's a good visual here
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/upload_2017-5-9_7-9-5-png.4813/)
I also think the pre UV signalling and exposure is important for preparing the body to efficiently handle the coming UV battery charging (I think my brain registers the crescendoing frequencies - get ready for some serious energy gathering gang, UV is on the way .....).

### drezy — 2017-05-09T21:31:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nijas-journal-depression-fatigue-obesity.19189/post-213765>

As a mea culpa for hijacking your journal earlier, I'll go ahead and act appropriately now.

I liked this forum post from @[Mystic Rose60](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/14425/) concerning her mom's BP:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...pressure-stress-and-anger-low-dopamine.17784/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/after-years-of-high-blood-pressure-stress-and-anger-low-dopamine.17784/)

If an elderly lady is able to reverse this than the odds for a young woman like you look good as long as you keep an eye out on your environment.

I had issues with BP that traced back to methylation & high homocysteine. I thought I had corrected it and that going from high to not-so high was all I was going to get because it was in my SNPs. A little over 2 months into regularly getting UV (using [dminder](http://dminder.ontometrics.com) to help keep me methodical) I started getting this "weird" feeling more and more. It wasn't painful or annoying, but just felt different. After a week or so I realized that my hands were warm now all the time. I tested and my BP was 120/70. That's something I had given up on earlier. The "weird" feeling is how I'm supposed to feel.

I can't separate the causation to either just UV or just CT since I started them both at about the same time.

Notes based on your journal:

1-3 minutes(I do at least 10 to be safe) of morning sun is enough to kick off your SCN

If you can restructure your day a little to maximize UV exposure that'll help. FWIW today UV kinda starts at you location a little before 11AM, is maximum at 3:06, and ends at 7:25.

Your skin type requires more UV than mine(dminder can help you a bit with timing). My son's skin is essentially imported from China via his mom's genes. He can spend hours in the sun no problem. I used to bo the white guy that would turn red lobster color after 35 minutes. I've fixed that too. Just keep this in mind. My son jokes about the sun being racist, but when my wife and I do our naked-as-the-day-we-were-born sunbathing and take skin types into account my Polish & Swiss butt usually tops up at about 35 minutes these days and she needs about 1 hour.

The too much information section:

Using empirical knowledge from @[Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) ( [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...with-a-brain-on-fire.19343/page-2#post-213003](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/journal-of-a-man-with-a-brain-on-fire.19343/page-2#post-213003) ) and a good suggestion from @[SatoriHeart](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19303/) we put lugol's infused coconut oil on our respective bits (so to speak). If you have the privacy it's worth trying.

There were many benefits I can detail, but the big one is that my wife's bad sleep that she's been struggling with before we met is gone. She sleeps like a baby 90% of the time now. As a husband, having a well rested wife is a big deal!

# Thread: I think I'm turning into a mitochondriac
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-think-im-turning-into-a-mitochondriac.19191/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### drezy — 2017-03-09T18:36:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-think-im-turning-into-a-mitochondriac.19191/post-209960>

Hi all. I've been lurking in these forums and reading and viewing all things Doc Kruse has to say. Yep, with all that reading I think I've gone and finished the whole internet! \[\*spoilers\*\]The middle was a little slow but it has a great ending and I'm hoping maybe for a sequel!

Joking aside, I've been lucky enough to just be in "health optimizing mode" after some 40 year old weight gain. I've flowed from a Paleo diet into a more ketogenic diet over the last 10 years and I think that has helped me maintain my health.

Full disclosure: my Christmas into New Years season got a bit boozy and I packed on same unwanted pounds. I reengaged intermittent fasting, which helped, but one thing blew me away. I started walking 5-10 miles a day outside. My extra weight flew off. It didn't make sense to me since it was zero exertion and I was walking at a speed that even grandmas would point and laugh at.

The number of calories I was burning should have been nowhere near the weight loss I was experiencing. I hit the internet and that's why I'm here now. The only factor that changed for me (as a software engineer) was the light. I stumbled across Doc Kruse and this community backwards by digging through the internet with the question "why would light make a difference?"

An Epi-Paleo head explosion ensued!

I'm glad that I did that search. I've now added 3x / week CT , blue blockers, way more shellfish and other changes into my life with more changes pending and I'm feeling great.

Thanks Doc Kruse and all who are in this forum. It's a life changer.

Weight loss pic below(Don't freak at the "BMI" I'm 6'4" with a fit build and ~ 15% bodyfat now):

# Thread: Brain disease/chronic fatigue/ocd diet hack
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-disease-chronic-fatigue-ocd-diet-hack.19193/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Chaostheory — 2017-03-10T03:49:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-disease-chronic-fatigue-ocd-diet-hack.19193/post-210003>

So I've recently come to the realization that my disease is probably mitochondrial in nature. I have testing that shows it, mito hacks make me feel stronger mentally...but I'm stuck on what to do to get more resilient so I can continue to heal versus fall back into my old habits.

The other day and yesterday I went and looked at the sunrise, and went on a walk. I stood barefoot outside and waded through a local lake. I also spent $5 to be in an infared tanning bed for 30 mins (this felt really good).

Most of my lab tests are fairly normal, but they were done after receiving fecal transplants, which I think may have normalized a lot of things in my body. However, I do still have gut inflammation, adrenal burnout, and mitochondrial issues.

I have a lot of signs of choline deficiency. I'm going to try duck eggs (intolerant to regular eggs) as well as a phosphatidylcholine supplement or maybe MSM (I think I'll do MSM). Phosphatidylserine from soy has helped a little in the past. I'll also supplement uridine. I've also been taking resistant starch for the butyrate (from potato starch). Before going to a doctor, I was trying some crazy things like nsi-189 which didn't work as well as I'd hoped.

I've been occasionally taking acetyl-l-carnitine and alpha-lipoic-acid (is also in one of my blood sugar supplements).

Some hacks I've been thinking of trying: restore4life to help arrest the gut issue, creatine for overall body energy/strength, and I really need something to help with my food cravings/addiction and I was thinking forskolin might be able to help me with that as well as help with thyroid (feel free to tell me other ways to do this).

The infared made me feel better and more resilient to the cold lake, but is being in a tanning bed healthy? I'm not sure. I'll probably just buy an infared reptile lamp anyway so I don't have to go to the tanning salon except maybe for their uva bed.

One question: the only big things I've changed recently are pqq versus coq10 for the last week (ran out of coq10 & had some pqq lying around), infrared bed for 30 mins one day plus some outside sun last few days, and wading through a local lake the last few days. I'm starting to feel a little more alive, or aware, really. I'm not entirely sure, but I think the lake is what did the most for me. What do you think?

The three things I'm adding into my RX: reconnecting with busy friends I've been avoiding, tai chi or qui gong to move things through my body, and maybe babysitting or some kind of social work so I can feel good helping others. Also I printed out the Change RX and skimmed over that.

Oh, another thing I've been doing based off of what I watched on a youtube video: Primal Play. It's specific physical exercises that are good for your brain, I found it through the terry wahl's website.

Would just a regular infrared reptile lamp from a pet store be good enough as far as infrared goes?

I also have been taking magcalm at night and doing some epsom salt baths, and think I may be low on potassium since i’m getting muscle twitches. How much potassium should i take and what brand? Should I also be supplementing boron? I’m working up to 37.5 mg iodine (was low in it since i live in michigan), and some selenium.

My final idea for my brain is to put LLLT on my thyroid and brain to help for myelination purposes. Would that be better than the infrared?

### matthewsb77 — 2017-03-15T20:03:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-disease-chronic-fatigue-ocd-diet-hack.19193/post-210424>

Hey Chaostheory! Thanks for the post. Sounds like we're in a similar position, though I'm a little worse off than you. I'm learning a ton and would love help if I can.

First, a question for you: what tests are showing that your mitochondria aren't working properly? I mean, what was measured? Also, what kind of UV bed were you using? Did it have UVA, -B, and -C?

Also curious about the lake -- what is the benefit there? I'm genuinely curious, as I don't think I've gotten that far in Kruse's discussions yet.

As far as NIR light therapy goes, look at what Dr. Lawrence Wilson suggests on this for a potential design. He also has a whole book on the subject of NIR therapy, but you've gotta ignore the Nutritional Balancing system he advocates. (I've used it, it's good, but a couple decades behind all the EMF and other modern problems.) [http://drlwilson.com/SAUNAS/SAUNA PLANS.htm](http://drlwilson.com/SAUNAS/SAUNA%20PLANS.htm) If you don't want to or can't build a whole unit, you can use one Sylvania 250W red heat lamp. I highly recommend doing a full four-bulb set-up, though (not three as might be shown in Dr. L's plans). It makes a huge difference to get your whole body exposed. And do it naked. More fun, and more exposure. I felt a huge difference between one lamp and four, seriously. And if you can do the full set-up, get this light bulb as well for the UV: [http://www.blacklight.com/items/ftbpa19blb](http://www.blacklight.com/items/ftbpa19blb)

And if you can spend the money, I recommend the Magnetico that Kruse recommends. First INSTANT on it I felt different. The next morning I woke up with a smile on my face for the first time in years. I was so sick when I started it that I sometimes couldn't bathe myself.

I don't know enough about the cholines or LLLT to help you there. I DO know what a biohacker friend of mine says, which is that the laser therapies aren't necessary if you have red bulbs and UV. But that's just one person telling you what one other person has said. . . don't take my word on that.

For iodine, I recommend Nature's Way Kelp. Cheap and effective, though some might disagree with me on it. If you're trying to get boron, Endomet Laboratories has a Calcium/Magnesium/Boron supplement that is relatively inexpensive and works fine. Others may have a negative opinion about Ca/Mg, but it's where I've gotten mine. Also make sure that if there's a thyroid issue, that you're also taking selenium. I can't remember exactly why this is necessary, but you need that and iodine together.

And though it might sound like obsession, people on this forum aren't kidding when they say to manage your light environment. If you aren't already doing it, make sure you take AT LEAST the step of doing morning sunlight right away to set your clock, and blue-blockers, covered skin, and minimal light at night. Some on here say total darkness or red only after sunset. I find that a little extreme because then I would have no social interaction whatsoever (I'm coming out of a phase of being unable to even do staircases, so my interaction comes from the two people I live with, in the evenings). And again, can't say enough for the NIR sauna units. If you or anyone you know has decent hands-on skills for building, you can build the whole thing for $100 or less, including the bulbs. I have a timer added to mine, too, which is nice.

Gut-wise: don't overlook the simple stuff. Sauerkraut, kombucha (sparingly, as it contains a lot of aldehydes), probiotics.

I'm also finding CBD is helping a lot.

EMFs: what's your environment like? Are you grounded? Around computers and cell phone a lot? I'm sure you already know this, but if you're not at least minimizing your EMFs, that can affect your whole system, including your thyroid. I believe I remember reading something about radiation destroying the thyroid? And Kruse talks about how the thyroid can get destroyed by the wrong light, over time. . . so again, refer back to the bit about light environment.

For improving mito capacity and health, you can also look into Magnesium bicarbonate. It's the form of magnesium best suited for improving energy in the mitochondria. My friend Matt Blackburn makes the stuff still, I believe, or you can get it from PristineHydro.

This one's a little "out there" and some would say pseudo-science, so don't do it on its own, but worth looking into: German New Medicine, and German New Medicine thyroid. Google those. You can get a look at some potential emotional conflicts and how they can cause actual rings on the brain (shown in CT scans) and how that affects certain functions of organs in the body. It didn't do anything, but I know some people whose lives totally changed overnight with awareness of a traumatic trigger and how it related to their symptoms.

Muscle twitching can also be about Mg deficiency, I believe. Instead of supplementing potassium long-term and potentially imbalancing something you're not aware of, I'd recommend trying a megadose of K for a couple days (like, too much if you were to take it regularly) and see what changes.

I do Epsom salt baths every few days. I don't know how much they help me.

Hope this helps. I tried to be as detailed and exhaustive as possible, addressing all the points I could, as I really appreciate it when people do the same for me. Much Love.

Thank you for a very informative post!

I have CFS too, and also look for ways to improve.

Looking at your blacklight bulb, I'm thinking maybe one at 365nm, like the one below could be better, since yours goes up to 410nm, which is into the blue, and we want to avoid blue.

[https://www.blacklight.com/items/BL7WLEDBULB](https://www.blacklight.com/items/BL7WLEDBULB)

Yes, as Dr. Wunsch points out below that all shortwave light does damage to the retina, and that naturally sunlight balances ample red and NIR with UV, so it can be prudent to always use red and NIR as well anytime one uses UV indoors, like you do in your setup.

Also a couple of questions re. your blacklight, about how long per day do you use it, distance from eyes, and if you let light from it enter the eyes directly?

### Jack Kruse — 2018-02-21T05:29:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-disease-chronic-fatigue-ocd-diet-hack.19193/post-235259>

5G waveforms: The findings of Dr. Kurt Oughstun and Dr. Richard Albanese on radar technologies which show that mmW (from 5G) might penetrate deep into the body because they create re-radiated waves called Brillouin precursors.

Brillouin precursors show up in Debye media. In thermodynamics and solid state physics, the Debye method is a method developed by Peter Debye in 1912 for estimating the phonon contribution to the specific heat (heat capacity) in a solid. Debye methods now have been extended to liquid crystals like water. Water in the body acts as an anisotropic polarizer. Liquid crystals are examples of anisotropic liquids. Water can be ordered to be a liquid crystal when it is bound to proteins in hydration shells. Anisotropy is the physical property of being directionally dependent, as opposed to isotropy, which implies identical properties in all directions. Water controls the flow of currents using this physical property it contains when it is in a liquid crystalline form. Hydrogen and oxygen alone do not have this ability, so anisotropy is an “emergent” property of water molecule when it is free of deuterium. Life takes massive advantage of this. We also know in disease this breaks down. This occurs in our brain as tau proteins lose electrons and become oxidized, they become denser because they have more protons than electrons within them. This is an unstable state in nature. Things must have a balance of charges and when they don't matter must change. Anytime matter undergoes a phase transition, the matter loses energy and information. This is why 5G is going to spike brain degeneration, mood disorders, mental illness, and prion diseases. Since charges must be balanced, this immediately changes their shape and length of proteins in Debye media (human tissue) to form beta sheets and lose their alpha conformation. That is really what Alzheimer's diseases is. We also know that when water loses its normal dielectric constant (think D, Br– or F–), alpha sheets transform to beta sheets because of the massive shifts in the magnetic moment of the PrPc protein. It also increases the amount of deuterium in cell water which also increases the magnetic moment of water because of the quantum spin of deuterium. Maybe now you can see why I call 5G a coming buffet of deuterium for anything with a human brain. When electrons are lost for any reason at all, the dielectric constant drops and the magnetic moment of the tissue or prion protein in question changes dramatically. This is the quantum thermodynamic change induced by 5G waves I expect to explode soon. When a magnetic moment or field is changed in a tissue, protein, planet, energy drops and the quantum system loses information because entropy rises. This drop, when it occurs in tissues, also induces alpha sheets in prions to transform to beta sheets. This is where neuro-degeneration and autoimmunity begin in all human cases. This is why neurodegeneration is spiking as we move from 1G to a 5G world. The 5G world is the tipping point for our bio-physics based upon my Debye math or probability. All Debye media all have serious dielectric properties as a consequence. The study of dielectric properties concerns storage and dissipation of electric and magnetic energy in materials and tissues. Water is biology's most important dielectric. When the sun hits water its dielectric constant goes from 78-160. 5G waves will interfere with this operation because of the topology of the waves and the power density involved. Dielectrics are important for explaining various phenomena in electronics, optics, solid-state physics, and cell biophysics. Brillouin precursors are visible only at propagation distances such that the impulse response of the medium is essentially determined by the frequency dependence of its absorption and is practically Gaussian. Water has a normal magnetic dipole moment of 1.85 Debye. Polypeptide chains in the α-helical configuration normally have enormous dipole moments. They are upwards of 500 Debye because the individual moments of the peptide bonds are all aligned properly. Beta-sheet amyloids do not have a large dipole moment because they are smaller in size and the shape and their bonds are not aligned properly. This improper bond alignment is what changes the protein length, shape, and mass. Deuterium causes many of these conformational changes. This is why energy and information are lost on the neocortex. This is how cognitive haze or bad memory begins from a quantum perspective. This is why cognition suffers and disease ensues when the beta sheet predominates. This will explode in a 5G world.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Why do we crave oxidizing and dehydrating agents
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-we-crave-oxidizing-and-dehydrating-agents.19194/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### drezy — 2017-03-10T04:16:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-we-crave-oxidizing-and-dehydrating-agents.19194/post-210004>

Hi all. I'm reading through Epi-Paleo RX and watching as much Jack Kruse youtube as I can find.

Can someone point me to any material to help me better understand why we seem to crave oxidizing and dehydrating agents?

When I think about all addictions to substances, they are either oxidizers, dehydrators, or often both. Epi-Paleo RX(Chapter 5) has a mention of the occasional usage of red wine to boost endotoxin binding.

Aside from maybe some wild corner cases or poor pica sufferers I don't know about nobody in history that seems to have ever been compulsively addicted to hydrating or antioxidants. What is it about the substances we abuse that draws us to them while they demolish our mitochondria?

The only answer I've come up with is that in some weird Malthusian way people are self selecting themselves out of evolution. That's a bit creepy even for me.

### Nicolaj Sølvsten — 2017-03-10T09:00:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-we-crave-oxidizing-and-dehydrating-agents.19194/post-210023>

I think people are addicted to their frequency of reality. I'm bumping this thread because I find myself addicted to a lower frequency, achieved by eating junk etc, and is interested in explanations.

### drezy — 2017-03-10T23:15:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-we-crave-oxidizing-and-dehydrating-agents.19194/post-210072>

Wow! The forum really is a goldmine of information and I'm glad to have it.

@[Nicolaj Sølvsten](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19289/) because you are also interested I'll share the breadcrumb trail I've picked up so far:

Doctor Kruse often mentions Nora Volkow. Here is a good starting point for Volkow's work(gets juicy 7-8 min in but it's all worth it):

Searching the forum with "Volkow" will point you toward her more detailed and related work.

Dopamine RX:
[https://www.jackkruse.com/the-dopamine-rx-good-choices-or-bad/](https://www.jackkruse.com/the-dopamine-rx-good-choices-or-bad/)
pay particular attention to:

> Dopamine codes for repetition of good choices for survival in our species, in case you were wondering. That repetition does not have to be all positive or all negative. It can be both of a combo of both and still help us in many obtuse ways. The better balanced the system is, the better choices we make and the more likely it is we will survive a long time without illness.

These are starting points. If you find anything interesting down this rabbit hole please post it here.

### Lindasmullin — 2017-03-10T06:26:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/linda-mullin-health-hx.19195/post-210012>

depression on and off most of my life
Was very severe 2 years ago.
I'm taking no meds. Currently thank god!
I attempted almalgam removal years ago and got very sick despite seeing holistic dentist still have 10 fillings 2 root canals
ADD like symptoms my whole life never formally diagnosed.

Constipation
Fatigue
Low mood
Candida
Poor digestion
Poor sleep I work nights in sleep lab
5'9.5 height. 200 lbs approx

Surgeries total hysterectomy 2011 fibroids and anemia
Cholecystectomy 1987
UPPP2011 tonsils very enlarged
Eye surgery 1965 age 3
Ankle ligament repair 1986
Deviated septum repair1985?
2 miscarriages
2 live births

Pyroluria test positive 2 yrs ago. By
Dr in Chicago was on lots of his supplements Did not feel any better
Stopped them.

All regular labs w Primary are within normal range as of jan 2016. W recent physical for work.

. I avoid traditional medicine. Except for acute infections trauma etc I had natural unmedicated births breastfed both my kids and did not vaccinate them.

taking supplements magnesium, digestive enzymes ,betaine hcl fish oil iodine, Sublingual b12. Thorne methyl guard and a few others I can't recall I'm at work at this writing

Diet recently stopped all grains and most dairy still crave sugar
Eating more veggies Leafy greens organic eggs Kerry gold butter
Spring water. Coffee at sleep lab
Organic chicken.( gotta try seafood I know! Only like lobster)

Cold showers and 2 ice baths so far
Exercise is inconsistent but jog outside 2-3 x per week
Meditate daily

My Mother died age 67 copd and complications from
Hernia repair Poor health her whole life etoh abuse, smoker. Depression. Anxiety.
Her mom, my maternal grand mother died when my mom was 13 from supposedly Colon cancer this was 1950's.
One sister age 58. Alcoholic h/o ovarian cancer
One brother age 59 alcoholic in jail now for 3 rd. Dwi. Hepatitis c

Thank you for this forum

linda

welcome to journal land Linda!

Have you read the leptin RX? It is all about resetting your circadian rhythm.

The Leptin Rx will help get rid of all the cravings and get you on track. Don't worry too much about exercise just yet. Walking is good!

It is really important to get outside and see the sunrise with little to no clothes on if possible!

start your day with a BAB and leave 4 hours between meals and at least 4 hours before bedtime

drink spring water

eat seafood

get as much grounding as possible....walk barefoot and hug lots of trees! Can you swim in a river? stream? lake? ocean?

no fake lights after sunset.

Hopefully you will be able to change your work situation as soon as possible.........

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) @[fitness@home](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/5474/) @[Sue-UK](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9658/)

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-11T11:26:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/list-biohacking-gears-method-you-follow.19198/post-210100>

Hi Nija

My rule of thumb is to spend smarter, not harder.  I'm willing to spend the cash if I understand what something is doing, and what its not doing, and any negative implications of using it at the wrong time, or for too long. I find it a huge temptation to spend too long in the wrong environment, because I think my biohacking gear is making me safe .... and it isn't, at best I'm just not getting as damaged as I was before I made the changes. Its not just about the visual colour of lamps, its also about circadian rhythm, (what does say a 4 hour fake sunset do to it?) nnemf, resonance, unexpected spikes of blue that aren't visually obvious, flicker (i.e. what do my mitochondria "see" that my visual system isn't aware of)...........If I worked in an office all day under fluorescent lights, blue lit screens, nnemf etc, my protection and mitigation choices would be different. I think its about looking at the whole, not through a hole.

I also don't see any point in getting anything where I won't be sure what the results mean, how the environment my mitochondria faced impacted the readings, or what to do about it if it if the results look "bad", it just becomes another source of stress.

To test how much difference it makes before investing in a proper blackout blind, I just made an extra blackout curtain from an old blanket clipped to the existing curtain with clothes pegs. It gave me the information I wanted, and I can choose to buy a proper blackout curtain or blind, or if money was tight it might not look as good, but what I've got does the job. I wouldn't spend 200 dollars on a fancy clock that when I started on my journey I might have bought, but would have finished up out in the shed with my other mistakes .... that same 200 dollars will get me two months gold membership, with access to all the webinars, and Q and A's. The cold vest price could get me another month, and there's other ways to do it cheaper .... Hell just not being a slave to the latest food fads, tests that might not be necessary or trying to supplement my way out of a hole pays for my membership ....And those webinars and Q&A's are what give me the information to make better decisions about the purchases I do make. My membership is my ultimate biohacking tool.

My two recent investments were a Cornet to assess my home for things I might be missing .... that was well worth the money. My other purchase was a cheap spectroscope (under £10) which doesn't give me info on spikes if the light source covers the same range, but does show a spike in a colour that is otherwise not there and gives me a better general idea of the colour ranges that my lights and screens are emitting. The results from both gadgets were surprising .... changed a couple of things I was doing, and a couple more things headed for the shed .....

I have a light and colour therapy system (had it years but it would cost me around £2000 if I needed to replace it), but if I didn't have it or when I need to replace it, I would get a Quantlet, because I understand how it works (and compared to my system is a bargain .... and I do love a photonic bargain.).

### Lahelada — 2017-03-12T02:26:21+0200
