### nija — 2017-03-13T05:29:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/list-biohacking-gears-method-you-follow.19198/post-210229>

@[Sue](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/854/) .. I love ur all post .
Can you please provide your cheap/Alternative biohacking method ?For example without cold vest or 20/30 lb ice , how do u di CT ?
Please suggest tour few alternative biohack

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-13T10:23:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/list-biohacking-gears-method-you-follow.19198/post-210232>

> [nija said:](/goto/post?id=210229)
>
> @[Sue](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/854/) .. I love ur all post .
> Can you please provide your cheap/Alternative biohacking method ?For example without cold vest or 20/30 lb ice , how do u di CT ?
> Please suggest tour few alternative biohack

I have 2 baths in the garden (I'm rural and have the space). One is a metal small seated one, cost about £10? at an auction. I have it so that seated I'm facing east. The second is a conventional fibreglass one that I nabbed from my neighbours when they were putting in a new bathroom. In that one I can face east in the morning (plug end), but its more for west in the evening, and its easier to float on the top of the water to harvest photons. I sometimes get in that one first, then as it warms up I get out and go to the second one. When I started if the water was too warm I used plastic bottles to freeze water as ice cubes, but I don't do that now, I take what the environment gives. In the summer the water is coolest at dawn, and I get the early morning light at the same time. If I get out at first light in the summer, I can fit in an hour, and its a lovely meditative experience. In winter when the water is colder, I am in for a lot less time as sunrise is much later, but the stimulus is much stronger, so even if its a few minutes after having to break the ice with hammer, I still love it. The only time I won't do it, is if its a full blown storm or a very cold strong wind. I even like outside CT in the pouring rain as long as there's a reasonable light stimulus. I am lucky enough to be able to CT nude unless I have visitors, then I use a tan through costume. (A good investment). If I had to CT indoors I would probably do it in the evening, using plastic bottles to get it colder before I got in, and perhaps use candlelight, try to meditate, get more out of it than just getting the cold stimulus, but nothing beats being able to watch the clouds, listen to the birds and watch the seasons change ... But either way is working with what I've got, or can do cheaply.

In terms of an ice vest, it would be too limiting for me. A friend who sews made me an apron that fits two freezer packs (belly cooling). I have a migraine cap that I keep in the freezer. (Cost £30, my most expensive CT purchase, I bought another for a hack I was doing at the time, but for my current hack one is enough). I can use it anytime, but my current hack is to put it on when I'm getting ready for bed, and for a few minutes when I first get into bed, to help cool my CSF to help signal sleep. Then I just take it off, and to stay horizontal I slide it just under the bed, and put it back in the freezer in the morning. I also have a shoulder cold pack, that goes from the shoulder down the back, stimulating the brown fat area. I have a couple of cold wraps that I strap round my wrists. But one cap, belly cooling apron packs, the shoulder wrap and wrist straps costs less than a vest would cost me, and if money is an issue they don't all need to be bought at once. I can target something specifically, (blood cooling through the wrist), stimulating brown fat, cooling CSF, unwanted belly fat etc etc... or put them on in any combination, or all together. If I've worked on the computer I can put on the cap, pull the flaps down in front of my closed eyes and rest them, put the wrist straps on ..etc etc. Far more possibilities than a vest ....

### nija — 2017-03-13T23:13:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/list-biohacking-gears-method-you-follow.19198/post-210283>

Copying from another thread just for reference . Please feel free to add

1\. Biohack DHA/Seafood (Oysters from PacNW, Shrimp from Florida, Salmon from Norway, grassfed foods/butter ~ 7x per week, staples in diet): For increase magnetism and mitrochondrial funneling of electrons for optimal energy use in body tissues
2\. Biohack Cysteine (Garlic, Asparagus, Bell Pepper, Egg, Chicken ~ staples in diet): Hardest to find amino acid in nature to help with energy management with Glutathione.
4\. Biohack Gut flora boosting (Probiotic and Saukroot)
5\. Biohack Gaining Electrons (RO Water, Taking flights of stairs down holding metal-railing connected to ground, 30mins minimum Grounding/earthing, etc.) Replenishment of electrons to compensate for electrons suckage from environment
6\. Biohack nnEMF (Backreflecting RF-EMF with silver/copper threaded clothing & caps on brain, mitigating lots of tech, hardwiring connections in home instead of Wifi, etc.) Less overall suckage like the leech in the sky that it is
7\. Biohack Light (Morning light exposure, using certain types of indoor lights at various times, blue-blocking with amber glasses, F.lux, etc.) Controlling light for optimal use in the time of day .2-3 hours of sun . Primarly morning and evening
8.CT on my eyes several times a day . CT face dunk in water and old ie pad
9\. Sungazing --->Sun gazing 20 min a day bare foot .During my sun sessions, I always looked into the direction of the sun
10 . Daily mindfulness practice 20 min
11\. Upgrade my water source( RO or spring water)
12.Change my lighting environment: I want to get UV bulbs and red lights inside of my room, maybe see if I can utilize a type of light source that can wake me up like the sun does as well?

All I changed was adding the sunshine/grounding in the morning,
eating more protein in breakfast,
more seafood and reducing the nnEMF (which I need to do more).
I will focus a lot on grounding and sunlight and CT
blue blockers Extremely important sine I am feeling better sleep and relax eye.

This picture might help save you wasting money on artificial lighting that (apart from any eye irritating flicker, blue light spikes you can't see, nnemf or potential mercury resonance) is giving either a signal at the wrong time, or leaving your circadian rhythm stuck at a particular time of day.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/upload_2017-3-14_17-16-56-png.4514/)
Although the red frequencies might predominate at dawn, there is enough blue to switch off melatonin. There is a crescendo of light up to noon, and then a decrescendo. The sun gives the right signals at the right time, no errors.

CT 7 is also a good blog to read about how light influences hormones etc as the day progresses ...... I found Nonchalant's drawing she did in relation to that blog helpful too, its on page 14 post 270 of her journal "Goodbye City Life."

If I left for and arrived at work in darkness, I'd work harder at finding a good artificial set up to use before I left. If the lighting I worked under all day was crap and I could do nothing about it, its about mitigation. At home my current hack is to keep all artificial lights off in the day now that spring has arrived, and go out for regular breaks in the natural light. I think of it as an animal who goes out to collect food and takes it back into the den for her cubs. She is regularly going back out and sampling the natural changing environment. Maybe nuts, but at least with no flicker and blue spikes its easier on my eyes.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-blocking-glasses-recommendation.19200/post-210127>

It is a dutch company so I am not sure if they shop internationally without a big fee. Gunnar has some crazy assortement as well but I have no personal experience with them:

### MatRusLab — 2017-03-11T11:20:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuck-and-scared-trt-and-fertility.19202/post-210099>

Is there anything to help bring back sperm production if on TRT?

I ask that question first just to save time and it is where I am at. I realize I am not asking the right question yet and need some help.

My wife and I would really like to have children. And have been trying for a few years. There was a pregnancy a few years back that miscarried. That was prior to TRT.

I feel kind of running out of time. My wife is going to be 40. I am turning 38. This is a bigger long term problem that needs correcting.

I am wondering if there are some thoughts about how to make the best short term modifications and maybe a longer term plan.

We realized recently that I am producing ZERO sperm and this is probably related to TRT.

I have read about HCG being able to possibly preserve production while on TRT.

My Doc does not feel comfortable using HCG and referred me to endocrinologist.

I saw endo who wanted me to stop TRT completely for a few months to see what would happen.

I did try to to cut back. 1 less pump per day was okay. Did that for a few weeks. 2 pumps less per day and I became very emotional and nonfunctional.

I am limited financially by what can be done to correct this to insurance mostly as far as treatment. I cannot afford to order HCG online.

I know it is not ideal to be on TRT. I have been on it a few years now. I really did not educate myself on it.

A bit stuck and scared of how to proceed.

Also scared to ask here but I know this is the smartest place to be even though I am already not doing what I should be doing by taking testosterone.

Thoughts are appreciated. Please be gentle.

Matt

Welcome to the forum

### JanSz — 2017-03-11T14:59:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuck-and-scared-trt-and-fertility.19202/post-210109>

LH--->testosterone
FSH--->sperm
HCG--->similar to LH (but not to FSH) (already expensive)
HMG--->similar to FSH (actually expensive)

Sometime ago, prior to TRT, you were making sperm, got your wife pregnant, she miscarried.

Now, you are not making sperm because supplemental testosterone shut out your LH and FSH production.

You must understand that you have two problems at hand not just one.

Using Testosterone (without) simultaneous use of HCG, destroys testicles.
Testicles and scrotum, in some men, shrink to nothing.

......................

It is sensitive and emotional
for currently born
someone who may get born despite this great difficulties will have much greater chances to have high head winds
this is the place where love should start talking

....

### JanSz — 2017-03-11T22:35:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuck-and-scared-trt-and-fertility.19202/post-210159>

HCG gotten over internet is just as good, easier to get and may cost less depending on insurance and how much you have to beg your doctor to give you script and how often he feels that you should come and beg.

In normal life, aiming just to maintain physical shape and size of scrotum and testicles,
you may want to think of 800-1000iu/week average dose, divided at least 2x/week
You can just watch the effects and adjust dose.
You do not want excessive HCG dose, for some it raises E2, estradiol.
High E2 & E1 = loss of erections, gyno, emotional distress.

When buying you prefer in vials over ampoules. Larger cheaper.
10000iu is usually largest, but I have heard of 11000iu.
Think of yearly supply to make life easier.
If you end up with ampoule you have to purchase sterile vials.
Get also
Bacteriostatic Water (BW)
Box or boxes of 31Ga 5/16 insulin syringes 3/10cc for injections
and
5-10 or more 3, 5 or 10 mL syringes with needle (larger gauge), for mixing
currently I use
10000iu HCG
450iu each shot
so I get 22 shots
assumed volume of each shot=10iu
required volume of added BW=222.22units=2.23mL
after mixing, I draw all into insulin syringes
then put them into tea glass and into freezer
take one out as need

...
Fertility mode is different, look up that chart.

### JanSz — 2017-03-14T22:50:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuck-and-scared-trt-and-fertility.19202/post-210360>

> [MatRusLab said:](/goto/post?id=210356)
>
> Hey Jan,
>
> I've been trying to figure out how much to buy. Thank you again for what you have provided here.
>
> I think I understand this part...
>
> 10000iu HCG
> 450iu each shot
> so I get 22 shots
>
> 1 vial of 10k will make 22 @ 450iu so if I did 450 every 3 days for example I could go 66 days at that dosage. Which I have seen in other places that is an amount that can help.
>
> I don't understand this part
>
> assumed volume of each shot=10iu
>
> In the graphic from earlier in this thread it says 2000iu would that be per dose? Like a 10000iu vial would be for 5 doses?
>
> Sorry this is so new to me.
>
> Matt

We are discussing here two protocols.
Fertility protocol----2000iu per single injection (3 injections per week)
Long term HCG supplementation to maintain size of testicles and scrotum----450iu per single injection. Two of 450iu injections per week
Year =52 weeks
one HCG vial gives 22 shots good for 11 weeks
52/11=4.7
Five ampoules are good for 5\*11=55 weeks (or slightly over one year) for maintenance protocol.
\-------------------------
HCG is a powder.
To be injected it is mixed with Bacteriostatic Water (BW).
Amount of water is not fixed.
One can use more or less BW.
When mixing you must assume some amount of BW.
HCG powder does not increase or decrease volume of mixture.
I assumed 10iu volume of each 450iu shot.
In above sentence I used iu twice. They are not the same.
iu in regard to injection volume is a mark on insulin syringe (marking volume).
There are three types of insulin syringes, 1cc, 0.5cc and 3/10cc (that I suggested for you to use)
On any insulin syringe there are marks.
Most 3/10cc syringes have marks to mark half unit.
1mL=1cc=100iu
so 10iu on insulin syringe =0.1cc
The other iu is in regard to HCG.
You likely are going to buy vials containing 10000iu worth of HCG powder.
From each HCG vial, you are going to have 22shots, each shot will have a volume of 10iu=0.1cc
You will need BW 22x10=220 units (add half unit for wetting walls)

////////////

### JanSz — 2017-03-15T00:35:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuck-and-scared-trt-and-fertility.19202/post-210367>

This post is about testicle and scrotum maintenance only, by using 450iu shots of HCG done twice a week.
Assumptions:
You are buying HCG powder that is contained in vials.
There is 10000iu worth of powder in that vial.
You have:
Bacteriostatic water
31Ga 3/10cc insulin syringes
mixing syringes 3, 5 or 10cc with 1" or 1.5" long needle
calculations
there is enough poder to make 10000/450=22 shots
assume that each shot have a volume of 10iu=0.1cc=0.1mL=10units
therefore, combined, 22 shots have total volume of 2.2mL=220units (add half a unit for extra)
put
225units=2.25mL BW into mixing syringe
insert needle into vial, put all water in
rotate gently vial to mix, do not shake
fill up all 22 insulin syringes, each up to line indicating 0.1cc
put all syringes into freezer
take syringe out of freezer as need
///

### kris90 — 2017-03-15T12:50:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuck-and-scared-trt-and-fertility.19202/post-210393>

I was diagnosed with secondary hypogonadism back in 2013. My wife and I at the time were trying for 5-6 months to conceive with no success. I had to wait for an appointment to see a Urologist, but I decided to self medicate with HCG. I ran a 3-week protocol and BOOM wife got pregnant. Our daughter is 2.5 years old now, and doing extremely well!

Fast forward, and when I saw my Urologist, he prescribed me HCG monotherapy as my HRT rather than testosterone since I'm young and wanted to have more children. We attempted a Clomid restart several times with no success, but I was able to dial in an HCG dose that put my T levels up where they need to be and that was fine and dandy for a while. I am in the middle of attempting another Clomid restart and learned that my history of chronic restrictive diets (low cal/low carb) and spending too much time in the gym with insufficient recovery is likely what led to my dysfunctional HPTA.

I suggest you order some good quality HCG if your doc is not willing to prescribe. As long as you have good functioning testes, it should work wonders. It did for me.

We don't know for sure if he's secondary or primary? Sometimes healthcare professionals go ahead and prescribe TRT not taking into consideration that secondary hypogonadism can be treated with gonadotropin (HCG) replacement and/or possibly clomid to restart the HPTA. At this point, if we don't have a baseline of LH and FSH, the only way to find out testicular function would be to begin HCG and stop the T injections.

You are right about the unknown status of LH & FSH before starting on testosterone.
In my discussion with (MatRusLab) I have found:
1--- hi is using supplemental testosterone
2--- when his testosterone is low he is in terrible state not able to live comfortably or run his business. Having proper testosterone level is very high priority for him
3--- he have very high drive to become father regardless of his own problems and his wife's, when overall there is higher possibility of having health deficient child if conception is successful
4--- indeed his LH & FSH levels from before testosterone are unknown (to me)
5--- his testicles already are somewhat shrunken after testosterone use

On post #8 I provided one possibility of conducting fertility protocol.
That starts with halting exogenous testosterone supplementation.

You when you saying that:
"the only way to find out testicular function would be to begin HCG and stop the T injections"
that is about how this type of protocol starts.

I would like to add that I have heard of protocols that are planned similarly as in chart on post #8 except that testosterone supplementation is not stopped.
I do not know more details about that protocol,
but
I think that in such a protocol one would keep blood testosterone on some rather minimal level,
when without fertility consideration that would be rather high normal.
Say
1000ng/dL high normal
500ng/dL when attempting to conceive.

### kris90 — 2017-03-16T21:24:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stuck-and-scared-trt-and-fertility.19202/post-210477>

It's too bad you weren't diagnosed as secondary or primary, because if you come off the meds now, your LH and FSH will be suppressed due to the exogenous T in your system, so you won't get a true measure of these hormones.

I think the best bet would be HCG monotherapy for a month or so, then get bloods and a semen analysis to see if you responded. Typically within 2 weeks on HCG, I feel a difference and notice significant growth and fullness in the family jewels.

you do not have to use it.

I do not know what I was diagnosed with (primary or secondary). I think maybe primary.

My blood level was 128. It was ridiculously low I was told.

Prescribed 4 pumps a day of Androgel and brought it up to 300s.

Then doubled it and now I think 800-900 range.

It is good idea to keep your testosterone at 800-900ng/dL range
Additionally, when you are using Androgel your DHT is increasing, which may make you feel better.
Personally, I had to switch to Androgel 1.62% for that reason.

I know I am not on the numbers here.

When I tried to go down to 6 pumps I was not stable.

I have definitely had shrinkage. Was hard to notice incrementally and then one day was just like yeah these used to fill up my hand really well. Hahaha.
Do not worry too much about shrinkage, when you get spare $100 you can re-build it (cosmetically).

I see a urologist in May.

When I saw Endocrinologist he said that my primary should have tried to figure out why someone in mid 30s had such low testosterone. Wanted me off everything for a while to evaluate pituitary functioning.\[/quote\]
\=====================================================
Figuring out your pituitary is important.
Unfortunately that it is a endocrinologist's job, not GP's.
So the answer endo have given you is only half right.
You may try to go back to him,
unless you can find another endo.

The way it is now, you more or less do not have to worry about LH & FSH.
So, do not let him push you off testosterone/Androgel.
But,
pituitary gives numerous other hormones
and he should help you evaluate all of them or at least most of them.

One question to ask is:
did the all thing started suddenly?
Have you been healthy, energetic, full of life,
and
suddenly that changed?
If that is so, have you been hit in the head, forehead at the tip of your nose where glasses rest?
Car accident can do that.
That could have messed up your pituitary.

If above does not ring the bell,
Have you been healthy, energetic, full of life,
and slowly life got tough.
If that is more likely:
get pituitary MRI.
Look for pituitary tumor.

....
You already know that your testosterone is affected.
The other may be
thyroid
and
Growth Hormone.

If you get right endo he can write you couple scripts and make new man out of you.

...

# Thread: Hi all! Greetings from Holland.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-all-greetings-from-holland.19203/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Remo — 2017-03-11T17:20:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-all-greetings-from-holland.19203/post-210125>

It is very nice to meet you all

I have been following Dr. Jack Kruse for a while now and greatly enjoyed listening to his podcasts so I decided it about time to sign up for the forum!

My health (especially mental) has been a big problem for me since I was a child. Chronic fatigue, extreme sensory input issues, panick attacks, light and EMF sensitivity, IBS, hyperventilation you name it. I have been diagnosed with a form of high functioning autism since I was a kid but I never accepted this myself as I could not indentify with the dozens of other people with the same diagnosis.

When I started looking into the meaning of Autism and brain disorders it became pretty clear that many of the complaints associated with these disorders were caused or severely worsened by the environment these people were in. I noticed this heavily in myself as I felt like a completely different person when I was on vacation compared who I was at home! When I got back my IQ and brain functioning would drop fluctuate to the extreme negative ( I honestly believe I was much smarter as a 10yo then I was at 17 !! )

Over the years I discovered some relief by: Grounding, Raw Fish (Sushi, herring!), walking, being outside, drinking mineral water..
This was waaaay before I ever heard of Jack Kruse/Paleo/Bluelight etc. Most of these positive effects I just "noticed" while not knowing why they make me feel better. I am VERY thankful to all of YOU that I do understand now (still need to learn SO much though).

Seriously, I have felt like I have been on the brink of death a few years ago... I could barely get out of my bed, my oxygen levels were extremely low as well (hypoxic). Looking back I suspect it was severe adrenal fatigue/thyroid disfunction. I am still far from optimal functioning but still , the difference is very positive!

Again it is very nice to meet you all. I have allot of questions which I hope some of you can help me with. If any of you want to know anything from me just let me know

Sincerely,

Remo

### JanSz — 2017-03-11T17:24:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-all-greetings-from-holland.19203/post-210126>

diagnosed with a form of high functioning autism
\=================================================
Get tested.
Check
EPA
DHA
AA

Good likelyhood that you have
high EPA
low AA

If you do, get more detailed fatty acids analysis.
Make corrections to anything that is over (plus-minus 25% of laboratory range, counting from mid range).

/////

///////////////

hey Jan, thanks for the reply! i tested these in the past and my ratio wasnt optimal (results are from some years ago, will find them).

Moreso, I think it says enough that seriously the moment I eat a fresh Herring my brain just feels some kind of "relief". ..my brain fog usually goes away but only for a small while.

I tried doing ketogenic several times but it made my adrenal fatigue worse (not keto flue!). However, during these past trials I did not keep in mind the seasons (did keto all year).

# Thread: Sun Diciples Labs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-diciples-labs.19204/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 5

### Sun Disciple — 2017-03-11T18:09:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-diciples-labs.19204/post-210138>

I just got back from the Yucatan but before I left I decided to get some labs with the left over change from my trip budget. Ill be doing something more comprehensive in a few months once I make and apply some adjustments to my environment. So any comments, questions , tips and ticks and critiques are welcome.

test name Result reference range

Urea 3.6 mmol/l 2.7-8.4

creatine 82 umol/l 67-117

EGFR 109 >90

I did the conversions to US units and made calculations and came up with 10/1 Bun creatine ratio.

HS-CRP 0.60 mg/l

Free-testosterone 60 pmol/l 196-636

DHEA-s 87 umol/l <15

Us conversion on DHEA-s here is 320 ug/dl

estradiol-17 beta <40 pmol/l <162

US conversion for estradiol is 10.89 pg/ml

25-hydroxy vitamin D 128 mmol/l 75-250

Free T3 3.4 pmol/l 3.1-6.2

Free T4 12 pmol/l 9-19
Us conversion gave me .9324 ng/dl .8-1.8

TSH 2.86 mIU/l .32-4.00

Ok so I made my analysis based off Jacks optimizing labs linked here [https://www.jackkruse.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/Optimizing-Labs.pdf](https://www.jackkruse.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/Optimizing-Labs.pdf)
[https://www.jackkruse.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/Optimizing-Labs.pdf](https://www.jackkruse.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/04/Optimizing-Labs.pdf)
Bun creatine ratio of 10/1 is ok not optimal need to get down to a 7/1

HS-crp of .6 is ok but should get it down below .5

My testosterone is in the basement. so basically Im workin with the mojo of a 90 year old man

DHEA needs to come up.

Estradiol is ok assuming jack is measuring in pg/ml his recomendation is <25 and Im at 10pg/ml

As for my thyroid T3 is ok. T4 is low at .9 Jack looks like he wants a 1.5 or greater.
TSH Looks on the high side as jack is looking for .5-1.00 and im clocking in at 2.86

Ok so a little context these have been my hacks over the last three months .
I did 2 months of CT before this blood draw. Now my bathroom is close to the electrical box so im hoping to move my CT outside here in the next few weeks with a metal feed troth or rain barrel and get it grounded.
\- focused heavily on light hacking rising with the sun and making like the sphinx.
\-Getting outside during the day when sun is out.
\-using natural light during the day eliminating blue light after dark.
\-Wearing blue blockers before and after sunrise.
\-Eating epi paleo with a focus on seasonal eating.
\-Eating within the light cycle 2 meals a day eaten within 8 hours giving me a 16 hour fast.
\-Last meal is always atleast 6 hours before bedtime.
\-black out room before bedtime.
\-Getting to sleep before 9 rising at dawn.
\-Using sperti Vit D lamp with near infrared lamp for 10 mins 2-3x per week.
\-Occasionally using solar glow lizard lamp to simulate sunrise when we've had a few days drought of morning sun.
\-Aiming to go out for hikes in the woods 2x per week.
\-meditation/breathing exercises.
\-yoga or bodyweight excersises after ct.
\-limiting screen time to an hour or 2 per day all before sunset with use of Gunnars.
\-Ive tried to reduce my EMF exposure as much as could without purchasing meters.
\-I Turn off breaker to room and airplane mode phone at night.
\-Always keep phone at a distance and use with airtube headset or speaker phone.
\-Eliminated blue tooth devices.
\-disconnected stereo and amplifier in car.
\-Keep distance from computer with usb mouse and keyboard.
\-2 to three litres of spring water a day.
\-Getting outside barefoot as much as possible.
\-keeping room cool and sleeping with thin sheet naked.
\-installing chlorine shower and batrh filters(water is not fluoridated here).

Now im really surprised my testosterone is still trashed after all these hacks Its still at the same level as a year ago. After Ct my mood and energy and aggressiveness is all elevated so I was almost sure It had to be somewhat higher. I guess Im just feeling an increase in neurotransmitters like dapamine and seratonin? My energy levels and motivation are up as well. Now my libido is still almost non existent so that is consistent with my low test result. My sleep is a lot better. I fall asleep immediately after lying down in bed and im only getting up once a night to pee and falling right back to sleep. I used to wake multiple times
and have trouble getting back to sleep. I always wake up just before my alarm just before dawn. No issues with lying around in bed or not wanting to get up. My number of dreams have increased at night as well as my recall of them.

I wish I had a bench mark immediately prior to these hacks but at least I have definitely felt some positive changes. Im a little discourage3d and surprised my testosterone hasn't budged at all. If anyone has any specific guidance there Id appreciate It.
My plan going forward was to purchase EMF meters and test my environment, Get an outdoor CT tank and hopefully purchase a magnetico.

Look forward to hearing your thoughts.

### JanSz — 2017-03-11T18:23:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-diciples-labs.19204/post-210140>

### kris90 — 2017-03-11T21:08:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-diciples-labs.19204/post-210148>

What are your meals? Are you on strict keto, or eating carbs? Are you eating low calorie? Do you have Cortisol levels? What is your weight/height? Bodyfat % ?

Honestly, it looks to me like your thyroid is suppressed probably due to chronic stress, as is your testosterone. Did you know CT is a stress? Low calorie diets are as well, as is low carb IF you are already quite lean.

Also, the whole no eating within 4-5 hours of bed is bullshit in my opinion, UNLESS you are overweight and need to lose weight. The most crucial time for our brain to acknowledge being in the "fed state" is before sleep. Being fed = stress relief = solid sleep. Being semi-fasted before bed = stress = poor sleep. Again, the exception is if overweight, because the excess fat feeds the body, and fasting/keto becomes beneficial.

### Sun Disciple — 2017-03-13T16:53:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-diciples-labs.19204/post-210252>

> [kris90 said:](/goto/post?id=210148)
>
> What are your meals? Are you on strict keto, or eating carbs? Are you eating low calorie? Do you have Cortisol levels? What is your weight/height? Bodyfat % ?
>
> Honestly, it looks to me like your thyroid is suppressed probably due to chronic stress, as is your testosterone. Did you know CT is a stress? Low calorie diets are as well, as is low carb IF you are already quite lean.
>
> Also, the whole no eating within 4-5 hours of bed is bullshit in my opinion, UNLESS you are overweight and need to lose weight. The most crucial time for our brain to acknowledge being in the "fed state" is before sleep. Being fed = stress relief = solid sleep. Being semi-fasted before bed = stress = poor sleep. Again, the exception is if overweight, because the excess fat feeds the body, and fasting/keto becomes beneficial.

Im 5'11 178 pounds bodyfat is probably like maybe 14 - 15% I havnt done a dexa so im guessing on that.
Im doing Keto because Its winter and nothing is growing where I live. my macros are generally 30 to 40 grams of carbs a day 90 grams of protien 250 grams of fat.
I eat 2 meals a day. Breakfast is at dawn with either sardines or sockeye with 100 grams of duck fat some veggies and onions with turmeric and black pepper.
second meal is late afternoon either grass fed beef, lamb or seafood with lots of butter or ghee sea weed on the side. Im satiated and not hungry before bed as long as i get enough fat.

I havnt done cortisol in over a year but I can go get it done. should I be doing a full 5 point or just a morning spit in the tube? Anything else I can throw in there test wise that I missed on the last one?

I assumed CT was a stress but Its usefull to jumpstart hormone production no? I eased into it slowly following jacks protocol.
How many hours before bed would you suggest my last meal be? I can bring it up a bit since the sun is now setting a little later with the clocks moving forward here.
So what im hearing is based on my labs I should hold off on Jacks recomendations that you pointed out until my test and thyroid come up. specifically up my carbs cut CT maybe add in another meal to eliminate intermitent fasting? How many carbs can I eat without interfering with circadian biology in a low light cycle when nothing is growing here. It might be spring where you are but im looking at -15 degrees C and a couple feet of snow right now.

### kris90 — 2017-03-13T20:44:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-diciples-labs.19204/post-210273>

Cortisol would be helpful, and maybe rT3 as well, as it will be elevated under chronic stress conditions.

CT can be helpful to your hormonal profile similar to lifting weights, but the key is that excessive stress from CT, much like exercise can hurt you. Especially if there are other environmental factors causing chronic stress (i.e. malnutrition, toxins, allergies, etc). If your hormones are trashed currently, CT is not going to help you at all. You need to address the cause of havoc on your hormones (which again is usually chronic stress. Cortisol test could confirm).

I can't put a time on when you should eat, that's an N=1. I just had an idea that if you wait too long between your last meal and bed, your body might be in a hungered state going to sleep which is not good. The best sleep is when your stress is low. Naturally, when we are fed (especially with carbs) we sleep better with low stress. Does a lion sleep when he's hungry? No, he hunts his prey. Then after the feast, he sleeps.

Regarding carbs, don't stress over it. Just try adding them back to your diet slowly and see how you react. I eat most of my carbs at night now, and the results have been great. Libido is improving, as is sleep and body composition. I can eat way more calories now too, and my temperature is ALWAYS 37 C now, so it feels like my thyroid function has improved substantially. I don't care if it's winter, the carbs are helping me, and I personally think it's BS not to eat carbs in winter, unless you plan to hibernate. But if you are still required to function the same way you do in the summer, then IMO you need the carbs year round.

### Remo — 2017-03-17T22:07:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-w-me-your-how-depression-was-eliminated.19205/post-210546>

what Jack said.

+
I have been heavily bi-polar since I was a little kid (n=1) and after trying out everything the only things that consistenly worked for me where: DHA (from raw fish only! supplements do nothing for me), Cold Showers, Walks (lots of them!), Sunlight (of course), being self forgiving (work on this everyday), but most of all..... EXPRESSION! you have to find a way to creatively express your sorrow. I cannot state how important this is! for me this is music composition and improvisational techniques but maybe it is gardening for you... or maybe a certain sport or game ? i do not know, i do not know you.. do you ? explore, challenge your brain constantly. You can do it! Love.

ps. listening to classically oriented music can be very therapeutic.

### Penny — 2017-03-20T18:13:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-w-me-your-how-depression-was-eliminated.19205/post-210756>

When I was a kid I figured out 2 tried and true ways to happiness:
1) Do something nice for someone
2) Make someone laugh

Having said that, I have heard chromium is great for depression - chromium is great for blood sugar:
[https://www.amazon.com/Lifting-Your-Depression-Psychiatrist-Discovered/product-reviews/1591201640](https://www.amazon.com/Lifting-Your-Depression-Psychiatrist-Discovered/product-reviews/1591201640)

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/11343609](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/11343609)
**Chromium treatment of depression.**
[McLeod MN](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=McLeod%20MN%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=11343609), [Golden RN](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Golden%20RN%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=11343609).
**Abstract**
Eight patients with refractory mood disorders received chromium supplements and described dramatic improvements in their symptoms and functioning. In several instances, single-blind trials confirmed specificity of response to chromium. Side-effects were rare and mild, and most commonly included enhanced dreaming and mild psychomotor activation. To our knowledge, this is the first case series describing the response to chromium monotherapy. The putative antidepressant effects of chromium could be accounted for by enhancement of insulin utilization and related increases in tryptophan availability in the central nervous system, and/or by chromium's effects on norepinephrine release.

Cold showers make me totally high too

But really, I don't know how you people stand living in these cold/rainy places - I just implode without the sun - maybe a sun lamp and/or UV lightbulb? I use a reptile light sometimes when it's cloudy - 18 degrees? I would die...

### FrankMathis — 2020-06-26T19:44:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-w-me-your-how-depression-was-eliminated.19205/post-290540>

Kratom isn’t technically an opioid, but its effects [are similar](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0361923016300582) to those of [opioids](https://www.healthline.com/health-news/should-we-care-that-kratom-is-classified-as-an-opioid), such as morphine or codeine.

The active ingredient in kratom is called mitragynine. Mitragynine binds to opioid receptors in the brain, relieving pain.

This action might be behind the antidepressant and anti-anxiety effects reported by some kratom users.

There’s currently very little research on kratom’s effects on mood.

One [2017 review](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.drugalcdep.2017.10.012) confirmed that among some users, kratom enhances mood and relieves anxiety.

The researchers also highlighted that kratom can have sedative effects. Researchers have yet to examine whether side effects such as sedation can interfere with its purported benefits.

# Thread: Inflammatory skin reaction to sweating ? (iodine, leptin, water related?)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/inflammatory-skin-reaction-to-sweating-iodine-leptin-water-related.19214/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Remo — 2017-03-13T13:33:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/inflammatory-skin-reaction-to-sweating-iodine-leptin-water-related.19214/post-210237>

Friends I need your help,

I was doing (relatively) incredibely well last summer: I was outdoors most of the time, I started climbing, weight training (inside). When fall transitioned to winter I started to loose my virality again and most of all my ability to sweat. Dry skin had always been an issue but during winter it was always worse ( i reasoned due to indoor heating mostly)

A few months ago I became very ill. I recovered but whenever I went to the gym to exercise I did a few weight reps and BAM... at the exact moment my body wanted to sweat I would feel a 1000 little needles causing excruciating pain and inflammed red skin spots. With cooling they would go away within minutes.
I have this reaction now also when im feeling panicked (especially in artificially crowded blue-lit EMF environments).
IF I prevail to the pain and my body starts sweating the reaction goes away so actually I do **not** think it is an inflammatory reaction of the sweat itself but rather some neurological problem (?)

Some history:
\-I used to get daily panick attacks almost daily since a little kid until I left high school (prickly skin, anxiety, red head, huge flight or fight response).

My other complaints I have now:
1\. Brain Fog, inability to focus.
2\. Fatigue
3\. Bi-polar symptoms
4\. General Anxiety, ADD
5\. Blue light sensitivity

Incidentely it is wise to mention that my gym switched their colored lights (green, blue, red) to bright white (blue lights) some months ago.

### JanSz — 2017-03-13T15:16:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/inflammatory-skin-reaction-to-sweating-iodine-leptin-water-related.19214/post-210246>

Exercise outdoor.
Minimize time indoor.
Maximize time outdoor.

You have already told me that you have messed up fatty acids.
That + autism do not go well together.
Fatty acids can be adjusted relatively easy,
you just have to put the on the list of your priorities.

...

### Remo — 2017-03-13T16:15:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/inflammatory-skin-reaction-to-sweating-iodine-leptin-water-related.19214/post-210248>

hey Jan, thanks for the input. My diet already consists mostly out of raw/fresh fish (herring is amazing).
Exercising outdoors is on the list... ! I will start with doing body weighted HIIT just outside.

I sleep grounded (directly to the earth) and within my own room I disconnect all devices when not used. Sadly I live with other people who are 100% against turning the Wifi routers off at night even if I would switch it on at 6am. yuk.
Any tips regarding EMF pollution are welcome.

# Thread: Severe CFS, Fibro, Lyme(?), Burnout, etc. Totally Bedbound
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-cfs-fibro-lyme-burnout-etc-totally-bedbound.19219/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 91
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### matthewsb77 — 2017-03-13T18:22:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-cfs-fibro-lyme-burnout-etc-totally-bedbound.19219/post-210256>

Hello all,

I’m new to the message board and relatively new Dr. Kruse’s work, but already heavily invested in it. Today I want to ask for your help on my specific issues, to see if anyone notices anything major that I might be missing. I’ll keep it as short as possible, though it’s complex and long-standing. Please understand that I’m doing the best I can, but am often so exhausted it’s too much for me to try to read, much less comprehend complex information like on the doc’s blog, so if I’m missing anything obvious here, or if this type of massive post is somehow violating the general rules, be gentle please. J I’m happy to adjust to whatever I’m not currently understanding.

My main symptoms are as follows (I’m leaving some of the “lesser” ones out here for the sake of brevity):

\- Insomnia + severely exaggerated sympathetic nervous system response since a young age, worsening over time to the point that now I sometimes can’t sleep if I have so much as a phone call the next day. For a long time I did not sleep at all and was in a state of such sympathetic dominance and biorhythm imbalance that I was never fully awake but never asleep, either, like being in a hellish purgatory. I couldn’t think, and had no control of my emotions. Most of the time I'm kinda half here, and thank God my brain can kind of run on autopilot so I can keep the thread of conversations somehow (and essays like this). I’ve had one seizure in my life, and I can say it was sort of like being in a state of prolonged seizure. With the things I’m doing (below) I’m starting to get good sleep most nights, finally.

\- Exhuastion so severe that there are periods of a week or more where I can hardly bathe myself; I pee into a bottle most of the time because I can’t walk the 10 feet to the bathroom everytime I need to pee. On good days, I can go down the stairs to sit in the living room with my parents, but still can’t cook for myself. I can go outside every so often with great effort.

\- Difficulty thinking, much less concentrating

\- Generalized, whole-body inflammation, including joint, nerve, muscle, and spinal pain and discomfort constantly

\- Severe heaviness in all my muscles – legs and arms feel like they weigh 50 pounds each sometimes

\- Difficulty breathing that is not related to any kind of anxiety

\- “Adrenal and thyroid burnout” – which I have come to understand have more to do with PVN/hypothalamus than anything else

\- Chronic infection in the teeth (after an idiot oral surgeon did a terrible job pulling my wisdom teeth) that I keep at bay with oil of oregano; without that, within 24 hours my joints are on fire and I can’t hardly bathe myself.

\- I had one seizure a few years back that almost killed me, but none since.

\- I have been diagnosed with Lyme disease, but for reasons I won’t get into, I think the issue is either secondary to the exhaustion factors OR not really Lyme. However, if I don't take oil of oregano constantly, as I've said, I go from being able to at least bathe myself every other day and walk to the bathroom to go #2, to not being able to bathe myself and sometimes needing help to walk to the bathroom once a day (and peeing in a bottle in either case).

\- Severe depression, anxiety, panic. I took antidepressants for 8 years, not because I wanted to, but because I started young and ignorantly, and then when I tried to get off them, it was a life-and-death kind of situation with how severely depressed I got. I have finally managed to get off of them permanently, and I’m 16 months free of them.

\- Blood sugar regulation is not diabetic, but definitely not great.

\- Bladder has been very overactive since I was a kid, and water seems to go right through me.

\- Short-term discomfort in liver and/or kidneys, periodically

\- Note that before I got this sick was a period of severe stress—and I really do mean severe, in the worst sense of the world.

A few concepts I already understand: basics on sunlight, water, and magnetism my body as a battery/the photoelectric effect and the role of DHA, the periventricular nucleus as the source of adrenal problems, biorhythms, etc.

Here’s what I’m currently doing:

Sunlight in the eyes in the morning when I get up for 5-10 minutes and on warmer days when I can stand I get on the roof to take in some sun in underwear (roof faces the road, so need UV-allowing swimsuit); blue-blocking glasses after sunset + body covered indoors; minimum 64-80 oz. of purified, non-plastic water every day; 20 gauss Magnetico sleep pad; almost entirely blacked out bedroom; magnesium bicarbonate, oil of oregano, shilajit, Master Amino Pattern in the morning, and some various supplements for “general nutrition”, and about to start a high-quality DHA supplement produced from algae in the SN2 position (I was taking simple fish oil before); an increasingly paleo diet (I do still eat some fruit and a little bit of wild rice, but I do eat lots of seafood, cooked vegetables, and bee products). I’m too exhausted to get down the stairs and outside (and it’s far too cold right now) but on warmer days I lay on the roof right outside the window as long as I can to absorb sunlight; full-spectrum light box on my body several hours a day; near infrared heat lamps; meditation and breathing practices; positive visualization; psychotherapy to help manage the hell that this is.

I’m too weak for cold thermogenesis right now, and my core temperature can't handle cold, but want to start doing face in cold water for just a bit and see how it goes. I walk down the stairs once on the days I’m able, which is about 2/3 of the time at present, an improvement from a couple months ago. I hope I’m not forgetting anything there. EMFs neutralized as much as possible, and since I live in a rural area, ambient EMF is less than 0.4 milligauss, thankfully. I can do only a little bit of NIR therapy or sunlight at a time because it can severely overstimulate my NS and adrenals and leave me exhausted. Again, it's hard for me to even walk much of the time.

I do take melatonin, which I realize now is a big no-no, but I’ll have to figure out how to taper off it later, as I’m been taking it for years to have any hope of sleeping. CBDs have helped some, though I realize I want my body to eventually be able to do it without them. But you have to understand, my body is just barely coming out of a life-and-death scenario, and I can’t do it all perfectly at once.

MY TAKE ON WHAT’S GOING ON:

I have worked with some decent doctors, but they were all clueless about the issues Dr. Kruse shares with us. It’s been a little over a year now, but I did have a full run-down of lab testing, and the main things that showed up were signs of severe exhaustion: low B vitamins, low alpha-lipoic acid, some relatively minor food allergies, and stool sample showing moderate gut dys-biosis and difficulty digesting proteins.

Methylation status: I'm not sure on this one, but I think I might have one of the MTHFR variants that isn't the really bad one, but isn't in perfect shape either. If that makes sense. Haven't been tested in a little over a year, but things haven't gotten worse in that time. Not sure what I should be doing for that precisely.

So it seems to me that my body is most likely extremely exhausted from years of nervous system overstimulation, that my biorhythms are severely out of whack, and that my nervous system and brain are severely depleted. I don’t actually think it’s much more complicated than that. And despite all the difficult crap in this post, I’m actually VERY OPTIMISTIC about all this, and I am convinced I’m going to be well, even if it takes a year or five. I have the support of my family, thank God, and with that comes a small budget to work with (which is how I’m paying for the Magnetico pad on a six-month payment plan).

So a few areas I’m interested in learning more about: modulating the stress/sympathetic response, and anything that relates to the adrenal glands, hypothalamus, and PVN; rebuilding energy production; **which tests** are necessary to get to check for things that could be messing with my recovery (things like methylation status, but of a variety I’m not even aware of) and anything else that seems relevant.

Thanks for reading such a long post, if you got through it all without skipping to the end. If you're going to tell me I can't get well, don't bother posting, please. I already get enough of that kind of thinking, so I want to hear from anyone who believes I can do this, even if it takes a while. Have an amazing day!

Matthew

A little health history: nothing major in the family comparatively speaking.

A few cases of cancer on mom's side. Some depression in the family, only one or two severe cases in a large extended family. Parents both fairly healthy. A couple cases of MS on dad's side. One diabetic cousin. Overall a fairly healthy family of mostly farmers. Their biggest problem seems to be they overwork themselves and don't sleep enough. I never did as much work as they did since I've been exhausted since fairly young.

I personally have received no diagnoses other than severe exhaustion, depression and anxiety (no shit!) and probable Lyme Disease, CFS, and Fibro.

Farmers, specially when wheat growing,
when near harvest time, to stop wheat grass from growing they spray it with Roundup.
Not sure if you guys call it Roundup there, but it is a chemical that kills vegetation.
You can't harvest green wheat, but you want it to grow to the last possible minute.
So Monsanto figured out how to do both.
Even if your family does not grow wheat and deal with roundup,
Roundup goes into air and falls on you during rainfall.
Roundup contains glyphosate.

This may be your #1 problem.
The rest is a downstream of it.

### Penny — 2017-03-13T23:23:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-cfs-fibro-lyme-burnout-etc-totally-bedbound.19219/post-210284>

I would eat oysters for the zinc (to make P5P/serotonin) and use rhodiola, shisandra during the day to mitigate the cortisol and ashwaganda and lemon balm tea for the GABA at night - your eyes need morning light to make serotonin - in fact, you could probably help yourself greatly if you looked at the sunrise and stayed outside for a while - you need a small hit of blue light, which signals mitochondrial repair - it also seems to me that you are low in electrons - it seems to me that when cortisol spikes, it uses a lot of electrons and depletes your collagen of them - so, I would eat gelatin and bone broth for the collagen and - he's going to blow a gasket on this one - vitamin c if you can't ground... in fact, if you read a book called Forbidden Healing, he has a lovely energy drink which is 1 tsp of vit c and a smaller amount of baking soda - you can substitute potassium bicarbonate for the baking soda - this will increase your exclusion zone and enable you to hold onto more water - in fact, the baking soda alone (just a pinch in each glass of water) will help - I have tried it after coffee and it will even mitigate some of the diuretic effect - of the adaptogen list, I would try the rhodiola first - the stuff is amazing - I have also had great luck using glycine as a sweetener -
and if at all possible, move downstairs - zero wifi in the house as wifi makes bugs grow - and see if you have a smart meter - you can go to a site called [www.electricsense.com](http://www.electricsense.com) for a lot of EMF mitigation articles - as far as water goes, you should be drinking it constantly - a gallon and a half a day is not too much - coconut water is loaded with potassium - which you are losing in droves if you are losing water - and always start slow with any hack because you can't take the hit - I like Janz post about the glyphosate - but Dr. K on his facebook page had a nice link to how glyphosate replaces glycine - I bet you're loaded with it - another reason to do bone broths (they have proline/glycine/glutamine) which is great for your gut wall - I get 2 lbs of it from Bulk Supplements - cheap way to fly -

Two more hacks for you to try are methylene blue and IR light therapy:
[https://selfhacked.com/2013/08/25/methylene-blue-the-cheapest-cognitive-enhancer/](https://selfhacked.com/2013/08/25/methylene-blue-the-cheapest-cognitive-enhancer/)
[https://selfhacked.com/2013/11/14/my-review-of-lllt/](https://selfhacked.com/2013/11/14/my-review-of-lllt/)

Just get one of those 250 watt IR lights like to brood chicks and lay under it naked - very therapeutic - and apparently glyphosate screws up manganese also - here is a great article about that - you need manganese for SOD -welcome BTW

[http://www.gmoevidence.com/dr-samsel-glyphosate-chelation-of-manganese-is-a-serious-problem/](http://www.gmoevidence.com/dr-samsel-glyphosate-chelation-of-manganese-is-a-serious-problem/)

3) Regarding the potassium: as I understand it, the adrenal glands function according to a ratio of sodium to potassium (Na/K), at least in part. The lower the ratio, the worse they'll get. So I'm nervous about supplementing potassium with that in mind.

### kris90 — 2017-03-14T13:20:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-cfs-fibro-lyme-burnout-etc-totally-bedbound.19219/post-210318>

Some thoughts:

\-Would like to see labs, maybe copies of old bloodwork, or go get updated comprehensive panels
\-Would like to know your endocrine profile (sex hormones, cortisol, thyroid, etc)
\-You keep mentioning that you think you got into this situation from stress. Have you identified the stress? Known, or unknown? Any allergies, or intolerances? Hidden toxin exposure? EMF sensitivity?
\-Sympathetic Nervous system is your immediate fight or flight system. It's meant for short term activation when: you are hungry, you face an immediate threat to your wellbeing, cold exposure and exercise. When you have chronic stimulus of sympathetic nervous system, the HPA-axis is stimulated to release cortisol which is our more long-term stress hormone, because at this point, the body needs energy badly. Chronically elevated cortisol will waste your body away, wreak havoc on immunity, decrease your T4 -> T3 conversion slowing down your metabolic rate, and suppressing your sex hormones. Additionally, it will interfere with sleep, etc. Need to identify the chronic stress.
\-From the above point, if this describes you, you may need to skip the winter ketogenic diet and feed your body with carbs. Cortisol is a glucocorticoid, its goal is to make glucose in the body out of whatever it can (muscle, bone and other proteins). Feeding your body exogenous glucose will help your body rely less on cortisol to make glucose, which automatically reduces your stress.
\-Look into supplementing with adaptogens like ginseng, it might help balance your sympathetic nervous response
\-After checking all your hormones, if you have deficiencies (at the pituitary level) consider an MRI of your brain to rule out a tumor. Sometimes you can have an ACTH secreting tumor in your pituitary that causes chronic cortisol. Need to rule that out.

I can't say for sure, but it would likely exceed the range. Maybe by a lot, or maybe by a little.

I think 4x Cortisol saliva test would be a better gauge, because you can detect both high cortisol, and a dysfunctional diurnal rhythm of cortisol.

Sometimes individuals have low cortisol in the AM, but high cortisol in the PM, representing an inverse diurnal rhythm.

### matthewsb77 — 2017-03-14T19:05:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-cfs-fibro-lyme-burnout-etc-totally-bedbound.19219/post-210344>

> [kris90 said:](/goto/post?id=210318)
>
> Some thoughts:
>
> \-Would like to see labs, maybe copies of old bloodwork, or go get updated comprehensive panels
> \-Would like to know your endocrine profile (sex hormones, cortisol, thyroid, etc)
> \-You keep mentioning that you think you got into this situation from stress. Have you identified the stress? Known, or unknown? Any allergies, or intolerances? Hidden toxin exposure? EMF sensitivity?
> \-Sympathetic Nervous system is your immediate fight or flight system. It's meant for short term activation when: you are hungry, you face an immediate threat to your wellbeing, cold exposure and exercise. When you have chronic stimulus of sympathetic nervous system, the HPA-axis is stimulated to release cortisol which is our more long-term stress hormone, because at this point, the body needs energy badly. Chronically elevated cortisol will waste your body away, wreak havoc on immunity, decrease your T4 -> T3 conversion slowing down your metabolic rate, and suppressing your sex hormones. Additionally, it will interfere with sleep, etc. Need to identify the chronic stress.
> \-From the above point, if this describes you, you may need to skip the winter ketogenic diet and feed your body with carbs. Cortisol is a glucocorticoid, its goal is to make glucose in the body out of whatever it can (muscle, bone and other proteins). Feeding your body exogenous glucose will help your body rely less on cortisol to make glucose, which automatically reduces your stress.
> \-Look into supplementing with adaptogens like ginseng, it might help balance your sympathetic nervous response
> \-After checking all your hormones, if you have deficiencies (at the pituitary level) consider an MRI of your brain to rule out a tumor. Sometimes you can have an ACTH secreting tumor in your pituitary that causes chronic cortisol. Need to rule that out.

I will work to get my labs from last year scanned up. I had pretty comprehensive work done at that point, and I think it'd still be a fair measure of what's going on now since not a lot has changed in that time.

Coritsol reversal has definitely been an issue for me for some time. It's shown up on tests. The last one showed a pretty classic pattern of low AM cortisol and higher cortisol at night. That was a couple years ago, and things have gotten a lot worse since then, so I have no idea what it looks like now.

IDENTIFYING STRESS - yes, I think you're right on the money with this one. Bravo, brother! I have had some severe trauma in my life, and a lot of it got dissociated and split off for survival, for reasons I won't get into here. But it did create some PTSD, HPA-axis dysfunction with chronic elevated stress, depression, anxiety, etc. etc. But I am doing EMDR therapy, which is a form of therapy unlike any other I've tried because it really bypasses the whole analyze-and-talk routine and just gets right into the neurology of it. I'm about two months in right now, and it does seem to be having some effect for sure, possibly quite a bit. Hard to say what does what when I do so much. I think it would have more if my body weren't so totally exhausted, but it is what it is. But yes, I've removed most of the sources of stress by this point. Sensitivities, allergies, EMFs are accounted for as well.

My only issue with the recommendation of a high-carb diet is that my blood sugar is not super stable. It's in the near-prediabetic or diabetic range, depending on the measure you want to use. I also have been taught that with adrenal/HPA-axis problems, you don't want to do lots of carbs. Everybody says ketogenic, ketogenic, ketogenic. Am I wrong about this?

THE ONE ISSUE I HAVE WITH TAKING TESTS, besides all the money it takes, is that often the approaches are the same regardless of what the tests show. I realize that sometimes there's a specific level you need to supplement, and that's where it's going to matter the most, but. . . I'll see what I can do.

### Penny — 2017-03-14T20:04:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-cfs-fibro-lyme-burnout-etc-totally-bedbound.19219/post-210350>

So, here's the low hanging fruit thing...

Are you in a second story room using a lap top? And is that lap top using wifi? If so, you are chronically raising cortisol -I would really start with moving to the ground floor and trying to be outside as much as possible - even touching a tree branch for the electrons - and if cortisol is turned around, do the ol' leptin reset with \*zero\* blue light at night - at least unplug the router at night, cordless phones and put all devices in airplane mode - make sure the wifi is turned off on your computer - even opeing up a window to let some sunlight in would be beneficial - do you have any relatives who live in a sunny climate you might visit for a couple of weeks? Also, see if you have a smart meter and where it is - this is probably the worst of the lot -

as far as getting some peace in your body, thus increasing resonance, you might want to try different ways to stimulate your Vagus nerve :
[https://selfhacked.com/2015/07/30/2...agus-nerve-and-all-you-need-to-know-about-it/](https://selfhacked.com/2015/07/30/28-ways-to-stimulate-your-vagus-nerve-and-all-you-need-to-know-about-it/)

Lie on your back, watch your breath, inhale and utter things to yourself like "love" or "I'm good" - at the end of it, you should feel a sublime feeling of peace - you can also "OM" and listen to music at 432hz - there's a ton of it on youtube - do that as much as possible -

lavender also is amazing as it resets your hypothalamus - lavender oil, lavender tea with glycine - also lemon balm, ashwaghanda and anything else that's GABA inducing - dump a bunch of drops on your bed at night -

Also, isolating yourself is probably not the hottest idea either - let me know if any of these help you - it'll be summer soon, even in MN...

You also might look into EFT (Emotional Freedom Technique) - people have had good luck with it -

### kris90 — 2017-03-14T20:11:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-cfs-fibro-lyme-burnout-etc-totally-bedbound.19219/post-210351>

> [matthewsb77 said:](/goto/post?id=210344)
>
> But yes, I've removed most of the sources of stress by this point. Sensitivities, allergies, EMFs are accounted for as well.

Good! Step 1 is identifying and eliminating the stressors.

> [matthewsb77 said:](/goto/post?id=210344)
>
> My only issue with the recommendation of a high-carb diet is that my blood sugar is not super stable. It's in the near-prediabetic or diabetic range, depending on the measure you want to use. I also have been taught that with adrenal/HPA-axis problems, you don't want to do lots of carbs. Everybody says ketogenic, ketogenic, ketogenic. Am I wrong about this?

I disagree. If Cortisol, our long-term stress hormone that's sole purpose is to provide glucose to the body anyway possible when we are deficient under chronic stress (excessive energy steal syndrome), then it means glucose is EVEN more critical. The more you can get in your diet, the less cortisol will have to steal from your proteins. Carbs in the diet will be anti-catabolic which is a good thing under chronic stress.

Regarding your high blood sugar, there is a lot of evidence that low fat/high carb diets can improve insulin sensitivity, and have been used successfully to treat type 2 diabetes. This is a good read: [https://deniseminger.com/2015/10/06...-a-call-for-some-evolution-of-thought-part-1/](https://deniseminger.com/2015/10/06/in-defense-of-low-fat-a-call-for-some-evolution-of-thought-part-1/)

### JanSz — 2017-03-14T21:51:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-cfs-fibro-lyme-burnout-etc-totally-bedbound.19219/post-210357>

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eye\_movement\_desensitization\_and\_reprocessing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eye_movement_desensitization_and_reprocessing)

**Eye movement desensitization and reprocessing** (**EMDR**) is a form of [psychotherapy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Psychotherapy) developed by [Francine Shapiro](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Francine_Shapiro) that emphasizes the role of distressing memories in some mental health disorders, particularly [posttraumatic stress disorder](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Posttraumatic_stress_disorder) (PTSD).[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eye_movement_desensitization_and_reprocessing#cite_note-shapiro-1) It is an evidence-based therapy used to help with the symptoms of PTSD

1--Is not able to make it (pineal gland deficiency)
2--you are supplementing it excessively, blocking pineal gland

\=======
Blue blockers are crutch.
They are used to fool Mother Nature.
Their original use is for after sundown time when people want to do stuff meant to be done during day time.

When you are as sick as you describe,
you must follow:
1--natural day/night light (preferably)
2--second choice, when there is no daylight use red light only

Trow away blue-blockers.
Do not be around light other than red after sundown.

### Penny — 2017-03-14T23:04:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-cfs-fibro-lyme-burnout-etc-totally-bedbound.19219/post-210361>

I agree - the ketogenic diet would be a fail for you - Jack Kruse has a cookbook you might want to check out - basically, you must eat seasonally - lots of protein - I would be doing brocolli and red cabbage daily for the sulforaphane - it'll get rid of excess estrogen which might be good for your hormone panel - veggies have carbs in them, but it's not like eating a banana -

The other idea I had - assuming you actually have a real diagnosis for Lyme - quite likely in MN - is the Shoemaker test - it's called the Visual Contrast Sensitivity test - it's a great diagnostic tool and it's cheap - also, if you have lyme and have taken the antibiotics, the toxins are still in your fat cells - to get rid of them, you use something called cholestyramine - someone on this forum had lyme and got complete relief with cholestyramine :
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/i-dont-understand.18687/#post-204220](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/i-dont-understand.18687/#post-204220)

Jack Kruse wrote a book called The Epi Paleo RX and in it he has the whole lyme protocol - great read BTW - in fact, every time I read it I pick up something new... he mentions the shoemaker test and cholestyramine -
[https://www.amazon.com/Epi-paleo-Rx...d=1489525441&sr=8-1&keywords=the+epi+paleo+rx](https://www.amazon.com/Epi-paleo-Rx-Prescription-Disease-Reversal/dp/0989057739/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1489525441&sr=8-1&keywords=the+epi+paleo+rx)

### matthewsb77 — 2017-03-15T01:24:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-cfs-fibro-lyme-burnout-etc-totally-bedbound.19219/post-210371>

**LAB RESULTS (some mid-2015, most early-mid 2016)

LYME:** I did the iGenex tests maybe 5 or 6 years back. The test was considered indeterminate because I tested positive on one marker of one test, out of about 6-8 different things on the panel. The one I tested positive on was the Plasmid B-Burgdorfi, "Southern Dot Blot." I can scan the tests in at some point if it would help, but the tests say that <1:40 = negative, 1:40 = indeterminate, and = >1.80 is positive. I was the middle one. So based on this, I went ahead and did the Cowden (herbal) protocol, and felt not change whatsoever in any way. Same with long-term antibiotics targeted at it. NOTHING changed. Not better, not worse, no die-off, no nothing. So I left it alone. Anybody able to comment on this apparent contradiction?
**DHEA -** High, believe it or not. HOWEVER, this was in the early months of severe stress, and I don't know if this is one of those levels that drops after stress. Could anyone inform? This was a test done in 2015.
**Low B vitamins** \- but when I take them, it's too stimulating for my nervous system, so I don't supplement them hardly ever at present. . . I crash and feel worse
**B-12** is HIGH on some tests, LOW on others (for the same period) - WHY?
**MTHFR - HETEROZYGOUS, C677T**. I understand this means I have a single mutation, but what's the proper supplement to deal with this properly?
**Low alpha-lipoid acid
High cholesterol** (though I was eating shittier meat at that time, and now better meat)
**High gadolinium
Low gut bacteria diversity
Slightly elevated E.Coli count in gut
Slightly low manganese and molybdenum
Low Testosterone
Poor breakdown of proteins in stool sample (high levels of un-broken-down proteins)
Cortisol - reversal pattern (obviously) in 2015. . .** haven't tested since
**Glucose** - high, in the near-pre-diabetic or pre-diabetic range

(I'm doing great on DHA and EPA, etc., by the way, as well as Vitamin D and pretty much everything else. If anything I could use less according to these tests, and I'm actually supplementing that more now than before.)

Possibly relevant, although within range:
**URINE CREATININE: 42.23 out of a range of about 23-205**

Look into boosting your redox potential so that you can excrete toxins naturally, lower inflammation and deal with infection effectively. Find the Leptin Rx and 'Redox Rx' in the blogs and pair them with an epi-paleo diet ( read the book and you won't be confused any more) - SEASONAL eating, ( more carbs in summer, more fat in winter, meals during daylight hours with the first immediately after waking), no high temp processed vegetable oils, plenty of fish/shellfish (no fish oil supplements), pork/bacon (for cysteine - sulphur source to help interaction with sunlight), add seaweeds for minerals. Drink spring water from glass bottles (or RO to avoid toxins). Natural sunlight first thing in the morning, and no food for 3 -4 hours before sleep is essential for making melatonin.

Read Kovita's journal, 'becoming me again'. Great info on how to deal with Lyme.

Gerald Pollock's book, The Fourth Phase of Water is a good intro to the way water forms exclusion zones beside hydrophilic surfaces. In our cells these EZs exclude toxins and, once present EZs are increased by red light and UV. Help the process by drinking plenty of good water (cooled to 4 degrees), cold face dunks and sunlight/ firelight on your skin ( extra red light and black light -UVA from bulbs can help but better hydration and redox measures need to be in place first). Farm OP chemicals cause MCS, irritable bladder, autonomic dis-function, most of your symptoms and circadian chaos. The circadian timing/light/magnetism procedures Dr K advises allow us to use primordial pathways to restore function. Things like Lyme are opportunistic infections that take hold when redox is low.

Hormones of the Pituitary
GH
TSH
ACTH
PRL
LH
FSH
MSH
Vasopressin
Oxytocin

So far, on your test report, it looks like this hormones are low:
Luteinizing hormone
Follicle-stimulating hormone
Thyroid-stimulating hormone

Growth hormone
is a major hormone, you should check it out.
Quickly you can do this two tests.

IGF-1
IGFBP3

You should ask your doctor to use (**accurate**) testing method for IGF-1
Commonly IRA (radioimmunoassay) is used.
But you want it to be done using chemoluminescence technique.
\================
For my self I did testing at LabCorp/Esoterix.
This was written on script to get that test done:

Growth Assessment Test Group X (Growth Hormone, IGF-I, IGF-II, IGFBP-3)
Test Code 500901 ... CPT Code 83003, 84305, 83519, 83519
\================
When treating low Growth Hormone with GH injections
optimal goals are not GH but:

IGF-1(300-350)μg/L
IGFBP3(3000)μg/L=3mg/L

.........................
On your first post you said:
"I never did as much work as they did **since I've been exhausted since fairly young."**

**Low Growth Hormone would explain that.**

**Please post your height and weight.**

### Remo — 2017-03-23T12:22:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-cfs-fibro-lyme-burnout-etc-totally-bedbound.19219/post-210919>

Hi Matthew, how are you doing ?? Im currently very brain fogged but I will try to type correctly.

I came to post in here because I have allot in common with your symptoms. Luckily I have seen improvements over the years but there will always be periods of extreme fatigue , brain fog, depression and fatigue.

Last summer I started seeing massive gains in health for 1 simple reason: I was outside in the sun more then I was inside (pokemon go gave me something to do). After this period I was able to start minor HiiT weight training which gave me a massive boost in testosterone/energy/muscle mass.
I started climbing indoors and for some months I was in "relative" great shape.
When winter hit I got ill and bam... it was like I was send back 6 steps back. I think this recurrence when your immune system drops is very common of Lyme disease or other chronic bacterial infections.

A few weeks ago I tried getting back to HiiT but I noticed that I lost the ability to sweat normally, whenever my body wanted to sweat I would feel a thousand needles pinching me. However If I continued trough the pain my body would sweat normally. I think this problem with sweating/inflammation is a sign of leptin resistance and Lyme disease in general.

1 . How are your sweating patterns ? Do you even sweat ? Looking back it was the combination of direct sunlight, earthing with sweating that really resetted my ability to detoxify. When I lost one of those three (especially sunlight!) it corresponded with a lack of detoxification.
2\. Have you ever done HiiT weight training ? Just lifting something heavy while your outside can promote an incredible spike in -IGF1 hormone growth. From personal experience you can do this even IF your are extremely fatigued. Actually.. the more reason to! walking actually increases cortisol for me while HiiT balances it out positively.

3\. I see you are taking quite allot of supplements. The only ones I would recommend (that has helped me):
Sulphur MSM (Gluthatione Detox),
CBD oil, **Hemp Oil is not the same as CBD**, hemp oil is a great oil but it is falsely advertised as CBD oil. I learned this the hardway! True CBD helped me allot with anxiety and concentration/pain.

4\. DHA, you **cannot** supplement DHA! It has to come from fresh raw fish: herring (biggest DHA), mackerel, Sardines, Oysters.
Read Jack's post on Fish supplements and you will understand.
I am curious to hear about your reactions to eating some raw salter herring. When I eat that its like my brain gets a zap! it usually fades away quickly (sadly) but it makes me feel better in general.

5\. Raw Milk Kefir, Jack is against american soiled diary (rightfully so imo) but if you can find a trustworthy local farmer who makes his own kefir then I would go for it. This stuff is magic for the gut bacteria.

Thanks for the info, Brother! Have you looked into getting the Quantlet for yourself? I can't at the moment because I'm paying for the Magnetico, but I may next fall when the weather goes "south" again.

My sweating seems pretty normal. It got a little excessive (mildly) for a while when my health got really bad. But it's pretty normal now, relatively speaking.

Are you doing sulfur, and MSM, or is that one supplement? **My question for you on that:** have you had your sulfur levels tested, and if so, were they low? My sulfur levels seem to be OK, but I don't know if that means more is needed or not needed.

I know Jack's opinion on fish oil. I've switched to an algae DHA supplement that's in the SN2 position. . . and still doing seafood as well.

I get raw milk from my cousin's dairy that's about 40 minutes away. They don't do kefir yet, but I put in the suggestion. Their cows are bred for A2 genes so they're not inflammatory, and my uncle, a cow geneticist, gives me milk from the best cows. Lots of sauerkraut, too.

I get real CBD, I'm certain of it. Omica Organics emulsified tonic.

Here's some more test results. Missing the first page for the Genova (just noticed this), but will get it up when I can. \[Edit\] For some reason I'm having trouble uploading files. . . not sure what's going on.
