### Lucien Burke — 2017-03-27T21:05:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-cfs-fibro-lyme-burnout-etc-totally-bedbound.19219/post-211239>

Hey Matthew ~ I've gotta say that your situation sounds a lot like mine. I became ill after a string of infections, stress, bad diet and light environment. I also came down with CFS about a month after a bad wisdom tooth extraction... I had bad cavitations in that area. I'm now basically bed bound with severe neurological symptoms and fatigue. Maybe we can bounce some ideas back and forth and see what works. Here's what I've been focusing on:

\-Cold (When I can, I also have bad body temp issues. But cold helps A LOT when I can tolerate it. Especially for cognitive function. I can tolerate this ice helmet even at my worst: [http://iceyourhead.com/cryohelmet/](http://iceyourhead.com/cryohelmet/))

\-Sun makes me feel a ton better when I make the effort to get outside

\-Intermittent fasting/Ketosis makes me feel better

\-Low Dose Naltrexone (Rx to take before bed)

\-Vielight intranasal LLLT infrared device. Use before bed gives me more energy the next day.

\-Ozone therapy. I bought a generator for home sessions. Rectal and ear insufflations. making ozone water, oil. Could help a lot with lyme and jaw infections.

\-Sauna

Good luck man let me know how it's goin...

### Lucien Burke — 2017-03-27T21:07:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-cfs-fibro-lyme-burnout-etc-totally-bedbound.19219/post-211240>

One more thing...

try NT Factor in conjunction with eating a lot of DHA to heal cell membranes and replace your lipids. I've just started to use it recently.

[https://www.amazon.com/Nutricology-...UTF8&qid=1490638066&sr=1-7&keywords=NT+factor](https://www.amazon.com/Nutricology-Energylipids-Powder-150-Gram/dp/B00908XN20/ref=sr_1_7_s_it?s=hpc&ie=UTF8&qid=1490638066&sr=1-7&keywords=NT+factor)

### matthewsb77 — 2017-03-28T18:22:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-cfs-fibro-lyme-burnout-etc-totally-bedbound.19219/post-211300>

> [Lucien Burke said:](/goto/post?id=211240)
>
> One more thing...
>
> try NT Factor in conjunction with eating a lot of DHA to heal cell membranes and replace your lipids. I've just started to use it recently.
>
> [https://www.amazon.com/Nutricology-Energylipids-Powder-150-Gram/dp/B00908XN20/ref=sr\_1\_7\_s\_it?s=hpc&ie=UTF8&qid=1490638066&sr=1-7&keywords=NT factor](https://www.amazon.com/Nutricology-Energylipids-Powder-150-Gram/dp/B00908XN20/ref=sr_1_7_s_it?s=hpc&ie=UTF8&qid=1490638066&sr=1-7&keywords=NT+factor)

Not cool that you're so sick, but cool that I'm not the only one. Yes, let's work together, man!

I'm seeing small improvements from managing my light environment, taking an SN-2 algae DHA supplement from MitoLife, CBD emulsified tonic from Omica Organics, eating ketogenically and with an eye to Leptin, taking loads of oil of oregano (without this I think I'd be dead), doing red-light sauna therapy, getting as much sun as possible (unfortunately I live in MN, but the next 5 months are a good time of year for sun here), and drinking lots of good water. I'm doing some other things, as you'll find in the thread here, but these are the things I know help me.

I've heard of people taking Low-Dose Naltrexone for this, but I was resistant to trying it. What do you feel it helps you with? I'd rather never take another long-term prescription for as long as I live, if I can help it, BUT I'm not stupid if something will help me a lot.

By ozone, do you just mean simply a box that makes O3? Because I have one in my room. It's a dual air-purifier/ionizer and ozonator, but I don't use the ozonator right now because I heard from a few sources that it increases free radicals or something. . . Now, full disclosure, with everything else I'm trying to do for myself, I haven't fully looked into that claim. Is it something you know helps you, or are you just doing it because you've been told it's a good idea?

I had not heard of NT Factor, but I was looking for something much like this, so thank you! Vielight I have heard of. . . can't purchase yet since I am still paying off the Magnetico. But I can tell you that the Magnetico has helped a lot. What seems to help me the most have been Kruse's big three (light, water, and magnetism, especially 1 and 3) + mind and emotions. (See next paragraph.)

Also maybe of use to you: brain retraining. I did the Lightning Process seminars (convinced one of them to come to my house because I was too sick to go anywhere) and although I didn't experience any improvement with it initially, it was part of a larger program of mind-body work I've been doing for over a year now. I don't necessarily agree with the New-Age community's somewhat naive belief that you can do anything just by the power of your mind, but I do think it's often overlooked as a factor in healing. I've also been doing EMDR therapy to neutralize triggers in my nervous system, lots of deep breathing, visualization, looking myself directly in the eyes in the mirror and saying "I love you" (and meaning it and feeling it). I even go so far as to teach myself to think like a healthy person. . . you know, planning what I'm going to do in the next few months and years with my newfound health, minimizing how much I speak about illness outside of contexts where I need to, teaching myself to make jokes about things that are hurting. I do what I need to do -- tests, supplements, rest, communicating what I need, whenever these things are practically required -- but I keep seeing wellness too. Don't get me wrong, I'm still spending a lot of my time and energy on healing, but with the right mindset, I like to think.

### Lucien Burke — 2017-03-29T20:39:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-cfs-fibro-lyme-burnout-etc-totally-bedbound.19219/post-211341>

LDN modulates your immune system, improves circadian rhythm, and reduces inflammation by boosting endorphins and enkephalins. It's been proven useful in cancer and various auto-immune conditions. A lot of times it's used for chronic pain but its also been observed to work with fatigue. It's definitely worth a shot, especially if you have a high serum sed. rate.

Ozone is an oxidative therapy that yes, does increase free radicals in the short term but is a hormetic therapy that increases the bodies production of endogenous anti-oxidants like glutathione peroxidase and SOD. It also kills or inactive any infectious issue you could possible have. It also increases NAD+ and improves oxygenation of tissues.

You can look into generators and accessories for home therapy here: [http://www.promolife.com](http://www.promolife.com)

As Jansz mentions often... I think and feel that a massive root of this disease is issues with fatty acid composition of tissues and dehydration... So yeah DHA and NT Factor are huge.
Along with Ketosis I'm also gonna try to implement more intermittent fasting to up regulate autophagy and mitophagy. (Cellular cleanup)

@[Lucien Burke](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19548/)

[http://ntfactor.com/#&panel1-2](http://ntfactor.com/#&panel1-2)
NTFactor® is:

-   **a proprietary formulation of phospholipids** (pronounced "fos-fo-lipids"), which are special fats that are the key building blocks of the membranes of cells. These membranes not only protect each cell from the outside world, but they also hold receptors that enable each cell to communicate with the rest of the human organism and function in a coordinated, healthy way.
-   Damaged membranes interfere with absorption of nutrients and energy production and lead to premature aging and disease.
-   Cell membranes are damaged by free radicals, harmful molecules that are a by-product of life and a result of exposure to polluted air and other toxins, stress, smoking, being overweight and eating poor quality, refined food. Antioxidants neutralize free radicals but cannot repair damage that has already been done, and neither can other supplements. NTFactor® is specially formulated to do the job.
    **\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[**

I wonder if you have some more information on NTFactor?
NTFactor is described in just about the way we would like to hear it.
The problem is: does it really delivers on that promise.
Details are missing (covered by the word "proprietary").

I am getting most of my information on this topic from what Patricia & Ed Kane post, mostly on their web sites.
[http://www.bodybio.com/](http://www.bodybio.com/)
[http://bodybio-wellness.com/](http://bodybio-wellness.com/)

Among other things, ED discusses imposters and how to figure them out.
Since NTFactor does not give much information, there is no way to figure it that way.

It is about eating XXX and figuring out a way to deliver XXX to cell membrane.

The word "Phospholipid" have been cannibalized and and turned in such a way that lecithin is called phospholipid.

The shorter version is that what BodyBio produces and call PC is a product that have phospholipids in form of liposomes. Those liposomes are not digested and are water friendly.

Lecithin, even if it was about same product, it really is not.
It is dissolved in fat and it is digestible.

What is NTFactor I do not know. Do you?

BodyBio PC (Phosphatidylcholine) 300 SoftGels (1300mg)

Yeah I don't really know any more about it than you @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) but to my knowledge it is recommended by the Kane protocol and seemed legit. I was talking to another me/cfs patient who saw great results from it so I thought it was worth a shot, and I think I feel a bit better since starting it. I think it should obviously be used in conjunction with lots of good quality marine and grass fed omega 3's. If you find any promising new info on NT Factor please let me know @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) because like you said, if it delivers on the marketing that would make it a big asset.

Both names have excellent products, I am only talking about lack of information.

Since you have used nice sounding words:
"good quality marine and grass fed omega 3's"
I assume
that you are not familiar with work of Patricia Kane and her husband Ed Kane
I suggest that you read all or most of their (short)PDF's
[http://www.bodybio.com/storecategory129.aspx](http://www.bodybio.com/storecategory129.aspx)
or at least the one on PC.
[http://www.bodybio.com/BodyBio/docs/BodyBioBulletin-Phosphatidylcholine-NC.pdf](http://www.bodybio.com/BodyBio/docs/BodyBioBulletin-Phosphatidylcholine-NC.pdf)
and then buy their book ($35.70):
[http://www.bodybio.com/storecategory126.aspx](http://www.bodybio.com/storecategory126.aspx)
To make it short
there is nothing good or bad about Omega3 or Omega6 or any other fatty acids.
We need all of them, in right proportions.
The problem
that I have when I see emphasis made on Omega3
is that
excess of Omega3 coincides with lots of (popular) illnesses that people have this days.
And it happens
that when tested many of those problems coincide with high omega3.
So,
I think that it is good idea to test and find out where one stands.

....
(popular) illnesses
Lyme
Multiple Sclerosis
Fibromyalgia
Autism
Parkinsons
ALS
PDD, pervasive developmental disorder
Chronic Fatigue Immune Dysfunction (CFIDS) also known as Chronic Fatigue Syndrome (CFS) and Myalgic Encephalomyelitis (ME)

number or other

>
> Yes, it's a chewable, but it lists the actual lipids used. And yes, it's made from soy lecithin. I'm sure if you're implying the lecithin ones are the GOOD ones, or the NOT good ones. Because its looks like the BodyBio product is made from soy, too.

When you read one of the PDF's I mention in post above,
it is really about lecithin

one is ordinary cheap, natural, lecithin
the other is ordered into liposomes (and cost more)

The fact that both may originate from soy is not important,
Or rather not important for the ordered kind.

.............................
One big assumption of the liposomal one is that it will be delivered to cell membranes in tact and then used in repairing cell membranes.

The other lecithin (the natural type) will be digested, taken apart, will cease being phospholipid.
Young healthy body could still pick up the pieces and make phospholipid out of them, but in this discussions we are concerned with sick people.

Kane protocol = PKProtocol
It is described in the book I posted above.
That protocol runs to blood tests.
BodyBio Wellness Report
and
BodyBio Fatty Acid Report

[http://bodybio-wellness.com/report-fatty-acid.html](http://bodybio-wellness.com/report-fatty-acid.html)

Wellness can be done at Labcorp
Fatty Acids must be done at John Hopkins

Both tests are post-processed at BodyBio
Samples of those post-processed tests are here:
[http://bodybio-wellness.com/report-blood-test.html](http://bodybio-wellness.com/report-blood-test.html)
[http://bodybio-wellness.com/report-fatty-acid.html](http://bodybio-wellness.com/report-fatty-acid.html)
\------------------------------------------------

In this conversation it is assumed that the NTFactor would be used instead of PC (from BodyBio).

As far as I can tell, need for initial PC use and further dossing decisions are evaluated based on this test:

Creatine Kinase (CK), Total Plus Isoenzymes Test Number: 002154 CPT: 82550 (total); 82552 (isoenzymes)
I included that test here:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4)

PC, liquid or in pills is oral.
In beginning, to speed up the process intravenous phosphatidylcholine is usually used.
That IV one, is phosphatidylcholine only, while PC contains also other phospholipids.

There is more to PKProtocol then just use of PC.
\----------------
As far as I can tell, most peoples on that protocol have already massive amounts of omega3.

Omega3 is strong, when present it suppresses Omega6.
It is not the same when Omega6 is in excess.

### JanSz — 2017-03-30T17:53:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/severe-cfs-fibro-lyme-burnout-etc-totally-bedbound.19219/post-211406>

> [Lucien Burke said:](/goto/post?id=211400)
>
> I also thought that the emphasis was supposed to be on DHA, and that the ratio of 6 to 3 was supposed to be 4:1

Yes, this ratio or similar ratios are quoted.
Usually that ratio is not really defined.
Those who read it, assume, that it is ratio O6 to O3, and that is bad assumption.
Besides
omega3 and omeg6 is not one component in each class.
Each class consists of many different fatty acids.
If you take those that are analyzed you got four in O3 and 6 in O6 (see picture below).
Then you could have zero in all but one in each class and you still may be fine if the ratio is right. (I doubt that it is actually the case, just opposite).

But wait @[matthewsb77](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19899/) test shows that his ALA is = zero.
For the matthew's sake, I hope that it is not true.

The best that I was able to figure about (O6/O3) (4:1) ratio is that it is advisable to have a diet that have
four parts of (LA) for each one part of (ALA)
That is stated in one of those PDF's.
Yehuda figured it out.

I called the lab that did that test. Apparently, it's not the test that's a problem. It just means that it was below the limit detectable. ">=0.09 wt%." Doesn't mean it's zero, but it means that it's way too low. I'm not really sure what if anything can be done about that. I'm wondering if there's possibly a gene issue there, since I'm also mildly allergic to flax.

I checked in with the lab about the ratio, too, and the ratio on my tests is: **5.2-to-1 O6 to O3** which the lab claims is relatively healthy (I know they're using the rest of the "healthy" population as a guideline). The overall number (%) for Omega3 was 7.0 and the value for Omega6 was 36.1. According to what I'm reading online, Omega 6s are typically supposed to be higher, and a good ratio is 4:1 Omega 6 to Omega 3. Everything I'm reading is saying the Omega 6s are often a bit higher and that's fine as long as it's not too high.

[I'm pretty sure Kruse used something from this guy's blog in his book, and he's saying in this post that the ratio might not even matter much. Providing it's not from vegetable that the O6 is coming. http://www.marksdailyapple.com/why-the-omega-3omega-6-ratio-may-not-matter-after-all/](http://www.marksdailyapple.com/why-the-omega-3omega-6-ratio-may-not-matter-after-all/)

Can't the body use another Omega 3 -- such as DHA -- that could be overly supplemented to sort of do the job of low or nonexistent ALA?

Like does it just reattach to a different enzyme? Pardon my - gaping hole - ignorance This is also why giving someone zinc might be a really bad idea...

# Thread: EMFs, the WiFi Router, and the Smart Meter
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emfs-the-wifi-router-and-the-smart-meter.19220/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### matthewsb77 — 2017-03-13T19:22:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emfs-the-wifi-router-and-the-smart-meter.19220/post-210268>

Quick question: why is it that the WiFi Router and the Smart Meter are so bad, and I mean the DETAILS?

I keep hearing people say this, but I have used a meter to test the magnetic, electrical, and microwave fields of the WiFi box and the Smart Meter, and while they do give off something, it's not that much by the time you're a couple feet away from it. My electric piano puts off a lot more of a field than either of those, measuring strictly by the number of milligauss it puts off. So what's the big deal? I feel like I'm missing something important here.

### Inger — 2017-03-13T19:53:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emfs-the-wifi-router-and-the-smart-meter.19220/post-210270>

I think it is because they are pulsed?

Smart meter too makes some weird pulsed EMF that goes through all the electrical devices in the house. NOT good. You might not see it on your meter but it is there.... The meter is not that very precise, you might have to buy a real expensive one top be able to measure everything. But a meter still helps a lot. To give an idea.

Are you shutting off the breaker to your room? That would be big. I do, and after I started to do it I do not have to pee at all in the night anymore! My meter shows the same... but something have changed..... believe me!

Just try to shut of everything you can.
Even if your meter shows no difference. I am very sure that it has a benefit

### StuartG — 2017-03-14T07:14:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-sydney-australia.19224/post-210301>

50 years of age. (feeling like a teenager)
Parents both living - (Dad) x2 bypass ops, pacemaker/defib, bowel reconstruct, due for heart valve next month. (Mum) Breast Cancer surgery/removal post 6 months.

Self employed. Went hard for years (indoors though physical job) Sport fell by the wayside.
Snapped Meniscus in 2008/surgery /took 4 yrs to come good.

Increasing chronic systemic inflammation - multiple crippling heal spurs, plantar fasciitis.
Add in a unfortunate and undiagnosed or investigated thoracic hyper-extension resulting in fracture.

Five years of gradually increasing discomfort/ curvature and degeneration lead to bone on nerve December 2012. (why are we hero's and don't get things looked at!)
Three months of every scan possible, I went on 12 painkillers daily and a back brace. Back spasms were insane.

Add in depression, pre-diabetes, high blood pressure, unfavourable cholesterol ratios, triglycerides and a few other markers all gone. SNP for MTHFR.
Stomach ulcers looming, April 2013 I went cold turkey on all processed foods and beverages AND I was 50kgs obese.

Intensive Chiro got me going, decompressing daily at home. Spent 3/4 of most days for 3 months wrapped up in heat gel packs to find relief. Eventually began swimming to open up spine.

Long story short, no meds/ nothing since May 2013.

4 years along, down 50kg,. Feb 2017 ramped up Blue Light mitigation and introduced CT (just cold baths twice daily)

Excited to report 3 years of lingering thoracic daily (crippling some weeks) has completely gone! Last week I completed 18 days straight mini walking Challenge for Charity walking 215 km. This amount of gravity’s compression usually would have made this impossible seeking many modalities for relief. I feel like a Ferrari. Like I said, thoracic pain is gone!! Amazing. Light, Water, Magnetism.

### JanSz — 2017-03-14T15:53:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions.19225/post-210331>

\[qu ote="Assadi, post: 210308, member: 19909"\]Hey i have a few questions would appreciate an answer thx

1-Is it ok to be in front of blue light computer mostly as long as you have blue blocking glasses and there are a lot of people who are in front of blue light but still seem healthy
Every human is unique. Some are strong other are weak. Some are tall other are short.
Think of situation, when being outside, when would you see the blue light and when not.
Answer, ---->when you can see daylight additional light from computer or cellphone screen is of lesser concern.
So, your major problem (mismatch) with light is from the screen is when sun is out.
Other considerations are less than major.
Major action of blue light is to tell your pineal gland if there is a day or night.
At night pineal gland is allowed to make melatonin.
Melatonin stops adrenals from making cortisol.

2-Is keto ok for teens and isn t it bad if you train instensly for the adrenals

3-How can i skyrocket energy levels other things then water , sleep and micronutrient
Support your mitochondria.
What you call micronutrient support is likely different than what I call it.
I do testing every 6 months to figure out my status and then adjust my diet and list of supplements.
Following tests:
At LabCorp:
Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum
Homocysteine, Plasma

At Spectracell
[https://www.spectracell.com/patients/get-tested/](https://www.spectracell.com/patients/get-tested/)
[https://www.spectracell.com/patients/products/](https://www.spectracell.com/patients/products/)
Micronutrient Test (MNT)
**$** 390.00

I also use selenium and Lugols.

4- what are the top 5 supllements and are hot bath bad

See above, do test, figure out your individual needs.
each person is separate individual with different needs.
\[/quote\]

### Jack Kruse — 2017-04-04T00:51:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions.19225/post-211697>

Since they are making health claims they will have to deal with the FDA soon.

As the sun sets, blue visible light is preferentially scattered (removed) from sunlight, leaving an emission appearing orange-red \[600 - 700 nm\]. At night, there is darkness with limited visible light emitted from the stars, with the exception of when there is a full moon. During the full moon, there is five times the amount of visible light emitted from the sky, and significant light emitted in the blue visible range (Ugolini, 2007).

Humans evolved being exposed to different spectra of light in the morning, the late afternoon and evening. So it should not be surprising that human physiology is profoundly affected by the daily and seasonal changes in the visible light spectrum. Exposure to the appropriate spectrum of light during the day and evening enhances human health and well being, immune response and productivity (Roberts, 2000; Vetch et al., 2004; Cutolo M et al., 2005; Heschong and Roberts, 2009). However, exposure to light sources that do not match the natural solar spectrum to the time of day or evening, is hazardous to human health (Wilson, 1972; Stevens et al., 2007; Rea et al., 2008; Erren and Reiter, 2008; Arendt, 2010). The reason visible light has such a powerful effect on human health is that light exposure through the eye modifies circadian rhythm (Gaddy et al., 1993; Roberts, 1995; Czeisler et al., 1995).

### WalterNL — 2017-04-04T12:12:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions.19225/post-211710>

> [Assadi said:](/goto/post?id=211016)
>
> Hey i was wearing blue light blocking glasses during the day and i felt super tierd is it beacuse my body started producing melatonin during the day or is it something else

Yes that could be. During the day you need full spectrum sunlight, which includes blue light.

# Thread: Tietze syndrome
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tietze-syndrome.19227/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thomas — 2017-03-14T19:02:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tietze-syndrome.19227/post-210343>

Hello guys.

I am asking if maybe Jack has any clues about the tietze syndrome? My mom got diagnosed with it, she has thoracic pain at times. Is it a problem linked with mitochondria? Is there anything else to do apart from symptomatic pain management?

thanks.

### Nicolaj Sølvsten — 2017-03-14T19:19:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tietze-syndrome.19227/post-210346>

I think tietze syndrom = inflammation = circardian mismatch. So to help fix it the cause needs to be addressed.. This includes sleeping when the sun is down, or atleast block blue light, Spending time outset during the day. If her sleep is messed up, it should be clear that factors disrupting the sleep are very important to fix. Best regards!

### Nicolaj Sølvsten — 2017-03-14T19:20:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tietze-syndrome.19227/post-210347>

I think grounding would be incredibly helpful because it reduces inflammation.

### Mito1 — 2017-03-15T15:58:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tietze-syndrome.19227/post-210403>

.
I know first hand I had the same issue due to a car accident - it popped the 2nd and 3rd rib out of the sternum. Very painful and even more painful to pop it back in place. Even if a D.O. could pop it back in place - poof it popped out again. Luck for me I ran into a very specialized chiropractor - perhaps less than 5 docs are trained in his field. It wasn't the rib per se the issue but the vertebrae in the back that was out. Mine was C-7 - popping that in place was beyond painful but once in place the rib never popped out again.

.

# Thread: Baby toys
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-toys.19228/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2017-03-14T21:08:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-toys.19228/post-210355>

Hallo,

do you guys have some suggestions which toys you can buy for a child 6 months+?
Some relatives want to make some gifts.

### Penny — 2017-03-14T23:10:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-toys.19228/post-210362>

> [Butters said:](/goto/post?id=210355)
>
> Hallo,
>
> do you guys have some suggestions which toys you can buy for a child 6 months+?
> Some relatives want to make some gifts.

Anything that helps their sensory perception - I love the whole Montessori method:
[http://www.montessori-on-a-budget.com/2013/04/top-10-things-for-montessori-baby.html](http://www.montessori-on-a-budget.com/2013/04/top-10-things-for-montessori-baby.html)
[http://www.dailymontessori.com/montessori-materials/montessori-toys-gifts/](http://www.dailymontessori.com/montessori-materials/montessori-toys-gifts/)

You can't go wrong with wooden blocks and anything soft -
Maria Montessori said don't give a child anything to do unless you are 90% sure he can successfully do it - it was great advice really

We loved the bouncy chair - just don't get one of those rolling chairs -

### drezy — 2017-03-19T00:39:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-toys.19228/post-210625>

I'm with @[Penny](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/446/) .

To back up what was said. Think textures that hands and a mouth could appreciate: parallel lines, wavy, spiky(within reason), smooth, abrupt depth changes, gradual depth changes, stepped depth changes. Don't discount warm and cold as well as still and vibrating. My child took to cold object so much that we threw random toys in the freezer. Cold items kinda makes sense based on this whole site/science.

Might as well see if your baby can explore textures as well as mini-ct at the same time.

### Inger — 2017-03-19T11:11:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-toys.19228/post-210649>

Check out it does not have any chemicals in it... many toys have  the baby will put it in the mouth etc. Babies want to taste everything!

### Penny — 2017-03-20T00:04:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-toys.19228/post-210701>

One of those ride on fire trucks was a great toy - and then there is the veritable wagon -

### drezy — 2017-03-20T03:40:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-toys.19228/post-210722>

\*Warning\* I'm ahead of 6 months a bit here, but it's worth mentioning while I've got it loaded in memory

My boy is 12 now, but I loved being a dad to a young child. I'm a little ahead of the 6 month age, but one of our favorite activities was "identify the spice". I do quite bit of cooking and have so many different spices.

Name 5-15 spices and then have the child smell them. Then, later, have the child smell the spice and name it. Sensory + lingual skills are involved.

Even though my boy is 12 now, he walked into my dad's place when my dad was making "maple" bacon and informed my dad that "That's fenugreek, not maple." My dad was taken aback a bit until he read the ingredient list t find out that, sure enough, fenugreek (since it smells like maple but is cheaper) was used.

### JuicerMoz — 2017-07-31T12:41:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-toys.19228/post-220500>

A toy is an item that is used in play, especially one designed for such use. Playing with toy scan be an enjoyable means of training young children for life in society. Different materials like wood, clay, paper, and plastic are used to make toys.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-31T16:58:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-toys.19228/post-220527>

Make sure none are from China.

### Butters — 2017-10-21T22:34:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-toys.19228/post-226347>

Thanks. Time showed she loves more running around and climbing on things

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/microcirculation-headache.19230/post-216798>

Welp, an improved microcirculation would definitely be beneficial to our overall health. One of the treatments I've seen online is carboxytherapy (C02 therapy) wherein therapeutic C02 levels will be introduced to our body. There's this video-microscopic study of nailfold capilliaries on patients having classical migraine wherein the findings demonstrated patients with migraine often have subclinical microcirculatory disorders. So there really is a correlation. The most convenient and promising I read was CO2 therapy introduced transdermally (through the skin) so it's non-invasive at all. Hope this helps!

# Thread: Mito Labs Question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mito-labs-question.19232/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### matthewsb77 — 2017-03-15T17:34:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mito-labs-question.19232/post-210412>

Making new threads for individual topics to focus my search.

My question for this one: if the labs show your mitochondria are working correctly, does that mean they are, or is that just one little indicator?

Mine was a Genova Diagnostics NutrEval test that included Metabolic Analysis Markers. Under the sections for Carb Metabolism, everything was out of whack (two low, one high). But under the Energy Metabolism section, everything was within range (see below for the names of what was checked) except one, which was trending low but not dangerously, and that one was Cis-Aconitic Acid. Malic acid was just about to leave the green (healthy) section toward high.

Tested: Citric Acid, Cis-Aconitic Acid, Isocritic Acid, a-Ketoglutaric Acid (AKG), Succinic Acid, Malic Acid, b-Oh-B-Methylglutaric Acid (HMG).

### matthewsb77 — 2017-03-15T19:21:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-rid-of-melatonin-supplements.19233/post-210421>

Hey guys! I've recently learned through Dr. Kruse how bad it is to take exogenous melatonin. I've taken it for about 8.5 years. The last two years, due to some severe health problems, I got very little sleep of any kind. Then, finally, after incorporating Jack's work (light environment, Magnetico, etc.), I'm finally starting to sleep consistently. I have had dreams three or four nights in a row now. I haven't had that . . . ever.

**My question now is, how do I get rid of the melatonin without going insomniac again?** Obviously some of you will disagree with this, but getting sleep is more important than being perfect and not taking melatonin. However, I want to detox from using it in a way that will allow my body to start producing its own again. How should I go about that? **Assume I'm already doing everything else correctly with light, magnetism, water, EMFs, etc.** **and that other sleep aids don't help. Assume this is going to be more of a process than an immediate cold-turkey.** Thanks!

(Phrased in another way, how important is stopping melatonin use in the hierarchy of important things to correct in order to be well? Obviously sleep is at or near #1.)

### matthewsb77 — 2017-03-15T19:36:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-rid-of-melatonin-supplements.19233/post-210422>

By the way, I don't find that relaxing herbs or teas, progress relaxation, or any other supplement helps me, except Diphenhydramine citrate (Benadryl, basically, which I'm sure is as bad as or worse than taking melatonin).

### Brent Patrick — 2017-03-16T10:04:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-bulbs-australia.19235/post-210442>

Hello Caroline ,thank you for your prompt reply

would you be able to tell me exactly what you purchased?

### JanSz — 2017-03-16T16:06:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/black-bulbs-australia.19235/post-210459>

> [Brent Patrick said:](/goto/post?id=210440)
>
> Hi hoping Members from Australia can steer me in the right direction regarding purchasing Black bulbs suitable for OZ ?
>
> I have acquired incandescent bulbs and NIR Bulbs but I am at a loss to find suitable Black Bulbs
>
> any advice would be greatly appreciated!

Black bulbs are about UV.
Those bulbs have insignificant amounts of UV.
Usually we do not even know what kind and how much UV they produce.
UV that we want to get is UVa and UVb.
We want it because
UVa makes vit A and skin tan
UVb makes vit D
One can do blood test and see where they are.
That is a best way to gauge UV exposure.
UV from sunlight is the best.
We need a lots of UV over max skin area.
To receive sufficient amounts of UV skin must be hardened first.
Without hardened skin one talks skin-burn, cancer.
It takes about a month of small incremental increases to harden skin.
That is a lots of work, make sure to do it only once (and do not loose it).
Suntan saloons have usually worry about skin tanning, so they have only UVa.
Besides, that is separate trip, hard to keep up daily.
Better solution is using Sperti lamp at home.
Again, there is a White and Red Sperti.
We want Red Sperti.
This one:
Sperti Fiji SUN Table Top Tanning Lamp
[https://www.amazon.com/Sperti-Fiji-Table-Tanning-Lamp/dp/B001ID8P78/ref=sr\_1\_fkmr0\_2\_a\_it?ie=UTF8&qid=1489672760&sr=8-2-fkmr0&keywords=red sperti](https://www.amazon.com/Sperti-Fiji-Table-Tanning-Lamp/dp/B001ID8P78/ref=sr_1_fkmr0_2_a_it?ie=UTF8&qid=1489672760&sr=8-2-fkmr0&keywords=red+sperti)

I have already hardened skin.
My skin is originally white. Sort of normal white, not darkish not overly white.
Got this lamp last beginning of September
Use it 16min every morning.
Recently got blood test, waiting for results of my Vit D

Vit D goal:
minimum:
Vitamin D (25-Hydroxyvitamin D)(250nmol/L=100.16ng/mL)
up to 1.5 of above is still within normal
do not
go above 2x of that (it would be really really hard to do)

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I still must supplement vit A.

Includes: Complete Blood Count (CBC) w/ Differential: Hematocrit; hemoglobin; mean corpuscular volume (MCV); mean corpuscular hemoglobin (MCH); mean corpuscular hemoglobin concentration (MCHC); red cell distribution width (RDW); percentage and absolute differential counts; platelet count; red cell count; white blood cell count; immature granulocytes. Hemoglobin (Hgb) A1c Insulin,

Fasting Lipid Profile: Cholesterol, total; high-density lipoprotein (HDL) cholesterol; low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol (calculation); triglycerides; very low-density lipoprotein (VLDL) cholesterol (calculation) Magnesium, RBC
Thyroid Profile w/ TSH: Free thyroxine index; T3 uptake (THBR); thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH); thyroxine (T4)

Chem Panel: Glucose, Serum; Uric Acid, Serum; BUN (Urea Nitrogen); Creatinine, Serum; BUN/Creatinine Ratio; Sodium, Serum; Potassium, Serum; Chloride, Serum; Calcium, Serum; Carbon Dioxide; Phosphorus, Serum; Protein, Total, Serum; Albumin, Serum; Globulin, Total; A/G Ratio; Bilirubin, Total; Alkaline Phosphatase, Serum; LDH (Lactic Acid Dehydrogenase); AST (SGOT)(Aspartate Aminotransferase); ALT (SGPT) (Alanine Aminotransferase); GGT (Gamma Glutamyl Transpeptidase); Iron, Serum;

Cholesterol, Total; Triglycerides -Kidney Function Profile (Included in the Chem Panel) Liver Function Profile: Bilirubin, direct (all other tests are included in the Chem Panel)

Ferritin, Serum Iron, Serum w/ TIBC: Percent of saturation; serum iron; total iron binding capacity; unsaturated iron binding capacity

Urinalysis, Complete Profile: Color; appearance; specific gravity; pH; protein; glucose; occult blood; ketones; leukocyte esterase; nitrite; bilirubin; urobilinogen; microscopic examination of urine sediment.

The Female Hormone Optimization Panel provides a detailed evaluation on the hormone levels in women, giving you enough information on how you can improve your metabolism, balance your hormones, and optimize your health through lifestyle changes or hormone replacement therapies. Significant deviations from the normal range may require further evaluation by your physician.

Includes:
Complete Blood Count (CBC) with Differential: (Hematocrit; hemoglobin; mean corpuscular volume (MCV); mean corpuscular hemoglobin (MCH); mean corpuscular hemoglobin concentration (MCHC); red cell distribution width (RDW); percentage and absolute differential counts; platelet count; red cell count; white blood cell count; immature granulocytes)

Comprehensive Metabolic Profile (CMP) (includes eGFR): (A:G ratio; albumin, serum; alkaline phosphatase, serum; ALT (SGPT); AST (SGOT); bilirubin, total; BUN; BUN:creatinine ratio; calcium, serum; carbon dioxide, total; chloride, serum; creatinine, serum; globulin, total; glucose, serum; potassium, serum; protein, total, serum; sodium, serum.)

### Stardust — 2017-05-28T08:32:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-i-go.19236/post-214910>

Grandmother: Appendix removed & hysterectomy 3years later developed Dementia
Mother: fibrocystic ovary, Appendix removed, High Cholesterol, hysterectomy & undiagnosed intestinal flare up in the last 3 year.
Me: Diagnosed with dyslexia as a child, Lethargic my whole life, brain fog, Migraines since childhood, Tension headaches as adult, Appendix removed at 10, PMS at youth & PMDD after childbearing.

Grew up in a suburban neighborhood until 18 when I moved to Hayward Ca. Moved back to my hometown after a year. At 19 I developed a chronic cough that sound like a tick. I was on many different types of medications in an attempt to calm or stop the cough. I also had many tests done. After 5 years I was referred to UCSF where I started receiving botox injections into my vocal cords. I still coughed just could not talk for 2 months until it wore off. Right before the doctors cut my Vagus nerve as the next course of treatment I got pregnant. After reading Dr. Kruse's work I look back and am thankful they didn't cut it. I coughed until my 3rd trimester where my cough calmed down on its own. My hormones after I had my first child got very bad. After my second even worse. I would spend 2 weeks after my period started ok. Right before I ovulated I would panic because I knew the cascade was coming. After I ovulated I became someone different. 1.5 years ago after my first child was diagnosed with ADHD I started my journey to health and wellness for myself and my family.
**First years health wins:**
\*Removed cleaning chemicals replaced with Essential oils and safe options
\*Stopped using all medication (Tylenol, Aleve, Ibuprofen and allergy medications) for myself and family switched to Essential oils
\*Switched to only Organically grown produce.
\*Switched to free range chicken,grass fed beef and wild caught seafood

At the end of last year I read Dr. Kruse's book. Dots started connecting and so my real to optimal began.
**Current**:
**Morning**: 1-2hours outside 75% skin exposed while grounded.
**Noon**: 1-3hours outside 100% skin exposed while grounded.
**Evening**: 1-2 hours outside before sunset grounded.
**Blue blockers on at sunset**.
Wifi is shut off in the morning
Doing face dunks 3 times a week.
Taking colder showers and jumping in the pool every day.
Started Win Hoff breathing technique this week.
Eating Epi-Paleo
**Supplements:**
_GI Repair containing:_ L-Glutamine, Aloe Vera, Methylsulfonylmethane, Slippery Elm Bark, Marshmallow root, D-Glucosamine HCI, Deglycyrrhizinated & Proprietary Enzyme blend (Amylase,Lactase,Cellulase,Invertase&Maltase).
CoQ10

Although I know that I can not get health in the environment I got sick in this is where my family needs to be until we are ready financially to move. My husband works in IT in the bay area which breaks my heart everyday he leaves for work. #needsaquantlet ( yes,I just hash tagged for no reason)
**Goals:**
\*be more disciplined! parenting can have you running in circles
\*increase seafood
\*increase CT
\*hack my environment to protect from EMF
\* keep reading and studying

The test was taken at 1 pm. My doctor recommended Adren-All supplements. Should I take the supplements first or the medication?
Is this me?....
**Adrenal Fatigue Rx: Chronically depressed cortisol: The more common clinical situation we see in AF:**
When the body is under chronic stress, pregnenolone, the precursor to all other steroidal hormones, is diverted to produce cortisol (known as pregnenolone steal syndrome). When this occurs, it is to the detriment of all other steroidal hormones, like DHEA and its metabolites, including progesterone, testosterone, and the estrogens. Vitamin D production will also be down regulated. As pregnenolone is diverted to cortisol, DHEA depletion begins quickly. The result is an elevated cortisol to DHEA ratio. A normal ratio is approximately 4:1 to 6:1 in most ASI labs. This upside down ratio is able to inhibit the PVN of the brain quickly. Why? Cortisol is needed to make new nerve cells in the hippocampus. This is the key element to the Adrenal Fatigue Rx.

**The Key Problem for Healing:** Re-establish the progesterone level in the brain immediately. I also do this for brain surgery cases. General anesthesia also depletes our brain of progesterone and can lead to cognitive dysfunction for a few days post op.

A. We must re establish the Progesterone level quickly to increase BDNF signaling to make new neurons to replace the damaged nerve tracts that allow for altered signaling.

B. Once the new neurons are made, they need to be connected together. This is done with optimal diurnal cortisol AM and PM levels.

C. The new neural circuits created must wire together to become “hardwired” via Hebbian learning. This is done by optimal diurnal melatonin levels at night when we are chemically reduced. This is when the brain trims bad neural circuits and strengthens good ones to regain control of signaling. If melatonin is off you can not re wire a bad brain no matter what anyone tells you. This is why sleep is so critical to human biology.

D. Neurons that fire together wire together. This means that you should consider things that strengthen your newly built circuits when you use this Adrenal Fatigue Rx. Cold Thermogenesis, short explosive sprinting, and meditation are the best safest ways to increase chronic secretion of brain derived nerve growth factor (BDNF) and nerve growth factor (NGF) in the brain to get it to heal quickly.

E. NT building blocks and co factor supports to power the synaptic clefts. DHA and iodine are hugely important to synaptic cleft signaling. It provides the bio-energic power and antioxidant protection to explosively powerful signaling found in all neurons.

F. Strict control of all inflammatory cytokines and acute phase proteins to keep the system humming and immune system working in unison with the PVN.

G. Consider the rest of the things in my initial [Adrenal Fatigue Protocol](https://www.jackkruse.com/what-might-casey-anthony-and-oj-have-in-common/) at this point. I only use this in minor cases. Most cases need a rewire as I am laying out here in detail in this blog.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-03-19T15:38:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-devein-or-not-to-devein-that-is-the-question.19237/post-210670>

Eating the exoskeleton provides you with massive amounts of chitin, iodine and carotenoids that help with solar callus and light assimilation. **Carotenoids** are [organic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Organic_compound) [pigments](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pigment) that are found in the [chloroplasts](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chloroplast) and [chromoplasts](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chromoplast) of plants and some other [photosynthetic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Photosynthesis) [organisms](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Organism), including some [bacteria](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bacteria) and some [fungi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fungi). Carotenoids can be produced from fats and other basic organic metabolic building blocks by all these organisms. The only animals known to produce carotenoids are [aphids](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aphid) and [spider mites](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spider_mite), which acquired the ability and genes from fungi. **Carotenoids from the diet are stored in the fatty tissues of animals, and exclusively _carnivorous animals obtain the compounds from animal fat.
Carotenoids are photoinactivators for our eye clock. When excessive blue is present more Vitamin A (retinol) is made initially, before it becomes extinct in the macula. Why?

Ever heard of Carotenoid protection? Aphids use it and so do we to protect us from nnEMF like RF radar..............

Most photosensitizers are organic dyes, but we use carotenoids and parts of proteins as photosensitizers and inactivators.
Carotenoid protection takes singlet oxygen and extincts it. Singlet O2 + 1CAR = triplet O2 + 3CAR
The deactivation 3CAR >1CAR + liberation of heat or IR light.

Photosensitizers have rules......quantum optical rules they must follow.

1\. Excitation
2\. Decays
3\. Type 1 or Type 2 reactions (which are O2 dependent)
4\. Carotenoid protection

After absorption of light photons, the photosensitizer is first transferred to an excited singlet state by the quantized energy in the photon. Then 3 possible decay channels are possible. This is why quantum physics works on probabilities and not absolutes. The 3 cases are non radiative, radiative singlet state decay to the ground state, and intersystem crossing to an excited triplet state.

Anyone who avoids seafood/crustaceans for ANY reason is not part of my tribe._ SIMPLE QUANTUM RULE.**

### Jack Kruse — 2017-03-19T15:46:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-devein-or-not-to-devein-that-is-the-question.19237/post-210672>

Mankind has been made "surfers" by governments, militaries, and by telecom corporations dollars. For "oceanic surfers" all over the world, the perfect wave in the sea is a dream, a myth that will probably never materialize. But many "surfers" know that to tame and control waves in the sea is a matter of calculable strength, physics, and power. Likewise, dealing with all the possible implications concerning electromagnetic field exposure is an exquisite matter of equilibrium between public health and social benefits. New insights on the current knowledge about radiofrequency (RF) effects on a biological system are being published every week now, yet this won't slow the roll-out of 5 G that uses massive amounts of RF radiation to operate. RF radiation is used on RADAR systems that are shown in the video below. There is now a mounting literature regarding the consequences of extremely low frequency-electromagnetic field (ELF-EMF) and RF exposure on cancer and the possible exploitation of RF in cancer treatment has been thoroughly reviewed in the literature. Sadly too few people or their oncologists know the data. They continue to be fooled by their beliefs. Early in vitro studies of RF-EMF were mostly designed to study the toxic effect of RF on a cell by looking at the nuclear genome and DNA damage. Some of them aimed at clarifying the intracellular pathways that would sustain induced DNA damage and cellular fate. Not one of these studies every considered the other genome in the cell, namely, the mitochondrial genome that creates all of the energy needs of the cell. The more energy that the mitochondria can produce at any one time keeps the nuclear genome quiescent. That means that when energy generation is lowered from the mitochondrial genome this will cause an INCREASE of nuclear genomic changes by increasing ubiquitin rates via epigenetic expression. This is controlled by calcium second messenger system. The published research largely ignores these links this is why every scientist believes that RF radiation is safe for humans when this is not true. Watch the aphids on this flower bounce every time they are shot with RF radiation from an airport radar 14 miles away........If this affects them at this distance what do you think is happening to your mitochondrial with every spin of ANY RADAR you cannot see in your local environment?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-03-19T15:50:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-devein-or-not-to-devein-that-is-the-question.19237/post-210674>

Radars usually operate at radio frequencies (RF) between 300 MHz and 15 GHz. They generate EMFs that are called RF fields. RF fields within this part of the electromagnetic spectrum are known to interact differently with human body.

RF fields below 10 GHz (to 1 MHz) penetrate exposed tissues and produce heating due to energy absorption. The depth of penetration depends on the frequency of the field and is greater for lower frequencies. Absorption of RF fields in tissues is measured as a Specific Absorption Rate (SAR) within a given tissue mass. The unit of SAR is watts per kilogram (W/kg). SAR is the quantity used to measure the "dose" of RF fields between about 1 MHz and 10 GHz.

-   An SAR of at least 4 W/kg is needed to produce known adverse health effects in people exposed to RF fields in this frequency range.

RF fields above 10 GHz are absorbed at the skin surface, with very little of the energy penetrating into the underlying tissues. The basic dosimetric quantity for RF fields above 10 GHz is the intensityof the field measured as power density in watts per square metre (W/m2) or for weak fields in milliwatts per square metre (mW/m2) or microwatts per square metre (µW/m2).

-   Exposure to RF fields above 10 GHz at power densities over 1000 W/m2 are known to produce adverse health effects, such as eye cataracts and skin burns.

Cataracts are now produced at an explosive incidence in today's microwaved blue lit world...........coincidence or not? The wise will realize the connections.

Likewise, dealing with all the possible implications concerning electromagnetic field exposure is an exquisite matter of equilibrium between public health and social benefits. New insights on the current knowledge about radiofrequency (RF) effects on a biological system are being published every week now, yet this won't slow the roll-out of 5 G that ues massive amounts of RF radiation to operate. RF radiation is used on RADAR systems that are shown in the video below. There is now a mounting literature regarding the consequences of extremely low frequency-electromagnetic field (ELF-EMF) and RF exposure on cancer and the possible exploitation of RF in cancer treatment has been thoroughly reviewed in the literature. Sadly too few people or their oncologists know the data. They continue to be fooled by their beliefs. Early in vitro studies of RF-EMF were mostly designed to study the toxic effect of RF on cell by looking at the nuclear genome and DNA damage. Some of them aimed at clarifying the intracellular pathways that would sustain induced DNA damage and cellular fate. Not one of these studies every considered the other genome in the cell, namely, the mitochondrial genome that creates all of the energy needs in the cell. The more energy that the mitochondria can produce at any one time keeps the nuclear genome quiescent. That means that when energy generation is lowered from the mitochondrial genome this will cause and INCREASE of nuclear genomic changes by increasing ubiquitin rates via epigenetic expression. This is controlled by calcium second messenger system. The published research largely ignores these links this is why every scientist believes that RF radiation is safe for humans when this is not true. > [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=210670)
>
> Eating the exoskeleton provides you with massive amounts of chitin, iodine and carotenoids that help with solar callus and light assimilation. **Carotenoids** are [organic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Organic_compound) [pigments](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pigment) that are found in the [chloroplasts](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chloroplast) and [chromoplasts](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chromoplast) of plants and some other [photosynthetic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Photosynthesis) [organisms](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Organism), including some [bacteria](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bacteria) and some [fungi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fungi). Carotenoids can be produced from fats and other basic organic metabolic building blocks by all these organisms. The only animals known to produce carotenoids are [aphids](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aphid) and [spider mites](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spider_mite), which acquired the ability and genes from fungi. **Carotenoids from the diet are stored in the fatty tissues of animals, and exclusively _carnivorous animals obtain the compounds from animal fat.
> Carotenoids are photoinactivators for our eye clock. When excessive blue is present more Vitamin A (retinol) is made initially, before it becomes extinct in the macula. Why?
>
> Ever heard of Carotenoid protection? Aphids use it and so do we to protect us from nnEMF like RF radar..............
>
> Most photosensitizers are organic dyes, but we use carotenoids and parts of proteins as photosensitizers and inactivators.
> Carotenoid protection takes singlet oxygen and extincts it. Singlet O2 + 1CAR = triplet O2 + 3CAR
> The deactivation 3CAR >1CAR + liberation of heat or IR light.
>
> Photosensitizers have rules......quantum optical rules they must follow.
>
> 1\. Excitation
> 2\. Decays
> 3\. Type 1 or Type 2 reactions (which are O2 dependent)
> 4\. Carotenoid protection
>
> After absorption of light photons, the photosensitizer is first transferred to an excited singlet state by the quantized energy in the photon. Then 3 possible decay channels are possible. This is why quantum physics works on probabilities and not absolutes. The 3 cases are non radiative, radiative singlet state decay to the ground state, and intersystem crossing to an excited triplet state.
>
> Anyone who avoids seafood/crustaceans for ANY reason is not part of my tribe._ SIMPLE QUANTUM RULE.**

wow. Great stuff. makes total sense too. And yet another reason not to need to take a pill (like astaxanthin)!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-still-feel-cold.19238/post-210496>

I still find that I cannot go naked for many hours in about 50-60 degrees fahrenheit. Being outside for half an hour is one thing but being able to study outside in the cold for hours is a different story. I am a college student and find that I have to wear a couple of layers if I want to study otherwise I cannot concentrate with the cold air and wind blowing onto me. Will it help if I do cold thermogenesis in cold water? I have only done cold showers so far. Can you all be naked outside for hours in 50-60 degrees or you still wear clothes?

### Scott Hardin — 2018-11-21T18:50:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-still-feel-cold.19238/post-256209>

"I don't know much about the guy, but someone once told me the heart crushing story behind Wim Hoff's usage of the cold. Holy shit! If you ever read or listen to that account \*Warning\* only do so in the sun. It's as crushing as it is powerful."

Wim's story is VERY, VERY powerful.

I did the Wim Hoff Method about 4 years ago and it felt like a neat trick - I could walk barefoot in the snow for example. However, I was living somewhere with beautiful views of nature, but ZERO direct sunlight. I was always sick, always depressed. The breathing exercises were torture with a permanent sinus infection. I'm about to get back into it now.

I've been 8 months without antibiotics for a sinus infection which is the longest I have ever been in my 42 years. I think my higher vitamin D status helps AND I suspect that UV irradiates the blood and Red and NIR are helping my body regenerate damaged tissues.

Can't wait to try WHM again when deep breathing doesn't feel like razor blades cutting through my sinuses.

Also - I now realize that Wim does so much of his actual work outside that he likely doesn't even realize the importance of the sun.

### Sheddie — 2018-11-22T00:18:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-still-feel-cold.19238/post-256254>

> [Scott Hardin said:](/goto/post?id=256216)
>
> I live in Atlanta, pollen capital of the USA; However, I think my issues were 1) lack of sunlight and 2) mold and mildew. Most homes here have basements and nearly all are damp. 1.5 years ago I moved to a condo and Im on the 3rd floor… terrible for EMF but the first place I've lived in 20 years that is 100% dry.
>
> I've been offered sinus surgery numerous times, but I've never met anybody it helped.

For some reason since moving here years ago, I never developed the very common sinus problems. People kept saying, just wait 7 years and you'll suffer, too. BUT, just last August I got my first sinus infection ever -- after plane travel. It was very unpleasant (I finally learned empathy!) and I tried a number of ineffective remedies for weeks. Finally, the thing that worked was rinsing my sinuses religiously, three times a day, then twice, now once a day (maintenance) with a baking soda and sea salt mixture (1 tsp each in a quart of distilled water). The immovable 'goop' in those inside cavities flanking my nose was gone the third day and stayed gone after about 2 weeks. I use the linked apparatus but there are many that work similarly. [https://smile.amazon.com/Neilmed-Sinus-Rinse-Starter-packets/dp/B000RJGB5C/ref=sr\_1\_6\_a\_it?ie=UTF8&qid=1542837492&sr=8-6&keywords=sinus+bottle+rinse&th=1](https://smile.amazon.com/Neilmed-Sinus-Rinse-Starter-packets/dp/B000RJGB5C/ref=sr_1_6_a_it?ie=UTF8&qid=1542837492&sr=8-6&keywords=sinus%2Bbottle%2Brinse&th=1) (Unfortunately I don't know if a dev. septum would contraindicate this method?) Last week I took another plane trip, used the _Humidiflyer_ while traveling (thanx to this site) and the sinus rinse later -- NO sinus issues this time! Hopefully, never again.

### JanSz — 2018-11-22T00:36:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-still-feel-cold.19238/post-256257>

> [Sheddie said:](/goto/post?id=256254)
>
> For some reason since relocating from Canada to Georgia 40 years ago, I never developed the very common sinus problems. People kept saying, just wait 7 years and you'll suffer, too. BUT, just last August I got my first sinus infection ever -- after plane travel. It was very unpleasant (I finally learned empathy!) and I tried a number of ineffective remedies for weeks. Finally, the thing that worked was rinsing my sinuses religiously, three times a day, then twice, now once a day (maintenance) with a baking soda and sea salt mixture (1 tsp each in a quart of distilled water). The immovable 'goop' in those inside cavities flanking my nose was gone the third day and stayed gone after about 2 weeks. I use the linked apparatus but there are many that work similarly. [https://smile.amazon.com/Neilmed-Sinus-Rinse-Starter-packets/dp/B000RJGB5C/ref=sr\_1\_6\_a\_it?ie=UTF8&qid=1542837492&sr=8-6&keywords=sinus+bottle+rinse&th=1](https://smile.amazon.com/Neilmed-Sinus-Rinse-Starter-packets/dp/B000RJGB5C/ref=sr_1_6_a_it?ie=UTF8&qid=1542837492&sr=8-6&keywords=sinus%2Bbottle%2Brinse&th=1) (Unfortunately I don't know if a dev. septum would contraindicate this method?) Last week I took another plane trip, used the _Humidiflyer_ while traveling (thanx to this site) and the sinus rinse later -- NO sinus issues this time! Hopefully, never again.

That apparatus is nice.
But cupped hand and stream of cold water from bathroom faucet works just as good.

I’m glad you found a method.

I’ve tried that method and every other method I could ever find on the internet (except surgery). I’ve averaged 3 sinus infections a year for my entire 42 years. I haven’t had one in 8 months and the main thing I have changed is Sun exposure.

I feel great, but it makes me a little sad if I think how much of my life I spent in pain with low energy due to infections.

### Inger — 2018-11-23T09:59:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-still-feel-cold.19238/post-256306>

> [Phosphene said:](/goto/post?id=256280)
>
> This reminds me of perhaps an uninformed question, but I’m asking anyway. Why were isolated populations near the equator (like Mayan, etc.) so devastated by the diseases brought by invaders? Why wasn’t their redox strong enough? Lack of DHA?

Well, what I have read is, the invaders brought alcohol with them and probably foods that were not natural, and traded these for other things with the native indians.
That would be one reason how their redox lowered.

The other reason I guess is, invaders brought viruses and bacteria that the native indians had had no chance to adapt to, as it was from another continent... so they just did not have immunity to it. That is often an issue when foreigners enter tribes that live far away, without any contact to the modern world.

Otherwise, their redox was indeed really strong. Their wounds healed super fast, they were really strong and healthy.

### drezy — 2018-11-23T23:34:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-still-feel-cold.19238/post-256375>

> [Phosphene said:](/goto/post?id=256280)

> Why were isolated populations near the equator (like Mayan, etc.) so devastated by the diseases brought by invaders? Why wasn’t their redox strong enough? Lack of DHA?

I like the critical thinking exercise. I thought about this one overnight.

I'm with [@Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) on the germs and alcohol and I'll throw in Jared Diamond's steel as well (since guns were not available yet). [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Guns,\_Germs,\_and\_Steel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Guns,_Germs,_and_Steel) .

I also tend to think along the lines of what I worry about current highly urbanized people these days. If a supply chain breakdown occurred (ala conquistadors in the Aztec case) how many people would have the skill to survive 3 months? Just because they are in the past does not necessarily imply the city dwellers were all that much more connected than our current city dwellers. Invaders/attackers will usually severely disrupt supply chains.

It makes this post I think frequently about more ominous, doesn't it?
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...nd-hunting-you-might-need-those-skills.21667/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/are-you-up-to-speed-on-your-ct-and-hunting-you-might-need-those-skills.21667/)

[@Phosphene](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19648/) If your mind is in this corner then I'd recommend you read this blog with that mindset:
[https://jackkruse.com/cpc4-evolutionary-friend-or-foe/](https://jackkruse.com/cpc4-evolutionary-friend-or-foe/)

There was also another blog that discussed young people dying of a disease versus the elderly and infants. I forget exactly which blog it was. I think it mentioned cytokines and or cytokine storm. [@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) , do your remember that one?

> I suspect that mold in modern buildings is different from mold found in nature. I bet a cave has dozens of species all competing and balancing each other out, while drywall and plywood preferentially reward a few species

Ah yes. Good point. I neglected a good thought tool we've been introduced to on a recent Q&A that suits your argument here as well. -- The space station trends:

Very rapid bacterial growth (there are similar mold findings)
[https://www.upi.com/Science\_News/20...-space-is-better-than-on-Earth/4651458830699/](https://www.upi.com/Science_News/2016/03/24/For-bacteria-life-in-space-is-better-than-on-Earth/4651458830699/)

SO, all those transmissions and fields of modernity sure aren't helping. The Hepa filter + requisite power grid may hurt more in the end especially when modern environments lack UV. Strangely the hospitals filled with doctors, most of which otherwise have all bad things to say about UV, utilize UV to kill germs.

### ALEXIS TUDOR — 2018-11-27T21:12:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-still-feel-cold.19238/post-256758>

Rather than start a new post I will spin off this one. Had a question about post CT cold. While doing CT I am fine with the cold and then afterwards I seem fine for about 25 min. Then I figure I should warm up a bit and the cold just works its way deep into my bones. It seems like taking a warm shower makes it worse. What would be a practical way to warm up. Mind you I don't have a lot of fat to burn- sometimes it feels like none to burn. Also is it better to CT half the body in 40deg water or the whole body in 60deg. water. I am still concerned about keeping my core warm(torso) as my legs can take the cold but my organs especially my kidneys seem to have quite a negative reaction to the cold.

Also the goal of CT is to cool the surface temperature of the body and not the core right?

### Inna — 2018-11-27T22:00:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-still-feel-cold.19238/post-256765>

> [ALEXIS TUDOR said:](/goto/post?id=256758)
>
> It seems like taking a warm shower makes it worse.

You are correct, there is usually temperature after-drop that happens 10-20 min after swimming in cold water; it happens during rewarming phase due to reversal of vasoconstriction that occurs during cold exposure.
Taking warm showers is NOT recommended - as it could amplify this T after-drop (redirect / increase blood flow to skin and away from the core).
You might want to warm up with exercise, also hot drink is often helpful

[https://outdoorswimmer.com/blogs/after-drop-is-real-and-how-to-deal-with-it](https://outdoorswimmer.com/blogs/after-drop-is-real-and-how-to-deal-with-it)

### Inna — 2018-11-28T01:22:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-still-feel-cold.19238/post-256801>

> [ALEXIS TUDOR said:](/goto/post?id=256786)
>
> What do you mean by "this T"

T - temperature

> [ALEXIS TUDOR said:](/goto/post?id=256787)
>
> One more question while we are on this topic. If there is no fat to burn what does the body use to rewarm?

I believe warming effect like rewarming after cold water swimming happens via blood flow in the warm blooded creatures like humans, and is regulated via vasoconstriction or vasodilation - which is very rapid and responsive to the immediate needs of the organism; cold exposure makes body (mitochondria) generate more heat (like thermostat). You don’t necessarily need to fat burn for heat generation to occur...

### Inna — 2018-11-28T17:56:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-still-feel-cold.19238/post-256847>

> [ALEXIS TUDOR said:](/goto/post?id=256819)
>
> Would it be more effective to dunk half the body in 40 degree water(up to navel)

yes, especially if the other half (non-immersed) of your body is exposed to sunlight - Fournier effect. CT outside is probably the best if you can find suitable body of water. It would also provide grounding for you.
Re water temperature, according to Jack optimal water temperature is 50-55 degrees F, reducing below which is not necessarily providing additional benefits, though not harmful either as long as one is cold adapted
If doing CT indoors (less preferable), might consider reptilian UV lamps / full spectrum lamps for non-immersed skin

### Penny — 2018-11-29T22:40:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-still-feel-cold.19238/post-256988>

> [Scott Hardin said:](/goto/post?id=256274)
>
> I’m glad you found a method.
>
> I’ve tried that method and every other method I could ever find on the internet (except surgery). I’ve averaged 3 sinus infections a year for my entire 42 years. I haven’t had one in 8 months and the main thing I have changed is Sun exposure.
>
> I feel great, but it makes me a little sad if I think how much of my life I spent in pain with low energy due to infections.

I use this stuff for anything to do with the nose... like if I feel myself getting ill - I also use Trader Joes Tea tree soap and stick some in my nose and other parts every morning - a kid that visits us occasionally said it definitely did some "knock down" when he used the tea tree soap - I first got wind of tea tree oil when a Vietnam vet moved in next door to us and said it's the only thing he's every found that killed "jungle rot" - but UV light will also kill any bug - just stick a Rubylux UV light on any capillary and it feels great - another great one is hydrogen peroxide - stick some in a humidifyer and run a UV light and most nose diseases go away

### Penny — 2018-12-01T00:14:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-still-feel-cold.19238/post-257057>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=256996)
>
> Where do you put H2O2, on mask surface or inside the air filter?

It's a cold water humidifier so just fill it up with water and add 1/2-3/4 cup of H2O2 - then run a UV light - I have the one from batteries and bulbs sticking out of my casablanca fan -

### ALEXIS TUDOR — 2018-12-07T22:01:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-still-feel-cold.19238/post-257535>

Thought I would post this part that I added into my journal on CT.

I am now able to do CT relatively comfortable with about 45 deg water for about 20 min. I could go colder but have been told there is no reason especially if you are super skinny. I am starting to like it more and more now that my kidneys are not feeling like they are clinching up and causing mid back pain. I am not a hundred percent sure but I have in the last few weeks had almost zero back pain which would come about after the nights when I play pickleball at the gym and would like to attribute that improvement to CT.

One thing that is quite important is that I am starting to use Wim Hoff's breathing techniques and that has helped keep my body calmer in the cold.

I plan to get deeper into using Wims techniques along with Jacks approaches to mitigating nnEMF and lights while avoiding mismatch foods. I look forward to future results.

I have modified CT a bit to make it more doable too for now where I wear a sleeveless sweat shirt hoodie on and go in the water with it. I go about up to my mid chest so the upper part of my core body stays relatively warm. The rest is under water. It is quite comfy once I settle in and stay there for about 20 min. Warming up is still a bit of a endeavour for me where I do get drop down(can't remember official term) after about 10 min and for about half an hour feel a bit queasy when my blood feels a bit "poisoned" from the cold. However somehow it seems like the warming up period is far shorter and less drop down when I go in after playing pickleball for 2 hours. For me working out afterwards just seems to stir up the cold blood too fast. Also with doing it at night after pickleball, I can get into a warm bed where my body is going to drop my body temperature down anyway. The only catch is, it is at night so it is a lot colder outside where just the other night it was 25, and also there is a lot of NNlights out. If all goes right I should move into my own house in a few months where that can be mitigated better.

### Steele — 2017-03-17T17:19:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lab-help.19239/post-210530>

Hello all,

If anyone is willing to help me understand my labs I would greatly appreciate it.

Age: 23
Sex: male
Height: 6' 1"
Weight: 157
BMI: 20.7
HR: 50 bpm
BP: 120/80

Name: David
Occupation: Physician Assistant student
Home: Lynchburg, Virginia.
Exercise: 2012: College soccer athlete, tons of HITT training; June 2015: adopted LCHF which destroyed my athletic performance, decreased aerobic capacity, cramping muscles; 2017: currently weight train and hike when able
Diet: June of 2015: ketogenic (Horrible Idea/mismatch) - could barely walk up stairs without being short of breath (decreased HR significantly, increased respiration rate); past 1.5 years: seasonal, paleo, high seafood, LCHF
Environment: 2012-2016: college campus; 2016-2017: full time student, currently inside (no sunlight), Wifi environment, and sit from roughly 8am-5pm M-F. Attempt to get sunshine/exercise every chance I get
Observations: 2015: after going low carb: poor ability to clot blood, scab, and wound heal (lots of scars), decreased aerobic performance, continual 'low carb flu' sxs past 2 years that have been improving with time (considering the idea that LCHF may not be ideal for me)
Biohacks: sleep with grounding wire, wear blue-light blockers nearly every evening, sleep with Wifi off and phone on airplane mode, drink filtered water, CT: I have extremely cold hands and feet even in the summer to the point of chilblains on my toes, I do not feel myself warm-up afterward or acclimate easily
Health: gluten intolerant/Celiac disease since 1 y/o

\***abnormal values bold** and the questionable values underlined

Labs:
**CBC:
WBC: 3.2 L (3.4-10.8)**
RBC 4.86 (4.14-5.80)
Hgb: 14.9 (12.6-17.7)
Hct: 43.9 (37.5-51.0)
MCV 90 (79-97)
MCH 30.7 (26.6-33.0)
MCHC 33.9 (31.5-35.7)
RDW 13.5 (12.3-15.4)
Plt 174 (150-379)
Neuts 34%
Lymphs 49%
Monos 14%
Eos 2%
Baso 1%
**Abs:
Neuts: 1.1 L (1.4-7.0)**
Lymphs 1.5 (0.7-3.1)
Mono: 0.5 (0.1-0.9)
Eos 0.1 (0-0.4)
Baso 0.0 (0-0.2)
Im Granulo: 0 (0-0.1)

**CMP:**
Glucose 97 (95-99)
BUN 17 (6-20)
Creatinine 0.9 (0.76-1.27)
BUN/Cr: 19 (8-19)
eGFR 120 >59
Sodium 142 (134-144)
Potassium 4.7 (3.5-5.2)
Chloride 98 (96-106)
Carbon Dioxide 27 (18-29)
Calcium 10.2 (8.7-10.2)
Protein 7.6 (6.0-8.5)
Albumin 5.1 (3.5-5.5)
Globulin 2.5 (1.5-4.5)
A/G Ratio 2.0 (1.2-2.2)
Bilirubin 0.4 (0-1.2)
Alk Phosp 52 (39-117)
AST 16 (0-40)
ALT 15 (0-44)

**UA:**
Spec Grav 1.011 (0.005-1.030)
pH 6.5 (5-7.5)
Urobilinogen 0.2 (0.2-1.0)
\*everything else negative or not present

**Lipid Panel:
Total cholesterol 275 H (100-199)**
Triglycerides 56 (0-149)
HDL 82 (>39)
VLDL 11 (5-40)
**LDL 182 H (0-99)**
Chol/HDL ratio 3.4 (0-5.0)

**Iron
TIBC: 235 L (250-450)**
UIBC 144 (111-343)
Iron 91 (38-169)
Iron Sat 39 (15-55)

**Vit D, 25-Hydroxy: 28.3 L (30-100)**

Total testosterone: 622 (348-1197)
**Free Testosterone 9.0 L (9.3-26.5)**
LH 3.4 (1.7-8.6)
FSH 8.8 (1.5-12.4)
DHEA-sulfate 219.6 (164.3-530.5)
**Estradiol (Roche ECLIA) <5.0 L (7.6-42.6)**

TSH 2.390 (0.450-4.500)
Free T4 1.09 (0.82-1.77)
Free T3 2.0 (2.0-4.4)

A1c 4.9 (4.8-5.6)
Insulin 4.7 (2.6-24.9)

CRP cardiac 0.23 (0-3.0)
Homocysteine 7.3 (0-15.0)

Pending: ASI (diurnal salivary cortisol)

Would anyone be able to:
1\. educate me on these labs/provide insight
2\. point me to a source so that I may understand these better
3\. provide recommendations for improvement, future lab testing, and the questions I have below:

1\. reasons for poor cold/CT tolerance
2\. abnormal lipid panel, should I consider decreasing the amount of fat consumption since my environment isn't necessarily yoked with winter, so my seasonal LCHF winter diet may not be optimal?
3\. low Vit D despite lots of fatty fish (sardines, salmon, mackerel) and some sunshine
4\. low TIBC

Thank you very much for your feedback
