### Sean Waters — 2017-03-23T13:58:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-for-a-keto-leptin-rx-bulk.19254/post-210927>

Danco,

Yeah I had a look on Leangains yesterday, I think I am pretty similar to you... 6am- BAB with fish or chicken + veg in butter, and a Fat bomb protein shake... then last meal is Lunch at around 1pm, big salad with fish and lots of fat. Then I sometimes eat another meal around 4 or 5pm, or just leave it, depends how I'm feeling. Try stay Ketotic and IFing though. Built a little gym in my garden in the sun, and I train MMA twice a week under blue light which I try to mitigate with CT before and after.

I've also got rid of my iPhone 6S. I turned it off for good last week, and bought a 2G Alcatel One Touch which stays on flight mode unless I make calls or texts Noticed a big difference in energy.

Can you give more detail on your cycle with the fats? Sounds interesting. What kind of goals do you have? (Hypertrophy/ Endurance etc)

I think Jack nailed it with the first comment. I have noticed that I feel completed sapped of energy in two places:
1\. The Office I work in
2\. Train I get to work.

Both of them full of EMF from 10 or more iPhones and Laptops in a confined space.

I deliberately get off at the stop before to allow a long walk in to work. Before and after each of these I feel like I could run, on the train I feel like I can barely stand. I remember Barry making a comment on his Fasted State Training Adaptions thread about noticing that his gains in performance is more about his reduction of tech/EMF rather than anything else.

In a couple years, once I've finished my training, I'm gonna move to a better location, either the Gulf South or somewhere optimal. My job is in construction, and I can go anywhere in the world once I have the qualifications. I just got to suffer now, to gain big later.

I notice this all affects my performance in my training way more than the Gin and Tonic on the weekend. Leangains even wrote an article on alcohol and performance here: [http://www.leangains.com/2010/07/truth-about-alcohol-fat-loss-and-muscle.html](http://www.leangains.com/2010/07/truth-about-alcohol-fat-loss-and-muscle.html)

Take it with a pinch of salt of course, all blue lit clinical studies and n=1 says I never feel like Superman after a drink, but I think the effects are exaggerated.

### kris90 — 2017-03-24T15:28:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-for-a-keto-leptin-rx-bulk.19254/post-211001>

Wow for a site dedicated to helping people reach optimal, some of the responses are not very productive.

Life is all about enjoying yourself and letting loose. You need to draw the line between optimal health and quality of life. Most people work in a crappy environment (i.e. office building). It's not always the easiest to just pick up and leave, quit your job etc. And just because people aren't willing to make those drastic changes, doesn't mean they aren't dedicated to achieving optimal health and we shouldn't help them.

Modern humans don't hibernate. We get ketosis during sleep, and maybe a little extra fasting will help with autophagy as well, but chronic ketosis ONLY works in NON-stressful environments, which today is unrealistic for most, because we are required to work 8 hour days YEAR round, and we attempt to be equally active year round even in low light periods. Ketosis = a mismatch in the modern world. To fix that mismatch, you can either do ketosis and take the entire winter off work and rest and sit in a cold tub 24/7, or you can continue to live a normal life, get shit done, and eat a well balanced diet with carbs.

So yes, these guys are right about your environment, because you are in a mismatch now. But I don't blame you for not changing that environment, but if you don't, I suggest ditching the keto. Keto in warm adapted humans = starvation mode. Keto in cold adapted humans = regeneration, but do you really want to live in the cold 24/7? Keto + modern living + bodybuilding = insane stress. Also keep in mind, humans are WARM blooded, not COLD blooded. Need glucose to thrive in stressful environments.

I agree with that and have long experience with it.
For a year I was living in tropical area.
As far as sun exposure is concerned all was 100% better.
But
I have found that constant 12hr day and 12hr night started bothering me.
But it took about 6 months to find that out.

So, vacation to sunny places are 100% ok,
living there different story.

That is me, others mileage may vary.
....
I am also thinking of a guy I was conversing with some time ago.
He moved from his native Somali to Norway.
Total mismatch.
That was before @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) got his website.
I was aware of light then, but not as much as now.

,,,,.,.

We get ketosis during sleep, and maybe a little extra fasting will help with autophagy as well, but chronic ketosis ONLY works in NON-stressful environments, which today is unrealistic for most, because we are required to work 8 hour days YEAR round, and we attempt to be equally active year round even in low light periods. Ketosis = a mismatch in the modern world. To fix that mismatch, you can either do ketosis and take the entire winter off work and rest and sit in a cold tub 24/7, or you can continue to live a normal life, get shit done, and eat a well balanced diet with carbs.
>
> So yes, these guys are right about your environment, because you are in a mismatch now. But I don't blame you for not changing that environment, but if you don't, I suggest ditching the keto. Keto in warm adapted humans = starvation mode. Keto in cold adapted humans = regeneration, but do you really want to live in the cold 24/7? Keto + modern living + bodybuilding = insane stress. Also keep in mind, humans are WARM blooded, not COLD blooded. Need glucose to thrive in stressful environments.

Nature requires you to sift through bullshit to find the prize..........If some comments or ideas or critic move you off your target then this site is not for that person. Interesting question and it opens a huge worm hole to a blog in the reality series yet to be released...........Pyruvate and glutamine metabolism have a deep link that most fail to connect.
One interesting facet, from a chemo-therapeutic point of view, is that, in a large number of cases, cells undergoing a Warburg effect exhibit a marked dependence upon **glutamine and glucose**, to the extent that these cells are referred to as being ‘glutamine or glucose addicted’. Glucose catabolism is not specific enough to tells us more about the source of this redox shift Warburg found but it appears glutamine is, and it points the finger of causation of aging and neolithic diseases at mitochondrial heteroplasmy rates. How? Glutamine addiction arises from the need for extracellular glutamine to be consumed for anaplerotic input in \[SB1\] the citric acid cycle (pyruvate/glucose metabolism), which accounts for the majority of the bioenergetic needs of normal (nontransformed) cells. The key gatekeeper of this input is the enzyme _glutaminase._

The primary function of the glutaminase enzymes is to catalyze the hydrolysis of l-glutamine to l-glutamate, the latter being generally **_unable to enter the mitochondria directly_.** As l-glutamate is formed it is converted to α-ketoglutarate by the enzyme **glutamate dehydrogenase** (GDH). This enzyme is controlled by calcium as a secondary messenger controller. [HYPERLINK](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3294041/) This product can then be utilized directly in the citric acid cycle, leading to energy and building block production.

**Non native electromagnetic fields are well known disruptors of calcium control and calcium gated channels.** This implies we do not have to have ionization of the nucelar DNA to get oncogenic transformation. It implies that a change in _**energy variance**_ from the AMPk pathways maybe the key signal in a Warburg redox shift. Most of the science world thinks that for radiation to cause a biologic effect they think it has to ionize DNA or RNA because they think change occurs from inside out in a cell. The reality of epigenetic science tells us it is exactly the opposite; outside in is the key effect in turining on genes more than we would like. Remember that cancer is a CHRONICALLY turned on version of growth that is uncontrolled because light cycles are decoupled from ubiquitin.

### JanSz — 2017-03-24T19:24:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-for-a-keto-leptin-rx-bulk.19254/post-211024>

> [Sean Waters said:](/goto/post?id=211011)
>
> so if CK is high = shows need for PC ?
>
> Not sure about creatine kinase but pyruvate is for glucose metabolism, **and I lack the gene for pyruvate kinase. Or the expression of.**
>
> Mother, Grandmother and Great Grandmother all the same.

[https://rarediseases.info.nih.gov/diseases/7514/pyruvate-kinase-deficiency](https://rarediseases.info.nih.gov/diseases/7514/pyruvate-kinase-deficiency)
Pyruvate kinase deficiency

Other Names:
Pyruvate kinase deficiency of red cells; Pyruvate kinase deficiency of erythrocytes; PK deficiency
Categories:
Blood Diseases; Congenital and Genetic Diseases; Metabolic disorders
Summary

Pyruvate kinase deficiency is a genetic blood disorder characterized by low levels of an enzyme called pyruvate kinase, which is used by red blood cells. Without pyruvate kinase, red blood cells break down too easily, resulting in low levels of these cells (hemolytic anemia).\[1\] The signs and symptoms of the disease may vary greatly from person to person. However, they usually include jaundice, enlargement of the spleen, and mild or severe hemolysis (red cell breakdown), leading to anemia.\[2\] In some cases, the problems may first appear while in utero, causing a condition in which abnormal amounts of fluid build up in two or more body areas of the fetus (hydrops fetalis). Newborns may present with prolonged jaundice and anemia. Older children may be pale (due to anemia) and have intermittent episodes of jaundice. Mild cases may escape detection until adulthood.\[1\] Although the anemia tends to stabilize in adulthood, episodes of anemia may occur with acute infections, stress, and pregnancy.\[3\] Pyruvate kinase deficiency is caused by a mutation in the PKLR gene and is inherited in an autosomal recessive fashion.\[1\]\[4\] Treatment remains supportive rather than curative.\[3\]

Below

Danco,

Yeah I had a look on Leangains yesterday, I think I am pretty similar to you... 6am- BAB with fish or chicken + veg in butter, and a Fat bomb protein shake... then last meal is Lunch at around 1pm, big salad with fish and lots of fat. Then I sometimes eat another meal around 4 or 5pm, or just leave it, depends how I'm feeling. Try stay Ketotic and IFing though. Built a little gym in my garden in the sun, and I train MMA twice a week under blue light which I try to mitigate with CT before and after.

I've also got rid of my iPhone 6S. I turned it off for good last week, and bought a 2G Alcatel One Touch which stays on flight mode unless I make calls or texts Noticed a big difference in energy.

Can you give more detail on your cycle with the fats? Sounds interesting. What kind of goals do you have? (Hypertrophy/ Endurance etc)

I think Jack nailed it with the first comment. I have noticed that I feel completed sapped of energy in two places:
1\. The Office I work in
2\. Train I get to work.

Both of them full of EMF from 10 or more iPhones and Laptops in a confined space.

I deliberately get off at the stop before to allow a long walk in to work. Before and after each of these I feel like I could run, on the train I feel like I can barely stand. I remember Barry making a comment on his Fasted State Training Adaptions thread about noticing that his gains in performance is more about his reduction of tech/EMF rather than anything else.

In a couple years, once I've finished my training, I'm gonna move to a better location, either the Gulf South or somewhere optimal. My job is in construction, and I can go anywhere in the world once I have the qualifications. I just got to suffer now, to gain big later.

I notice this all affects my performance in my training way more than the Gin and Tonic on the weekend. Leangains even wrote an article on alcohol and performance here: [http://www.leangains.com/2010/07/truth-about-alcohol-fat-loss-and-muscle.html](http://www.leangains.com/2010/07/truth-about-alcohol-fat-loss-and-muscle.html)

Take it with a pinch of salt of course, all blue lit clinical studies and n=1 says I never feel like Superman after a drink, but I think the effects are exaggerated.

### kris90 — 2017-03-25T02:05:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-for-a-keto-leptin-rx-bulk.19254/post-211034>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=211022)

I think the key is curiosity and mito-hacking. No one's results will be identical to another because no environment is identical and neither is their mitochondria or their distance between their respiratory proteins. So when somebody bitches and moans about critiques.........words, grammar spelling..........you name........they announce their own limitations to optimal.

I agree with you in the sense that every person's environment is different and it is an N=1. But that being said, you could have one person who smokes, but everything else about their environment is optimal, and they'll probably be fine. It sounds like Sean has had some success with dealing with his crohn's, so obviously he must be doing something right, and is looking at improving performance/gaining muscle mass.

My N=1 is that no matter what the season, I do best on a mostly carb and fat diet with protein on the lower end (40/40/20). Keto this winter destroyed my testosterone, and I suffered from terrible eczema. Since adding carbs back to the mix, and sticking to saturated fats like dairy, butter, coconut oil and milk chocolate while cutting out PUFAs, and the improvement was nearly instantaneous.

I personally think keto is probably not such a bad idea for an overweight individual needing to train their body to burn fat by purposely inducing a state of insulin resistance so they can essentially simulate hibernation and burn fat. But at the same time, they might do well on a high carb/low fat diet so that the glucose can fuel the metabolism, and the lack of fats in the diet forces bodyfat metabolism. Bears reverse their insulin resistance coming out of hibernation by eating honey. Maybe humans need a little sugar too, and cut out the PUFAs: [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/18171691

Haha, also, forgot to mention that maple syrup is in season here in Canada in the late winter/early spring and it's pure sugar. Probably a good idea to eat lots of it](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/18171691)

### Sean Waters — 2017-05-18T14:49:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-for-a-keto-leptin-rx-bulk.19254/post-214249>

Time for an update............

1\. I've started Doug McGuff's Body By Science protocol and noticed very immediate improvements in strength, solidness of muscles and size of muscles as well as body fat. I also feel the endorphins post work-out - which I never did with regular Linear Strength Protocols.

2\. I've found that resting for 7 days is good, but resting for 9-10 days is even better and muscles start to pop up at this time.

3\. I have finally got a Keyboard and Mouse at work to keep away from my Laptop an extra few feet, and the eczema on my wrists and hands has reduced by about 90%. This is one of my LAST problems that I really wanted to address. I've still not used my smartphone, its been off for 2 months now, my 2G phone remains on airplane mode except during workhours.

4\. I have realised that "Bulking" with the goal of gaining "Mass" instead of "Lean Muscle Mass" is actually ridiculous, especially when it's so difficult to maintain Ketosis on such a high calorie diet.

With my pyruvate kinase deficiency (which seems to be attributed to non-native EMF) I really need to stay in Ketosis to remain of sound MIND and BODY - If not, then my energy levels drop and my mitochondria lose electrons rapidly. Sugar really is the difference in this non-native EMF environment I live in. Until I can change that, I must adhere to Ketosis to be optimal.

I think my dopamine levels were low this winter when I wanted to pile on 30 pounds of mass. I would now rather be Leaner and Slimmer with a better Mass Equivalence.

5\. Actually gaining LEAN MUSCLE MASS at the rate in which my impatient brain wanted to, was absurd, and impossible considering my environment. I realise now that a steady gain of 10 pounds for this year is probably the target I should aim for - any more will be a bonus but should not be expected.

6\. Following on from the above comment, the rate of growth often achieved by people is due to Steroids and/ or rare Genetics. I think CT could act as a natural steroid however, and I am CT'ing every day under Cold Showers. I am very close, if not definitely, Cold Adapted now... I never feel the cold and don't even flinch under the shower anymore or feel the Pre-CT "fear" that I suffered with before.

7\. I have noticed that anytime I get symptoms such as Twinges, Mouth ulcers, Headaches, Aches/ Pains, Sickness or Nausea, Eczema, Bad moods - paranoia or anxiety, Aggression and Bad tempers.......... It's ALWAYS because I am in the vicinity of some form Non-Native EMF.

8\. I want to go camping. Real bad.

Thanks for reading, Peace.

### Sean Waters — 2017-05-30T17:17:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-for-a-keto-leptin-rx-bulk.19254/post-215110>

Posted this elsewhere too, but I feel like it should go here considering it's relevance to both threads......
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-leptin-leptin-resistance.19563/page-2](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-leptin-leptin-resistance.19563/page-2)

**Please correct me at any point if I'm talking shit. Thanks in advance.**

Post:

The last few weeks I've been feeling really tired some days, especially if my sleep has been affected or disrupted, I was thinking this was due to:

1.  not being in ketosis after eating too many "accidental" carbs in my foods/ gluconeogenesis
2.  EMF
3.  disrupted sleep
4.  **a combination of the above**

So I started doing the following:

1.  Fasting longer (24 hours to increase ketones)
2.  Avoiding EMF even more
3.  Focusing on not eating post 8pm and blocking light so sleep is better

Results:

1.  Still had no energy. Felt like I had weights strapped to me.
2.  Felt defeated, unmotivated, very down and frustrated.
3.  Words, memories etc. not coming to me as quick

I was in Ketosis between 1.8 and 3.0 mm/mol , showering in cold water daily. But I had these problems. It didn't make any sense.
This was all on Saturday morning.
So I realised my problem and solution is either one of two things:

1.  **The nnEMF** I get at work and in my city could potentially be ramped up, beyond my perception, or just winning the battle against me. And **I need to MOVE to another country to get better** and be optimal.
2.  **I need to start eating more carbohydrates**, as it's summertime, and **Ketosis is mis-matching with my dark brown skin tan** that I have developed over the last few weeks through exposure to UV index of 1 every morning, and UV index of 5 or 6 all day on weekends and at lunchtime during the week.

I've started planning the move.
I've also started eating more carbs
Strangely, I do feel better. Which is weird for me, as when I tested in 2016 I had a Pyruvate Deficiency that blocks glucose metabolism - leading to low energy, cognitive issues, mood etc.
I was discussing this exact thing on a thread in Optimal Fitness: [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/help-for-a-keto-leptin-rx-bulk.19254/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/help-for-a-keto-leptin-rx-bulk.19254/)
This means my Pyruvate Kinase Genetic Deficiency - could quite possibly be "turned off" from the Sunlight, epigenetically... (Think I could be quite high on the Gerald Pollack "OUT ON A LIMB" meter with this one) OR I have just become Cold Adapted through CT'ing for over a year now, and developed Metabolic Flexibility.
So I'm not sure where to go from here...

-   Could I have a low testerone problem in Ketosis like you Kris? (_Caveat: I seem to have lots of energy during winter, in Ketosis, with low sun and very cold CT's)_
-   Is the nnEMF causing a perception that I need Carbs?
-   Is it natural for my body to want Carbs, as I've caught so much sun, and my circadian biology is mis-matching in Ketosis with all this sun?
-   If it is the nnEMF causing a false perception that I need carbs , do I stick with the Carbs anyway until Winter time?...
-   If I should eat more carbs, then how much more?

### Penny — 2017-03-20T17:55:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/least-expensive-diys.19255/post-210754>

Do you mean Qi Gong?

Baking soda - a pinch in your water and if you have a heavy duty workout, 1 tsp in water after working out - also, in the shower, rub epsom salts and baking soda on your body and leave it on for awhile - then turn the shower to full blast cold - the baking soda will heat you up so much you will actually want the water to be cold
[http://drsircus.com/water/secrets-life/](http://drsircus.com/water/secrets-life/)
[http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2013/08/18/exclusion-zone-water.aspx](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2013/08/18/exclusion-zone-water.aspx)

Face dunking in ice cold water - absolutely energizing -

### bionaut — 2017-03-20T18:35:19+0200

I initially did set them up a specific way on a 5 gallon jug, since then, I have been haphazard with setup. Any configuration eliminated all cramping in a relatives legs, removal of the magnets from the water source corresponded to the cramping returning, polarity was not a factor I could measure without running labs and more stringent testing. Play around with it

### Jerry Fulford — 2017-03-20T19:45:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/least-expensive-diys.19255/post-210774>

Too much to elaborate!

I've been practicing for about 10 years, still haven't scratched the surface.

Tangible benefits: better mood, better energy, more calm, more loving, better sex, longer sex, quiet mind, etc...

Jump into Tai Chi with both feet. Spend some time looking for a great teacher, they can save a lot of trial and errors.

Also, depends on your goals. There is martial, spiritual, and health styles of Chi Kung. Start with the health styles as they lead into the others nicely.

I see you are in the UK, look up Damo Mitchell over there for seminars and materials, he's legit.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-09-09T20:16:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/least-expensive-diys.19255/post-250584>

Well, if **amplified cross crawling** sounds too creepy, try other energy disciplines that tap into _the_ endless supply of **_life energy_** (Chi, Ki, Prana, etc.-- it goes by many names but has _One Cosmic Source_).

Chi Kung (Tai Chi, etc. etc) has already been mentioned. The practice I rely on is **Reiki**. Most of Dr Kruse's Swans have heard of it, but there might be some that don't understand it.

A crude analogy (I may get hammered on this) is that it is a little like Chi Kung or similar discipline but without a physical body workout. It is most similar in principle to energy practices like 'laying on of the hands' but without a religious message.

At a most modest investment in time and money for Reiki training and requisite _Initiation_ ceremonies you can access this unlimited energy rest of your life and for free (Initiation is not for show or pomp-- it is required to establish open energy channels-- you can't do it yourself, only a trained Reiki master can).

At Reiki Level I you are ready to heal and invigorate your body anytime anyplace just using your hands (who doesn't have plenty of time to do this during boring staff meetings-- and you don't have to meditate or be mindful while Reiki works-- although you will get more results if you do). At Level II and III you are ready to heal others and do **remote healing**\-- a huge benefit and a way to be of service to others.

# Thread: Hello From the Corn Fields, Ohio!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-corn-fields-ohio.19256/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### CFAN — 2017-03-20T19:48:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-corn-fields-ohio.19256/post-210775>

Hi!
So excited to be here!

I have been battling with weight all my life. I have always been in the overweight category, but when the scale said 306, I knew it was time to change. I am a 28 year old female. I tried hardcore exercise at a gym with frustratingly little success, changing my general diet with little sticking power. I fell into the paleo diet with some recent wins, but I still felt like I had no energy. Searching through books online, I found and read Dr. Kruse's book and it was a slap upside the head. So much science! Yet... it seemed like it explained so much!

I am just starting my journey down the rabbit hole, and frankly have no clue how to start using this forum to my benefit (recommendations/help here?), but I am excited for this new path!

### JanSz — 2017-03-20T20:22:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-corn-fields-ohio.19256/post-210779>

We are extremely efficient machine.
Hardcore exercise can only hurt you. It is meaningless as a way to loose weight.
When done indoor in the gym, it takes away precious outdoor time.

Fat is estrogenic.
Fat promotes aromatase,
aromatase promotes estrogen
estrogen makes things grow, including more fat

Body fat can/should be used for energy.
Body fat can be used for energy only if insulin,serum is low (2-3 range)
If insulin is higher than that certain minimum, there is no way to use body fat for energy (even if you are hungry and tired).
Time must pass to let glucose and insulin fall into levels low enough so use can be made of energy stored in body fat.
Practically it takes 12 or even more hours from the last bite of last meal before there is some chance of starting process of using body fat for energy.

Note, people using supplemental insulin
are (guaranteed) to not be able to loose weight.

So the easiest way is to eat within 8hrs window, have a first and last bite within 8hr window, and do not eat in other time (of 24hr period).

Next step is to eat just once/day
Next step is to eat once EOD, Every Other Day.
And so on.
Depending of how badly one wants to loose weight.

On top of above foundation,
come additions on the theme. CT is one of it. No carbs another one.

\-----------------------
Speaking of scale, you do not have to change religion,
but may be interested in habits of
Eastern Orthodox Catholics.
(About 200 days a year of severely limited food intake).

///

### fitness@home — 2017-03-20T20:42:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-corn-fields-ohio.19256/post-210780>

Hi CFAN,

Start a journal in the section I linked below:

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?forums/my-optimal-journal.17/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?forums/my-optimal-journal.17/)

Please give a history - medical, family (especially maternal side), labs if you have them and what your goals are. What steps have you taken to change your habits and health?

There are many people here that will chime in once you provide some background.

I know the blogs seem overwhelming, but just get started and read, read, read. Don't forget to read the comments at the end of blogs as you can learn a lot from others' questions and the answers they received.

### caroline — 2017-03-21T03:18:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-corn-fields-ohio.19256/post-210812>

welcome ..... listen to every single podcast several times at least.

read and start the leptin RX ...and the C/T RX....there are easy start guides.

what are you eating? drinking?

How is your sleep? libido?

where do you live? work?

This is doable...... get focused and take no prisoners!

Insulin61.6 uU/mL0.0 - 20.0 uU/mL

**I am not a doctor.**
Have you got any diagnosis? Post them.
\------------------------------------------------------------
From Mayo Clinic I got:
[http://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-.../expert-answers/hyperinsulinemia/FAQ-20058488](http://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/type-2-diabetes/expert-answers/hyperinsulinemia/FAQ-20058488)

Hyperinsulinemia is most often caused by insulin resistance — a condition in which your body is resistant to the effects of insulin. Your pancreas tries to compensate by making more insulin. Insulin resistance may eventually lead to the development of type 2 diabetes.

This is most likely reason.
There are two other that are rare:

A rare tumor of the insulin-producing cells of the pancreas (insulinoma)
Excessive numbers or growth of insulin-producing cells in the pancreas (nesidioblastosis)

\-------------

Congenital disorder is a condition existing at or before birth regardless of cause.
\-----------
Congenital hyperinsulinism
These conditions are present at birth and most become apparent in early infancy. Mild cases can be treated by frequent feedings, more severe cases can be controlled by medications that reduce insulin secretion or effects.
\-----------

nesidioblastosis looks like another name for Congenital hyperinsulinism
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Congenital\_hyperinsulinism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Congenital_hyperinsulinism)
Diagnostic
Inappropriately low levels of free fatty acids and ketones provide additional evidence of insulin excess.

There other ways are described.
\-------------

You have lots of inflammation going on.
You have unwanted organism identified.
It would be good idea to get closer look there.

......

My intestines feel like they are on fire and I get episodes of extreme diarrhea and then a state of extreme fatigue. Start with reading [https://www.jackkruse.com/easy-start-guide/](https://www.jackkruse.com/easy-start-guide/), there are lots of links to other pages about the leptin RX too, which could answer lots of questions and help you be more relaxed about trying it. Once you have the idea, and if you make an informed choice to do it, follow the instructions, continue with 3 meals as long as is necessary, and if your last meal is a bit later than ideal, to give you your current needed 12 hour window, then at this point don't stress about it. Do the best you can. My experience is that as circadian cycle improves, it gets easier. The liver is a solar organ, once it is running to its proper circadian cycle, your problem may resolve itself. Your longer term aim is to get your circadian cycle right, and inflammation low enough, for the leptin released from your fat stores to reach the leptin receptors in the brain whilst you sleep, to encourage fat burning whilst you sleep. It should signal a lowering of appetite, and help restore digestive and hormonal circadian cycles. I would not deliberately intermittently fast while you are leptin resistant, it is not part of the protocol, and could make things worse.

I'd also suggest starting a journal in the Optimal journal section, particularly if there's more of a history (such as the gall bladder issue). People who follow the optimal journal section but don't follow the labs forum are more likely to jump in offering their insight and experience.

# Thread: Cornet users beware of Mg readings
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cornet-users-beware-of-mg-readings.19258/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JoeT — 2017-03-21T02:08:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cornet-users-beware-of-mg-readings.19258/post-210805>

So, I have a Cornet meter, but recently sprung for the 300 bucks to get something with better accuracy, particularly at levels below 1Mg....mostly to find suitable places to ground. I went with this one.....

[https://www.lessemf.com/gauss.html#153](https://www.lessemf.com/gauss.html#153)

I didn't notice any highly unusual variations between Cornet and the Alpha Labs meter around my house; however, in my car the Cornet always read about 10 Mg....but the Alpha is reading only 1.5 Mg! Needless to say, I like my car much better now!

### caroline — 2017-03-21T02:47:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cornet-users-beware-of-mg-readings.19258/post-210807>

interesting Joe .... but how do you judge the accuracy of either????

### JoeT — 2017-03-21T02:55:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cornet-users-beware-of-mg-readings.19258/post-210811>

The Alpha is a higher end meter that measures specifically Magnetic fields only. It's more sensitive and accurate. It's not very likely that the inaccuracy is coming from it.

### caroline — 2017-03-21T03:19:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cornet-users-beware-of-mg-readings.19258/post-210813>

so - we really need a selection of meters?

### Sun Disciple — 2017-03-21T22:46:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cornet-users-beware-of-mg-readings.19258/post-210842>

Ya I chose to avoid the cornet and went with a acousticom 2 and alpha UHS 2 as per Michael Nuerts recommendation. The cornet ed88t has decent RF but the gauss meter function is not accurate enough. . Unfortunately if you want accuracy you need independent meters for Magnetic fields, electric fields and RF.

Here is his review of the cornet for anyone on the fence

# Thread: Tips for stroke rehab
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tips-for-stroke-rehab.19259/>

**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2017-03-21T12:48:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tips-for-stroke-rehab.19259/post-210831>

Hey guys,

my grandfather (87y) broke his femoral neck last monday (march 13th) and was operated short after for a endoprothesis of the head. All was going good and he kept doing progress but this sunday he had a stroke and was carried to the stroke unit.
Because of the surgery he couldnt get global lyse, but he now seem to make progress again fast.

Is there anything we can do to help him? My familiy probably wont do this, because they are not into this but I could try.

# Thread: Power Grids and International Competition
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/power-grids-and-international-competition.19261/>

### DC Hacker — 2017-03-21T17:56:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/power-grids-and-international-competition.19261/post-210837>

I was just talking with a friend who previously competed internationally, and her husband still does compete internationally, about the difference in the power grids between the US and Europe. She said it is difficult for American athletes to fly to Europe and compete, but it is not as difficult for European athletes to compete in the US, and she thought the power grids may play into this.

Does anybody have any thoughts on this?

Thanks in advance

### JanSz — 2017-03-22T00:34:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/power-grids-and-international-competition.19261/post-210851>

Mitochondria frequency --100hz
USA power grid frequency--50Hz
Europe power grid frequency--60Hz

IIRC @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) discussed that.
But that would put USA residents at advantage when in Europe. (2x50=100)
\--------------------------
The other consideration would be a jet lag.
Assuming that USA residents would fly eastward, that is disadvantageous when comparing to westward flights.
[http://www.cnn.com/2016/07/13/health/eastward-jet-lag-worse/index.html](http://www.cnn.com/2016/07/13/health/eastward-jet-lag-worse/index.html)
\-----------------------------------

# Thread: Blue Light Induces Mitochondrial DNA Damage and Free Radical Production in Epithelial Cells
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-induces-mitochondrial-dna-damage-and-free-radical-production-in-epithelial-cells.19265/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mystic Rose60 — 2017-03-22T11:25:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-induces-mitochondrial-dna-damage-and-free-radical-production-in-epithelial-cells.19265/post-210864>

"In conclusion, the vulnerability of mtDNA to mitochondria-derived ROS in response to blue light strongly supports a role for visible irradiation in cellular dysfunction. Such dysfunction will be maximal in tissues such as skin and eye, which are regularly exposed to blue light and thus ROS throughout life ([54](http://www.jbc.org/content/280/22/21061.full#ref-54), [55](http://www.jbc.org/content/280/22/21061.full#ref-55)). The accumulation of stochastic oxidative damage in mitochondria supports a number of hypotheses of aging in which the mitochondrion plays a central role ([33](http://www.jbc.org/content/280/22/21061.full#ref-33), [56](http://www.jbc.org/content/280/22/21061.full#ref-56)–[58](http://www.jbc.org/content/280/22/21061.full#ref-58)). Furthermore, the ROS-induced aging is also likely to contribute to a variety of age-related pathologies such as skin cancer and age-related macular degeneration."

[http://www.jbc.org/content/280/22/21061.full#fn-2](http://www.jbc.org/content/280/22/21061.full#fn-2)

Here is my breakdown:

My goals: Get Healthy! Loose 100+lbs and strengthen body to start martial arts regimen
My main issues: fatigue, balance problems, obesity, sleep disturbance, leg pain, back-flank pain, breast pain.

Steps so far:

1.  -   Focusing on this site/theory of health

    -   Got some labs from my GP

    -   Started tracking my meals and my water intake, focusing on Paleo eating

    -   Started really cooking at home or choosing healthy meals while out

    -   Just ordered my Blue blocking glasses!

I have attached my labs in a word document. The highlighted ones are the ones I am concerned about.

MY STATS:
Age: 28
Height: 5’ 8.5" (lost about 1" in last 10 yrs)
Weight: 294 (highest was 306)
Goal Weight: 170

Health History:
Me:

-   -   Broke left forearm (1996)

    -   Glasses needed for minor astigmatism (2006 ongoing)

    -   Gallbladder removal (2009)

    -   Pilonidal cyst draining (2009)
    -   Kidney stone (2010)

    -   Chiropractic needs (2010 on)

    -   -   General back/neck pain (2010 on going)

    -   Broke right ankle, fibula (2012)

    -   Migraines (2013 ongoing)

    -   Avulsion fracture of right ankle fibula (2016)
    -   Moderate inversion ankle sprainof left ankle (2016)

Severe back pain/muscle pull (2016 on)

Subject to 5+ car accidents in life from (1993 on)

Sister:

-   -   Pneumonia (1991)

    -   Bronchitis and Asthma (all life)

    -   Migranes (2000)

    -   Walking Pneumonia (2000-2002)

    -   Left arm numbness (2000-2003)

    -   Cellutitis (2003)

    -   Lupus Diagnosis (2003)

    -   Mixed Connective tissue disorder (2003-2007)

    -   Gallbladder removal (2007)

    -   Severely sprained hip and leg in accident/fall (2016)

    -   Walking pneumonia (2016)

Subject to 5+ car accidents in life from (1993 on)

Mother: Alive, adopted

-   -   -   -   Tonsils/Adeniods removal (1973)

            -   Severe Menstrual cramps (all life until 1995)

            -   Hysterectomy (1995)

            -   Migraines (mid 1990s on)

            -   Gallbladder removal (2003)

            -   Sjogrens Diagnose (2004 on)

            -   -   Eye issues/weakness related to Sjogrens (all life)

Subject to 5+ car accidents in life from (1993 on)

    -   Congestive heart failure (biological mother, dates unknown)

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-22T20:14:18+0200

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=210799)

> Latitude and altitude are no longer enough.........population density is a much better variable with respect to D3 production and quantum yield to sunlight to make D3. nnEMF dehydrates us and lowers water and this affects the isomerization step in D3 productions and lowers the amount of energy in blood plasma. For a woman this is a bad set of circumstances as Reality 9 lays out today.

One suggestion I have (and didn't do myself .... ) is to take some basic measurements, breast, waist and hips, or maybe even better choose a couple of items of clothing that are a bit on the snug side. They will help you gauge any body composition improvements better than scales can. If you gain 4 lb of muscle and lose 3lb of fat, the scales say you are 1 lb heavier.... but its a good sign you might miss if you rely on the scales.  If you put on 7lb but drop a couple of dress sizes  you will be more motivated to keep going... Don't let the scales rule your mood.

Looking forward to your journey. Welcome to journal land!

### CFAN — 2017-03-24T18:33:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-new-start-here-is-my-journal.19267/post-211020>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=210897)

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-25T11:29:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-new-start-here-is-my-journal.19267/post-211045>

> [CFAN said:](/goto/post?id=211020)
>
> Why? What's wrong with Columbus? I am in a suburb of it.

Hi CFAN

One of the teachings is that location matters. It could be because someone lives at a latitude that does not get good sunlight or what sunlight they get is affected by smog, or it could be because they live in a population dense area where, even if they limit their own use, their cells are being bombarded with damaging wifi signals from the people around them. Or it could be that they live in a city that is on the early rollout list for 5G. There are lots of potential reasons, and where someone lives could be exposing them to several factors, that could affect how well someone does following the other principles of the teaching. If someone knows their risks, they have the opportunity to avoid those they have control over, mitigate those they can't by things like extra CT, taking a holiday in a warmer climate etc etc. Or ultimately some may, at a later date - if its possible - decide to move, if they don't see a chance of healing, or staying healthy, in the environment they currently live in. Its about the issues in context with someone's whole life, family issues, job, whatever .....

To give you a flavour ..... try taking the quiz in Reality 5. [https://www.jackkruse.com/reality-5-scompys-quantum-quiz-mito-hacking-life/](https://www.jackkruse.com/reality-5-scompys-quantum-quiz-mito-hacking-life/). Just like your lab test, what you score, is your starting point. However low it is, don't panic. Once you've done it, you can look at the ones you didn't score on, and pick the low hanging fruit, ones that you have control of, and can do something about straight away. Some I can't change (I'm over 50), one I wouldn't change yet if I was leptin resistant (fasting), so think of it as a general guide, not a straitjacket. Your own context matters more than getting a top score on the quiz.

### JanSz — 2017-03-25T13:45:47+0200

One reason why exercises should be limited or done carefully.
\------
Avulsion fracture of right ankle fibula (2016)
\---------------------
An avulsion fracture is a bone fracture which occurs when a fragment of bone tears away from the main mass of bone as a result of physical trauma.
This is a **muscular contraction that is stronger than the forces holding the bone together.

...**
Another problem is loss of height.
Consider getting the suggested above tests,

Ah the importance of context....

Linda I have no experience of doing the leptin reset whilst working nights. In your position I would post the details in my journal, including whether you work 3 nights in a row, or whether its 2 and 1 later in the week blah blah, your meal timings on night shift, what your timings are on days you don't work. Then if you put what you are trying now, and anything you notice about how it feels and affects your sleep. In the context of your journal as a whole, you have a better chance of getting some helpful suggestions or feedback or being pointed to the right blog.

# Thread: Peeing as grounding?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peeing-as-grounding.19272/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### drezy — 2017-03-24T23:24:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peeing-as-grounding.19272/post-211030>

Does my bladder & possibly kidneys want to be regularly grounded?

I live on 3 acres between two other properties that don't seem to have wifi. That may be a reason why the basics of this site (light hygiene, CT, intermittent fasting, grounding...) seem to have worked very quickly for me and my wife.

After some reading, I'm regularly rethinking the most basic human daily actions. Since my acreage has significant privacy, I encourage my boy to pee outside. Urine is highly conductive. It seems like would be like a grounding rod into the ground and up into the bladder, which would normally be a fairly painful process otherwise.

Most people pee into a potty or urinal with a a plastic (non-conducting) P-trap.
So...
Does my bladder & possibly kidneys want to be regularly grounded?

I personally couldn't think of a better way to ground an internal organ (at least momentarily).

The incidence of prostate and kidney issues sure does seem to be rising...

# Thread: Helen Hall's journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/helen-halls-journal.19274/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Helen Hall — 2017-03-25T06:33:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/helen-halls-journal.19274/post-211043>

I'm 45 yrs old female.
I have been diagnosed pre-diabetic and PCOS since at least 2009. I'm of Chinese ethnicity living in LA and have stopped eating rice all together except maybe 6 times a year for at least 4 years as an attempt to cut carb. I also eat virtually no bread or potatoes. But still pre-diabetic and didn't know what to do. Last March I hired a building biologist (Oram Miller) who told me to install a kill switch, which I did. I really thought that would help with my fasting glucose b/c I heard Dave Stetzer said many diabetic who use his filter find they don't need as much insulin, and Oram told me when you have no electricity running on your wires, you don't have dirty electricity either. But last month my fasting glucose was 98mg/DL. I do yoga 3-4 times a week and do mid day sun bath in my backyard as much as possible, usually 1-2 times a week. I do wear blue blockers. I've installed incandescent light bulbs and put in these yellow bulbs (called bug light I think) thinking it would filter out the blue light. But I just heard on a webinar where Dr. Kruse said it's not just any yellow color that filters out the blue light, so it's probably uselesss. My mom died of stomach cancer at 68 she also had an auto immune disease called Sjogren's, my grandma died of some obscure lung disease where she only had 10% use of her lungs at age 87. I have twin girls who are almost 7. One of them is very bright and seems normal, the other has been diagnosed with Pervasive Developmental Delay and she constantly turns her head side to side all day, but she's not diagnosed autistic b/c she's socialable. Both of my kids had sensory integration issue when they were 2, but the one with PDD still has a bit. I sleep well now, but prior to kids I had a lot of trouble falling asleep. My dad often suffers from insomnia. I work as a freelance interpreter. I almost never use a laptop, my iPad is on nightshift mode (yellow screen) all day, I don't have wifi in my house anymore and no cordless phone. My ipad is on wired connection. The neighbors wifi do come thru. I've put aluminum foil in my kids and my bedroom, but sad to report it didn't work as well as I had hoped. My accoustimeter measured .13/Vm prior to putting foil and after foiling is .07/Vm. I was hoping for even more dramatic decrease. Btw, I found cornet meter isn't very accurate or sensitive b/c it almost always measured .0005mW/m2. Michael Neuert suggested I get the Acoustimeter. During the last month I've had 2 episodes of dizziness which I've never experienced before. I went to urgent care and found out I had very low blood pressure 95/50 sometimes the lower number would be around 45. My normal blood pressure is 100/60. Dr tested me for vertigo and told me I don't have that. I had a treadmill stress test (EKG) last week and was told I did well. I have on and off irregular heart beat. Years ago, a very highly esteemed traditional Chinese medicine Dr told me that my irregular heart beat at that particular time it was caused by a virus or something. He gave me herbal medicine and it did go away, but it came back pretty quickly. I don't drink alcohol or have many vices, but I do consider eating and snacking after dinner around 9pm as a big bad vice that I'm having trouble getting rid of, my other vices is going to bed around 10:30-11pm. Btw, I like to offer an advice to all interested in keeping cell phone on airplane mode as much as possible. Get a google voice account (it's free). Set up your google voice so that it forwards all calls to both your home and cell. Stop giving out your cell phone and only give out your google voice number. This way when you are at home you can turn your cell off and all calls can ring on your home phone. This works well with Verizon and att, but I don't think it worked well with sprint 2 years ago. I think you can even port your current cell number to google voice so you don't have to tell everyone your new google voice number, but I haven't tried it.

### JanSz — 2017-03-25T15:48:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/helen-halls-journal.19274/post-211059>

Consider to look at my idea of:
**Artificial lighting management for a human locked in small room.**
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738)
\--------
Day/night are distinguished by presence or absence of 460-480nm light.
Red light (rather than yellow) for the night time.
When getting off bed announce day to your pineal gland by looking in direction of sun or bright light (ExoTerra Solar Glow 160W).
Keep wakeup time constant.
Variations cause jet lag.
Easy on blue-blockers, make sure that you get enough UV.
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-176178](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-176178)
\---------
Worry less about your fasting glucose level,
instead
work on your fasting insulin level, goal=~2-3uU/mL
if fasting insulin gets too high it is impossible to use body fat for energy and therefore weight gain becomes inevitable.
\----------
Eating seaweed as a source of iodine is good practice.
But;
(this days) we need large excess of iodine/potassium iodide
It is not only about thyroid hormones, Hashimoto and some other AI diseases
but also
detoxification of halides, fibrocystic breast, uterine fibroids, etc
iodine for thyroid--->50
iodine for the rest of the body--->1500
selenium+(iodine+potassium iodide)

1/day-->Super Selenium Complex, 200 mcg 100 capsules (lef.org)

1 dropperfull/day=40mg/day=16drops
Lugol's Iodine Family Pack -- 6 (2 fl. oz.) bottles
[http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00E3B49R2?psc=1&redirect=true&ref\_=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o04\_s00](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00E3B49R2?psc=1&redirect=true&ref_=oh_aui_detailpage_o04_s00)
\---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
I'm 45 yrs old female.
I have been diagnosed pre-diabetic and PCOS since at least 2009.

I have twin girls who are almost 7. One of them is very bright and seems normal, the other has been diagnosed with Pervasive Developmental Delay and she constantly turns her head side to side all day, but she's not diagnosed autistic b/c she's socialable. Both of my kids had sensory integration issue when they were 2, but the one with PDD still has a bit.
+++++++++
Omega3 and Omega6 should not be over or under-estimated
they must be JUST right (or else).
+++++++++
To support my health, I use tests to figure out my next move. Most posted here:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4)

Homocysteine, Plasma

[https://www.spectracell.com/order/](https://www.spectracell.com/order/)

[https://dutchtest.com/shop/](https://dutchtest.com/shop/)
DUTCH Cycle Mapping & Complete Bundle (Female Only)

[https://dutchtest.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/04/Steroid\_Pathway\_Jan2016.pdf](https://dutchtest.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/04/Steroid_Pathway_Jan2016.pdf)

### caroline — 2017-03-26T08:33:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/helen-halls-journal.19274/post-211112>

Hi Helen ...and welcome to journal land.

### Mystic Rose60 — 2017-03-27T00:10:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-light-in-the-morning.19275/post-211163>

I always wake up between 4 and 6 in the morning and I light quality candles and have one 3w red LED and black uv that I use. I wear my Carbonshades and make sure that my skin is covered in light cotton and if I get on the computer to do my research then, I put my light hoodie on too and tie it around my face. Also, light cotton gloves that have the fingertips cut off, so that I can type. I don't do any kind of light therapy, using my UFO, etc., until later in the day. I wait for the sun to rise to start my hormone panel for the day. I go to bed early and after 7 or 8 hours of quality rest, I have to rise and shine  I do have a black uv light in the bedroom , just in case my husband needs help, which he did when he first came home from rehab. I had to see to help him turn with his brace on his arm, etc. I reserve that for emergencies only.

# Thread: Is there a concensus regarding the use of MMS ? (Miracle Mineral Solution)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-concensus-regarding-the-use-of-mms-miracle-mineral-solution.19276/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 40
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Remo — 2017-03-25T21:05:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-concensus-regarding-the-use-of-mms-miracle-mineral-solution.19276/post-211098>

> MMS is the general acronym term used throughout theworld to indicate many of the different uses of a solution of sodium chlorite in water. _Sodium chlorite is a mineral/chemical, that when mixed with a food grade acid generates_ **chlorine dioxide**. Chlorine dioxide kills nearly all diseases known inside and outside of the human body, and, it is chlorine dioxide which is the active ingredient used in most of our protocols.

Does anybody have experience using this substance ?

Someone adviced me to try this but I am still hesitating.

[http://www.mms-supplement.com/](http://www.mms-supplement.com/).

### Remo — 2017-03-30T00:07:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-concensus-regarding-the-use-of-mms-miracle-mineral-solution.19276/post-211346>

nobody ?

I have never seen such a big divide in an alternative health protocol as in MMS. Forgive me for my own ignorance but I need your input.

Andreas Kalcker (physicist) seems like a smart and honest man:

Quote from a very informative site: [http://mmswiki.is/index.php/Basic\_Science\_of\_MMS](http://mmswiki.is/index.php/Basic_Science_of_MMS)

> The chemical ClO2 is a weak oxidizer with an oxidation potential of .95 volts. The oxidation potential determines the strength of the ability of an oxidizer, to oxidize other compounds. Chlorine dioxide is the weakest of all the oxidizers that are ever used in the human body. For your reference, oxygen has an oxidation potential of 1.28 volts, hydrogen peroxide has an oxidation potential of 1.80 volts, and the strongest oxidizer ever used in the body, ozone, has an oxidation potential of 2.07 volts.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-03-30T00:13:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-concensus-regarding-the-use-of-mms-miracle-mineral-solution.19276/post-211347>

### Remo — 2017-03-30T00:47:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-concensus-regarding-the-use-of-mms-miracle-mineral-solution.19276/post-211352>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=211347)
>
> **I'll just put this here: Hope is the strongest tool a supplement marketer has. Stupidity of his customers is number two.**

Jack! thanks for responding. I agree with your sentence and vision regarding supplements. In general I do not consider myself stupid. Sometimes I just act like it

Im the first to admit that my chemistry/physics is downright awful. I come to you because I want to know _why_ you think Chlorine dioxide cannot be used as a tool to help kill of bacteria and funghi.

Chlorine dioxide is the active ingredient in MMS after it is activated by acid. The Chemical formula of chlorine dioxide is ClO2. That formula shows that there is one atom of chlorine (Cl) and 2 atoms of oxygen (O2) in a molecule of chlorine dioxide. These 3 atoms are held together by electrons to form the molecule of chlorine dioxide. Chlorine dioxide is a gas, and MMS are in most cases used as a gas dissolved in water. The ClO2 gas can be used at times directly on the skin, or even in the mouth. Chlorine dioxide is one of the most effective killers of pathogens such as bacteria, molds, fungus, viruses, bio-film and other disease-causing microorganisms, which includes the vast majority diseases of mankind.

Chlorine dioxide is generated from sodium chlorite, which is NaClO2. Sodium chlorite has a pH of 13 which means it is highly alkaline. When citric acid or most any other acid is added, they bring that pH down towards the acid condition, causing the sodium chlorite to become unstable and thus begin to release chlorine dioxide (ClO2) from the sodium chlorite (NaClO2). The more acid that is added, the more chlorine dioxide is released, and at a faster rate. That is until all of the chlorine dioxide is released. You can see that sodium chlorite is an unusual chemical that is made largely of chlorine dioxide, but the chlorine dioxide does not become available until the acid is added. As it was stated above, chlorine dioxide is a gas, but it dissolves in any water that is present or that it is mixed with

(Industrial strength Chlorine dioxide solutions has absolutely nothing to do with MMS.) MMS is only a few drops of Chlorine dioxide (or even just Sodium chlorite) diluted in plenty of water – it simply does not have the potential to bleach anything at all. That is ridiculous - to push the idea that MMS is a bleach, as no one has ever used MMS for a bleach.

Chlorine dioxide is unique in the chemical world because the chlorine dioxide molecule has a chemical characteristic that changes and makes it selective for pathogens. The change happens when the chlorine dioxide molecule pulls one electron from a pathogen, which normally is not enough to damage the pathogen, but that electron then comes over to the chlorine dioxide molecule and changes it. When this happens a thousand times or a hundred thousand times, the pathogen is destroyed, mainly because this process blows a hole in the side of the pathogen. There is another oxidation process which is called "chlorination", which involves the chlorine coming over and combining with the item to be oxidized, thus making new compounds. When this happens with chlorination, many times, one of the new compounds because of the chlorine involved, is carcinogenic (cancer causing). This information is just added for reference. Chlorination has nothing to do with MMS, but it might confuse some of the critics who happen to use Clorox bleach in their laundry. But again, chlorination has nothing to do with MMS.

Chlorine dioxide has the weakest oxidation potential (.95 volts) of all the oxidizers. Oxygen has the next highest oxidation potentials of 1.28 volts. Those oxidation potentials of .95 and 1.28 volts can do very little damage to the human body. _This is because these voltages cannot overcome the opposing oxidation potentials in the human cells and tissues. And as you can see, if oxygen does very little or no damage, then the lesser oxidation voltage of chlorine dioxide is not likely to do any damage at all._ Chlorine Dioxide is highly selective and does not harm stomach and intestine useful microorganisms: Chlorine dioxide is highly selective for pathogens that cause diseases in the body, and does not kill the various useful flora of the stomach and intestines. This is due to the unique properties of the chlorine dioxide molecule. Please see several explanations given in the references.

interesting Tammy.. Can I ask how long you used MMS ? Did you follow the official protocol ? Did you notice any other side effects ?

Keep in mind (I believe) that MMS, despite it's name, is not a miracle cure like its name suggest . It does not negate an unhealthy lifestyle or an abundance of EMF exposure/Blue light directly.

However, I do believe that MMS can be a very potent and powerful **tool** in overcoming infections and chronic yeasts/fungus which are increasingely linked to many of the neolithic diseases we speak of.

So far I have had mixed but interesting results:
I had a "keloid" scar tissue bump on my knee which was the result of a knee injury when I was a child. It was there for many years and this bump, how little it may be, was actually very sensitive to touch. Now, these things almost never go away from themselves but after using MMS it has started to decay ! really remarkable and interesting actually. It is slowly drying out and it seems to just fall of in the right time.

Also. I had a "calcified toenail" which just "scattered away" a few days after I started MMS. It definitely has an effect on fungus/calcium deposits (?).

After several days I became very nautious and had much diarhea. I persevered and this problem went away. My backpain cleared up a bit but most of my problems remained.

I do believe I need to follow the strict protocol for a while before I will see results. Enemas and detoxification are important steps in this.
More soon.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-15T18:59:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-concensus-regarding-the-use-of-mms-miracle-mineral-solution.19276/post-348670>

What happens when you add dopants to a semiconductor that is not supposed to be in the recipe? Anyone know?

### Adamo — 2025-03-15T19:01:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-concensus-regarding-the-use-of-mms-miracle-mineral-solution.19276/post-348671>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=348668)
>
> If you ran a semiconductor factory that was alive.......would you add atoms and elements during photolithography?

Not to my knowledge, no.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-15T19:02:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-concensus-regarding-the-use-of-mms-miracle-mineral-solution.19276/post-348672>

When you add dopants to a semiconductor that aren’t part of the intended recipe, you’re essentially altering its electrical properties in ways that might not align with its original design. Dopants are impurities added deliberately to tune a semiconductor’s conductivity—n-type dopants (like phosphorus) donate extra electrons, while p-type dopants (like boron) create "holes" by accepting electrons.

If you introduce an unplanned dopant (supplement/peptide/drug/jab), a few things can happen depending on what you add, how much, and the base material (say, silicon or gallium arsenide).

First, the carrier concentration changes. If you accidentally dope an intrinsic (pure) semiconductor with an n-type dopant when it wasn’t meant to be, you’ll flood it with free electrons, making it conductive in a way it wasn’t supposed to be. If it’s already doped (say, p-type) and you add an opposite-type dopant (n-type), you could compensate the existing carriers—holes and electrons cancel each other out—reducing conductivity or even flipping the material’s type entirely if the new dopant overpowers the old one.

Second, the bandgap and electronic structure can shift. Dopants don’t just add carriers; they can mess with the lattice and energy levels. An unintended heavy dopant (like a rare earth element in silicon) might introduce deep-level traps—states in the bandgap that snag carriers and kill efficiency in devices like transistors or LEDs.

Third, crystal defects can pile up. **Semiconductors are finicky; their performance hinges on a pristine lattice.** Random dopants, especially at high concentrations or if they don’t fit well (wrong atomic size), can strain the crystal, creating dislocations or clusters. This tanks carrier mobility and reliability—think slower transistors or leaky diodes. Details matter in quantum biology and the marketers know you are all dumbasses.

What does the world of physics tell us about semiconductor contamination?

Real-world example: in silicon solar cells, iron contamination (an unintentional "dopant" from processing) acts as a recombination center, slashing efficiency by trapping electrons before they contribute to current.

Another case: if you’re making a p-n junction and sneak in an n-type dopant on the p-side, you’ll blur the junction, wrecking its ability to separate charge.

If you cannot charge them even going into the sun does not matter...........GOT IT? It is a big freaking deal.

### Adamo — 2025-03-15T19:03:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-concensus-regarding-the-use-of-mms-miracle-mineral-solution.19276/post-348673>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=348669)
>
> Why does AMD and Intel build their semiconductors in clean rooms but people here continually shit the bed and think adding exogenous atoms that have no place in their semiconductuve phase makes any sense?
>
> You know this book lays it out?
>
> The semiconduction story right?

Having worked in tech (hardware and servers), you want your chips, boards and environment as clean as possible. The wrong thermal grease, or anything messing with the hardware can cause odd behaviours. Even dust can cause the boards to fry over time.

### Sunshine Surfer — 2025-03-15T19:07:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-concensus-regarding-the-use-of-mms-miracle-mineral-solution.19276/post-348674>

It's easier and it does not have the terrible taste that MMS does, nor does it contain any residues from the part A and B as CDS is only "gas" infused in distilled water.

# Thread: Old new member
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/old-new-member.19278/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Andrea — 2017-03-26T08:36:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/old-new-member.19278/post-211114>

Hi...

It's time for me to intruduce myself... I've been hanging around here for a while. In the beginning of my journey here, I even wrote a journal, [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/)

However, during the last year or so, when I look at my old journal I don't recognize myself. I have been through all that's written there, but I feel like a different person now.

When I first came here I was a fat, sick, tired, brain dead zombie, feeling like I was 80 - I suppose I looked like 80 - and I was waiting to die.
Now... I feel lighter, I AM lighter, my whole body functions far better. I think differently - and I see the world very differently. I am alive.

So I have decided to change my forum name.
My old name, Rerun, is a nick I've used for ages on the internet. It refers to Peanuts, the old comic strip, where Rerun was the little kid who sat on the back of his mother's bicycle, scared to death. That used to be me.

Now, I don't let my mother - or anyone else for that matter - control me. Only my environment does. Also, I don't want any reruns of my old life...

So bye bye Rerun, here is Andrea.

### JanSz — 2017-03-26T18:04:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/old-new-member.19278/post-211128>

That is powerful, intriguing statement Rerun, I mean Andrea.

Please tell us how you did that.
I am 77yo, but do not think of 80 as a major stopping point.
But one newer knows, may be change of name is also in my cards.

....

### caroline — 2017-03-27T03:03:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/old-new-member.19278/post-211181>

Welcome Back Andrea!!!! love what you have done/ accomplished.....

I am pretty positive if we all went back and re read our journals we would hardly recognise ourselves!

What a quantum journey we are on - Thanks for sharing Andrea!

I assume you are keeping the bike?

### Andrea — 2017-03-27T10:27:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/old-new-member.19278/post-211195>

Jan, you are an amazing man and you seem to be in an amazing shape. There's nothing wrong with 80 - except for when you feel like it when you're 35.
And you know, at your age, you have a far sounder hormone panel and libido than I have ever had - until just recently anyway...

What I have done to change the way I have? I have embraced discomfort.
Being comfortable and doing what's easiest never really got me anywhere but closer to death, faster.

On my way now, I actually got some help from you. I didn't get and use the supplements you suggested, but your suggestions helped me see what was wrong and what I needed to work with. I really do appreciate that you took the time to help me, more than you can imagine. Thank you!
(Ok, I am using a little Lugol's, although topically, on specific parts of my body. But that's it.)

The most significant changes to my life are sunlight, limited fake light, ocean based diet, grounding, and less nnEMF.
For more than a year now I have reduced my time in my nnEMF hell home to less than half. (I call it nnEMF hell, although in reality it's nothing compared to what many are facing in large cities in the US and around the world - but it's more than bad enough for me). Among other things. Most of my time now I spend in far better, nnEMF wise, environment than before.
Also, this winter, from October to December, I spent 4 weeks on places with lower population densities at lower latitudes. I should have gone somewhere in the beginning of this year too, but that I failed, unfortunately.

It's so frighteningly obvious that my so called home is killing me, there's no way to ignore it anymore, so I'm there less and less. For now I keep my head above water. But I do have a very uncomfortable decision to make if I'm ever to reach my potential...

### Andrea — 2017-03-27T10:27:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/old-new-member.19278/post-211196>

Thank you, Caroline!
Yes, I'm keeping the bike for now, although for other reasons than before. It's a means to be out in nature... in sunshine... with certain people...

# Thread: Weight loss plateau on Leptin RX
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-plateau-on-leptin-rx.19281/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Josh Tamblyn — 2017-03-27T01:57:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-plateau-on-leptin-rx.19281/post-211177>

I have been following the leptin rx protocol as close as I can for the past three weeks. Being on a somewhat limited budget I have eaten the same two meals everyday for the past 2 weeks
AM Meal:
4oz grass fed beef
2 eggs
2tbs butter
2tbs mct oil
1/2 avocado
3tbsp of bullet proof cologne protein

PM:
4oz wild caught salmon (canned)
1/2 avocado
1egg
2tbs beef tallow or Coconut oil (seasonal)
1 grass fed organic beef hotdog
2tbsp butter

I also eat oysters and bone broth once a week.

I have not been working out and within the three week time frame I have lost 21 pounds.
Sleep has improved ( although I still wake up around 2/3 and 4/5am) I wake rested and ready to start the day. Mental clarity is 100% better but I still have some motivational issues. Could just be I'm in my senior year of college and stress is grinding on me.

For the past 4 days I ha e stopped loosing weight and have stayed the same. I was 291 @5'11 when I started and am now 270.4. I have not been using CT. My bathtub kind of sucks. I've done face dunking but I can handle a lot more. I'm wondering if this quick weight loss and a plateau is common and is it as simple as adding CT or have I run into a possible hormone issue. I have been eating between 2000-2200 calories per day. Eating at 63o am and 6 pm. Any help would be appreciated!

Josh

### caroline — 2017-03-27T11:14:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-plateau-on-leptin-rx.19281/post-211202>

Likely your body is adjusting ....give it some time. You are doing great!

C/T is a game changer. Why does your bathtub suck? Can you take cold showers?

can you get in an ocean? lake? river?

what about sardines? easy on the budget.....

Roast lamb?

bullet proof protein??? that must cost a fortune?

What is your nnEMF situation? How much grounding do you get? early am sunshine?

what water are you drinking?

It isn't about the food....... It is all about the three legged stool

### Mitchell97 — 2017-03-28T13:28:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-plateau-on-leptin-rx.19281/post-211277>

Up your protein... and eat a lot of fish. Light, water and magnetism is the key.

### JanSz — 2017-03-29T17:39:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weight-loss-plateau-on-leptin-rx.19281/post-211338>

> [Josh Tamblyn said:](/goto/post?id=211177)
>
> I have lost 21 pounds.
> Sleep has improved ( although I still wake up around 2/3 and 4/5am) I wake rested and ready to start the day.
> I was 291 @5'11 when I started and am now 270.4.
> I have been eating between 2000-2200 calories per day.
> **Eating at 63o am and 6 pm.**
> Any help would be appreciated!
>
> Josh

Consider eating your daily food
either all at once or at least shorten the time between meals.

To be able to burn body fat one must arrange for insulin to fall below some rather low level.
The longer you insulin is below that level the more time you spend burning body fat.

...

# Thread: RF Survey of JK Members
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rf-survey-of-jk-members.19282/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JoeT — 2017-03-27T02:25:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rf-survey-of-jk-members.19282/post-211178>

I thought it would be interesting to take a survey of what everyone's average RF exposure is in the workplace. We talk a lot about "EMF Hellholes," but has anyone really defined what constitutes good, bad, and "hellhole"? For the purposes of this thread, lets use Cornet's standard unit of measurement of MW/M2. The "safe" level for the general public (non emf-sensitive), as per Michael Neuert is .1.

My Environemnt
My workplace averages about .3
My home is actually really good in most spots, at about .005ish.

This got me thinking....I live 20 miles from NYC. Shouldn't I be seeing "hellhole" type levels? As an example, when I physically go to NYC, the streets of midtown register between 3 and 8, with intermittent spikes. That is pretty hellhole like i would say!

### JoeT — 2017-03-27T18:24:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rf-survey-of-jk-members.19282/post-211224>

I'm a little surprised here...no takers on this??....I thought it would provide some interesting insight into our RF environments! ?

### JoeBranca — 2017-03-27T20:25:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rf-survey-of-jk-members.19282/post-211233>

My workplace, specifically where I stand most the time, the Cornet hits between .15 and .55
Home seems to be an RF safe haven, it remains stable everywhere at .0005

However if it is the case that the Cornet does not truly pick up everything in the range it claims, I'll figure out another RF meter to get which picks up any gaps.

### JoeT — 2017-03-27T21:04:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rf-survey-of-jk-members.19282/post-211237>

Not to bad, Joe.
@[Scompy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17718/) can probably weigh in with how much RF is floating around out there above Cornet's 8ghz range?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-03-27T21:15:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rf-survey-of-jk-members.19282/post-211242>

Mine varies because where I go every day varies in surgery.

### Scompy — 2017-03-28T05:30:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rf-survey-of-jk-members.19282/post-211260>

> [JoeT said:](/goto/post?id=211237)
>
> Not to bad, Joe.
> @[Scompy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17718/) can probably weigh in with how much RF is floating around out there above Cornet's 8ghz range?

This 8+ Ghz concerns me the most... why I left Seattle because I knew a guy that was telling me about the 5G going in everywhere as a test-ground not too far away (and ironically close to the indoor soccer-arena I used to play in under fluorescent lights). I'm going to be investigating into meters that will go up more into the 50 Ghz range. We may just have to wait until the companies make them more available and not as 'scientific equipment' at a high price point.

My instincts are telling me to stop messing around with hormones - for me .....my n=1

After getting lots and lots of ocean/sunshine time - I was feeling fantastic.

I pretty much stopped taking Pg. and still felt really good. I then had to start taking an oestrogen cream for an issue. Months later I realised that I had thrown my hormones out of sync. I should have added back the Pg. for a time.

It does seem that Mother Nature has us covered..........

So - should we leave the balancing act up to Mother Nature? ...... providing we are doing a lot of other things right?

What say you.......

Cool  I was thinking of starting a thread on this blog (after the discussion on @[Debntx](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19762/) journal). Entangled ....?
If menopause gains us time, do we start losing time again if we start messing with exogenous hormones? Perhaps if women have survived long enough to reach menopause by thinking outside the box ..... there's more opportunity, more time at menopause to experience that there is no box ....?

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-27T09:28:32+0300
