### SlamSlask — 2017-12-21T14:29:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/slamslask-journal-reversing-3-years-in-it.19368/post-230785>

**Eye "pain":**
So this has gone away again after talking to my dad I found the reason :
we both visited some family for a few hours on the weekend which are currently renovating their home
talked to him on the phone today and he asked me if my eyes had been acting out too - Has to be the dust or something that got out eyes
This has be worried for a bit

**Epi-Paleo:**
Have sourced some great grass fed meat which are raised close to the same river that i plan on getting my water from
Went there yesterday to see where the cows where grassing etc. - and to say the least this was a very good teaching point for me
The farmer was really passionate about his job and really tried to make sure that all the animals were doing well

Measured the nnEMF there using the ESI 24 - the RF got only up to the second indicatior/led so was really good
My cell phone was also only getting a pretty poor 3G signal

The farmer got a funny look on his face when i told him i was measuring the radiation from the cell towers
After explaning some of it to him he got really interested so without one of us noticing we talked for 2 hours about health and everything in between
This end with me getting a great deal on the execces fat from butchering which im going to be make tallow from

**Water:**
Have stocked up on San Pellegrino think i have about 80 L
So now im ready to refill the bottles with spring water when i run out
The spring centered in the center of Jutland (region in Denmark) so hopefully this means it has lower amount of Deterium
Sadly can't get some from a high mountain but think this will pretty good also
[http://www.findaspring.com/locations/europe/gudenaens-udspringvonge-midtjylland-7173/](http://www.findaspring.com/locations/europe/gudenaens-udspringvonge-midtjylland-7173/)

**Indoor lighting:**
Will get a UVA bulb that I can use in conjunction with candles and the sunlight from my window

**nnEMF:**

### WalterNL — 2017-12-21T15:18:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/slamslask-journal-reversing-3-years-in-it.19368/post-230787>

If you do well with that local food and water that's great. Ideally we would all eat local, but the reason we get stuff from far away is because we screw up our environment or we don't understand why we need to eat what's tied to the local seasons.

### drezy — 2017-12-21T15:50:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/slamslask-journal-reversing-3-years-in-it.19368/post-230789>

> [WalterNL said:](/goto/post?id=230787)
>
> If you do well with that local food and water that's great. Ideally we would all eat local, but the reason we get stuff from far away is because we screw up our environment or we don't understand why we need to eat what's tied to the local seasons.

On top off that I think there are psychological implications too.

There is an arrogance in modern people's decision to reach for a \*box\* of food. Not only do we now know that it's deuterium loaded but it anonymizes every human in that supply chain. Reducing everything down to perfunctory transactions during a "busy" day blinds us to the stories of others (farmers, grocers, butchers). If having less of others stories intertwined with yours isn't a definition of self-centeredness I don't know what else is.

### Michalis — 2018-01-17T07:45:12+0200

### SlamSlask — 2018-03-03T18:07:39+0200

Thanks for linking me to this thread much appraciated

Will make an update soon

### SlamSlask — 2018-03-04T13:58:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/slamslask-journal-reversing-3-years-in-it.19368/post-236255>

So long time since i made an update but here goes
**TLDR:**
Im currently in the middle of moving (Yes ones again) this time to my dads place
My health is increasing rapidly since i quit my office job (IT) my last day was the 30 of jan.
Thanks for all the comments so far they have most certainly been helpful

**nnEMF:**
I used the the advice Jack gave me on the FB which was to go and talk to the "local" tele workers
It toke some time to get in contact with but all the 5 i talked to was all very happy to talk about the expansion of the network (5G/new towers)
This gave me quiet some insides to when and where they will start to roll out 5G due to the fact that we only have 3 different phone networks in Denmark i was also told that we will not be using the very high frequencies (+50Ghz) or atleast that the tele works i talked to had not heard anything about this
Another important things was that 2 of the carriers had recently started to "rent" the bandwidth on the the other carries towers and therefore they are currently upgrade the 4G network with more antennas - This means less towers but higher power denisity
I made some "Contact cards" which i putted in the gift cards i had bought in case they got some new info

This rest of this section can be skipped as it is about me fighting against a new cell tower next to my house:

So fast forward to about 1 months ago one of them send an e-mail telling me that many of my neighbours had complained about the mobile network begin slow and they therefore was discussing to put up a tower about 400 meters from my place
2 weeks ago there was a meeting where about half of the pepole from my area showed up to complain about this
I knew this was gonna be a uphill battle but decided i was not going down without a fight - The arguments was that pepole could not surf the web etc in the evening my coworker even complained that she was not able to play wordfeud on her phone
Then the carrier was talking about how much better the signal would be and he shows some speed tests etc which everyone was clapping at
Then last comments was from me presenting about the health risks of cell towers (Hard to explain in about 5mins)
Pepole found it funny and some was even trying to hide their laughter when i was going back to my seat in the crowd
3 days after i got another e-mail from the same tele worker telling me that had been told to start planning the setup of the new tower
So know im going to have a cell tower pretty much in my backyard no thanks

**Smart meter:**
Had one of my physics friends help me measure this meter and it clearly is sending out lots of microwave and pulsing RF about every 15 secsGetting one of the old meters is pretty much impossible i think i really tried reaching out to the alot of companies etc to figure out why they had to sent data to the goverment about my electricity use etc and they just keep sending you in a ring of pepole even though i would say my questions was spot on
Was visiting my dad not to long ago where he asked me to read his electricy consumption so i went to to meter and was suprised that it was an analog meter
So this will be taken care of with my move aswell

**Sunlight:**
I have increased my amount of sunlight from close to none trough the winter (due to my work) to begin outside about 3 - 5 hours nearly everyday
Due to begin able to tolerate the cold i have lately been able to go in only shorts when outside so my skin is getting a good amount now
The winter does not allow for much UVA but im trying to get as much as i can
Found a nice weather site that also has graphs for the UV index so i know what times to priotise other then sun rises/AM and sunsets
[https://darksky.net/details/55.5008,10.648/2018-3-4/si12/en](https://darksky.net/details/55.5008,10.648/2018-3-4/si12/en)
I have gotten pretty consistent at getting a good amount of AM sun but the sunsets are still lacking

**Solar callus:**
I have started to get a slight brownish color on my skin which not to long ago was all pale and nearly white
Last summer i was still working in the office and living in the city but managed to get some sun at times
At UV index 5/6 I was starting to get roasted after about 1,5 hours so can not wait to test this when the summer is back this year
**
Work/Light:**By quitting my work i been able to cut down Bluelight and nnEMF by a ton
Im still on the computer for quiet some time still but no way close to when i was working
I have bought a IR and a UVA bulb which i used when im on the computer which of course has IRIS running at "0K/Sleep"
I can managed a few hours without getting headaches or it having an impact on y congitive skills
Same goes for my using my phone which i have hacked to be all red (1000K)
Im using my quantlet alot here in winter which is giving me a nice boost of energy when i have to be indoor
Other than that i use blueblockers when im under fakelight

**Cold thermogenis:**
After having done the face dunks and shorter periods of air CT i decided to to take it to next step
For the last week i been walking for about 1 hours each day only wearing shorts, gloves and shoes trough the -5 to 0 C winter
This is so refreshing and im starting to slight addicted lol
Just half a year ago I would have said that was never gonna happen

**Mood/Cognitive:**
My mood has turned around on a plate im much more positive and starting to fell like i was slighly depressed before
Cognitive function is also getting much better before i lost my train of thought many time trough a day of work and had very hard time staying focused for more then 10 mins at a time
This has gotten way better

**Facical skin:**Before i started looking into diets etc some years ago this was horrible
The diets and a dermatolog help this alot but about 2 weeks after she said she had cured my skin i started going down hill again
cutting nnEMF and blue light has helped so much in just 2 months and cost nothing
Now i have 3 smaller spots left compared to many more since the start of the year

**Music:**
I have always listened to lots of Electronic music but in the last months i started to listen to mostly Metal and older rock
This seems to have timed perfectly with me fixing my enviroment
Best thing is im going to see Metallica live later this month

**Viceral fat:**I lost pretty much all of the small amount of fat I had since quiting my job
but my viceral "bloat" still persist
Then again i havent had to much sun on it yet let alone UV so hopefully this changes with more sun and cold

**Diet/Leptin:**
I started getting meat from the local farmer as i talked about also found a place for EGGs and seasonal vegies
As i start buying bigger batches i will also save a little more on the price
At the same time im have gotten way more leptin sensitive as i eat way less and doing a 16/8 fasting schedule is no problem anymore
I still do BAB as this seems to be working the best for me still i rarely eat lunch anymore and dinner i only for social situation
I have cutted my food budget down to half of what i was 2 years ago when i was lifting and doing low carb

**Water:**
My thirst i slowly dropping so im down to drinking around 2 liters a day down from 3-4 again this correlated with quitting my job
I tried to get some water from the spring but it was very slippery so could get the car down the small dirt roads will have to retry this when it gets warmer
So i have bought some Pellegrino so now i can also pick up more water each time
**
Sleep:**
This is slowly getting better and better, my dreams are getting clearer and im down to using between 20-30 mins falling asleep
Been visiting my dads place for a few long periods and my sleep seem to improve when im there pretty sure this is due to him not having a smart meter

Im still amazed at how much Jacks teaching have helped me in such a short timespan especially since new year when I got off work
Huge thanks to @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) and all you other mithocondriacs in helping me navigating these new ways of quantum health !
I currently pretty broke but trying to save as much as i can to be able to meet some you at the next members event

### shiran — 2018-03-04T19:49:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/slamslask-journal-reversing-3-years-in-it.19368/post-236278>

Good job ! Looks like your on the right path ..

### SlamSlask — 2018-03-05T16:07:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/slamslask-journal-reversing-3-years-in-it.19368/post-236333>

Been watching the newer webinars and some of the podcasts for the last week
Have connected so many dots !
I had completely missed the fact that leptin also has benzin rings (and lots of them) so no wonder when i start to expose the areas with the most fat (my back and stomich area) to the sun i see my hunger dropping dramatically

At the same time i guess this is the key to getting my viceral fat to go away?

I finally started becoming pretty familar with all the "technical terms" that Jack uses in his work and have a somewhat idea why he is saying what he does
And now everything is finally starting to connect
Guess i can say that i understand most of the groundwork now...

# Thread: Opinions on CT & niacin flush
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/opinions-on-ct-niacin-flush.19370/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### drezy — 2017-04-20T21:55:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/opinions-on-ct-niacin-flush.19370/post-212668>

I try to get sun before my CT to bring blood to the skin surface. I can also get lots of blood to my skin by using a flushing niacin.

I'm thinking of experimenting with a niacin flush + CT unless anyone has any warnings or other advice.

### Sheddie — 2017-07-23T04:08:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/opinions-on-ct-niacin-flush.19370/post-219792>

I have experience with gradually increasing niacin/B3 in daily IR saunas over a 30 day period, now over a decade ago. Incredibly fabulous sweating, as I recall, and afterwards energy with great sleep -- at the time suffering low thyroid and AI conditions.

What would go on in the body with CT and niacin use I would wish to have explained, and want to try. IF you find out, or try this, please share what happens? Thanks!

### drezy — 2017-07-23T04:17:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/opinions-on-ct-niacin-flush.19370/post-219793>

FWIW, I do not have your answer. I defaulted to CT and then solar noon light (in the strong TX light) so I get my NO vasodilation from light right after CT and I've never felt more relaxed. Someday maybe I'll experiment and I'll report back, but why mess with a good thing?

### Sheddie — 2017-07-23T05:12:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/opinions-on-ct-niacin-flush.19370/post-219796>

Thanks drezy. I'm new here and still need to do profile and other stuff. (I'm MTHFR, too.) Lots more 'scouring' to do.

I'm glad you got a good bio-hack the way you're doing it. Face-dunking is as far as I've gotten, and, some 'lazier' application of ACE Cold Compresses on the Trigeminals.

I may monitor the age of posts better from now on, but that was such an intriguing question. Maybe one day someone will try it, or explain why it shouldn't be tried!

### drezy — 2017-07-23T05:28:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/opinions-on-ct-niacin-flush.19370/post-219797>

I suspect the regularity working up to cold adaption is most important versus trying to hack it, but I'll give it a go sometime and report back.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-ever-wondered-how-lit-up-your-area-is.19372/post-212733>

When I try to explain to power density to friends that don’t have a clue I give them the mental image of the difference of light intensity of a single flashlight in a darkened room versus 100 flashlights in that same room. Then once they get the general idea I explain Specific Absorption Rate and how it is based on the measure of a single RF device over a given volume... then I sit back and see if it sinks in or not!

### JanSz — 2017-05-16T17:50:11+0300

@[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/)
My daugher lives on the same street I do, 1/2 mile away.
I could locate her router, mine is absent.
Hers was old fashion from Verizon.
I had my own.
Wonder how often that map is updated.
Since the last time I checked, we both "upgraded" to new Verizon technology, fiber-optic and all.
I pay for 150/150 internet but when checked I have

[http://www.speedtest.net/?test-5](http://www.speedtest.net/?test-5)
Should I complain?
............
This is a much higher speed than I had before, but I do not see any improvement in how fast web pages are loading.
What gives.
.............
Previously when I was trying to use VPN page loading would slow to a crawl.
Possibly now I could use VPN?

//

### drezy — 2017-05-16T19:39:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-ever-wondered-how-lit-up-your-area-is.19372/post-214149>

@[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/)

The map:
The map is updated by volunteers so I'd say it's random and spotty anyone could join but keep in mind you'd be carrying around your device actively on the whole time. I don't do it and I don't recommend it to anyone here. [https://wigle.net/faq](https://wigle.net/faq) .
**
Also, you kinda don't want to show up on that map if you're a subscriber to half of what's discussed here. Ideally you've turned your wifi off and ran ethernet cables.**

**After they finish crying that they just lost their game of Candy Crush, tablet users could use something like this** **for whatever tablet they use** [http://www.cultofmac.com/425400/when-wi-fi-is-out-use-ethernet-to-get-online-with-your-ipad/](http://www.cultofmac.com/425400/when-wi-fi-is-out-use-ethernet-to-get-online-with-your-ipad/).

That's general info for all users. Info specific to your situation, I just pm'ed.

### drezy — 2017-05-16T21:29:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-ever-wondered-how-lit-up-your-area-is.19372/post-214155>

I still have work to do myself on the wifi front. For reference here's a snippet from my world and a peek at my laziness. I currently use wifi when I work from my "oak office" which is more and more these days. I've still have to run 50' of cable from here to my back office that already has a 150' underground run to my house. Time for me to stop be lazy.

My wife and I decided to move out of the city and into the country. It's the best we're able to accomplish so far but we love it. Here is my "oak office":

[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/oakoffice-jpg.4846/)

Note in red:

-   shoes off
-   shirt off
-   50% oak cover from the sun (I always felt like LLLT beneficial frequencies mimics foliage cover) About 3-4 times a week I'm working here from 9:30 until about 4pm.
-   My big "Conan" pitcher of water with lemon. I mimic Conan while drinking for the following reason:

-   OK, does the Conan posturing really work? Never measured, but sometimes it gets my wife to laugh. I suspect since the first man, us guys know that once we make ladies laugh, let alone look at us, we've got a green light to move closer and take things to the next level.
-   Not pictured, but usually my phone goes up in that tree about 15' away

Note in green:

-   Workout equipment functioning as clothes rack. It makes it appear as though I workout frequently. Chicks dig that. At least every blue moon when I do pick up heavy things, it's outside.

Changing my environment more:
I still have to stop being lazy and run the last 50' of ethernet. Here's why:

Cornet reading with laptop wifi off:
.0040mw/m^2 overwhelming avg & very occasional .0200mw/m^2 max

Same Cornet reading with laptop wifi on (currently):
3 mw/m^2 max regularly enough to be weirded out by it

Caveat for me and big thanks for another member:
@[SatoriHeart](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19303/) has been very helpful in pointing out that more rural locations near 5G test cities will likely face lots of 5G transponders. I appreciate his heads up but, at the same time, worry if he's gotten himself into wifi trouble with overuse of the i.Con here : [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/5g-rollout-tracker.19121/#post-211288](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/5g-rollout-tracker.19121/#post-211288) .

### drezy — 2017-05-16T22:55:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-ever-wondered-how-lit-up-your-area-is.19372/post-214164>

Uncle! the i.Con usage was joke! I need to laugh sometimes so I don't cry. I've worked with a few companies that have big IoT (Internet of Things) plans. The plans seemed ridiculous and my running joke was "When do we get wifi underwear?" I stopped using that joke for the fear of startup launching with the idea.

If I were to scaffold on your world prediction (and maybe side with your wife a bit) the less effected 3rd world would have "generous" foundations that, along with fighting things like malaria will ensure the poor (aka those lacking North Face jackets and Nike shoes) get plenty of satellite internet since it's surely lack of internet bandwidth that is keeping them from being wealthy (aka possessing North Face jackets and Nike shoes).

I've got some measurement equipment and some strategies. We'll see. You've at least got a baseline for my current rough health and mental state.

There's no denying that I could end up with your perspective. That's why appreciate your perspective regardless of it's convenience. You've had your eye on the ball longer.

I'll take all that to mean that you didn't try the drinking from a huge pitcher like Conan thing...

### Scompy — 2017-05-17T22:08:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-ever-wondered-how-lit-up-your-area-is.19372/post-214216>

[http://www.joyoftech.com/joyoftech/joyarchives/2370.html](http://www.joyoftech.com/joyoftech/joyarchives/2370.html)

### drezy — 2017-05-17T22:40:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-ever-wondered-how-lit-up-your-area-is.19372/post-214219>

Ok you guys are piling in on the IoT trend. I've worked with several IoT companies and there is nothing I love more than an IoT or stupid tech crash.

I present "Juicero" a machine that squeezes preserved juice out of a bag ( that you can do by hand anyway ). original price tag = $700. After the internet laughed them out of CEO $400.

This is just the ti of the iceberg of what I've seen in development, but still I love the smell of stupid highly funded ideas failing:

[http://www.npr.org/sections/thetwo-...-much-more-than-juice-internet-is-unimpressed](http://www.npr.org/sections/thetwo-way/2017/04/21/525055713/juicero-ceo-says-luxury-juicer-is-much-more-than-juice-internet-is-unimpressed)

# Thread: CT stage 2 compression shirt
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-stage-2-compression-shirt.19374/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TANN — 2017-04-22T07:46:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-stage-2-compression-shirt.19374/post-212727>

I have been doing the face dunk for a week and I want to push on to the next step.
Jack says to put 20-30lb of block ice into torso.
So this is over the compression shirt right?
Wasn't sure if it's supposed to be tucked under the shirt. Also I have no idea where I am going to get block ice? Can you just use a load of ice cubes in a plastic bag?

### Allin — 2017-04-22T22:43:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-stage-2-compression-shirt.19374/post-212751>

Wear the compression shirt and place the plastic bag full of ice cubes on top of your chest and abdomen.

### TANN — 2017-04-28T10:22:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-stage-2-compression-shirt.19374/post-213034>

> [Allin said:](/goto/post?id=212751)
>
> Wear the compression shirt and place the plastic bag full of ice cubes on top of your chest and abdomen.

Thanks Allin
I completed my first session with compression shirt.
I found it ok so for the last 25 minutes I put the bag of ice directly into chest and abdomen. Total session was for 1 hour after breakfast.
I wasn't measuring skin temp as I assumed I was comfortable enough. After the hour I measured and found I had a skin temp of 46.7. Woops! I see that the directions don't actually say to measure temp in this stage but do assume that actually the same rules apply with not dropping below 50 degrees?
After my skin stayed very pink for up to 1 hour but no hives.
Any experience/ knowledge from you or others I would be most grateful for.
Best regards Tann

### drezy — 2017-04-30T00:24:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-stage-2-compression-shirt.19374/post-213083>

> After my skin stayed very pink for up to 1 hour but no hives.

That's your key vs worrying about each 0.1 degree F.

I think you did really well. Welcome to this stage. How do you feel now(benefits, side effects,...)? You'll know by now if you did anything terrifically wrong.

### TANN — 2017-04-30T10:39:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-stage-2-compression-shirt.19374/post-213089>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=213083)
>
> That's your key vs worrying about each 0.1 degree F.
>

I have not noticed any adverse effects but will be more careful next time. Today is the first day that I haven't done CT for two weeks. I actually got an extra hour sleep which I think is down to not eating any carbs at dinner time. Read about sleep apnea being caused by bacteria/ carb activity. It seemed to do a lot of good plus I slept with a mask.
What stage are you at with CT and how's it working out for you drezy?

### caroline — 2017-04-30T11:12:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-stage-2-compression-shirt.19374/post-213092>

sounds like you are doing fine. Pay attention to your own intuition ....the best Doctor is the one in your head!

Are you drinking ice water before C/T? Are you having a high fat meal before C?T?

### TANN — 2017-04-30T11:37:06+0300

Thanks Caroline

### caroline — 2017-04-30T11:53:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-stage-2-compression-shirt.19374/post-213095>

how old are you? your libido should be crazy good.......

### TANN — 2017-04-30T15:41:30+0300

### drezy — 2017-04-30T19:59:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-stage-2-compression-shirt.19374/post-213114>

> [TANN said:](/goto/post?id=213089)
>
> What stage are you at with CT and how's it working out for you drezy?

I average about 3 C/T's per week since mid February(2 months running so far) . My tub temperatures are anywhere from 47 to 55 depending on ambient temperature and how much ice I bought.
My wife (58 degrees or so) and son (50-52) join me once a week now.

The biggest changes I've noticed right upfront is:

-   Wife sleeps well almost all the time (In the past I've seen her sleeping, but she'd still claim she didn't sleep well)

-   With few exception I've always slept well, but my clock got readjusted. Now after sunset my body sends me signals that it's time to sleep. This was odd and mildly annoying at first since I'm the type that would usually be up till 2AM on a computer. I'm not complaining though since BP is down and libido is up
-   I ditched some saddlebags I had. Weight can creep on tall guys right over their kidneys. There a psychological effect when you go from feeling that it's OKish to pull off a shirt to being happy to pull the shirt off and have people ask what it is you are doing to be in shape. Down from ~16.9% bodyfat to ~ 13%
-   My wife's nighttime itchy skin went away. This seemed like a hormonal thing that was assisted by CT.

Coupled with grounding (I ran a separate ground to my tub AND do separate grounding as well) and UV I also noticed:

-   My cracked heels are almost completely healed.
-   My complexion is the best it's ever been.

With my wife's sleep and oxy squared away, my son is where my focus is currently. Since he's growing so quickly it's not easy to tell what CT effects are on him. I don't see any negative effects and he really likes doing the CT every week.

### Dennis Durost — 2017-05-11T06:18:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-stage-2-compression-shirt.19374/post-213844>

I’m going with a little different CT hack. My only issue is visceral fat and love handles. Everything else is a-ok. I plan to use a waist wrap and insert ice packs my wife obtained when she had her knee replaced. I will be using a compression shirt between my skin and the ice and plan on doing a daily 1 hour routine for 30 days. I will also follow the advice to drink a glass of ice water before as well as consume a fat containing meal. I am prepping by taking colder showers and doing a daily shoeless and shirtless round of put put golf at day break. I am starting with a waist circumference of 37”. I will post my progress. My wife says I'm crazy. Her prescription for addressing my visceral fat issue is to stop drinking beer! What?

# Thread: Vision Improvement!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-improvement.19377/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 30
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Leniumic — 2017-04-23T05:06:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-improvement.19377/post-212760>

Last week I visited my Optometrist because my eyes were hurting when I wore my glasses. I explained to her that my vision seemed to have improved and my glasses now seemed too strong. She politely laughed and explained that now that I am 50 it was extremely unlikely that my eyesight had improved and had likely worsened which was the cause of the eye strain with glasses.

Imagine her surprise to find out that in fact, my vision was greatly improved! As she shook her head in disbelief, she asked what I attributed it to. I began to tell her how about 3 years ago, I stopped wearing the contacts I had worn for 25 years. I recounted Jack's visual of putting a tarp over a tree and expecting a healthy plant. I also explained how I made sure to look directly at the sunrise and sunset as often and as long as possible and even midday look toward the sun. I also shared how I no longer ever wore sunglasses and in fact didn't need them anymore despite the fact that I used to be very sensitive to sunlight. I rounded out the conversation with the fact that I also stopped using any sunscreen and frequently sat in a tub filled with ice for some CT.

She was almost speechless. I told her that as a medical professional she should acquaint herself with Jack's website and the importance of sunlight to the eye.

Although I still need glasses to drive at night she shared the great news that I no longer needed glasses to drive during the day! She told me that she appreciated me sharing and she was definitely going to look Jack up because I was all the proof she needed to believe something amazing was going on that she couldn't explain!

### Jack Kruse — 2017-04-23T05:18:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-improvement.19377/post-212762>

Copy this and hand it to her:

TODAY'S QUANTUM BIOLOGIC FACT: Why are contacts, sunglasses, and eyewear really bad for our eyes? They polarize incoming light from the sun. Sunlight is un-polarized naturally. All versions of man made light is polarized in some way. This means that man made light always has qualities in it that are non natural and manufactured. Are animal cells mitochondria and/or chloroplasts able to handle this situation? NO..............why? Miotchondria and chloroplast both uses excitons to work. Excitons always degrade in vibrations or oscillations in both system. What happens to an exciton when polarized light is used as the incoming signal from your environment? DISEASE AND ILLNESS. Why? The study of sensitized fluorescence is an elegant method to follow the fate of excitation energy in multipigment systems in plant and bacterial cell as well as in pigment-protein complexes isolated from them. The main conclusion from these study is that transfer of excitation energy (or excitons) occurs from the accessory pigments to the reaction center chlorophyll A or bacteriochlorophyll depending upon the system we are investigating. Although these transfers are rather fast (femtoseconds to picoseconds), some energy is also lost as fluorescence, or as heat, depending upon the value of the various rate constants of the various de-excitation pathways. This is why mitochondria liberate heat and why plant chloroplast need water to quench the photosynthetic process to absorb this heat.

Excitation energy transfer between identical pigment molecules, particularly between chlorophyll A molecules in "photosynthetic units" of chloroplasts cannot be determined by the "sensitized fluorescence" technique, i.e., observation of acceptor fluorescence when you excite the donor molecule. However, one observes quenching of fluorescence, i.e., reduced fluorescence when excitation energy transfer is a major event leading to photochemistry in this system. Also, if the molecules are arranged randomly, excitation with polarized light leads to DEPOLARIZATION of fluorescence in these photopigments if there is excitation energy migration. Mitochondria use cytochrome c oxidase as a red light photopigment and NAD+/NADH is a UV light photopigment. Chloroplasts use chlorophyll A and B as its photopigments. What does this post mean? This means energy in the system DECLINES........electron chain transport and the exciton process of photosynthesis SLOWS........what happens when electron chain transport slows? Read up on what happens to electron chain transport when it is exposed to CYNANIDE. So.........about those contacts, glasses, and sunglasses........you still think they are wise decision?

CITES:[https://www.jackkruse.com/ubiquitination-24-are-myopia-light-and-aging-linked/](https://www.jackkruse.com/ubiquitination-24-are-myopia-light-and-aging-linked/)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-improvement.19377/post-212764>

I decided to take a leap of faith and believe that it was the right thing to do, even though it wasn't the easy thing. ### ScottishEmma — 2017-05-30T15:18:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-improvement.19377/post-215090>

I'm currently -4.75 diopters. I had the same story. Told my optometrist that my eyesight had improved. He was very dismissive when I told him I don't wear my glasses unless I absolutely need to and I'm avoiding blue light and eating lots of fish. So yeah, it had gone down from 5.25 in the short space of time I've been doing this (literally less than two months) and I told him when I go back next year it'll be even lower. He laughed. They just don't believe it!! I can't wait to prove him wrong

### ScottishEmma — 2017-05-30T15:44:52+0300

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=323771)
>

Yes, and I wear them mostly on the tip of my nose. The goal is to get rid of them asap!

### Branden G — 2023-07-16T19:43:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-improvement.19377/post-323803>

Thank you guys for this thread. I’m -7.25 in both eyes with astigmatism in my left eye. I’ve worn contacts for 10 years now. I plan to buy glasses and trying to correct my vision.

### Gcazz — 2023-08-25T10:32:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-improvement.19377/post-325561>

For some one that truly needs glasses to see. (not me) Born with one eye that works the other the optic nerve not connected. Would blue light blocking glasses with the prescription be an option?
Thank you all for your stories.

### Miriam Richard — 2023-08-25T19:14:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-improvement.19377/post-325577>

> [Leniumic said:](/goto/post?id=212760)
>
> Last week I visited my Optometrist because my eyes were hurting when I wore my glasses. I explained to her that my vision seemed to have improved and my glasses now seemed too strong. She politely laughed and explained that now that I am 50 it was extremely unlikely that my eyesight had improved and had likely worsened which was the cause of the eye strain with glasses.
>
> Imagine her surprise to find out that in fact, my vision was greatly improved! As she shook her head in disbelief, she asked what I attributed it to. I began to tell her how about 3 years ago, I stopped wearing the contacts I had worn for 25 years. I recounted Jack's visual of putting a tarp over a tree and expecting a healthy plant. I also explained how I made sure to look directly at the sunrise and sunset as often and as long as possible and even midday look toward the sun. I also shared how I no longer ever wore sunglasses and in fact didn't need them anymore despite the fact that I used to be very sensitive to sunlight. I rounded out the conversation with the fact that I also stopped using any sunscreen and frequently sat in a tub filled with ice for some CT.
>
> She was almost speechless. I told her that as a medical professional she should acquaint herself with Jack's website and the importance of sunlight to the eye.
>
> Although I still need glasses to drive at night she shared the great news that I no longer needed glasses to drive during the day! She told me that she appreciated me sharing and she was definitely going to look Jack up because I was all the proof she needed to believe something amazing was going on that she couldn't explain!

Do you still have the great effects?

### Pete Collins — 2024-10-03T03:05:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-improvement.19377/post-340347>

Unfortunately I have a genetic condition called small eye from birth (nanophthalmos)
Vision prescription of contacts +17, so I am screwed without visual aids.
I still sun gaze AM/PM.
I am looking for ways of avoiding the surgery that the specialist tells me is going to be needed at some stage in life.

anybody aware of this condition?

### Jana C — 2025-05-29T20:29:51+0300

Hi Doc,

This page is not found for me to learn from. Second one today.  How can I view/listen to this one?

### CindyR — 2025-05-30T05:14:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-improvement.19377/post-353559>

> [Leniumic said:](/goto/post?id=212760)
>
> Last week I visited my Optometrist because my eyes were hurting when I wore my glasses. I explained to her that my vision seemed to have improved and my glasses now seemed too strong. She politely laughed and explained that now that I am 50 it was extremely unlikely that my eyesight had improved and had likely worsened which was the cause of the eye strain with glasses.
>
> Imagine her surprise to find out that in fact, my vision was greatly improved! As she shook her head in disbelief, she asked what I attributed it to. I began to tell her how about 3 years ago, I stopped wearing the contacts I had worn for 25 years. I recounted Jack's visual of putting a tarp over a tree and expecting a healthy plant. I also explained how I made sure to look directly at the sunrise and sunset as often and as long as possible and even midday look toward the sun. I also shared how I no longer ever wore sunglasses and in fact didn't need them anymore despite the fact that I used to be very sensitive to sunlight. I rounded out the conversation with the fact that I also stopped using any sunscreen and frequently sat in a tub filled with ice for some CT.
>
> She was almost speechless. I told her that as a medical professional she should acquaint herself with Jack's website and the importance of sunlight to the eye.
>
> Although I still need glasses to drive at night she shared the great news that I no longer needed glasses to drive during the day! She told me that she appreciated me sharing and she was definitely going to look Jack up because I was all the proof she needed to believe something amazing was going on that she couldn't explain!

Thank you for sharing your amazing story. I'm hoping to improve my vision, too. I wore contact lenses for 39 years and switched back to glasses last year.

### Freespirit — 2025-05-30T20:59:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-improvement.19377/post-353596>

I’m in my mid 50’s and have been sun gazing now for over 2 years. I had to go in to get my eyes tested because I could barely see with my current glasses. The double mask wearing eye doctor told me the my eyesight has improved significantly. I wasn’t at all surprised, she told me that I probably have the beginning stages of cataracts. I told her that I doubt it. She wanted to perform a plethora of tests, I refused.

### Flynn Jones — 2025-05-31T23:36:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-improvement.19377/post-353657>

I have also seen improvement from -2.50 to around -1.00 now and 20/20 in high uv environments. Still need glasses for driving at night . It is amazing what nature can do !

### Freespirit — 2025-06-10T01:16:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vision-improvement.19377/post-354382>

> [Jana C said:](/goto/post?id=353663)
>
> That's remarkable! I'm so happy for you! Do you mind if I ask you how to "safely"sun gaze??

### Jana C — 2025-06-11T15:26:14+0300

# Thread: Moving from Sub Optimal to Optimal - Journal of Jody
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/moving-from-sub-optimal-to-optimal-journal-of-jody.19379/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jody Spencer — 2017-04-23T08:33:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/moving-from-sub-optimal-to-optimal-journal-of-jody.19379/post-212777>

Hi all,

In short, I want to lead a life of optimal health, I understand that there are going to be some real big changes to take place. Looking for whatever guidance and wisdom can gain and possibly share.

My first question is what labs should I begin with?

Goals - Stop the ringing in my hears.
\- Rectify Shoulder pain
\- Repair damaged vertebrae (See below)

My diet is reasonably clean, although to many carbs as my partner is vego.

Only getting seafood once or twice per week.

Plenty spray free, local fruit and vegetables.

My Stats :

Vintage : 1970 (47 years old)

Sex : Male

Height : 5 10”

Weight : 175lb

Race : Caucasian – English decent – Red Head.

Blood pressure : 137 / 88 taken 5pm 7 April

Lets start with the little bit of family history,

**Mothers Side**

Grandfather died of a heart attack at age 52

Grandmother died with Alzheimer's and Parkinson’s at age 79

Mum alive 69 years old – no health issues, eats well, exercises – just believes Neolithic disease is just the luck of the draw.

**Fathers Side**

Grandfather died at 73 heart disease .

Grand mother currently 87 in care suffering from Alzheimer's

Dad – 69 years , this year has had 3 stents put in after suffer chest pain and lack of energy for 1 year.

**My health history**

Born with a heart murmur – had it checked and 30 years of age cardiologist said it had healed over not to be concerned with it anymore .

Hospitalisation at age 8 for unknown ongoing pains revealed a shrunken lower pole of one kidney. (no sure what side)

Herpes Simplex 1 + 2 confirmed positive 1996. Marked increase in 2016 and 2017.

Especially after to much sun.

Laser Eye Surgery for Short sight 1998

Right Knee ACL replacement - 1998 - Rugby

Right Knee MCL tightening 1999

Have hematomacrosis – Last ferritin level – 2 years ago 180.

Has been as high as 1500 when ill in 2011. 2013 – 400

**Tinnitus** \- My ears popped on the morning of 5 Decemeber 2014 – have been ringing ever since.

**Excruciating referred pain down left arm** – April 2015 - **MRI and Xray results** below-

\-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**X-Ray Cervical Spine**

Clinical History :

Suspected left mid-lox cervical radiculopath secondary to foramina osteophyte

Technique :

A five view x-ray examination of the cervical spine was performed.

Comparison :

None

Report:

This is no evidence of an acute fracture of any of the cervical vertebrae. There is moderate degenerative disk disc disease located at the C5-C6 Level. There is mild degenerative disc disease located at the c4-c5 level . There is a mild retrolisthesis of C3 on C4, C4 on C5 and C5 on C6 of upto 0.2cm. There is mild kyphosis of the mid to lower cervical spine. There is no prevertebral soft tissue swelling detected.

There is a mild right-sided neuroforaminal narrowing located at the C5-C6 level.

There is a questionable mild left-sided neuroforaminal narrowing at the C4-C5 and C5-C6 levels.

The bodies of C1 and C2 are aligned. The dens is intact.

There is a mild overbite.

Impression:

The is moderate degenerative disk disease of the mid and lower cervical spine.

\-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**MRI CERVICAL SPINE**

Clinical History : Left-sided radicular symptoms. C6/7distribution.

Technque : Sagittal and axial T1 and T2 and oblique sagittal T2 through the left intervertebral foramina.

There is minimal degenerative retrolisthesis of C5 on C6. There is some straightening of the normal cervical lordosis but alignment is otherwise normal.

Vertebral body height is maintained at all levels. No destructive osseous lesion or any paravertebral soft tissue mass or swelling seen.

Normal appearances are seen the craniocervical junction. There is mild hypertrophic degenerative change around the atlantodentate articulation. The lateral C1/2 articulations are normal.

C2/3 Normal

C3/4 Normal

C4/5 Disc height is preserved and there is no significant disc herniation or any central canal stenosis. Minor uncovertebral and facet joint osteophytes cause moderate left foraminal narrowing with abutment but no definite impingment upon the left C5 nerve root. Mild left facet joint degenerative change.

C5/6 Moderate loss of disc height. There is a moderate posterior disc osteophyte ridge which is most marked in a right paracentral location. This causes mild to moderate stenosis of the central canal with focal indentation of the anterior thecal sac and loss of most CSF signal surrounding the spinal cord. There is no spinal cord compression. Uncovertebral and facet joint osteophytes cause moderate to severe left and at least moderate right foraminal stenosis. There is mild compression of the left C6 nerve root and potential for compression of the right C6 nerve root also. Mild left facet joint degenerative change.

C6/7 There is very shallow posterior disc dulging but no significant central canal stenosis. There is minor unconvertebral osteophytic lipping bilaterally but no significant foraminal stenosis is seen.

C7/T1-T2/3 normal

The visualised soft tissue structures of the posterior cranial fossa are unremarkable. The spinal cord is normal in calibre and signal intensity throughout its visualised course.

No significant soft tissue abnormality is detected within the neck.

\-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Looking forward to the ride and your thoughts.

### PaulG — 2017-04-24T04:35:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/moving-from-sub-optimal-to-optimal-journal-of-jody.19379/post-212812>

I know Telstra have been trialing wifi hotspots since 2014 in Bunbury so if you work or live close to them this may be the culprit -> dehydration of your discs and calcium efflux are documented effects of wifi/mobile radiation.
[https://crowdsupport.telstra.com.au...on=wifiHotspots&searchQuery=Bunbury, WA, 6230](https://crowdsupport.telstra.com.au/t5/Find-your-nearest-Telstra-Retail/ct-p/tstore#?searchOption=wifiHotspots&searchQuery=Bunbury,%20WA,%206230)

This causes mild to moderate stenosis of the central canal with focal indentation of the anterior thecal sac and loss of most CSF signal surrounding the spinal cord. There is no spinal cord compression. Uncovertebral and facet joint osteophytes cause moderate to severe left and at least moderate right foraminal stenosis. >
> Looking forward to the ride and your thoughts.

The MRI reports is strongly suggestive of a C5/6 osteophyte that is impinging on the C6 root and there is some sagital balance issues associated with telling us that the you likely have a had a chronic D3 problem fo rsome reason since you are young and have many changes of advanced arthritic disease. As D3 is lowered osteoarthritis rises. They made no mention of the erector spinae muscles but my bet is the fiber type is changed and the evidence would show more fat replacement in the muscles and bone. This would be obvious to the trained eye of someone who knew to connect these dots on the MRI. The kyphosis is strongly suggestive of this mechanism based upon the description provided. Your mitochondrial history is not so good so it is clear you have lived a large part of your life in a low quantum yield environment and this is a problem. You'd be wise to confirm some of this with basic labs I covered in my book. Ones to consider with your doc are D3, free and total testosterone SHBG BUN/creat ratio, H/H, CO2 and K+, full thyroid panel (your story suggests chronic mild hypothyoidism is possible due to bone/spine changes) And Adrenal stress panel to include DHEA, cortisol and melatonin. I would disagree that no significant soft tissue injury is present based upon the fact that they used the words kyphosis in your report. It is not normal for a 47 year old to have kyphosis. It is indicative of chronic biomechanics failure.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-04-25T15:31:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/moving-from-sub-optimal-to-optimal-journal-of-jody.19379/post-212909>

"Humans inherit mitochondria from their mothers and mtDNA through the oocyte. In a human female embryo, the first primary oocytes develop from the primordial germ cells from two to three weeks into the process of embryo development. As reported by various scientists, the number of mitochondria in the primary oocyte ranges from fewer than ten to two hundred. Robert P.S. Jansen in his 2000 article "Germline Passage of Mitochondria: Quantitative Considerations and Possible Embryological Sequelae" reports fewer than ten mitochondria per human primordial germ cell. However, by the time the female infant is born, each primary oocyte has approximately 10,000 mitochondria per cell. There is another tenfold increase in mitochondrial number during adult growth and development. For most female mammals, the mature oocyte has from 100,000 to 600,000 mitochondria. The amount of mtDNA in each mitochondria in the female germ-line is slightly more mtDNA than the number of mitochondria. Ovarian insufficiency is associated with major depletion of mtDNA in the oocyte." [https://embryo.asu.edu/pages/mitochondrial-dna-mtdna](https://embryo.asu.edu/pages/mitochondrial-dna-mtdna)

### Jody Spencer — 2017-04-25T16:22:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/moving-from-sub-optimal-to-optimal-journal-of-jody.19379/post-212914>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=212897)
>

As D3 is lowered osteoarthritis rises. They made no mention of the erector spinae muscles but my bet is the fiber type is changed and the evidence would show more fat replacement in the muscles and bone. This would be obvious to the trained eye of someone who knew to connect these dots on the MRI. The kyphosis is strongly suggestive of this mechanism based upon the description provided. Your mitochondrial history is not so good so it is clear you have lived a large part of your life in a low quantum yield environment and this is a problem. You'd be wise to confirm some of this with basic labs I covered in my book. Ones to consider with your doc are D3, free and total testosterone SHBG BUN/creat ratio, H/H, CO2 and K+, full thyroid panel (your story suggests chronic mild hypothyoidism is possible due to bone/spine changes) And Adrenal stress panel to include DHEA, cortisol and melatonin. I would disagree that no significant soft tissue injury is present based upon the fact that they used the words kyphosis in your report. It is not normal for a 47 year old to have kyphosis. It is indicative of chronic biomechanics failure.

Southern parts of NZ and not eating much seafood. Age 16-40 was a complete disaster. Smoking, drinking far too much landed me in AA nearly 7 years ago. Excess air travel and living in NYC, NOHO for 7 years 2003-2010 really just iced the shit cake.

### Dennis Durost — 2017-04-24T19:56:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-question-cataract-surgery-and-morning-blue-light-absorption.19381/post-212844>

Can I absorb morning blue light even if I have had cataract surgery and implantation of an artificial lens?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-04-25T01:36:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-question-cataract-surgery-and-morning-blue-light-absorption.19381/post-212868>

Depends upon the implanted lens. Most of them block 50% of blue. In Japan they are making lenses differently now because it is the one country where the eye docs are realizing the key about the eye clock and its circadian timer.

### Dennis Durost — 2017-04-25T04:05:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-question-cataract-surgery-and-morning-blue-light-absorption.19381/post-212885>

I have the Alcon Acry Sof Toric Lens that filters 50% of the blue light up to 470nm. According to the literature I read, a healthy 53 year old eye transmits about the same. However, my 75 year old eyes were transmitting only about 24% of blue light. Unless I have this figured all wrong, with this IOL I am actually absorbing more blue light than with my god given lens. Bottom line, what is the affect on the Sphynx AM practice for my situation?

### Jack Kruse — 2019-08-14T16:16:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-question-cataract-surgery-and-morning-blue-light-absorption.19381/post-275951>

When the lady who built the laser for laser eye surgery says this you might want to ask why she feels this way. What does she know that the eye doctors do not? I submit she is very wise. This is why she won the Noble Prize almost a year ago. Just because we can do and invent things does not mean we should use those things in daily life when they happen to break universal laws around light. She wears thick glasses because of her high myopia which is most ironic. The reason is that she refuses to get the corrective surgery her Nobel work made possible. Dr. Strickland said, “I have great faith in lasers, but no one’s putting one near my eye”. A very wise position she has. When a laser is close so is blue light and an intraocular lens implant that will block the terrestrial frequencies of the sun and lead to other issues for you down the road. [https://www.theguardian.com/science...-myself-as-a-scientist-not-a-woman-in-science](https://www.theguardian.com/science/2018/oct/20/nobel-laureate-donna-strickland-i-see-myself-as-a-scientist-not-a-woman-in-science)

### Jack Kruse — 2017-04-25T01:32:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/canned-oysters.19382/post-212865>

Big news today out of NOLA: I've always suspected this was the case but now I know my intuition was correct. For many researchers and clinicians this is quite an unexpected finding but not for me. I've been on Bazan work since 1993. The identification of a lipid mediator that instructs the key protein IDUNA to do the right thing in the retina and brain is a massive finding for my thesis about the semiconductive pathway of the anterior visual system. Another Epi-paleo Rx win!!!! The fish oil component of seafood helps damaged brain & retina cells survive insults from the environment. DHA, as a precursor of NPD1, counteracts the deficits by neural damage by increasing IDUNA expression in neurons and astrocytes after stroke onset. This means that Dr. Bazan's findings establish a CAUSAL relationship whereby NPD1 modulates IDUNA abundance and cell survival after neural injury/stroke, resulting in neurological protection. Big deal for all mitochondriacs! [https://www.nature.com/articles/cdd...lDgLe3Ro0Raxl6ZVOugciU47smUyDyIi54bAFiEJlYAru](https://www.nature.com/articles/cdd201755.epdf?author_access_token=mwynnelQmE0l7EYyA01N7dRgN0jAjWel9jnR3ZoTv0M0BEeYnVHav2NDrnJqDWcClesV4I8pfQiu4Xm4_KwlDgLe3Ro0Raxl6ZVOugciU47smUyDyIi54bAFiEJlYAru)

### malc0088 — 2017-04-25T19:33:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/canned-oysters.19382/post-212919>

I was going to whole foods and paying between 1-1.99 per oyster, eating 5-10 per day, 7 days a week. After alot of lost cash I got in contact with the wholesale seafood supplier here in Kansas City. We have Seattle Fish Co. here who supplies to the surrounding 6 states in the midwest. I'm now able to buy the same or better oysters than in the store for 1/4 the price. I'm paying between .65 - .80 cents per oyster now, and I get them in cases of 100. Its an all you can eat oyster fest at my house now! Find your local wholesaler, it's a game changer!

### Penny — 2017-04-25T21:22:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/canned-oysters.19382/post-212927>

This might help someone figure out what you are talking about: Reality 4...

[https://www.jackkruse.com/reality-4-eye-brain-diseases-exploding/](https://www.jackkruse.com/reality-4-eye-brain-diseases-exploding/)

**THE TAKE HOME: WHAT IS THE BAZAN EFFECT AND WHY IS IT IMPORTANT TO A QUANTUM CLINICIAN?**

Dr. Bazan is a neuroscientist who’s work influenced me while I was a resident at LSU and long before I had a quantum perspective. He was the first person I can recall in my training that was able to link clinical diseases to the RPE in the retina by discovering something called the Bazan effect today. He was initially studying what effect seizures and lack of blood flow had on the CNS. He found when the brain is stressed for any reason neurons release two types of essential fatty acids, namely omega 6 and omega 3. There names are arachidonic acid (AA) and docosahexonic acid (DHA). These are essential FFA’s because the body has a poor capacity to make them endogenously.

Arachidonic acid is found ubquitously all over the body in all cell lines but DHA goes mainly to the retina and brain. Both become critical parts of the cell membrane EMF antenna in cells that work in wireless fashion using various non linear apsects of the electromagnetic spectrum.

The release of both of these fats created major questions for science 30 years ago. Bazan found that the release of AA and DHA occured very rapidly. This speed was initially felt to be a post-mortem or a pathologic side effect of a stimulus. His lab also raised the question whether the event was related to normal physiologic function. He used electroshock experiments to figure out the truth. He found once the EMF was used to create the electroshock that once the fatty acids were relased they RETURNED to the cell membranes by two pathways. He found the release was a REVERSIBLE event anf this told him that the release of AA and DHA to EMF’s was tied to physiologic function. This set the stage for his lab finding the key links between seizures and neurodegenerative diseases.

AA is a precursor molecule for all the prostaglandins and many other important mediators that were eventually discovered by Bengt Samuelson at the Karolinska Institute. Because these mediators all have 20 carbons they are called eicosanoids. In 2003 and 2004 he linked these mediators to diseases of the eye and brain via the discovery of neuroprotectin D1 (NPD1) which is the FIRST MESSENGER or mediator of DHA metabolism. He found that neuroprotectin D1 is intimately involved in electroshocks, in siezures and in ischemia of the retina and brain disorders. NPD1 functions to protect neurons from metabolic, photolytic, and EMF damage. NPD1 comes from DHA which has 22 carbons so it comes from a family of chemicals called docosanoids.

If my eyes hurt from the computer, eating seafood cures it quite quickly...

This seems like low risk heart attack but what quantum model things need to be considered regarding this rising LDL readings. I assume this is poor environment leading to dehydration that leads to inability to convert some of this LDL. Not that its at bad levels. I'm more concerned about BUN/Cr and hs-CRP.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-04-25T14:41:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandys-labs.19384/post-212895>

BUN/Creatinine ratio 26 with a low D3 and your location means that she is so dehydrated that she cannot complete the isomerization step from sulfated cholesterol to the D3 molecule........as such the D3 stays too low and the LDL cholesterol rises because of the lack of conversion..........what does it mean? Michigan does not have enough power for her and/or there is something else also present in her zip code that is really hurting this conversion and it is very likely nnEMF because her BUN/creat ration is woefully bad.

She needs to hack this. A women with a chronic low D3 is very at risk for bad things like breat Ca and hypothyroidism. In 2009 the WHO linked low D3 levels with breast Ca as a major risk factor. Low D3 is linked with depression, hypothyroidism, AI's, gut issues, and many immune issues related to skin and eye and brain.

I need a way to lower libido but i don't want to mess with my hormones.

# Thread: A Mitochondriacs' Thoughts on Light, Vibrations, Ancient Pyramids and Geometry
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-mitochondriacs-thoughts-on-light-vibrations-ancient-pyramids-and-geometry.19389/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lohd2015 — 2017-04-27T16:18:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-mitochondriacs-thoughts-on-light-vibrations-ancient-pyramids-and-geometry.19389/post-213009>

I decided to create this thread after reading Dr. K's post on FB, April 25, 2017.
[https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/1747482461982767](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/1747482461982767)

I am truly excited as I believe this to be MASSIVE.

There has always been theories on the vibrational effects of Ancient pyramids, and the use of special materials such as mica.

**ANCIENT PYRAMIDS WERE HIGH FREQUENCY POWER STATIONS**
[http://revelationnow.net/2015/09/27/ancient-pyramids-were-high-frequency-power-stations/](http://revelationnow.net/2015/09/27/ancient-pyramids-were-high-frequency-power-stations/)

**A New Theory for the Great Pyramid**
[http://www.newdawnmagazine.com/arti...-how-science-is-changing-our-view-of-the-past](http://www.newdawnmagazine.com/articles/a-new-theory-for-the-great-pyramid-how-science-is-changing-our-view-of-the-past)
"the subtle electrical field created by the Great Pyramid would be of little use for means of powering equipment. However, such a field would deflect very low frequencies (VLF) and extremely low frequencies (ELF), frequencies that exist at all times in the atmosphere as a result of thunderstorms around the world. In agricultural experiments, VLF and ELF have been shown to promote plant growth. With the Great Pyramid operating as the engine, all of the pyramids could be connected by a subtle electrical field to create a canopy of sorts thereby deflecting VLF and ELF into the surrounding fields."
**The Place of the Gods**
[https://www.bibliotecapleyades.net/stargate/stargate10.htm](https://www.bibliotecapleyades.net/stargate/stargate10.htm)
This is an article on the Stargate at Abu Ghurab, or “the crow’s nest" as it is called, is a closed to the public archaeological site in the pyramid fields that run along side the Nile south of Cairo. Egyptologists quaintly refer to it as a ‘sun temple’, a ‘burial center’ or ‘funerary complex’ for a new cult of Ra (they usually use these terms when the actual function of a place is unclear).

There is a striking similarity between Abu Ghurab and Teotihuacan, which Dr. K talked about.

"Underneath the Pyramid of the Sun archaeologists discovered mica, a dielectric mineral composed of delicate crystal that is a semiconductor. The word “mica” is thought to be derived from the Latin word micare, meaning to shine, in reference to the brilliant appearance of this mineral (especially when in small scales).
Mica has a high dielectric strength and excellent chemical stability, making it a favored material for manufacturing capacitors for radio frequency applications. It has also been used as an insulator in high voltage electrical equipment. Sheet mica is used as an insulating material and as a resonant diaphragm in certain acoustical devices. Sitchin was perplexed by the presence of this mineral beneath the pyramid.......
Indeed, the Abu Ghurab site was beginning to look a lot like the Teotihuacan site.
Those basins, it turns out, were decanters.
The only thing missing was the mica."

**The ancient use of mica in pyramids**
[https://www.bibliotecapleyades.net/ciencia/pyramid\_thoughts/pyramidthoughts03b.htm](https://www.bibliotecapleyades.net/ciencia/pyramid_thoughts/pyramidthoughts03b.htm)

****Pyramid of Secrets - The Singing Pyramid and the Myth of Creation****
[http://robertbauval.co.uk/articles/articles\_guest/pos.html](http://robertbauval.co.uk/articles/articles_guest/pos.html)

"ANCIENT Egyptian literature testifies to the importance of sound at the time of the creation. In several versions of the myth, the Creator-God's emergence from the earth is accompanied by a great noise and commotion. The gods shout. The Earth resounds."

# Thread: Johnny's Journal of Brain Fog
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/johnnys-journal-of-brain-fog.19391/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 50
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JohnnyAZ — 2017-04-28T07:32:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/johnnys-journal-of-brain-fog.19391/post-213025>

Not sure how much to put in here so I figured I would go for too much instead of too little.

Grandmother's health: Died of an aortic aneurysm in 1999. Date of birth 4-21-27. Grew up in Washington and then moved to Illinois before getting married and having children.

Mothers Health: Developed many health issues in her 40's including Hashimoto's, Fibromyalgia, tinnitus, estrogen imbalance (I don't know the specifics of this one or if she was high or low but there was an issue, and for a year of her life she suffered from uncontrollable tremors that no one ever diagnosed. Finally she succumbed to Lung Cancer at age 57.

Aunts on my mothers side: One died from hemacrhomatosis and another has battled anxiety her entire life.

Now on to me.

I was born 11/16/1980 in the Northwest suburbs of Illinois. I spent my first 13 years in Roselle, IL and I was a healthy active child who spent most my time outdoors. I did sleep with a TV in my room from a young age (maybe 9 or 10 when I started). I did get extreme Migranes as a child but I was told it was my teeth (they were all out of place) and once I corrected my bite these vanished. I went to a private school and we did utilize computers on occasion but not too much.

In September of 1994 I moved to a more rural area and it was here issues began to arise. The first was a sort of depression/fear that developed and it was only present when I would approach my school. I chalked this up to the fact that I moved and was in a new location. It took me months to get past this and once I got through I never thought of it again as I figured it was just the symptom of moving to a new school. One day in gym I was tackled strangely and compressed my neck and ended up in the ER. Nothing was wrong but ever since then I had a neck issue that got worse and worse and really became problematic at age 23 (explained below). Through High school I was your traditional skateboarding punk who spent 50% of his time outside and 50% sitting in houses with his friend playing video games (I have always enjoyed video games). I also started smoking in sophomore year and smoked for the next 21 years. During this time my stomach became very irregular and was either emptying too much or not enough and I developed a minor case of depression and slight insomnia. All of these continued to evolve and in college most of the minor symptoms from high school escalated. Throughout most of high school I worked in both a restaurant and a warehouse where we fixed broken cash registers (rewired and rebuilt them).

In college I spent my first two years at a community college and continued to live in the same house I had been since 13. During this time my stomach issues really became problematic and I went to every type of doctor to find an answer but nothing was every truly diagnosed. I did the scope (both entry points) and nothing was really found. I had prostate exams and was told it was "mushy"...whatever that means. To this day my stomach is still a major concern and I have to be very careful with certain foods. It was also during this time that I started to take the train to downtown Chicago to go to UIC. Each was was over an hour and I would sleep both ways in an awkward position and when my neck finally went bad and I was told I had degenerative disc disease I thought the jarring of the train ride was the cause. This is another issue that persisted and got especially bad later in my story. I was still dealing with depression and insomnia but they were getting worse.

After college I got a job in a bank staring at a computer screen and hated every moment of it. My health issues were continuing to get worse. Finally, I decided I had enough and convinced my wife that we should move to AZ with no job, no money, and a slight hope of using online schooling to get teaching degrees. In AZ my issues persisted but maintained a level of not progressing except for digestion. One new thing popped up and I spent a lot of time in eurologist's offices and one told me I had cystic Fibrosis while another told me I had interstitial cystitis. I had a camera put into a different hole during this time but my bladder seemed ok and eventually I decided it was connected to my worsening digestion. Eventually I succeded at pulling off my plan and I became a high school teacher. My room had no windows and was right next to the computer lab. My neck issue got worse and I actually ended up on pain meds and ativan to keep the pain down. I spent 3 years doing this before they moved me to an antidepressant (not for depression) that was knows to help with fibromyalgia as this seemed to be the only thing that explained the existence of all my issues including the growing fatigue and dependence on caffeine. During this time we moved to a new house and the people in the house before us were not healthy. One of them suffered from a sever respiratory disease and was on oxygen. There was a leaky water heater that had created a massive mold buildup we didn't find for a few years. My mother would come visit every weekend to see her grand kids and it was around this time that she was diagnosed with cancer. One doctor told her it was from the mold exposure of the house.

One day I got fed up and joined the gym. I started working out and eating a little bit better and taking natural supplements and succeeded in removing all meds from my life within one year. I still had some neck pain and the digestion was still a mess but the depression and neck pain were minimized and I was sleeping better but I started waking up in the middle of the night to use the restroom 3-4 times a night (I assumed this was from my new water intake). These changes were all in response to loosing my mother and slowly becoming a food fanatic that believed all my issues resulted in diet and that they could all be cured with a supplement. We still lived in the "problem house" and I had moved from traditional cigarettes to an electronic one (I assume this was not a great switch based on what I am learning about EMF). I also switched jobs and became a District Manager for a company and spent most of my days driving from location to location and working with my assigned locations in their offices. I was taking massive amounts of supplements including ginseng and maca for the CFS and MSM for the joint pain.

I eventually succeeded in quitting smoking all together but about 3 months after quitting, I developed massive brain fog that has persisted to this day. I thought I was the healthiest I have been buy this issue came along and I have tried everything (and every diet) to cure it but there has been no success.

I have always been suspicious of this house, especially since my mother passed, but no one (besides my wife) really felt it. My wife has always been suspicious of it too but we live in the foothills of a mountain and we are on the edge of cell phone tower range (our phones don't work in our house or neighborhood unless you know were to stand).

Current supplements:
Niagen 250 in the morning
Pterostilbene 100 mg in the morning
Zinc Picolinate 20 mg in the morning
C0Q10 100 mg in the morning
Ashwaghanda 1200 mg before bed
Gotu Kola 1400 mg before bed

Diets:
Most recently trying ketogenic but I have tried everyone you can think of including GAPS, Paleo, and even just eating no diet and seeing if there was a change...there never is and I feel the same on all diets...the only thing that changes is the size of my belly.

I am 6 ft and 195 lbs (muscular..not fat...except for belly).

Hopefully I didn't bore you with too much info...

### JohnnyAZ — 2017-04-28T07:43:21+0300

I would also like to mention I spend a lot of my time listening to Jack's podcasts and reading his material and getting great advice on the forum. I am currently trying to make sense of leptin and I just subscribed to be a gold member so I can start working through the webinars.

Not boring at all! great detail - all necessary to be a great detective.

You said something new here ......You have poor cell phone coverage - BINGO!

Your phones are always searching for a signal! keep them in airplane mode.

Dr. K. gave himself bell's palsy on a live Q&A one night. His property had no cell coverage so he would drive to the nearest road to talk to us. The details are somewhere.....

I was trying to learn as much as I could about it all but I am starting to feel I should divide and conquer. I have adjusted a lot of my routine for sleeping and being at home.

### JohnnyAZ — 2018-05-09T20:00:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/johnnys-journal-of-brain-fog.19391/post-240911>

Been awhile since I posted in here but in the 1/16 QA Jack mentions the need to keep updating these.

I have come to a new concept. I originally thought I was born of a bad egg but I am not sure that is the case. I think that may be more my sisters issue as my mother was still very healthy when I was born. I think I slowly polluted myself over time because since I was 10 I have slept with the TV on for the entire night. I became almost dependent on this and you know how Netflix and Hulu ask if you are still there? Well before I found this site I used to wake up during these times and have to restart the show. If cable or internet was out, I was unable to sleep. If I am understanding the material, this kept me producing cortisol all night which means I had no melatonin.

It has taken months but I am now sleeping without TV thanks to a few hacks I learned from this site. I think my issue may be more due to this 28 year habit than it was to my mothers egg. Maybe it was both.

# Thread: Alergic Reaction to Seafood from Smell?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alergic-reaction-to-seafood-from-smell.19393/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Huck — 2017-04-28T19:24:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alergic-reaction-to-seafood-from-smell.19393/post-213051>

A couple of years ago I had either an allergic reaction or more likely, histamine reaction, to shrimp and cod. I have eaten both for years and never had a problem prior to this.

I cooked cod today for lunch and felt my left eye starting to swell up. This was while I was cooking, not eating. I immediately washed my hands and flushed my eyes with cold water. That seemed to resolve issue.

The obvious answer is that I touched the fish and then touched my eye, but I was careful to avoid touching my face while cooking. So that got me wondering. could it be that when I put pepper on seafood or fish, that histamine is released and the smell of pepper acts as the carrier?
