Hi ToddC
I'm also doing the Endmyopia course, although not very comprehensively tbh. Theres so much else to do! I took my diopters down 0.5 so at least I'm trying to strengthen my muscles and not be reliant on such strong lenses. Who knows if its working, but I'm guessing its better than using the stronger ones. I also know I'm not helping my eyes by seeing the blur so much, but I can't do everything.
Well done you on reducing your prescription. What do you think that is from? I am focussing on the natural light on my eyes and blue light protection at night. Again I know this makes my vision trickier at night, I'm sure this is not the EM protocol, but I can't do it all and protecting from blue light is my Number 1.

I am unable to take the UV out of my glasses as I am -11.5 so no chance apparently at that strength. Good luck in finding some though. I'll let you know if I find any.

### ToddC — 2018-07-10T18:17:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/myopia-partially-reversed.19397/post-245542>

> [Penny said:](/goto/post?id=244789)
>
> What does that mean "I know I'm not helping my diopters by seeing in a blur for a good portion of each day?" I just take them off most of the day unless I'm reading on a computer - I have fantastic close vision though -

This is the way I understand it:
If all the eyes see is blur, they adapt to the blur eventually, and make it the new normal. This is called blur adaptation. If eyesight is overcorrected with 20/15 correction, or 20/20 correction in extended close-up situations (like computer work), then hyperopic defocus occurs, which means the eyeballs elongate to meet the new normal, and diopters increase. When we undercorrect our prescription so that distance vision is both a bit clear and a bit blurry (like 20/30 acuity), then this type of stimulus gives the brain a chance to make the image clearer. If we actively try to clear a slightly blurry image, we can (with practice) make it clear. When the new clarity starts to stick, and becomes the new normal, then we can reduce our diopters a little bit. The idea is to reduce .25 diopter at a time, mostly keeping our distance vision at the edge of clarity. Ourselves and our brain will get the stimulus it needs to always move towards clarity, instead of blur.
I have a pair of glasses for distance vision, and a pair of glasses for close-up vision. The close-up prescription is reduced to where the text is at the edge of blur and clarity when I'm seated at an ergonomically comfortable position from the computer screen.
I also spend time going without glasses at all, especially the first hour of the day. I awake at dawn and go outside to greet the rising sun. I don't want my glasses to interfere with the nourishing morning light, and it's also good for our brain to know how much blur remains. The rest of the day I just go with the flow. Sometimes I'm going without glasses, and sometimes I am. It depends on how I feel. It's a balancing act between getting the clarity we need to improve eyesight and getting the uninterrupted sunlight we need to nourish our bodies. Right now, I'm trying to get at least 2 hours of natural light exposure during the day without glasses.
Hope this helps, Penny. If not, try going over to endmyopia.org. That should help for sure!

### ToddC — 2018-07-10T18:52:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/myopia-partially-reversed.19397/post-245549>

> [Emma Sabin said:](/goto/post?id=244970)
>
> Well done you on reducing your prescription. What do you think that is from? I am focussing on the natural light on my eyes and blue light protection at night. Again I know this makes my vision trickier at night, I'm sure this is not the EM protocol, but I can't do it all and protecting from blue light is my Number 1.

Protecting from blue light is also my Number 1. In order to both improve eyesight and protect from blue light, I had to change my lifestyle a bit. I realized that I was doing lots of close-up after sunset. I would catch up on emails and read books mostly after dinner time. With protecting from blue light, we only use amber incandescant light bulbs or candle light at night. This dim lighting is not good for reading (especially when trying to improve eyesight), and computers are not good for protecting against blue light, flux or no flux. So, I don't read or do any computer work after sunset anymore. Dim lighting like candlelight is better for distance and peripheral vision. Daytime is for focusing on the fine details. My favorite evening activity is telling stories and singing songs around a campfire. My goal is to do more of that. As well as, star-gazing!
The first eyeglass prescription we tend to have is from ciliary spasm, which is about -1.25 diopter prescription. After that, any increase in prescription is usually from hyperopic defocus. I think the improvements I have made are from correcting the initial ciliary spasm. Correcting the hyperopic defocus is where, I think, following Jake's advice at endmyopia.org is most helpful.

I was doing the Bates method and he said the more dim light you read by, the better... ? But JK doesn't like reading by a red light and says if you do it chronically you can get a cataract - I think this is due to the flicker effect which speeds up ubiquitination in your eye - I think I have to kiss reading at night goodbye... every fake light flickers - I suppose even the UV lights flicker - even though they seem to make \*tons\* of serotonin -

Such a mine field we walk...

# Thread: My Journal - hormonal imbalances and low blood pressure
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-hormonal-imbalances-and-low-blood-pressure.19398/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sun-sybarite — 2017-04-29T10:15:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-hormonal-imbalances-and-low-blood-pressure.19398/post-213063>

I'm a 39 yr old woman living in south India ( Latitude: 12.9716° N, altitude:920 m). I am lucky for the good health I have , I love my body and try taking good care of it because it does its best for me.The things in this blog resonate with me and I also like some of the technical stuff bcos I'm a bit of a science geek. It has also helped my myopia.
At the moment I have two problems that I am grappling with:

**Hormonal Imbalance:
**
From childhood I have faced debilitating tonsilitis infections almost everymonth that took away a large part of my energy and I was a runt. Didn't have much energy for life or sports. I remember my teacher called me lazy, but I wasn't I did the best I could but at times couldn't help feeling apathetic.
At 16 I developed PCOS - fatigue, acanthosis nigricans,hair falling in clumps, weight gain, irregular cycles etc. Ayurveda controlled this for a while. My pregnancy was aided by fertility treatments but after that I stopped treating my PCOS as there seemed no permanent cure.
A few years back (maybe due to working in a night shift) my hormones began to go crazy. I was amenorrhic for 18 months, my thyroid was low, I ballooned in weight which won't go away - it seems like low DHEA and HGH from the symptoms I understand - hair falling in clumps till I am almost bald, extreme fatigue(like tired and bed ridden all day), terrible memory, confused thinking, sagging flabby skin, even if I get sun for a day the sunburn takes months vanish ,excessive sweating, thirst which just won't go away.

Worse when I exercise, my bones and joints(knee, lower back ) hurt, so beyond gentle walks I don't do much. my bones also feel weak and easily breakable. Maybe osteoporosis, which a lot of women in my family have.

With the help of Ayurveda Panchakarma - and changing to a job with proper timings - I was able to bring my thyroid and my cycle back to normal - this was over a period of 18-20 months. But my other hormones it is not easy to find a doctor for this here. Endocrinologists only look at diabetes or thyroid or serious diseases if anything else they send women patients to gynacologists. All my gyno said ( after a scan) that I still had PCOS and she told me to check my Vit D levels. She prescribed me D chriro and myo inositol. She wouldn't even take me seriously when I asked about DHEA and HGH.
At the gyno clinic they seem more focused towards fertility treatments as that is quite a lucrative business these days. I am looking for a place here where they check the complete panel of hormones for women, maybe I will have good news soon.

My lab test results:
Haemoglobin:12.8 (Normal Range(NR): 11-16)
T3:0.82 (NR:0.60-1.81)
T4:8.90 (NR:5.01-12.45)
TSH:3.86 (NR:0.35-5.50)
Serum calcium:9.00 (NR: 8-10.5)
Fasting blood sugar:87 mg/dL ( NR:70-100)
Cholestrol total:156 (NR:<200)
Triglycerides:96 (NR:<150)
HDL :46 (NR:>50)
LDL:90.80 (NR:<100)
VLDL :19.20 (< 30)
Ejection Fraction :60 %

**Low Blood Pressure**:

Another problem I have developed in past 2 yrs is low blood pressure/fast heart rate. I don't know from where this came. Most times my BP is 90/60 and the pulse was 85 yesterday.On the net some articles say this can be the beginning of autoimmune disorders which is a bit confusing and scary. I have tried detoxing with sodium thiosulphate, methylene blue, homeopathy, maca root. On these supplements/meds, my diastolic BP gets pushed up artificially - it goes upto 100-87 or 98-85 - and gives me a headache and nausea.

It is so strange that only the diastolic goes up but the systolic remains unchanged. Has anyone here faced this? Or even know why? I read here somewhere that the diastolic is the pressure the blood pushing against the walls of the vessels. But then what is the systolic?

Low blood pressure also interferes with other medications. Like I tried evening primrose oil for my horrible hairfall to help with the DHT( bcos of the PCOS) but I got palpitations, chest pain.sweatung. I checked it out at the cardiologist. They checked my calcium, troponin, did a heart scan, ECG and everything was okay. The doctor said that there were minor abnormalities in the ECG and to visit after few months but after 6 months it was fine at the next checkup. So i believe it is not my heart but something else.

Doctors here don't take low BP seriously. They just told me to have regular meals and lots of salt. But I want to know the reason for this, blood pressure seems a very mysterious subject. Also when I sit in the sun now, I feel worse, my BP gets even more low and I get a headache all day. So I can't be in anything except very early morning sun and not long.

The low BP makes the fatigue because of my hormones worse. I used to sleep for 9-12 hrs( love my sleep!!) but now only 7 hours and wake up with palpitations. I used to fast one day a month but now get palpitations even if I miss a meal and pain in the chest area. Chest muscles feel weak and not able to life heavy stuff, esp. with left arm. Confusion in thinking.
Only thing which makes me better is vitamin C. I take it as sodium ascorabate, The more I take the better I feel. I don't take any other medication.

I've stopped using my mobile phone, wifi is off at night. There is population and technological density here but I don't watch TV and I want to improve my eyesight and have( see my [post](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/myopia-partially-reversed.19397/) on this). I eat healthy- lots of steamed veggies, low quantity of carbs - though I can't seem to burn fat - maybe bcos of low DHEA, Progesterone due to the PCOS. I am a vegetarian, but after reading Dr.K's stuff do try to eat fish once a week, and eggs too. I am trying to quit vegetable oils and switch to coconut oil - its easily available here and fresh too - but get chest pain if I take too much coconut oil and put on even more weight. Again maybe problem burning fats?

So my next step is to find a good doctor to deal with the hormonal imbalance. The myo/D-chiro-inositol the gyno gave for PCOS caused me severe gastritis and burning sensation in my food pipe for days which was unbearable. And I need to get my other hormones tested as well.

As for the low BP, I am all out of ideas. Maybe once my hormones are in balance I can try for ketogenic diet. I have read of others trying it with some success and even having fun with it. It might help my poor confused brain.
If anyone else has faced unexplainable low blood pressure like this - or would like to share anything that might help or that is interesting - I would love to hear their thoughts/their journey/their story!

What type of water do you drink? Do you CT in some way? Any grounding?

>

@[WalterNL](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19189/) Thank you! This is a city of millions but residential parks do offer some breathing space. A few times in a week. I try to go larger parks which are not surrounded by establishments which have EMF. I will try and see if there are any completely signal free places close to nature that is easily accesible - something that you can go at least weekly. That is a good idea thanks!

I live in an apartment , its located on the third floor. I also have a roof top garden where I get sun everyday. I ground everyday especially after spending time on the computer(for the job)- I feel like I was a battery it just sucked the energy from. I ground for an hour and feel human again, I could do it all day and not eat anything! Even if I'm hungry if I ground the hunger goes away, its like the earth feeds my whole body. Btw, I lie down on the ground wearing minimal clothing - just keeping my feet on the ground does nothing for me. Since I can't be in the sun much( as it makes me dizzy and my BP lower) I do earthing more nowadays.

For some reason I never could bear white lights on my skin, even before I read Dr.Kruse's blog. So most of my lights are orange/yellow lights(LED, tubelights) and the remaining I plan to switch to yellow too. In my home after 7pm we use only ambient lights, so no problem. Only exception is my school going kid who needs strong white light for studying - I don't know what to do for her. She also spends so much time on the computer, I am getting her blue blocking glasses.

Unfortunately since I have prescription glasses with high power, I am not able to wear blue blockers.

Indians use turmeric all the time, every meal 3/4 times a day, so its all good. And yes, definitely I eat only the local/seasonal produce, its more accesible and fresher. I eat an egg everyday bcos its a good source of vitamins and someone here also said of glutathione. I like it now and even crave it(so weird!). I will think about other animal products maybe chicken. I was thinking of gelatin because I hear its good for the bones.

I also want to do something for my muscles , they are so weak, even sitting up straight for more than a half hour make my back muscles hurt something fierce. I am trying to do exercises with small weights and they help. But I wonder if this is normal to have such weak muscles...I can't lift anything too heavy as well. Looking back to my teens , when compared to my peers I always seem to have had less stamina, able to lift lesser weights.....wonder why.

As for water I boil it and put a small leaf of holy basil it it, let it cool down and drink it - I like it this way. Some Indians drink it this way in copper vessels, but I don't use copper. I was also thinking about heating the water in the sun....

For CT, I know Dr.K said that pathway is there for all mammals but IDK, I've never thought about doing it. If you have low hormones, like low progesterone, will CT work for you?

I went and checked out the CT page and maybe I could try the face dunking for 2 weeks, its hot enough here and it might help my sleep!

Thanks again for the welcome and your time @WalterNL

### WalterNL — 2017-05-01T11:33:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-hormonal-imbalances-and-low-blood-pressure.19398/post-213145>

Well you're looking in the right direction. That city will be a problem though, within an appartment building is usually impossible to get away from nnEMF, and cities have a lot of such buildings. If you can't get well even while following all protocols on this site, the problem is most likely the city. If you can afford it there are magnetic pads to improve sleep, your body has to experience a magnetic field at night for optimal sleep.

The water you use is tap water? If so, do you know if it's clean enough i.e. doesn't contain fluoride? It's important to drink water with the least amount of fluoride. The water should be cold too. You can install f.lux on your computer to limit blue light exposure. Face dunks are fine to start CT, getting cold helps me sleep much better. Yes gelatin is good stuff, you can make bone broths from animal or fish bones.

There are also blue bockers (from UVEX) you can wear over your normal glasses.

These pages have more general tips: [https://www.jackkruse.com/redox-rx/](https://www.jackkruse.com/redox-rx/) [https://www.jackkruse.com/paleo-rx-easy-start-guide/](https://www.jackkruse.com/paleo-rx-easy-start-guide/) [https://www.jackkruse.com/organizational-structural-failure-6-mitochondrial-rx/](https://www.jackkruse.com/organizational-structural-failure-6-mitochondrial-rx/)

### Sun-sybarite — 2017-05-03T18:20:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-hormonal-imbalances-and-low-blood-pressure.19398/post-213301>

Monday was a good start to the week. I tried out a fish stew(mackerel) recipe and it came out well. I also did face dunking; it was refreshing in the heat. It was for 30 intervals of 30 secs each after dinner. When I woke the next day I had slept for 9 and a half hours! I was so happy bcos - me and my sleep, we're kind of attached to each other,lol. Due to my BP I hadn't been able to sleep for longer than 7 hrs for over a year and I had missed sleeping longer. Also my skin felt good - soft and firm.
I guess I felt good, so I went out midday, got a lot of hot Indian sun, for around 12noon to 3 pm. I was able to handle it. But after work was too tired to do the facedunking, earthed maybe 20 mins. Today I woke up with a debilitating headache, nausea- couldn't keep even a mouthful of water down. The pounding in my head was unbearable and seemed to echo my pulse so I checked my BP and it was high 130-90 and over the hour it rose to 140-97! I was surprised, I had never had high BP before, it was usually ranged around 90/60 to 100/70.
I think my blood pressure centre in my brain( or wherever) is going haywire. I went to my GP and she gave me something for high BP. I slept in a dark room after taking that and only now feel human. Have no idea what brought that on. But I still want to do face dunking today it feels soothing and refreshing for the mild headache I still have.

Hi @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/), thanks for all the links, I was poring over the steroid pathway pdf and trying to understand it, some of it made sense but a lot went over my head unfortunately.

Thanks also for all the useful advice, yes I will try to take lots of sun - already get morning sun, but noon sun?? Here its like 38 Celsius - 100 Fahrenheit - and it only gets hotter after noon. I can try and get some in say at 10 am. Yes, I do get sulphur and beta carotene foods. Right now I eat eggs with very orange yolks - so maybe thats beta carotene and cholestrol? Also I crave onions. People here also drink Aloe Vera juice -say 15 ml a - day for some months of the year. Its supposed to have lots of MSM(sulphur) I think. When I drink it my skin looks so good! Its available in plenty here and I also grow it. Maybe I will try that also.

It makes sense Myopia is a recent disease. Sometimes I wondered what early man did when he was shortsighted before glasses were invented, lol, but now I know this was a disease of lack of sunlight in the eye. When I developed myopia , it was more from less sunlight in eye in combination with reading lots of (paper) books with fine print. It was the lack of sun plus eyestrain that did me in, IMO.

JanSZ, I have posted the normal ranges( as well as my latitude) with my labs.I'm sorry I didn't post the normal ranges earlier. At least 3 doctors had a look at my thyroid tests and confirmed my thyroids were normal. Can you please have a look again, with the ranges and let me know if you still think they're below par? Also the same thing for cholestrol.

It is very difficult to get bio identical hormones here without a prescription. They're usually prescribed for menopausal women. I have been talking to a doctor attached to a compounding pharmacy here and she has asked me to send all my reports which I will have to do tomorrow. They have hormone panel tests and bioidentical hormones, so hopefully something will work out.

With the way I'm ballooning - I look obese though I weigh like 60 kg - it would be grossly unfair if my cholestrol was too low,lol! Just my luck - instead of using it for my good hormones, my body is using it to make visceral fat, or whatever aargh . I will ask my doctor about the fatty acids test and the mineral tests too @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/). Tests here are available and affordable, its only that all of them need a doctor to prescribe them for you. There are exceptions like [preventative](https://www.thyrocare.com/wellness/Emailer/emailer1.aspx?enc=bYH3u1NA92Gem1tKShNZqcZzVF7430XUwMbz5j0QMRX8LNcGxwhUG/fCPsE2IA4tM2VAvj0jXkCL7kiibqjXTQ====GxwhUG/fCPsE2IA4tM2V&enc2=Rxhjgsdhf%20%20&val=UFVCTElD) health checkups, but they're tests which are prepackaged together and I don't think you can add on to those.

Yes, I read that about living near the equator and not needing that much DHA.Thats good because I wasn't having much luck convincing my kid to try fish, she didn't seem that much enamored with the smell! So can I give her those codliver capsules then? My HDL is low( it appears that a lot of women with PCOS have low HDL) but the truth is we eat a lot of Omega 6 oils and practically no Omega3/9. Some people with heart conditions do try and include flaxseed but that is not DHA, I think.

Thanks again for all the detailed suggestions and your time; you've given me a lot to think about/work on!

### JanSz — 2017-05-04T15:02:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-hormonal-imbalances-and-low-blood-pressure.19398/post-213354>

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-2#post-113774](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-2#post-113774)

**Jack Kruse's way to optimize labs.**

[http://jackkruse.com/what-are-the-optimizing-labs/](http://jackkruse.com/what-are-the-optimizing-labs/)

**What Are The Optimizing Labs?**
**August 26,** **2011** By [Jack Kruse](http://jackkruse.com/author/JackKruse/) [Comments (100)](http://jackkruse.com/what-are-the-optimizing-labs/#idc-container)

-   [Facebook26](http://jackkruse.com/what-are-the-optimizing-labs/?share=facebook&nb=1)
-   [Twitter3](http://jackkruse.com/what-are-the-optimizing-labs/?share=twitter&nb=1)
-   [LinkedIn](http://jackkruse.com/what-are-the-optimizing-labs/?share=linkedin&nb=1)
-   [Google](http://jackkruse.com/what-are-the-optimizing-labs/?share=google-plus-1&nb=1)
-   [More](http://jackkruse.com/what-are-the-optimizing-labs/#)

  [](http://jackkruse.com/what-are-the-optimizing-labs/)

**Readers Summary**

1.  What labs might I consider with my healthcare provider?
2.  How do I start?
3.  Is the quantified self platform of testing a commitment?
4.  How long should it take before I become rockstarish?
5.  The secret sauce is not the lab data but in how they are interpreted!

When you finally decide to take total control of you life and optimize yourself I always suggest testing. Many of you have bombarded my email and my [twitter account](https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse) for a blog to a list of those labs. Well, today’s post is for you. I was resistant to do this because I felt having the list of labs is a waste of time if you don’t have a physician who can decipher what they all mean for you. 7 years ago, I could not tell you what this group of labs meant at all to your optimal health. I learned it by reading and going to classes to optimize myself. After much reflection I have decided to give you what you asked for. I am fortunate because many of the PCP’s I work with understand these tests well. I may also update it as I think it needs to be updated over time.

The first set of labs are what I call the core lab set for optimization. There are other panels I add to the core lab based upon the history, physical, and the food logs I will have patients give me. This blog is meant to be a resource for you to refer back to when the need arises. Discuss these with your doctor but don’t assume they will run out to order them because they may not know what all these tests will tell them. That part takes some time. I recommend starting the dialogue with your doctor to assess their willingness to help you. Most of my PCP doctors are awesome “helpers” to their patients with these issues. This requires a lot of work on the doctors part and the patients part. Do not be surprised if this is not covered by insurance. The time required to optimize someone is unreal. I know how long I spent optimizing myself 6 years ago. It was a tedious process but I was not going to give up because I believed my life depended upon it. For most people, 2-3 years you can expect the changes you want. If you are really in tough shape it may take longer but that should not deter you. I love patients who bring big challenges. They are the most appreciative patients I have had in the last 5 years. We can never settle for a C or D when an A is possible.

**Lab Panels**

1.  VO2 Max assessment (cardio pulmonary findings lead to a work up if needed.)
2.  Initial visit DEXA with body comp. Optimization a DEXA scan to include body composition scores.
    F/U visits I use BIA assessments to monitor progress and limit radiation exposure.
3.  Neuro cognitive battery of tests to assess processing speed, response speed, memory , learning, and attention repeated annually.
4.  Chemistry 20
5.  Liver function testing
6.  Serum Iron, TIBC, Serum Ferritin and Iron % Saturation
7.  Lipid panel: VAP and/or NMR analysis
8.  Homocysteine level

9.  Complete CBC with differential and platelet count (sometimes an Ivy bleeding time)
10.  Hormone panel includes (gender specific)……serum and salivary cortisol, Total testosterone, Free testosterone, % Free T, DHT, LH, FSH, Estradiol (E2) high sensitivity, DHEA-S, PSA, IGF-1, Consider based upon history testing levels of progesterone, pregnenolone, melatonin, serum and urine Osmolarity, Prolactin levels
11.  Complete thyroid panel with antibody screening (TPO etc)
12.  Complete Urinalysis (women get beta HCG if they menstruate)
13.  Highly Sensitive CRP (Not a regular CRP)
14.  Complete Vitamin D panel

15.  Omega 6/3 serum test (for severe findings consider tissue assay)
16.  Fasting serum insulin and HbA1c assessment
17.  No panel is complete without an ASI and salivary melatonin level. Read Brain Gut 11 to understand why.

The next set of labs are considered add ons if the patients’ history or physical require it being looked at.

**Cancer history of colon, breast, ovary**

1.  CEA for colon cancer
2.  CA 125 for ovarian cancer
3.  CA 27.29 for breast cancer
4.  AFP blood test
5.  CA125, 15-3, 27.29, 19.9,
6.  BRCA 1 and 2 screen

**Previous heart disease**

1.  Apo A-1 and B
2.  B Natriurietic Factor
3.  LpA status, Lp-PLA2
4.  CIMT
5.  Calcium Index Score

**Bone pathology**

1.  Osteocalcin frozen blood analysis
2.  Se blood test
3.  RBC blood testing for Mg
4.  Deoxypyridinoline (DPD) Cross Link Urine Test
5.  Parathyroid Hormone assay

**Inflammatory/autoimmune panels**

1.  EBV panel
2.  ANA panel
3.  Complete Cytokine Assay
4.  Candida Antibodies
5.  Helicobacter IgG panel
6.  Hep A, B and C titers
7.  Breakout of Cytokine panel
8.  ESR
9.  RA factor
10.  SLE screen
11.  T Lymphocyte helper suppressor assay
12.  IL 8, IL 1 B or TNF alpha analysis

I also think you need to have a thorough history and physical exam. You need to retest on a quarterly or biannual basis to get feedback on your lifestyle and regimen to get things right over time.

Once this is all correlated, you come up with a customized dietary plan, a medication/supplement plan, and exercise plan, basically a game plan for things you might consider altering for optimal health. It should be a collaboration between you and your doctor and your other health care providers.

This is a very labor intensive task for both patient and doctor. But if you remain persistent and consistent and your relationship with your physician congruent and always focusing in on your health you can reach any goal you have for yourself. It must be adaptable but yet have some core values to it to get you to the promised land. The secret sauce is not the lab list; it is found in the interpretation of those labs and how they correlate with your epigenetic switches. That is what determines how you partition calories and what diet and exercise program are best for your current biology. You should expect this to change over time as you become optimized. Nothing stays they same in biology. I learned this on my own journey and it has proven true in those I have helped re-engineer themselves back to optimal. Enjoy!

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-58#post-204931](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-58#post-204931)

[http://blog.bodybio.com/download/why-41-ratio-oil/?wpdmdl=1268](http://blog.bodybio.com/download/why-41-ratio-oil/?wpdmdl=1268)

The membrane is the outside skin that surrounds every cell,
however, there is much more membrane on the inside of
the cell, actually ten times more, surrounding all the tiny
organelles inside the cell.

**Cholesterol and Arachidonic Acid**
Two Vital
FATS
That Get No Respect

Cholesterol is not only a vital cellular molecule, it is also a large part of us, as it occupies 30-40% of our membrane (Gurr 2002 6). It is the precursor for vital hormones such as the adrenals, our fight or flight hormones, and the gonads, hormones that drive our reproductivemachinery. Cholesterol is important for the metabolism of the fat soluble vitamins, A, D, E and K, and is a precursor for bile acids which manage our fatty acid intake from the gut to the cells. It is intimately involved in regulating membrane fluidity over a wide range of temperatures,as well as creating a strong membrane structure, which,incidentally, equates to a strong overall metabolism. Most individuals with high cholesterol have a strong metabolism--- and they know it.
Very little appears to impact one with **high cholesterol**, like airborne disturbances such as pollen with its potential for allergy, etc. “When present at high concentrations, cholesterol enhances the mechanical strength of the membrane, reduces its permeability, and suppresses the main-phase
transition of the lipid bilayer” (McMullen 19967).

autistic spectrum disorder
cognitive abilities
Cholesterol is an essential element of myelin, the insulating material crucial for nerve function in the brain and central nervous system.
hypo-cholesterolemia is common among tuberculous patients

**How to raise Total Cholesterol?**
[http://blog.bodybio.com/download/why-41-ratio-oil/?wpdmdl=1268](http://blog.bodybio.com/download/why-41-ratio-oil/?wpdmdl=1268)
****How to raise Total Cholesterol?****
****How to raise Total Cholesterol?****
**“Women who are having a difficult time becoming pregnant should first look at their cholesterol level as it is invariably low.** As the diet is expanded to include essential fatty acids and phosphatidylcholine, the hormones derived from cholesterol normalize and pregnancy can more
easily follow, and often does.
Similarly, patients with environmental illness almost always have low cholesterol including those with sensitivity to foods, chemicals, or even frequencies, like Wi-fi , which are commonly due to
the instability of the cell membrane. One patient complained that he had developed such a severe sensitivity to Wi-fi frequency that he could not travel or go into many business settings. His lab analyses revealed that he had a very low cholesterol level with a low Total Lipid Content
within his red cells. The patient found relief from his symptoms **after receiving high dosing with**
**IV phosphatidylcholine and a diet high in eggs, butter, wild salmon and balanced essential fatty acid supplementation.”**
**\----**
****Personally, I would first do fatty acids analysis and then decide about salmon.****

****about lowering cholesterol'****
****Life is a balancing act. There****
****are a large number of studies on the effectiveness of lowering****
****cholesterol by using the primary parent EFAs, the****
****omega-6 linoleic, and omega-3 à-linolenic.****

### Sun-sybarite — 2017-05-08T23:57:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-hormonal-imbalances-and-low-blood-pressure.19398/post-213689>

Wow, JanSz, thank you from the bottom of my heart for caring and taking your time to help me ( and I'm sure many others as well). I am meeting my doctor this week and was feeling a bit down as the consultation costs were high (higher than even the cardiologist) and I had already spent so much on doctors and endoscopies, scans and alternative treatments with no clear answers or solution. But seeing all this and Dr.K's pages I feel totally energised and hopeful that with all the information and guidance here I will be able to make sense and solve the problem.

I am taking down notes so that I can ask the doctor some of these things. Hope the doctor will prove a bit open minded!

Oh and also I paint myself with Iodine tincture everyday( alternating abdomen or chest). It gakes a while to absorb so I don't think I am completely deficient. There is Chlorine in our bathing water plus Iodine is good for PCOS so I do that. i don't think this Lugol's solution is available in India. I will ask in the pharmacy and see. Selenium is available as a part of a multimineral capsule, I also take Shilajit(fulvic acid) and have some orange sea salt in my cucumber juice and have lots of garlic.

### Sun-sybarite — 2017-05-09T00:07:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-hormonal-imbalances-and-low-blood-pressure.19398/post-213692>

> [WalterNL said:](/goto/post?id=213145)
>
> Well you're looking in the right direction. That city will be a problem though, within an appartment building is usually impossible to get away from nnEMF, and cities have a lot of such buildings. If you can't get well even while following all protocols on this site, the problem is most likely the city. If you can afford it there are magnetic pads to improve sleep, your body has to experience a magnetic field at night for optimal sleep.
>
> The water you use is tap water? If so, do you know if it's clean enough i.e. doesn't contain fluoride? It's important to drink water with the least amount of fluoride. The water should be cold too. You can install f.lux on your computer to limit blue light exposure. Face dunks are fine to start CT, getting cold helps me sleep much better. Yes gelatin is good stuff, you can make bone broths from animal or fish bones.
>
> There are also blue bockers (from UVEX) you can wear over your normal glasses.
>
> These pages have more general tips: [https://www.jackkruse.com/redox-rx/](https://www.jackkruse.com/redox-rx/) [https://www.jackkruse.com/paleo-rx-easy-start-guide/](https://www.jackkruse.com/paleo-rx-easy-start-guide/) [https://www.jackkruse.com/organizational-structural-failure-6-mitochondrial-rx/](https://www.jackkruse.com/organizational-structural-failure-6-mitochondrial-rx/)

You are right there are lots of such buildings in the city. Before this I live in detached houses on the ground level but still had many wi-fi around. In this building now for the last few months I've noticed at night all of them switch off their routers so no wifi signal. I don't know why. Its a small apartment building not too overcrowded maybe 20 tenants.

That still leaves cell tower signals though. I have bad cell reception in my room which I always used to get irritated about as I had to crack open the window to make or receive a call. But maybe thats a blessing,lol. I have asked a couple of companies for quotes on anti-EMF window curtains as it seems to me most of the signals comes in through them. Apparently they also sell anti-Radiation bed canopies - suggested for pregnant women - which maybe can consider down the road.

@[WalterNL](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19189/), thank you for bringing this to my attention about the water! I think I am what you call late to the party. I researched the water quality here and it appears we have some excess fluoride, nitrates and arsenic in drinking water. Totally shocked, and feel a bit like an ignoramus. I was only worried about the chlorine( as we can smell it) but had no awareness of the other pollutants. It appears clean drinking water is a bit of a myth Reverse Osmosis systems are not cheap, but I am saving up for it, also I have to convince my family, they are not ones for change especially when it doesn't seem urgent, but I care too much for my health. I am also painting myself regularly with iodine which in retrospect is a good thing.

I had bone broth twice this week, tasted so good.
Have installed flux on the desktop, thanks.

I have ordered the amber glasses to wear over my prescription ones and am excited to try it. Hope no one looks at me weird though, not too many people wear 2 glasses at the same time,lol.

I am doing facedunking, maybe on alternate days( somedays this week I was so exhausted I fell asleep abruptly) before bedtime and getting good sleep. Doing regular earthing. Doing exercise with small weights for posture - I have that forward neck posture going on and weak back muscles.

The magnetico sleep pad sounds interesting but maybe too prohibitive for me given the exchange rates and shipping charges. There are magnetic pads available here too but I don't think they're the same as mentioned by Dr.K.

Its also very hot here, I'm craving lots of fruits. I have maybe one meal a day, eggs, and some fruit and juices - mangos, water melon - the rest of the time. Having butter as it will help my cholestrol.

My blood pressure was weird for a couple of days -around 108-87 - had mild headache.

### Beristere — 2022-06-17T14:34:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-hormonal-imbalances-and-low-blood-pressure.19398/post-313628>

Hormonal problems are often caused by the thyroid gland, which secretes varying amounts of hormones. This problem is more and more common among women, so it is recommended that they give tests at least once a year to check the condition of the thyroid. Low blood pressure is closely related to lifestyle, and of course, it is related to your hormonal instability. Your body cannot interpret these changes, and your heart rate decreases. Patients also face skin problems, which is why [ipl skin rejuvenation](https://aestheticbureau.com.au/ipl-machines) is necessary.

# Thread: Why are some people who live indoors 24/7 not overweight or obviously sick?

**Subforum:** The Cave

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matty_M — 2017-04-30T22:02:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-some-people-who-live-indoors-24-7-not-overweight-or-obviously-sick.19403/post-213120>

A friend brought up a point and I actually was unable to refute it.

Why are so many people who live indoors all the time not overweight? Like tech CEO's; Jeff Bezos is the example he mentioned.

My inclination is to think that because obesity did not manifest, something else will in the future. The outside might look fine, but the inside is not.

Separately, there are times when it's cloudy in Philadelphia and I feel depressed if I'm alone. **But, if I'm with someone having an interesting conversation with someone, it seems to "trump" the negatives of the environment.**

**Could the dopamine boost from conversation/doing something you love override the effects of a bad environment to some extent?** If so, how, and to what extent? Is the dopamine like a credit card, bound to run out eventually?

Thanks!

# Thread: Trying to figure out why I sleep like shit at my house.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-figure-out-why-i-sleep-like-shit-at-my-house.19407/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### RyanMSauce — 2017-05-01T23:24:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-figure-out-why-i-sleep-like-shit-at-my-house.19407/post-213180>

I am having trouble sleeping at my house that I moved into a few months ago. I picked this house because it was relatively low in EMF and the magnetic fields from the power lines is lower than most homes I checked out in the area.

The only negative thing I know about this area is that they have the bad type of smart meters that send a signal out every minute probably. I had mine removed and I'm trying to get my neighbor to do the same.

It seems I can't get a deep nights sleep. Sometimes I can sleep a straight 4 or 5 hours then I wake up and its hard to fall back asleep or I will fall back asleep and toss and turn the rest of the night.

I turn my power off at night to my house and my cell phone is off as well.

As I lay in my room it feels like I have a small headache.

I wake up here and feel like there isn't enough dopamine in my brain. I am trying to get a lot of creative work done and I don't have enough willpower.

I live in phoenix Arizona. I work from home so I am able to get outside a lot.

I know something is up because when I go to California and am sleeping in terrible emf environments with wifi and florescent lights at hotels and hostels I still seem to sleep better and my dopamine comes back. I have more motivation there.

I wear blue blockers at night.

I can't live like this my life is wasting. I have too much to accomplish.

I am going to get a mycotoxin test. Maybe there is mold somewhere. I know I have allergies to all the pollen here.

Any ideas of what else I can test for? Maybe its the smart meters? I have to fix this!

### Inger — 2017-05-02T11:33:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-figure-out-why-i-sleep-like-shit-at-my-house.19407/post-213204>

How much space do you have to your neighbors?

Geopathic stress maybe? Have you tested where you sleep?

### caroline — 2017-05-02T12:03:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-figure-out-why-i-sleep-like-shit-at-my-house.19407/post-213205>

what about hiring an electrical engineer to come over and test everything? Sometimes water pipes can be an issue - sort of cross contamination as I understand it.

Is moving an option?

### RyanMSauce — 2017-05-03T20:02:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-figure-out-why-i-sleep-like-shit-at-my-house.19407/post-213312>

The neighbors are close. But there is no on behind me. There smart meter is 40-50 feet away maybe?
Sometimes my eyes burn if I touch them. Im allergic to something.
I could move its just a huge pain in the ass. I would need to break my lease. I looked a so many places until I found one in an area I like and that didn't have a 1mg field coming from a power line.

### caroline — 2017-05-04T03:53:41+0300

underground wires?

### Jason Coates — 2017-05-04T13:36:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rosemary.19409/post-213347>

In 2 studies I found rosemary raised adiponectin in rats. Would rosemary tea be good with CT?

Carnosic acid (CA) and carnosol (CS) originating from dried rosemary leaves activate Nrf2 pathway \[[21](https://www.hindawi.com/journals/omcl/2013/184598/#B21)\]. These two potent compounds decrease the number of cells containing lipid droplets in 3T3-L1 cells dose-dependently through Nrf2 pathway in the study of Takahashi et al. \[[22](https://www.hindawi.com/journals/omcl/2013/184598/#B22)\].

[https://www.hindawi.com/journals/omcl/2013/184598/](https://www.hindawi.com/journals/omcl/2013/184598/)

[http://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0094687](http://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0094687)
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/24550204](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/24550204)

### Jason Coates — 2017-05-04T13:42:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rosemary.19409/post-213348>

[http://www.news-medical.net/news/2007/10/31/31994.aspx](http://www.news-medical.net/news/2007/10/31/31994.aspx)

### Inger — 2017-05-03T14:42:08+0300

Sleeping naked is awesome...there is no way I could have anything on in the night, huh! Especially the skin to skin contact with a partner all night  I realize it gives me an even more, awesome, super deep and relaxing sleep. After sleeping all night skin to skin I wake up feeling like drugged, from well-being feeling. I have not experienced this as strong as lately.I wonder why it is? Very very nice
Unfortunately we sleep just maybe 2 nights in the week together.. I think I need to work on it being every night! Because something feels very healing about it!

# Thread: Thaddeus Journal Looking to Optimize
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thaddeus-journal-looking-to-optimize.19413/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Thaddeus Owen — 2017-05-02T18:11:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thaddeus-journal-looking-to-optimize.19413/post-213238>

Hello Everyone. It's good to be here.
I've been following Jack for many years and finally at the point where I'm ready to engage and get more involved.

I have been biohacking for years, but took the route of getting too technical and moving away from nature. I recently have come back to the concept of getting better by removing things rather than adding.

I engineered a work from home assignment to be able to better manage a healthy workplace. I spent 10 years in Pharmaceutical R&D and now have spent 10 years keeping toxic chemicals out of furniture as a full time job. By removing myself from the plant and R&D lab, I am able to work remotely and hack my lifestyle and environment for health (but still am missing the tribal component of working alone all day).

I run a company at night with a business partner (ErisFit and PrimalHacker) where we blog about biohacking as we learn our way through this stuff. We intend to make it more accessible to others and ensure people understand the basics and the science, which is one huge reason why we're here!!

I have two boys in elementary school, and I'm currently going through a divorce.

I am looking to clean up as much of my environment for health as possible and use this to teach others how to do the same as well as keep my boys healthy in this crazy environment.

I recently ran a bunch of labs and I think my BUN/creatinin is too high and need some help on how to hack this one. I'm 41 years old.

I live in the suburbs in Minnesota. I turn off the wifi and cell phones at night, have wrapped aluminum around the smart meters on my water and natural gas meters.

I get outside each morning for 5-20 minutes and spend another 20 minutes walking at lunch.

I ground myself in bed at night with an earthing mat plugged into the outlet ground.

I eat higher fat and lower carb but not low carb. 80-190g carbs per day from sweet potatoes. Sometimes I eat soaked oatmeal. I do a BP coffee for breakfast with some protein and sometimes plantain pancakes with butter, no sweetener. Seafood 5 times a week from pacific salmon. Oysters once a week.

Here are some of my numbers I ran last month (APril 2017)
LDL-P 1168
LDL-C 112
HDL-C 49
Triglycerides 74
total cholesterol 176
HDL-p 29.2
Small LDL-P 156
LDL Size 21.1
Large VLDL-P 1.2 nmol/L
Small VLDL-P 156
Large HDL-P 4.5
VLDL size 44.6
LDL size 21.1
HDL Size 8.8
LP-IR score 39

sodium 142
potassium 4.0
chloride 104
co2 27
GLucose 97
Calcium 9.9
Bun 19
creatine 1.08
BUN/creat 18
Free T 10.9
T total 775
LpA 7.71
Homocysteine 8.6
HS CRP 0.35
T4 6.5
T3 total 95
Reverse T3 25
Free T4 1.06
Free T3 2.62
Vit D 65.4
cortisol 15.3
DHEA sulfate 178.3
Insulin 4.1
sex hormone binding 64
estradiol 20.0
ferritin 70.0
TSH 2.6

**Health History** -
Anxiety. I have struggled with this since I was 5. I'm now 41. Mostly have hacked this through EFT, coaching and deep work over the last 4-5 years. About 90% better.

High Blood pressure - sometimes 115/74 and sometimes 135/90 and sometimes as high as 145/94... comes down quickly after deep breathing. This has been coming down lately as well per the anxiety work I have done in the last few years.

**Reynauds** \- this is a big one for me. Getting worse the past year. My fingers and toes turn white and go numb when exposed to cold and take hours, literally hours to get blood flow back. C/T makes this a real issue for me

**No surgeries** ever. No medications. Was on Lexapro (anti anxiety, 10mg) for 2 years (10 years ago). No history of disease.

**Mother -** Anxiety and on anti anxiety meds for last 20 years. Reynauds. No other issues or disease. Perfect health otherwise. Low blood pressure. Very active 67 year old woman.

**Grandmother** - died at 90. Had dementia in her late 80s. Died because she did not want to be alive, no joy in life and wanted to die. She had no history of disease or medical issues until dementia

I **sleep** great. Usually 6-7.5 hours a night. 7.5 is my ideal. I fall asleep quickly and sleep well.

Wifi turned off at night. Phone in airplane mode. Blue light blocking glasses used nightly.

I **travel** for work for 5 days a month (sleep in hotels, fly on an airplane for 1 hour there and back). Sometimes add 1 additional trip to CA with 3.5 hours flying time.

**Physical**
I'm in good physical shape, 8% bodyfat, weight lift 2 times a week using strong first techniques (Squat, bench, deadlift, pull ups), practice Qigong, use kettlebells 5 times a week for a 10 minute warm up each day.

I do not do cardio.

I get out in the sun 5-20 minutes at sunrise daily.

I ground outside from May - October (I live in MN and it's cold outside of those months).

I have a Sunlighten Infrared Sauna acquired a few months ago, use it daily in the winter months.

I cannot currently move zip codes, as my ex-wife is not going to leave this place. So I need hacks for the long MN winter to stay optimal up here (perhaps it's heading down to Mexico and FL a couple times in the winter, using my sauna, using the Redjuvenator light at night and C/T by just stepping outdoors for 5 minutes in the winter..Looking for ideas)

**Libido** is decent, but not great. Although I'm going through a divorce right now, so could be related.

**Water**
Reverse osmosis. Been using this for 10 years. Add oceanic ionic trace minerals to water before drinking.
I also drink Mountain Spring water, carbonated, from Whole Foods - about 1 bottle a day.
WHole house water filter was just installed to remove chlorine from shower and bath.
I shower and do not take baths (Perhaps I'm missing out on something not putting myself in water once a day?)

\================================================================
Grounding to ground in power outlet is always questionable.
You may be better off not using that ground or good checking of it.
\----------

When posting any laboratory tests results it is better to scan each page of report, wrap it into PDF file and attach to post.
Retyping, post name, value, units, laboratory range, name of laboratory
I assume that your have
Vit D 65.4ng/mL
That would be not bad, but goal is to have:
Vitamin D (25-Hydroxyvitamin D)(250nmol/L=100.16ng/mL)
or more.
The problem is that you want that to be made by your own body, using sunlight or UV lamps.
When you see sun in Minnesota for 5 minutes or 20 min/day, that is not going to help.
Sunlight creates vit D,sulfate.
Pills give vit D
two different items.

Your
T total 775 is decent
but
sex hormone binding 64
is too high
that is why your
Free T 10.9
is low
and
Libido is decent, but not great.
Lets assume that it would help when you

get better vit D
homocysteine
and
your temperature under your tongue will be at 98.6F= 37.00C or real close to it --->>(thyroid + cortisol)

Your early morning cortisol =15.3
is not bad, but could be higher.
Better observing of Circadian Rhythm should improve on this.

Your
Free T3 2.62 too low
Reverse T3 25 too high
that
may be due to insufficient
selenium, iodine, potassium iodide
or
you just can't make better thyroid hormones and need to supplement with Natural Thyroid hormones from pigs.

I suggest this first:
1/day-->Super Selenium Complex, 200 mcg 100 capsules (lef.org)

# Thread: Trying leptin reset- wondering how broken I am...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-leptin-reset-wondering-how-broken-i-am.19414/>

### Lyse — 2017-05-02T21:37:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-leptin-reset-wondering-how-broken-i-am.19414/post-213253>

Hi there everyone. Hi Jack! You are brilliant. I am so glad I found you and your book and this forum! I have a couple questions... wanted to give you all some background on me... was hoping for some answers....

Where to begin... lets see. I was an avid HCG user... I did probably 11 rounds over the course of 5 years. Horrid I know... Homeopathic and RX HCG, Patches, Inhaller, I tried it all. I followed Colleen Coble as my mentor for HCG for years. She helped me get back on course when I started to regain the weight. I kept regaining the 30-40 lbs I tried to keep off which put me at my "ideal weight" of 145. I always made excuses like, I didn't do it right, I added to many things back, I had to many cheat meals etc which is why the weight always came back, even after 9 months of holding it etc. I kept going back to HCG because it was always so easy to knock off 15 lbs in 3 weeks... and do 2 rounds back to back... basically I think I broke myself.

I have tried to lose weight now without HCG by severe low carb/ and crazy amount of exercise and I actually gained weight... I had signed myself up for personal trainer 3x a week and 2x a week HIT class and was literally watching weight come on me... It was horrible. I have had 2 pregnancies but only 1 child. The other was in my tubes and had to be removed via a chemo shot last september 2016. That probably caused even more issues... basically the second I eat carbs even in small amounts or even fruit I gain weight... I try to stay away from sugar completely. Unless I do constant steak days I am literally watching weight come on in a mater of days.

When I found this Leptin Reset protocol it sounded just like me. I am extremely irritable. Very low energy, cold all the time, I sleep with so many blankets in the summer, low sex drive, constant headaches, fat, could never wake up in the moring, extremely low Vitamin D Levels, thyroid normal apparently etc. I read about the exercise actually causing harm when Leptin resistant and thought- wow- that's what was happening to me! My husband thought I was crazy blaming the exercise as actually causing more harm than good....anyway....

My first question is- how long do you think it will take me to fix my hormones? 10 weeks?? longer? I feel like I really messed myy body up.... also when should I start cold therapy? what is your opinion about people that used HCG ???

I have been doing the Leptin Reset protocol for 1 week. I am trying to follow it to the T. I hate seafood, but none the less, I have been buying shrimp and scallops and tuna by the boatload. Eating within 30 minutes of rising, 3 meals a day only, nothing after 7pm with at least 4 hours between meals. I have completely removed dairy, grains, fruit and nightshades.. except for 2tbsp of cream in my morning coffee... I could remove it if you thought it would help.... I am trying to lose this last 30 lbs I keep regaining.... none of my clothes fit. I only do walking as exercise right now- I quit everything else. I am taking Vitamin D 5000 IU and K2 supplements, Magnesium, a probiotic and fish oil with my multivitamin.

2nd question- the first 4 days I was eating humas with my meat at dinner thinking it was allowed.... I quickly learned it wasn't. Do I have to wait 2 weeks now to restart the protocol? Did I mess up already? I actually have been feeling pretty good. I can wake up for the first time ever without even trying at 7am! This is like amazing for me. I feel refreshed. I already see my energy level doing well. However I wasnt sure if I messed up. I was going to try to start doing the cold therpy in the next couple days- I was waiting to get almost 2 weeks in....

3rd question- Can we cook with coconut flour or almond flour? I am eating meat and seafood and veggies only- very low carb no fruit and tons of eggs. I have horrid cravings for other things but can abstain, just curious if I can cook with coconut flour and make something other than meat 3x a day. I am really good at making coconut pancakes with eggs, coconut flour and coconut oil with a tiny bit of stevia. I know no other sugars are allowed- I only use stevia anyway. Its totally fine if I can't I can do anything to fix myself- just was curious about this because it would be nice to have more variety.

4th question- Can I add nuts in or should i continue to avoid them? I have just been eating the seafood, bacon, eggs, steak, turkey, sausage and leafy greens/cauliflower but was wanting to have some almonds or cashews etc. Yes or Hell no?

5th question- Why does Paleo have so many contradictions on what you can eat and can't. I have been reading non stop paleo cook books at the library and they all have varrying degrees of things you can and can't have.... is there a EPI Paleo cookbook I can look at- I don't want to mess this Leptin Reset up by using things I shouldnt... like peanut butter or cottage cheese etc.

### JanSz — 2017-05-02T23:39:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-leptin-reset-wondering-how-broken-i-am.19414/post-213256>

\[qu ote="Lyse, post: 213253, member: 20042"\] "ideal weight" of 145.
\---------------------------------------------
Very low energy, cold all the time, I sleep with so many blankets in the summer, low sex drive, constant headaches, fat, could never wake up in the moring, extremely low Vitamin D Levels, thyroid normal apparently etc.
\----------------------------------------
My first question is- how long do you think it will take me to fix my hormones? 10 weeks?? longer?
\-------------------------------------
I am taking Vitamin D 5000 IU and K2 supplements, Magnesium, a probiotic and fish oil with my multivitamin.
\-------------------------------
4th question- Can I add nuts in or should i continue to avoid them? I have just been eating the seafood, bacon, eggs, steak, turkey, sausage and leafy greens/cauliflower but was wanting to have some almonds or cashews etc. Yes or Hell no?\[/quote\]
///////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
Start with Circadian Rhythm. Follow what sun tells you.

You will know you did good job with Circadian Rhythm when:
1-- your saliva cortisol is real close to a top line (on test report sheet).
2-- your vitamin D (without any supplementation) is about:
Vitamin D (25-Hydroxyvitamin D)(250nmol/L=100.16ng/mL)
3-- your sleep is good

To fix hormone you have to test them first.
To know what to eat ------>test:
fatty acids
micronutrients

Fish oils, krill oils are most abused and hurtful fatty acids, do not use them.

When you have to eat something that have something real 100% very good.
First be careful, someone is trying to use magic trick and turn off your attention from the fact that with that good stuff comes poison (in small amounts).

............
The more hours between meals, the faster you will loose weight.
You cannot loose weight if your fasting insulin is over 2-3.

......
What you should eat highly depends on latitude of your residence.

...

**Nothing is very good.
Nothing is very bad.
We need everything in just right amounts.**

///////////

Hi Lyse and welcome.

Where do you live? work?

nnEMF?

do you wear blueblockers ?

what water are you drinking?

The leptin RX is very specific - if it isn't listed - don't eat it .....at least for now.

This is a light story .... not a food story.

Dr. K. is all about The three legged stool .....light, water, magnetism

### Lyse — 2017-05-05T06:43:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-leptin-reset-wondering-how-broken-i-am.19414/post-213427>

Hi!
Ok sounds good. I will start a journal. I did read his book and have it on my kindle. I don't listen to the podcasts yet and do not have John Ott's book. Who is this? Sorry I am a newbie... I am trying to digest Jack's his blogs and reading other peoples messages on the forum as fast as I can. I just started this protocol and so far only am doing the diet piece. I will quickly remedy that. I wanted to add the cold therapy in the next couple days starting with the face dunks. How do people do the ice bath in a normal tub? Do we just run to the gas station and buy ice everyday? I don't have a deep freezer yet so no where to store ice in my tiny freezer at moment.

I do not have the blue blocker glasses... I ordered them today.
I started going outside first thing in the AM 2 days ago barefoot... I am still wearing a jacket.
I am a stay at home mom from Chicago. I drink bottled water or smart water.
I guess I am a bit overwhelmed with all the things to do... I found the leptin reset and thought this was my answer, and now see it is but one piece in the puzzle to my health. I am excited to do everything just catching my breath as to where to begin....

Thanks for your help. I will start my journal.

### caroline — 2017-05-05T07:02:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-leptin-reset-wondering-how-broken-i-am.19414/post-213428>

Hi Lyse .....yes there is so much here - but just start slowly with the leptin reset and face dunks and listen to Jack's podcasts. It is a much quicker way to get information right now.

concentrate on light, water and magnetism......

Chicago is a huge issue .....your postcode matters.

You absolutely need blue blockers. Fake light is a huge issue.

Have you got all electronics out of your bedroom?

John Ott's book is a small book and a very easy read ......Helath and light.

Don't stress ......just follow the leptin reset and do face dunks.

If you can't make and store ice - just buy a bag when you are ready to start C/T.

You can buy an ice vest when you have time to look into that.

We were all newbies at one time ....have you read the thread that Dr. K. started ? The importance of newbies....

I didn't ask you ....how is your libido? That is a great indication of where your hormones are at.

Your sleep?

### Brent Patrick — 2017-05-04T11:21:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/krill-oil-for-ct.19415/post-213345>

JanSz

Jack mentions krill oil at around 53 mins in on his cold Thermogenesis webinar
also,,,

Cold Thermogenesis 11: Paleo FX To Practice

Beginning in week 1, I began an aggressive Mg supplementation program as follows:

-   Daily A.M. – [Mg Threonate @ 200mg orally](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B006P536E6/ref=as_li_qf_sp_asin_il_tl?ie=UTF8&camp=1789&creative=9325&creativeASIN=B006P536E6&linkCode=as2&tag=jackru-20) / Mg Chloride Daily
-   Daily P.M. – 60 minutes foot soaks w 2 oz / [ZMA Supplement](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B0013OZGQ0/ref=as_li_qf_sp_asin_il_tl?ie=UTF8&camp=1789&creative=9325&creativeASIN=B0013OZGQ0&linkCode=as2&tag=jackru-20) w 450mg Mg & 400mg Mg Glycinate.
-   b) I also have added [Grass Fed Beef Liver](http://www.grasslandbeef.com/StoreFront.bok?affId=135230) (offal) and other sources of dietary Mg into my diet.

7) After Week 4, I moved into a maintenance CT mode @ 2-3 times per week at 60 minutes each session.

8) Additional supplementation of:

-   [Krill Oil](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B003O1Y4C2/ref=as_li_qf_sp_asin_il_tl?ie=UTF8&camp=1789&creative=9325&creativeASIN=B003O1Y4C2&linkCode=as2&tag=jackru-20) @ 750mg daily
-   [Curcumin](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000X9P5GM/ref=as_li_qf_sp_asin_il_tl?ie=UTF8&camp=1789&creative=9325&creativeASIN=B000X9P5GM&linkCode=as2&tag=jackru-20) @ 750mg
-   [Resveratrol](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B003Q8SAZU/ref=as_li_qf_sp_asin_il_tl?ie=UTF8&camp=1789&creative=9325&creativeASIN=B003Q8SAZU&linkCode=as2&tag=jackru-20) @ 1000mg

Cold Thermogenesis 5: Biologic magnetism ( I think I just foumd the answer to my question in the next paragraph)

-
    This is why omega three fats would be favored over omega six fats in mammals who live in the coldest environments on Earth. The reason is that omega three fats have more unsaturated bonds that allow for more fluidity in the cell membranes for ideal signaling. There are also electrical reasons for this as well. DHA can select for certain phospholipids to work in a more optimal electric manner. Take a guess where the omega three sources come from? The deep ocean. It appears evolution is using form for function ideally, once again. The best natural source is krill and other fish that [support huge ecosystems in our polar seas](http://brent.xner.net/pdf/JungeXnerStaley2011_ExtremeHandbook6.3.pdf) for these mammals. Seafood is always the right choice, but the use of krill oil use makes a lot more sense for warm adapted mammals than fish oil does because of its stability in warm temperatures. DHA is also not optically stable outside of fish. This is a major down side to fish oils.

-
    cheers WalterNL reading it now!

# Thread: Emma's First journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-first-journal.19416/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 50
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### emmao — 2017-05-03T23:12:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-first-journal.19416/post-213323>

hello everyone,
I'm Emma, I'm 17 years old and I'm from Nebraska. I wanted to become a member here to post about how open minded I've become because of my encounter with Jack.

My family has followed Jack Kruse for about 5 years now.
I started transitioning into a healthy lifestyle when my mom peaked interest into organic food. I was always embarrassed by what others thought so I tried to hide the food my mom packed in my lunch and would try to avoid having people over so they wouldn't lurk in my pantry. Given this was about 11 years ago, when organic food wasn't looked upon highly. I slowly started to accept the fact that I was helping my body out, despite my friends opinions.

Then when I was about 12, my mom and dad latched onto the biohacks and everything Jack preached. Every night I would arrive home to my mom and dad listening to podcasts in the dark. I despised coming home to have my mom say "Emma come listen to this I think you'd like it!"

I wanted none of it.

In middle school I struggled with acne. I look back at how stupid I probably looked covering my face in makeup to hide the volcanos. I never understood something was wrong with my body internally, so I just wanted to go to the dermatologist to get rid of it. My mom refused to take me, and I despised her for making me struggle with it. It wasn't until I was a sophomore that my parents started to pay me to eat oysters. Slowly but surely I started to see a difference in my skin. My acne was nearly gone and in the summer my skin would be bronzed. To this day I notice when I don't eat oysters, I break out horrendously. After I learned about DHA and how it helped my skin, I started to ease my way into the lifestyle of my parents.

My parents have worn orange goggles for what seems like forever now. I've never let them wear them when my friends were over and I'd always make them go upstairs to be out of sight. About a year ago, my curiousity got the best of me and I wanted to see if they actually worked. I would wear them for awhile then one night I wouldn't and I noticed I couldnt fall asleep without them. I worked my way into wearing them every night and started being open about the goggles. I send out snapchats to my friends and stopped caring that they say they're "weird".

In December, my parents and I took a vacation to Mexico and Met with Dr. Kruse a few days. Honestly, we were waiting outside the Royal to meet him, and I was so embarrassed because I felt like a fan girl. All I could think is how I'm 17 waiting outside for some fancy doctor to come tell my mom "hi" and leave. Dr. Jack and Jeremy treated us like normal people and really emphasized why all these things were important. Hearing this all in person changed my entire aspect on what's in store for my generation!

So here's the summary.

Ive convinced a few friends to try the glasses and even one of my friends parents. I worry about all the things my generation is going to struggle with because of blue light!
I am ready to graduate high school In a few weeks and I'm starting to think about my future. Jacks advice to me was "make money fast then go what you want". I've still kept this close to heart, but I'm very interested in some sort of natropathic dermatology. I want to learn to heal skin naturally with red light and doing what's right for your body. Hardly anybody in my generation understands the things I do, so I hope it gives me an advantage in whatever I do.

My mom refused to take me, and I despised her for making me struggle with it. It wasn't until I was a sophomore that my parents started to pay me to eat oysters. Slowly but surely I started to see a difference in my skin. My acne was nearly gone and in the summer my skin would be bronzed. To this day I notice when I don't eat oysters, I break out horrendously. After I learned about DHA and how it helped my skin, I started to ease my way into the lifestyle of my parents.
>
> My parents have worn orange goggles for what seems like forever now. I've never let them wear them when my friends were over and I'd always make them go upstairs to be out of sight. About a year ago, my curiousity got the best of me and I wanted to see if they actually worked. I would wear them for awhile then one night I wouldn't and I noticed I couldnt fall asleep without them. I worked my way into wearing them every night and started being open about the goggles. I send out snapchats to my friends and stopped caring that they say they're "weird".
>
> In December, my parents and I took a vacation to Mexico and Met with Dr. Kruse a few days. Honestly, we were waiting outside the Royal to meet him, and I was so embarrassed because I felt like a fan girl. All I could think is how I'm 17 waiting outside for some fancy doctor to come tell my mom "hi" and leave. Dr. Jack and Jeremy treated us like normal people and really emphasized why all these things were important. Hearing this all in person changed my entire aspect on what's in store for my generation!
>
> So here's the summary.
>
> Ive convinced a few friends to try the glasses and even one of my friends parents. I worry about all the things my generation is going to struggle with because of blue light!
> I am ready to graduate high school In a few weeks and I'm starting to think about my future. Jacks advice to me was "make money fast then go what you want". I've still kept this close to heart, but I'm very interested in some sort of natropathic dermatology. I want to learn to heal skin naturally with red light and doing what's right for your body. Hardly anybody in my generation understands the things I do, so I hope it gives me an advantage in whatever I do.

Awesome story Emma. You're already a winner.

I like your passion. I accepted that I won't meet many people my age who are into this stuff- Toby is 18 in Australia, there's Julia in Toronto who's 20 I think, Devin is 26 in Mass... I think that's most of us. When someone new comes along it's like we have a new best friend even though most of us haven't met in person. Also, my 3 best friends at home have come on board since I got home from a year abroad in Europe transformed.

I suggest you check out my and my friend's guest blog: [https://www.jackkruse.com/reality-6-quantum-thievry-mexico/](https://www.jackkruse.com/reality-6-quantum-thievry-mexico/)

Getting into this stuff is not easy. It took about 2 years of squirming and struggling until I met Jack (my friend and I waited outside the royal for him just like you, but I was really waiting for 2 years inside) in Playa.

Like the others said, you're gorgeous! I think this stuff works

I'd love to chat if you're interested. Jack gave me the same advice. Two brains together are far better than two separate. The whole is greater than the sum of its parts in this case.

Matty - I have a bone to pick with you! ..... you are gorgeous for lots of reasons ...BUT.... please don't exclude people because they are not your age.

I have friends that are older and lots that are much younger - you learn something from every single person in your life. Everyone has a story. You learn so much from everyone who passes thru your life.

Age is just a number!

Matty - Gather a tribe together of folks from diverse countries, nationalities, ages and life experiences ....and learn from each and every one of them.

Matty -You consider Jack a friend .....Jack is probably older than your Dad?

I am way older than you and older than Jack - does that mean I have no value?

Take people as they come into your life ...listen acutely \[like Jack does\] and really see them - It will be life altering what you will learn and discover ....and you will grow and prosper.

I met a young man on an overnite train to Beijing about five years ago. We still keep in contact. I adore this young man and I learn so much from him and he learns a lot from me - even tho he is an aeronautical engineer in China's space program....and even tho some of his emails are redacted!

### Matty_M — 2017-05-07T04:02:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-first-journal.19416/post-213551>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=213549)
>
> Matty - I have a bone to pick with you! ..... you are gorgeous for lots of reasons ...BUT.... please don't exclude people because they are not your age.
>
> I have friends that are older and lots that are much younger - you learn something from every single person in your life. Everyone has a story. You learn so much from everyone who passes thru your life.
>
> Age is just a number!
>
> Matty - Gather a tribe together of folks from diverse countries, nationalities, ages and life experiences ....and learn from each and every one of them.
>
> Matty -You consider Jack a friend .....Jack is probably older than your Dad?
>
> I am way older than you and older than Jack - does that mean I have no value?
>
> Take people as they come into your life ...listen acutely \[like Jack does\] and really see them - It will be life altering what you will learn and discover ....and you will grow and prosper.
>
> I met a young man on an overnite train to Beijing about five years ago. We still keep in contact. I adore this young man and I learn so much from him and he learns a lot from me - even tho he is an aeronautical engineer in China's space program....and even tho some of his emails are redacted!

Hi Caroline! Totally didn't mean for what I said to come off that way.

Like Jack says, I know what I say but I don't know what others take from it.

I absolutely agree that we can all learn from everyone. I never said that we can't or shouldn't.

But, it is quite natural for people to spend time and learn with other people at similar stages in life.

The entire forum is composed of adults who have graduated high school, college, worked, and raised families. I have done nothing but take advice from you guys because I truly believe you have tremendous wisdom to share.

But, it really helps to have people in similar age groups and situations in life so we can learn from each other at our respective places.

When I first got into this, no one on this forum had been in high school and navigated that whole social environment with the quantum eye. I still asked everyone for advice because you guys have dealth with difficult things in high school as well. From experience, I know that it really helps to have people in similar situations to share feelings with; that's why I shared these names with Emma.

Excuse me for not clarifying: this group I shared is in addition to the hundreds of extremely wise and experienced adults on here who have taught me more than most others in my life combined, seriously! Not to the exclusion of them.

Thanks Caroline for pointing out something that could be very easily misunderstood. Your wisdom has guided me very far. In addition, it helps to be able to discuss with people at a similar young age who is navigating similar challenges going through high school, into college, and considering beginning a career in this field.

I just spent four days in Vero Beach with Nick from this forum and his girlfriend, then a week in SF and Berkeley. There was not one cloud in the sky, I did yoga every day, swam in the bay, and walked around Berkeley shirtless in the sun. To my surprise, I was the only one barefoot and shirtless there in the streets... today I ran some dogs around a park and swam in the 50 degree creek. It's pretty magnificent. I took 8 total flights in all that traveling. Seeing the world from above 10,000 feet makes it difficult to take us too seriously . Hope you are well too!

now that we know better ..... we are doing so much better.
