>
> hello everyone,
> I'm Emma, I'm 17 years old and I'm from Nebraska. I wanted to become a member here to post about how open minded I've become because of my encounter with Jack.
>
> My family has followed Jack Kruse for about 5 years now.
> I started transitioning into a healthy lifestyle when my mom peaked interest into organic food. I was always embarrassed by what others thought so I tried to hide the food my mom packed in my lunch and would try to avoid having people over so they wouldn't lurk in my pantry. Given this was about 11 years ago, when organic food wasn't looked upon highly. I slowly started to accept the fact that I was helping my body out, despite my friends opinions.
>
> Then when I was about 12, my mom and dad latched onto the biohacks and everything Jack preached. Every night I would arrive home to my mom and dad listening to podcasts in the dark. I despised coming home to have my mom say "Emma come listen to this I think you'd like it!"
>
> I wanted none of it.
>
> In middle school I struggled with acne. I look back at how stupid I probably looked covering my face in makeup to hide the volcanos. I never understood something was wrong with my body internally, so I just wanted to go to the dermatologist to get rid of it. My mom refused to take me, and I despised her for making me struggle with it. It wasn't until I was a sophomore that my parents started to pay me to eat oysters. Slowly but surely I started to see a difference in my skin. My acne was nearly gone and in the summer my skin would be bronzed. To this day I notice when I don't eat oysters, I break out horrendously. After I learned about DHA and how it helped my skin, I started to ease my way into the lifestyle of my parents.
>
> My parents have worn orange goggles for what seems like forever now. I've never let them wear them when my friends were over and I'd always make them go upstairs to be out of sight. About a year ago, my curiousity got the best of me and I wanted to see if they actually worked. I would wear them for awhile then one night I wouldn't and I noticed I couldnt fall asleep without them. I worked my way into wearing them every night and started being open about the goggles. I send out snapchats to my friends and stopped caring that they say they're "weird".
>
> In December, my parents and I took a vacation to Mexico and Met with Dr. Kruse a few days. Honestly, we were waiting outside the Royal to meet him, and I was so embarrassed because I felt like a fan girl. All I could think is how I'm 17 waiting outside for some fancy doctor to come tell my mom "hi" and leave. Dr. Jack and Jeremy treated us like normal people and really emphasized why all these things were important. Hearing this all in person changed my entire aspect on what's in store for my generation!
>
> So here's the summary.
>
> Ive convinced a few friends to try the glasses and even one of my friends parents. I worry about all the things my generation is going to struggle with because of blue light!
> I am ready to graduate high school In a few weeks and I'm starting to think about my future. Jacks advice to me was "make money fast then go what you want". I've still kept this close to heart, but I'm very interested in some sort of natropathic dermatology. I want to learn to heal skin naturally with red light and doing what's right for your body. Hardly anybody in my generation understands the things I do, so I hope it gives me an advantage in whatever I do.

Your story cracked me up... they paid you to eat oysters? That's funny...

### emmao — 2017-05-25T08:21:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-first-journal.19416/post-214629>

When I met jack in Mexico I had been putting my phone in my back pocket (shame on me, but I know better now) and he had told me to remove it. So I did. He explained how my body is being hit with emfs and radiation right by my ovaries. Sometimes I subconsciously put it there, but I've gotten better at getting it away from my body.
Now that I try to avoid it, I can't help but notice when other girls put it in their back pocket or In their bras. Regularly I have to tell my friends to take it out.
Today I had been scrolling through the news portion of Snapchat. Here news articles are posted from buzzfeed, cosmo, MTV, etc. One article title struck me today from buzzfeed that said "secret benefits of boobies" with an animated picture of a girl with a phone between her breasts. It then stated how boobs were a built in phone holder.
The media these leads teens and young adult women into thinking this is totally okay. Millions of girls are seeing this influence and they don't have someone to tell them to stop! Nothing gets me more stirred up. So I took the time to email buzzfeed to let them know how I feel.
The media has a crazy influence on young women. I fear what's in store for my generation

way to go "starfish Emma"!!

### PaulG — 2017-05-25T10:12:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-first-journal.19416/post-214632>

Welcome Emma,

I have an 18 year old son who is interested in Science but can't turn him onto this stuff. I am always sending him interesting articles on insomnia, blue light, emf etc, though he may have blacklisted me! and we do discuss topics like effects of blue light on melatonin and sleep.

I also have two other kids, a daughter (13) and son (16)..it is hard to get the message across. If anyone knows of any video content that is easily digestible that let me know.

Generally speaking, people are not that pro-active with health and wellness and usually something has to go 'wrong' before they start looking for causes/solutions. I guess it's natural when you are young to believe you are somewhat immune to disease (true to a large extent) and its only an issue when you get to middle age or older. This is known as the invincibility fallacy. See [https://psychological-musings.blogspot.com.au/2011/02/middle-childhood-and-adolescence.html](https://psychological-musings.blogspot.com.au/2011/02/middle-childhood-and-adolescence.html)

The [EMF warriors library](https://www.emfwarriors.com/library/) is a very good resource of articles and videos.

Now way I am googling "secret benefits of boobies" would get me into trouble..

I guess it's natural when you are young to believe you are somewhat immune to disease (true to a large extent) and its only an issue when you get to middle age or older. This is known as the invincibility fallacy. See [https://psychological-musings.blogspot.com.au/2011/02/middle-childhood-and-adolescence.html](https://psychological-musings.blogspot.com.au/2011/02/middle-childhood-and-adolescence.html)
>

My mom sent me links and ALWAYS talked about things with me. Everytime we got in the car it was like a broken record. I hated it; I bet he does too. If he doesn't want to be helped, you can't do anything about it, frankly. It takes one thing to trigger the curiousity of a teen before they're hooked. For me it was my skin, since I was so embarrassed and just wanted it gone. Once I realized that, I simply kept following. Find ONE thing that would interest them. For your daughter, it will most likely be acne or problems with her period like her cycle is off or her cramps are "unbearable". Don't overflow their uneducated brains yet, its overwhelming.
If you can take your family to Mexico for the December trip, do it. Everything started to seem real to me after this. Talking with other members and Jack influenced me more than any article or anything my mom had said to me.

### Matty_M — 2017-05-25T16:34:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-first-journal.19416/post-214651>

> [emmao said:](/goto/post?id=214629)
>
> When I met jack in Mexico I had been putting my phone in my back pocket (shame on me, but I know better now) and he had told me to remove it. So I did. He explained how my body is being hit with emfs and radiation right by my ovaries. Sometimes I subconsciously put it there, but I've gotten better at getting it away from my body.
> Now that I try to avoid it, I can't help but notice when other girls put it in their back pocket or In their bras. Regularly I have to tell my friends to take it out.
> Today I had been scrolling through the news portion of Snapchat. Here news articles are posted from buzzfeed, cosmo, MTV, etc. One article title struck me today from buzzfeed that said "secret benefits of boobies" with an animated picture of a girl with a phone between her breasts. It then stated how boobs were a built in phone holder.
> The media these leads teens and young adult women into thinking this is totally okay. Millions of girls are seeing this influence and they don't have someone to tell them to stop! Nothing gets me more stirred up. So I took the time to email buzzfeed to let them know how I feel.
> The media has a crazy influence on young women. I fear what's in store for my generation

Natural selection at play . You will be much freer once you realize that you can't save everyone. In fact, you can hardly do anything well if you don't save yourself first. Otherwise, we will be slaves to the banks for education, marriages, and mortgages for most of our productive life. We would have to trade our time here on earth for a paycheck of numbers in a computer to get our daily bread (seafood).

We can only do what we can do.

People will hurt themselves because the programming system (education is a euphemism) in our country has taught us to trust authority and that scientists are doctors would tell us of any risk.

If we want to solve the problem, we have to think about how to teach people to think for themselves.

We aren't all that different from Germany in the 1930's....

[http://schoolsucksproject.com/new-start-here/](http://schoolsucksproject.com/new-start-here/)

^^^Jack, I think you should share this podcast. I suggest everyone here checks it out. It explains magnificently how our system has programmed people to be "obedient idiots".

The other day I was walking along a beach here in Maine with a friend while on my senior project. He was flying a $1600 drone to get footage of the seaweed harvest that our friends were out for. I imagine looking at myself through the lens of the drone, walking along the beach. No thoughts, no preconceptions; just things the way they "are". Anything imposed onto the situation is really a product of our neocortex and doesn't necessarily represent the truth.

Larch Hanson of [theseaweedman.com](http://schoolsucksproject.com/new-start-here/), who we are working for, is extremely connected and wiser than just about anyone I've met. Jack, you've got competition . He says that thoughts are ephemeral; temporary and fleeting, like a flower.

I realized that I can do literally just about anything with the time I'm given to visit earth. My real home is out in the cosmos. The only way I will ever achieve what I want is:

(Take a guess)

By deciding WHAT I WANT!

I have all of these vague images in my mind of things I can do or outcomes in the real world that would would be fantastic, but they are hardly concrete. If I don't know what they really are, then I will never achieve them. I could just work down whichever path "feels right", but I can't expect to feel significant progress towards any outcome if I haven't defined the outcome I want.

"If one does not know to which port one is sailing, **no wind is favorable**."
\- Lucius Annaeus Seneca

Larch rarely answers questions directly, rather he tells stories. I think it's rubbing off.

For me, the most attractive port is getting myself stabilized. Then, working on what I can do to change the global programming system so that people are indoctrinated with distrust of authority and love of logic, instead of the other way around, **which is where we are now**.

By the way! It's 9:15 am and I've been up since 2:30 am. I got up for a seaweed harvest at low tide at first light. The colors lighting up the Maine coast this morning were beyond godly. There were seals all around the rocks where we harvested, one was sleeping and we walked right up to it. Another swam right up to our little rowboat. It was godly. I was connected to the 40 degree water doing labor with my hands. I am charged up like never before. Maybe a little sleep deprived. But, the 71-year-old who runs this place consistently gets by on under 4 hours of sleep during harvest season... like his Norwegian ancestors' mitochondial haplotype...

Again,

"If one does not know to which port one is sailing, **no wind is favorable**."

I guess it's natural when you are young to believe you are somewhat immune to disease (true to a large extent) and its only an issue when you get to middle age or older. This is known as the invincibility fallacy. I'm working on making this stuff for young people. You'll find it on mattmaruca.com within the next many months. Not live yet.

### Billybats — 2017-09-27T20:48:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-first-journal.19416/post-224389>

> [emmao said:](/goto/post?id=213323)
>
> hello everyone,
> I'm Emma, I'm 17 years old and I'm from Nebraska. I wanted to become a member here to post about how open minded I've become because of my encounter with Jack.
>
> My family has followed Jack Kruse for about 5 years now.
> I started transitioning into a healthy lifestyle when my mom peaked interest into organic food. I was always embarrassed by what others thought so I tried to hide the food my mom packed in my lunch and would try to avoid having people over so they wouldn't lurk in my pantry. Given this was about 11 years ago, when organic food wasn't looked upon highly. I slowly started to accept the fact that I was helping my body out, despite my friends opinions.
>
> Then when I was about 12, my mom and dad latched onto the biohacks and everything Jack preached. Every night I would arrive home to my mom and dad listening to podcasts in the dark. I despised coming home to have my mom say "Emma come listen to this I think you'd like it!"
>
> I wanted none of it.
>
> In middle school I struggled with acne. I look back at how stupid I probably looked covering my face in makeup to hide the volcanos. I never understood something was wrong with my body internally, so I just wanted to go to the dermatologist to get rid of it. My mom refused to take me, and I despised her for making me struggle with it. It wasn't until I was a sophomore that my parents started to pay me to eat oysters. Slowly but surely I started to see a difference in my skin. My acne was nearly gone and in the summer my skin would be bronzed. To this day I notice when I don't eat oysters, I break out horrendously. After I learned about DHA and how it helped my skin, I started to ease my way into the lifestyle of my parents.
>
> My parents have worn orange goggles for what seems like forever now. I've never let them wear them when my friends were over and I'd always make them go upstairs to be out of sight. About a year ago, my curiousity got the best of me and I wanted to see if they actually worked. I would wear them for awhile then one night I wouldn't and I noticed I couldnt fall asleep without them. I worked my way into wearing them every night and started being open about the goggles. I send out snapchats to my friends and stopped caring that they say they're "weird".
>
> In December, my parents and I took a vacation to Mexico and Met with Dr. Kruse a few days. Honestly, we were waiting outside the Royal to meet him, and I was so embarrassed because I felt like a fan girl. All I could think is how I'm 17 waiting outside for some fancy doctor to come tell my mom "hi" and leave. Dr. Jack and Jeremy treated us like normal people and really emphasized why all these things were important. Hearing this all in person changed my entire aspect on what's in store for my generation!
>
> So here's the summary.
>
> Ive convinced a few friends to try the glasses and even one of my friends parents. I worry about all the things my generation is going to struggle with because of blue light!
> I am ready to graduate high school In a few weeks and I'm starting to think about my future. Jacks advice to me was "make money fast then go what you want". I've still kept this close to heart, but I'm very interested in some sort of natropathic dermatology. I want to learn to heal skin naturally with red light and doing what's right for your body. Hardly anybody in my generation understands the things I do, so I hope it gives me an advantage in whatever I do.

Hi Emmao,

Awesome story and a beautiful women. You have made me start focusing more on making an effort to take bigger steps. To rethink my strategy. Thank you for your story!

### caroline — 2018-02-17T02:45:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-first-journal.19416/post-234953>

> [aguzman said:](/goto/post?id=234936)

\[/quote\]
Please remember that everyone here is very busy with their own lives and issues. We are all trying to limit our computer/blue lite exposure time as best we can. Lots of people here put in a lot of their limited free time trying to help others.

You have only been here for one week???????

Dr. Kruse is a crazy busy guy ......please remember he is in full time neurosurgery practise. How he does all of this is a mystery to all of us. He has dedicated the last number of years to helping all of us here with all the blogs and webinars and podcasts ...and also answering the same questions - over and over and over for people who don't necessarily want to help themselves. If you come to jackkruse.com for a quick fix ...I am afraid - it ain't gonna happen.

Most folks here do pitch in as best they can with their own personal experiences - but we are not Doctors ....we are just regular folks like you.

Jack is incredibly busy with a huge new project that will take health care to a new level.....please cut him and us some slack.

Are you a paid member? Jack needs us all to step up and have skin in the game now. Of course you don't have to be - there is years of stuff here to get thru and process.

Have you listened to all his podcasts? The 2 Vermont talks? Are you following the webinars? the blogs?

There are people here with very serious and life threatening illnesses that are doing their due diligence - day in and day out and working really hard to help themselves.

Dr. K. is the guy with the flashlight. He answers questions when he can and always posts new information for us to check out.....and keep us on track.

There have been new people here that simply amaze me ....they read and follow the easy start guides that Jack has provided - and they just dive in....and they are making a huge difference in their lives.

There are a lot of us here who have gone to great expense to go to meet Jack at one event or another .....skin in the game. I flew from Australia to Orlando.

But it is more than that - a lot more than that ....we have to dive deep to make a difference in our own life - no one else can do it for us.

We really are all willing to help......

Dr. K. is the guy with the flashlight. He answers questions when he can and always posts new information for us to check out.....and keep us on track.

There have been new people here that simply amaze me ....they read and follow the easy start guides that Jack has provided - and they just dive in....and they are making a huge difference in their lives.

There are a lot of us here who have gone to great expense to go to meet Jack at one event or another .....skin in the game. I flew from Australia to Orlando.

But it is more than that - a lot more than that ....we have to dive deep to make a difference in our own life - no one else can do it for us.

We really are all willing to help......\[/quote\]

You had better restraint than I would have.
I don't know what most people's expectations are when becoming a member but I never expected any direct contact from Dr K, definitely not a welcome wagon. Caroline, you were, have been and always will be the best!
Koala bear \[{(hug)}\]!!!

### caroline — 2018-02-17T04:59:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-first-journal.19416/post-234956>

Thanks Brandie .....I didn't feel all that restrained! Later I did think to myself .....wtf .....Only beautiful, international people get a look in??????? In five years of being here almost daily - I never noticed that ......duh....

I better pick up my game! A Koala bear hug back to you.....

# Thread: ALL THINGS SUNSCREEN
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-sunscreen.19417/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-04T02:56:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-sunscreen.19417/post-213329>

Sunscreen with SPF of 8 absorbs between 92- 95% of UVB photons and reduces vitamin D3 synthesis by the same degree. [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/18290718](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/18290718)

### QuantumRadical — 2017-05-05T00:36:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-sunscreen.19417/post-213396>

I haven't used sunscreen for the past seven years. Before that, always used it--highest spf available. Now, I sunbathe just short of burning as often as I can. My skin health and health in general has greatly improved. My dermotologist just shakes her head.
Q'Rad Randy

### QuantumRadical — 2017-05-05T00:37:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-sunscreen.19417/post-213398>

I am very fair skinned and burned easily. Not so much now.

### caroline — 2017-05-05T07:25:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-sunscreen.19417/post-213430>

me too - haven't used sunscreen the past five years or so. I don't burn at all and I feel great. Everyone tells me I am so lucky because I have an olive skin tone. I have never had an olive skin tone - I have always been very fair and burned easily.

### Inger — 2017-05-05T13:43:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-sunscreen.19417/post-213436>

I hardly burn either anymore, and I am a fair skinned scandinavian....
I feel SO blessed to have got to know how horrible sun-blockers are for health!

After staring the Iodine protocol again I have the feeling my skin get even tanner faster ... like olive-tan colored..... need to follow how this evolve  I wonder why...

### Huck — 2017-05-05T22:31:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-sunscreen.19417/post-213462>

Since I got my vitamin d level up, I haven't burned. I was at the beach this week and spent 7 hours in the sun the 1st day. I turned red for a couple of days, but it just seems to be darkening my tan now. No blistering or peeling.

The wind was blowing pretty hard which is why I didn't realize how hot my skin was getting.

### drezy — 2017-05-06T01:18:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-sunscreen.19417/post-213468>

As a super-white Polish/Swiss(mitochondrially) boy, as I've got my D up I've experienced the same sun "callous" that you hint at.

Also, even at 15 yo I had a 138/89 BP. Now I'm coming in at 120/82. It's kind of alarming at first until I realize "oh, wait! this actually feels good." 2 or so months still haven't caught up with decades quite yet.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-19T01:21:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-sunscreen.19417/post-214276>

The world is changing and the clinicians in clinics and hospitals do not realize it. Patients are becoming more educated on the cutting edge of science because they are engaged with the reading new things out of curiosity and net research. Most scientists and clinicians like to deride the internet because they believe it is populated with quacks. There is no doubt that that is true in some spots, but when you have a blog and website deidicated to real science with cites to published research the truth become hard to deny. Believe it not some of them still do. The dominant belief today is that UV light causes melanoma. For ten years all the data I personally reviewed led me to the exact opposite stand point. Now we have this GEM of published research performed by a group or respectable scientists who performed a global worldwide analysis of cutaneous malignant melanoma (CMM) over UV-Radiation dose by sex, age group (0–14, 15–29, 30–49, 50–69, 70–85C) and Fitzpatrick skin types I-VI. Surprisingly to them and most readers, but no tto me, increasing UVR doses, which represent erythemally- weighted doses comprised primarily of UVB (290–315 nm) radiation, DID NOT significantly correlate with increasing CMM incidence for people with any skin type ANYWHERE in the world. The world is changing..........when will the skin and eye docs get this message? Might the real of melanoma can be found in the picture below? [http://www.tandfonline.com/doi/pdf/10.1080/19381980.2016.1267077?needAccess=true](http://www.tandfonline.com/doi/pdf/10.1080/19381980.2016.1267077?needAccess=true)

This is about my father (60 year old)who have had two heart attacks.First was when he was 45 years. Mostly vegetarian diet all his littleNever been overweight. Had kidney stones.No junk food ever. He smoked and drank heavily.Doctors put stents in his arteries long time ago.He has ICD planted in his chest for last two years, have had shocks that suggest he had sone heart arrhythmia at those times

Assuming that your father lives in
Srinagar, Uttarakhand, India
The latitude for **Srinagar, Uttarakhand, India** is: 30.2246751
Elevation: 548.56 Meters (1799.74 Feet)

He should have plenty of sun.
He should be outside, with minimal clothes most of the day.
At night, 2-4 hrs before bed time, do not watch TV, cell phone,
sleep in totally dark room

Ask doctor for (all) this tests:
scan and post results (do not re-type)

1------- Pregnenolone,
2 ------ Progesterone
3 ------- Cortisol, AM & PM
4 ------- Dehydroepiandrosterone (DHEA) Sulfate
5 ------- Testosterone, Total, Serum
6 ------- Testosterone, Free and Weakly Bound
7 ------- SHBG
8 ------- Dihydrotestosterone (DHT)
9-------- Estrogens, Total
10 ------- Estradiol, Sensitive
11 ------- Estrone, Serum
12 ------- Metabolic Panel (14), Comprehensive
13 ------- Complete Blood Count (CBC) With Differential
14 ------- NMR LipoProfile® With Insulin Resistance Markers (With Graph)
15 ------- Thyroid-stimulating Hormone (TSH)
16 ------- Thyroxine (T4)
17 ------- Thyroxine (T4), Free
18 ------- Triiodothyronine (T3)
19 ------- **Triiodothyronine (T3), Free**
20 ------- **Reverse T3**
21 ------- Prolactin
22 ------- **Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum**
23 ------- IGF-1
24 ------- IGFBP3
25 ------- **Insulin, Free and Total, Serum**
26 ------- Hemoglobin (Hb) A1c
27 ------- **Homocysteine, Plasma**
28 ------- **Vitamin D, 25-Hydroxy
**
**Make sure to get**
**22 ------- Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum

that will tell us about eating chicken or not.**

But he should eat lots of butter or ghee (2-4 tablespoons)/day or more and drink vinegar (diluted in some water) 2-4 tablespoons, after each meal.

\===============================
low fat/low salt diet does not sound good to me

low fat bad advice overall
human body is made of fat and water

low salt is usually due to high blood pressure
but
low sun exposure causes low vit d and that in turn may cause high blood pressure

////////////////////

# Thread: How do photons stored in hemoglobin exert a physiologic affect?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-photons-stored-in-hemoglobin-exert-a-physiologic-affect.19421/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matty_M — 2017-05-04T23:53:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-photons-stored-in-hemoglobin-exert-a-physiologic-affect.19421/post-213391>

I understand they are absorbed when blood comes to the surface because of UV stimulating NO.

Once stored in hemoglobin, what do they do and how? How to they get to the mitochondria?

Are there any posted blogs on specifically this?

### Lahelada — 2017-05-06T23:40:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-photons-stored-in-hemoglobin-exert-a-physiologic-affect.19421/post-213538>

**Got that in my collection from Dr Ks facebook:**
"
"Tonight's photon factoid: Can we explain in real simple terms the journey of a photons interaction with a protein or cell membrane inside our circulatory system?
\-Photon leaves sun after one million years of confinement

\-Travels to skin and hits the skin and skin releases nitric oxide. The NO causes a phototropism effect as the surface arterioles come to the surface of the skin and the blood gets irradiated by all frequencies of the light. (leafs move toward sun too)
\-Sun light is absorbed differently by each tissue.
Hemoglobin absorbs 270-600 nm with a sharp cut off at 600 nm. Hits electron in cell membrane of red blood cells and this changes DHA and sulfates them
\-Electron energy in proteins are raised called excitement. This creates an exciton. Since RBC's have no mitochondria excitons react with water in blood plasma. (93%)
...
\-electron is now close enough to reactant because of water and water is turned into a sea of EZ that creates forward flow in the closed tube This lowers BP and affects renin angiotensin syst,e far away. Sunlight is also a calcium channel blocker so it lowers calcium flows to lower BP too. And dermal pooling also has a BP lowering effect to cause local reaction in the vessels.
\-raised electron facilitates one of the many reactions constantly happening in cell simultaneously as excitons are apt to do. This happens in leaves too.

Or in the case of hemoglobin
\-Exciton ejected from iron when they come into contact with a mitochondria at some tissue.
\-Exciton finds the best route out of the protein using cool quantum effects that mimic what we see in leaves reaction centers.
\-The formation of EZ and a photoelectric change to the surface of the RBC somehow helps the red blood cell move through body electromagnetically. As a RBC travels through the vessel it sheds sulfate groups which carry a negative charge. This also means within the circulatory system we develop both and electric and magnetic field. The voltage change is caused by the motuons of the RBC itself.......DHA and sulfates in the cell membrane. The hemoglobin inside it a ferry boat of light to be given to cytochrome 1 of mitochondria. It is not involved in the shedding phenomena. Remember RBC's have no mitochondria by design......they have to bring light to cells with mitochondria. The voltage change with blood flow acts like an induction magnet. As the RBC's moves through the circulation the magnetic field induced allows the vessel wall to release nitric oxide and sulfate cholesterol. This occurs in the blood plasma which is filled with 93% water. The EZ augments blood flow this way.....because there is a lot of positive charge in the artery wall and a large negative charge in the RBC's and EZ of plasma. This allows the like like likes action to occur between the EZ and RBC's to improve laminar flow.

Cholesterol plays a huge role here......Cholesterol stabilizes the artery wall by stablizing the cells in this tissue by stabilizing the inner mitochondrial membrane to lower vibrations in the Q cycle. This makes sure the " light deli slicer" (recent webinar) is accurate in making the key red light frequencies from the 340nm excited electrons that enter at cytochrome that will be shuttled to cytochrome 3 for the distal cytochromes. Most people know that sunlight has 42% IR-A light present in it. Most people do not know that red light at 613.5 and 623.5 nm (in one spectrum, at 606 nm), in the red maximum have some interesting effects on human tissue. RBC's like red light up until 600 nm only. That is where hemoglobin's peak ends sharply for absoption. Are there other key frequencies buried in the sun for healing other parts of our tissues? Yes. The far-red maximum has exact peak positions between 667.5 and 683.7 nm in different spectra. Two near infrared maxima have peak positions in the range 750.7-772.3 nm and 812.5-846.0 nm. Might these peaks be critical in our mitochondria too? Might this be why low cholesterol kills us with cancers? I think so and this mechanism above shows you why.

Cholesterol is a polar molecule that needs the sun to sulfate it so that molecular vibrations are lowered. The isomerization step in the skin requires an elevated surface temperature, that UVB light gives the skin to photonically change cholesterol to its sulfated form and then S- cholesterol to its sulfated Vitamin D3 form. This is fundamentally why Ancel Keys 7 countries Study was completely worthless on quantum biology merits. He found that high un sulfated cholesterol linked to heart disease. That link should have been expected because in all seven countries he sampled the zip codes were missing UVB light. Lack of UVB light is what drives heart disease not the presence of LDL cholesterol. When you ascribe causation to food and not to light you make catastrophic errors in science."

# Thread: How does blue light expand the respiratory proteins?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-blue-light-expand-the-respiratory-proteins.19422/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matty_M — 2017-05-04T23:56:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-blue-light-expand-the-respiratory-proteins.19422/post-213393>

what is the mechanism here?

Can someone refer me to the papers or blogs on this topic?

Thanks

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-05T02:42:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-blue-light-expand-the-respiratory-proteins.19422/post-213407>

Do you understand how coupling works? The coupler of the two is called an ' interactive controller' . When a system loses negative feedback control you uncouple cycles from one another. Let me give you a clear example of a LOSS of negative feedback control: in this type of coupling mechanism two components of the system have opposite effects on one another. Consider prey and predator as a couple. Consumption of prey by predator has a positive effect on the eater, and a negative on on the eaten animal. The negative effect on predators on prey prevents uncontrolled growth of the predator's population. This stabilizes the population sizes of both groups when they are coupled.

Blue light from artificial bulbs and LED screens uncouple electron spin from ECT flow speeds to release massive amounts of light. At the same time the respiratory proteins stretch out because the size of the mitochondria changes and gets larger and this engages the allometric scaling laws of metabolism. This is a power law in mathematics. In Nick Lane's latest book he mentions it in there. Every one Angstrom increase in the distance between the respiratory proteins slows electron and proton tunneling by a FACTOR of ten. Anytime you slow ECT you are closer to death. Cynanide stops ECT hence we die. When ECT is slowed you get mitochondrial diseases. Health is simply the slowest form of death we create on the inner mitochondrial membrane. When these respiratory proteins stretch out in dimension and lenght we call this increasing heteroplasmy rate.

What is the linkage to these coupling events? Loss of redox power because of an increase of heteroplasmy of mitochondria in tissues. The redox potential is the critical linkage of the first two biologic cycle of energy conservation, namely ubiquitin and the cell cycle. These two processes are control by the central retinal pathways to couple to growth and metabolism cycles. The coupling of the mitochondria to the nucleus how light controls biology at all levels. This is precisely how light from our surfaces couples cell growth via ubiquitin rates via our surfaces. Blue and Red light in the sun are like predator and prey........they each perfectly balance one another. But modern artificial light has 4 times the amount of blue and no red. This destroys the coupled cycle. Why is this series so critical to understand? Because ubiquitin is the major stabilizer of negative feedback control on cell growth and it works via melanopsin which is the key opsin of the central retinal pathways. The actor of ubiquitin is LIGHT!!!! This explains why light is the most powerful drug we have. When you uncouple blue light from all the other frequencies you create an asymmetry that can be used to help or hurt you based upon the use of that blue light.
[http://www.nature.com/articles/srep27754](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.nature.com%2Farticles%2Fsrep27754&h=ATMuod73FifQC12BOPUE4YFyNpJfetuiiHOU0kVdNtBnAhjR2s4PcLB_KF8eTQot_fJxGXQH44SkNsTWQ7_CjxWblYDYiPxsb-V3LVOzho6zZZ7Qsz-hZklEy4S7ZH8pG-p4lL0DTg)

### Matty_M — 2018-04-07T04:56:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-blue-light-expand-the-respiratory-proteins.19422/post-238880>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=213407)
>
> Blue light from artificial bulbs and LED screens uncouple electron spin from ECT flow speeds to release massive amounts of light.

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) Can you refer me to the articles and papers on this, or the physical principles of how blue light unouples electron spin from ECT flow? The more I learn, the more I realize that I need to understand the mechanisms underlying this stuff, otherwise I'm toast.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-05T05:13:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haarp-and-circadian-disruption.19426/post-213421>

So you think man cannot try to control the natural heartbeat of the Earth that your alpha wave is yoked to by frequency matching? You think man made light cannot effect your brain waves or your heart beat? You'd be dead wrong. The Schumann resonance and alpha wave are one and the same at 7.83 Hz. On an electroencephalogram (EEG) we get wave forms that depict the electrical activity occurring at the surface of the brain on the neocortex. That neocortex oscillates at 40 Hz in humans when they are healthy. The most familiar classification uses EEG waveform frequency in the alpha, beta, theta, and delta waveforms. Did you know that all of these different waves are linked by the Fibronacci sequence which is related to the golden ratio called PHI and this is related to the type of hydrogen isotopes you have in yoru brain in the neocortex and thalamus??? The Fibonacci sequence are all based on the longest Phi wave; also called the carrier wave that couples these waves and allows the waves to share their energy by molecular resonance. This golden ratio is required to connect the waves and allow energy transfer in both human Electrocardiogram (ECG) and the brainwaves in electroencephalogram (EEG) of humans. This means that the geomagnetic pulsations of the Earth are phase matched to the human brain and the energy contained in them can be shared with the human brain in wireless fashion via the actions of proton motions in the cells of the brain. Amazing what nature can do, isn't it? The alpha wave in the human brain controls sleep and much of crcadian coupling in the SCN. Did you know this? Are you beginning to see how pulsatile RF nnEMF can ruin circadian cycles yet because of the link to protons???? "We report results from the experiment aimed at the artificial excitation of extremely-low-frequency (ELF) electromagnetic waves with frequencies corresponding to the frequency of Schumann resonance (typically, 7.5 - 8.0 Hz frequency range). Electromagnetic waves with these frequencies can form a standing pattern inside the spherical cavity formed by the surface of the earth and the ionosphere. In the experiment the ELF waves were excited by heating the ionosphere with X-mode HF electromagnetic waves generated by the High Frequency Active Auroral Research Program (HAARP) facility in Alaska. The experiment demonstrates that heating of the ionosphere can excite relatively large-amplitude electromagnetic waves with frequencies in the range of the Schumann resonance, when the ionosphere has a strong F-layer and an electric field greater than 5 mV/m is present in the E-region."
[http://adsabs.harvard.edu/abs/2014AGUFMSA11A3918S](http://adsabs.harvard.edu/abs/2014AGUFMSA11A3918S)

I am a newbie and have a couple questions...I recently discovered Coconut Butter yesterday and gave it a try today. I am on day 9 of the Leptin Reset and wanted to say it is the most amazing thing ever. I only had a couple spoons but I am wondering how much is over doing it? I know its healthy fat and a good thing but I shouldn't go crazy with it everyday right??

Also what about cooking with coconut flour to make something like a pancake? My receipe uses coconut oil and eggs with water and baking powder. Should I be avoiding using coconut flour on the reset? I want to do this properly and already love the way I feel.

### Inger — 2017-05-05T12:01:45+0300

I would say, listen to your body  I do not do too well with coconut butter... makes me feel weird... (but I am from the high north .. I know people that do great with coconut butter and for them it is just fine, food is just 10% from all things that impact our health anyways) and coconut pancakes are for me kinda junk food..lol The more you fry stuff the less nutrients you get.... I stopped reading Hancock 10 years ago but his flood v catastrophe wiping out an advanced civilization just got more legs.
Hancock believes the Gobekli stones not only describe an ancient cosmic collision, but predict another.

He thinks that what hit Earth in 10,950BC was actually a massive piece of debris in the Taurid meteor stream, a belt containing millions of space rocks.

Read more: [http://www.dailymail.co.uk/sciencet...ilisation-13-000-years-ago.html#ixzz4gCKZ7bak](http://www.dailymail.co.uk/sciencetech/article-4457530/Mini-Ice-Age-wiped-cvilisation-13-000-years-ago.html#ixzz4gCKZ7bak)

Hard for me to tell if a book that appears well researched is just cherry picking data and unexplained phenomena to strengthen a thesis, or he's really onto something everyone's overlooked. He does try to do justice to critics and mainstream (non-cataclysmic) interpretations of various sites and finds, while also poking various holes in the gradualism paradigm.

# Thread: Quantlet Success
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantlet-success.19432/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### emmao — 2017-05-06T03:24:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantlet-success.19432/post-213484>

A couple of months ago my family received a quantlet (just received a second and waiting on our third). I've played softball since I was about 5 years old and I'm continuing to play into college. I spend about 2 or 3 days a week playing softball and average about 7 games on the weekends during season. After high school season in the fall, I took a three month break because everytime I pitched I would get an overwhelming pain in my shoulder. When it was time to start in February, I had no energy to get through practice and dreaded pitching in fear that my arm would start hurting.

I started using the quantlet on performance and wellness for 60 minutes. Instantly I felt the effects of the quantlet on how I performed. I wouldn't be sore the next day and I could easily make it through a practice going 100%. I started throwing harder, more accurate, and confidently after using the quantlet. There's a huge difference in my performance depending on if I do the quantlet or not.

Downside, if I did the quantlet before practice at 6, for a full hour I could not sleep! I spent hours wide awake in bed even after wearing my goggles and turning all electronics on airplane mode. I have modified my time to about 30 minutes and I've noticed this reduction has let me sleep.

Is there such thing as too much light?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-06T05:20:49+0300

### KB48kg — 2017-05-07T02:30:51+0300

I purchased an old house and removed all walls, ceiling and floors to the studs.
My ultimate goal is to protect my family was emf. I thought about chicken wire shielding, that will create Faraday cage and may cause problems. Also it is unclear on how to protect windows and doors. I also thought about insulating only top floor with the bedrooms. Then I saw a suggestion of using using protection on the walls and ceiling, leaving it all the floor to avoid Faradey cage issues. Obviously netting will need to be grounded. I also saw something about using Shumann resonance generator for the Faraday cages.
I searched the forum and did not find a clear advise.
I would appreciate a guidence or pointing to the place where I can find one.

### mobeus — 2017-05-31T05:36:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-house-shielding.19438/post-215157>

Magnesium oxide wallboard shields EMF. It's available. Chicken wire won't do jack.

BUT....think "repattern" not "shield". Shielding is actually a misnomer. The nnEMF is not bouncing back to where it came, and it's not being absorbed by the "shield". It's actually being repatterned. Centripetal (fractal) charge is the opposite of "radiating" charge.

Your best defense is a strong 'aura'. Do the work here and build your aura.

There is also technology available that uses high dielectric paramagnetic minerals and holograms to repattern nnEMF. I think this is much more realistic and practical than believing you can "shield" it. Not sure this works, but it's easy to implement and there's no harm in trying it.

[https://www.safespaceprotection.com/products/](https://www.safespaceprotection.com/products/)

.

# Thread: Hi from Scotland!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-scotland.19442/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ScottishEmma — 2017-05-07T14:03:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-scotland.19442/post-213584>

Hi everyone,

I first listened to a podcast with Jack on Not Just Paleo about two years ago and whilst I enjoyed it I didn't truly implement the information.

As chance would have it I listened to another NJP podcast with Dr Mercola which led me back to Jack and now I've listened to what must be my sixteenth podcast interview thanks to this site and the wonders of YouTube.

So yeah, thanks to much of what I've been listening to (and bombarding my husband with - seriously, he must be fed up of the words Jack Kruse!), we're moving from the 56 latitude in Glasgow to the 36 latitude in Malaga this September and it's all thanks to this work! I've had enough of the constant cloud cover and lack of light here and I'm so excited to move to a place where I can (happily) be outdoors and on the ground most of the day!

I got into Daniel Vitalis a while back so spring water has been in my radar for a number of years. Even though we don't flouridate our water in Scotland, tap water is still pretty nasty and I can smell the chlorine from the water in our house. I get my water from a company who have a bore hole - not technically a spring - and it's pretty good. Occasionally I'll collect from a local spring but I'm not 100% convinced on the parasite front. When we move to Malaga I've found a spring about 20 minutes from where we are.

I have blue blocking lenses for me and my kids and I'm switching off circuit breakers at night. I also have just tried my first oyster, mussel and prawn! I've been upping my game fish-wise and I'm so frickin' relived to not be so bogged down with the food paradigm anymore. (I started my whole journey with raw veganism in 2007 and have been entrenched in all the usual, paleo, keto, bulletproof etc.)

I am totally ADDICTED to getting up at 5am to watch the sky for a half hour and getting outside as much as I can. The light in the morning is waking me up in new ways and I can't wait to see how this journey unfolds.

THANK YOU, JACK! I look forward to learning more...

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-07T17:25:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-scotland.19442/post-213593>

welcome. It sounds like you are becoming a mitochondriac........and that is a good thing. [http://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/19381980.2016.1248325](http://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/19381980.2016.1248325)

# Thread: CT chamber experiment, interested?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-chamber-experiment-interested.19445/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 140
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### drezy — 2017-05-08T22:30:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-chamber-experiment-interested.19445/post-213680>

Hey all

I've been able to get $4.50 worth of ice to accommodate my wife, my son, and then me. It's not a bad use model, It's just that it often requires the alignment of 3 people's schedules. I want more "bang for the buck" as well as an incentive to CT more often.

I've begun pulling apart and testing a cheap old dehumidifier(they're all built in the same factory anyway BTW).

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/dehu-jpg.4809/)

I've also dug the hole (OK I used some child labor only because it's so cheap!) for the tub.

[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/dig-jpg.4810/)

If more than a few "like" this project, I'll promise to report every detail, f\*ck up, protip, "wish I would have", and continuing info. I promise no bulls\*t. I'll commit to this for at least 2 years and offer any technical support remotely if anyone is so inclined to repeat this. Cost 0$, 1 "like". I'll hold to that like cray cray.

Why? I want the people in the world my 12yo son grows up in to be healthy. My wife and I made the tenuous decision to have just one child. If you hand any system, I can hack it. My son is beyond my ability...

FWIW, I'd commit to report every failure and what NOT to do. I have zero interest in appearing perfect. That's a load of BS that once one is convinced, one is also doomed. I only befriend and follow people who tell me what NOT to do, exactly for this reason. N=1 resonates very much with me.

Hit "like" if you'd like the full info on someone in Central Texas trying to make a CT system work.

Full disclosure: I'm industrious but also lazy assed, and any peer pressure will help me finish this sooner!

### drezy — 2017-05-09T19:04:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-chamber-experiment-interested.19445/post-213755>

So my inspiration came from this thread:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/stock-tank-tips-tricks.946/page-4#post-84062](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/stock-tank-tips-tricks.946/page-4#post-84062)

As well as here soI won't be the first person trying this:
[https://www.icmag.com/ic/showthread.php?t=280703](https://www.icmag.com/ic/showthread.php?t=280703)

I think 1/4 HP will do if I get the insulation spot on. Based on the above site, one dehumidifier should do the trick. I may plumb in the ability to have a second system if I don't get lazy.

I'm currently planning on housing the tub in a hardieboard box and filling a 3 - 6" gap around the tub with closed cell foam insulation. I'll add a simple grounding system too.

So far I'm pretty much all talk on this other than having the hole dug and dehumidifier ready. Next up antifreeze and a heat exchanger.

### drezy — 2017-05-22T17:36:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-chamber-experiment-interested.19445/post-214425>

The very first instructions for the closed-cell spray foam insulation was:
"KEEP ALL SPRAY FOAM OUT OF REACH OF CHILDREN"

My son would not have been happy at all if I did this without him. Here he is in full hazmat getup. Doesn't he look dashing in Tyvek:

I think we did sorta alright with just a few minor things I would have done differently. R value added:

### drezy — 2017-05-23T14:50:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-chamber-experiment-interested.19445/post-214479>

I'm motivated on this project. In 4 hours I got my system from 76 down to 54 degrees then it was bedtime for me.

Actually there was one extra hour because I forgot that I only had water in the little orange heat exchanger and ended up with a block of ice surrounding my cooler coil. Ice itself doesn't conduct heat/cold as well so the system held 60 degrees until the cooler was shut off and defrosted. I added antifreeze to the orange heat exchanger and I was off to the races.

Nothing blew up, melted, or sparked so I'm calling it a good day.

System beautification and better lids for the units are next.

Note: The heat exchanger does not exchange fluids. Nobody would want to make body contact with antifreeze.

Unattractive test setup:

### drezy — 2017-05-30T19:36:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-chamber-experiment-interested.19445/post-215126>

Here's a little update for @[Sean Waters](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19464/).

We've(wife, son, and me) used this system 11 times now. My wife actually started using it regularly before bed. She's my little insomniac no more.

My old digital kitchen thermometer can apparently only measure down to 12 F ( -11C). It gets colder than that in my little heat/cold exchanger that I've taken to calling Mad Max since it does look apocalyptic for now. Someday the ugly bits will be hidden in a nice looking wooden box in a garden.

Thermal exchanger inside a [10 Gallon cooler](http://www.homedepot.com/p/Rubbermaid-10-Gal-Orange-Water-Cooler-FG1610HDORAN/202260809):

I use an 800 GPH pond pump to keep the water moving through the cooler copper coils.

Pond Pump:
[http://www.homedepot.com/p/Total-Pond-800-GPH-Pond-Pump-DD11800/206463483](http://www.homedepot.com/p/Total-Pond-800-GPH-Pond-Pump-DD11800/206463483)

The exact dehumidifier unit I've pulled apart (although almost all 50 pint units across labels were made in the same factory anyway):
[https://www.appliancefactoryparts.com/dehumidifiers/kenmore/580-54501500.html](https://www.appliancefactoryparts.com/dehumidifiers/kenmore/580-54501500.html)

This dehumidifier is over 15 years old. There's almost something moral about reusing old tech.

Energy costs going forward:

electricity: $0.11 / kwh
unit power usage while on (with pump): 650 w
cost per hour to run: $0.76

Thermo dynamics at current ambient temperatures 82 F(27.8 C):
system off = +0.25 C / hour
system on = - 5 C / hour

system on time required (at 82 F ambient) to maintain a fixed temperature = 1.2 hours / day

daily cost from here on out = $0.92

For the [benefits](https://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-6-the-ancient-pathway/) it brings all 3 of us, I think I can handle that bill.

### drezy — 2017-05-30T22:09:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-chamber-experiment-interested.19445/post-215140>

Thanks. After [CT6](https://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-6-the-ancient-pathway/) and the results I've experienced, I'm not messing around anymore.

Extra costs:
[Tyvek suits](http://www.homedepot.com/p/TYVEK-XXL-Coverall-with-Hood-and-Boot-141242-12HD/203555525) (reusable and likely not needed): $13.47
[Touch n' Foam 200 board foot closed cell spray foam](http://www.homedepot.com/p/Touch-n-Foam-200-Board-Foot-Polyurethane-2-Component-Spray-Foam-Kit-4006022200/204962748) (biggest single expense): $333
The hoses were laying around in my shed
The 1/2" copper tubing in the thermal exchanger ran $60
The grand total was $450-$525. I may have gone a bit overboard and could have spent less.

I may get a [UV water filter ($47)](https://www.amazon.com/Green-Killing-Machine-Internal-Sterilizer/dp/B001KP9B2W/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1496171155&sr=8-1&keywords=9w+uv+water) eventually to extend time between water changes.

Obviously I shut down all systems when it's tub time for quieter EMF. Also the tub will be grounded from the bottom. The bottom will be 18" underground and the grounding rod will start there and be deeper than that.

### drezy — 2017-05-30T22:24:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-chamber-experiment-interested.19445/post-215141>

> [shah78 said:](/goto/post?id=215139)
>
> You need to change the title of this post to" Quantum CT with Tim "drezy" Tool Time Taylor."

LOL! I always just wanted something cool like your tub since I first started lurking on these forums.

FWIW, I don't consider myself all that mechanically adept so it's a pretty easy project.

There nothing quite like the looks on rancher's faces when you pull a cattle tank out from storage and lay down in it. One of them eventually asked what I was using it for. I was pressed for time so I just explained that I have a condition where cooling myself helps heal faster, regulates metabolism, and resets my hormones. I just didn't explain that it was the human condition that I contracted.

My wife now sleeps much better much more regularly. She is currently under tremendous pressure to finish a project. She's been there before but never slept this well during this time. Just not having a pissed off wife is worth enough to me that I could have gold plated the damn tub in gold and it still would have been a great investment.

We focus a great deal on health and longevity, but do you ever wonder about the social and family impact of all this and the low dopamine behaviors and consequences? I think the costs of those factors are likely incalculably high in the world we created.

### drezy — 2017-06-08T15:47:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-chamber-experiment-interested.19445/post-215625>

When you are 40 and are offered a way back to virtually being 20 again you'll do whatever it takes. Better yet, virtually 20 with a 40 year old prefrontal cortex programmed with 4 decades of experience (read dumb mistakes).

The tub is 24" high, but I only need to fill in 16" because my body displaces enough water that it come up to my shoulders. At my height I'm the only one that needs bend my legs. I just bend them about 20-30 degrees. The tip tops of my knees do stick out a bit if I don't also cross my legs guru-style a bit.

The only issue now is that at 24" high once the cold stiffens you up a bit it's a little more difficult swinging that leg up. The system will eventually reside 1' down in the ground and I'll probably have a grip or shower bar near by.

Before I put it in the ground I'm waiting for my son to finish an automated thermal and pump control system so the whole system stays between 11C - 14C with no user interaction. He's working with a 12 year old prefrontal cortex though and needs a little nudging here and there. His generation would love to put it on the internet of things and monitor it from an iPad, but that won't be happening. This was a really good project to work on with a kid too since construction, electrical, and thermodynamics are all involved.

Thermal sensors we decided on:
[https://www.maximintegrated.com/en/products/analog/sensors-and-sensor-interface/DS18B20.html](https://www.maximintegrated.com/en/products/analog/sensors-and-sensor-interface/DS18B20.html)

controller and setup:
[https://create.arduino.cc/projecthu...digital-temperature-sensor-and-arduino-9cc806](https://create.arduino.cc/projecthub/TheGadgetBoy/ds18b20-digital-temperature-sensor-and-arduino-9cc806)

Relays:
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B0087ZTN08/ref=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o03\_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B0087ZTN08/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o03_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1)

Last but not least I'm shopping for one big shut off like this

The microctroller and sensors are likely engineering overkill, but my boy finished his school year with all A's and requested not to attend any camps this year so this controller and programming is his in-house "coding camp".

### drezy — 2017-06-09T05:54:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-chamber-experiment-interested.19445/post-215666>

Caroline

Yes she is sleeping way better than I've ever known her to sleep in the last 14 years.

I personally notice a body shaping change that I like, though she says there has been little weight loss.

The problem with my gal is almost Heisenberg-ish. Measurement will impact results. I do not ask much, but lately she is the most CT driven of us all. She's not admitting some positive effect to me...

My son recognizes a benefit that I haven't asked about quite yet. His "allergies" go from low to zero when he does CT. The most direct benefit that he loves is that I (coming from a paleo past) now let him have one donut a week if he does 3 CT sessions. Watching a 12 year old boy pick his donut for the week is entertaining because he wants the absolute best donut of the batch! Also, It is interesting watching a young person after CT. After the warm-up phase he raids the fridge for any high fat foods, so I keep them well stocked. My boy likes working on the project and seems to realize the value of it.

Thanks for the encouragement!

FWIW, I have a close friend who saw me lose my weight and reshape myself. He just bought the same tub and is doing a 2 week ice trial.

### drezy — 2017-06-25T17:07:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-chamber-experiment-interested.19445/post-217065>

Our high was 102F two days ago so it was a good system test.

cost per hour to run: $0.76

Thermo dynamics at current ambient temperatures ~82 F(27.8 C)~102 F(38.9 C):
system off (heat gain) = ~+0.25 C / hour~ +0.33 C / hour
system on = - 5 C / hour

system on time required (at ~82~ 102 F(38.9 C) ambient) to maintain a fixed temperature = ~1.2~1.7 hours / day

daily cost from here on out = ~$0.92~ $1.29

Pushing the daily cost closer but still under the cost of one 3 1/8 oz bag of Doritos.

### drezy — 2017-08-06T21:34:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-chamber-experiment-interested.19445/post-221049>

What happens when you start convincing your mechanical engineer buddy (also trustee and guardian of my boy if my wife and I get hit by a bus) that cold is good?

Turns out he ends up building an insulated cold tub that is almost as low as 50% the price of my tub+insulation at this stage.... Electrical engineer totally schooled by a mechanical engineer on these things. Seeing another engineer totally out-maneuver you in a project phase is painful. But, knowing that he is the backup plan for your child is priceless. Now that I know he wants to out-CT me. Bring it!

Deep thoughts: Is it a little morally questionable to spur a competition that subtly coerces my son's trustee into CT and makes him more potentially useful to me? Oh wait, nope, turns out I give a flying shit cuz I'm in it for the long game and saving myself is a minor detail.

### Da-mo — 2017-08-07T23:23:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-chamber-experiment-interested.19445/post-221112>

Nice setup!!!

My ambitions would be a bit simpler coz I'm lazy . . . . so I've always wondered if a chest freezer could handle being filled with water. . . . . .

clickety click google . . . .well whaddyaknow . . . . [https://alanreiner.com/2017/06/04/chest-freezer-ice-bathing/](https://alanreiner.com/2017/06/04/chest-freezer-ice-bathing/)

I have an old chest freezer that must be over 30 years old but still works and I need a new bigger one these days. The old one should be big enough for 1 person to sit in and even has a built in seat on one side that houses the refrigeration unit underneath - just need to work out how to jimmy a thermostat to keep it at 10-12degC . . .

> **Chest Freezer Ice Bathing**
> [alanreiner](https://alanreiner.com/author/alanreiner/) / [June 4, 2017](https://alanreiner.com/2017/06/04/chest-freezer-ice-bathing/)
> I still want to write a post about the WHM Advanced Module itself (not just what I learned from it), but I’m too excited about my new chest freezer.
> Someone in LA mentioned doing this and I instantly got excited about it, because I’ve found it to be a remarkable burden to do a real ice bath otherwise. It’s time and money to go out and buy ice for $0.25/lb, then filling and draining the bath tub is a massive waste of time and water just for one bath. Now, I have a chest freezer that I can fit in comfortably, and can be 32-40F all the time.
> **IMPORTANT: Always unplug the chest freezer before getting in, even with all the other components discussed here!**
>
>
> Whirlpool chest freezer for always-available ice bathing! (14.8 cu.ft)
> The only thing I have left to do at this point is get a couple wooden steps and some kind of mat where I can do a horse stance and dry off when I get out.
> I wanted to document what I’ve done here for anyone else who is thinking about doing this. And I do **highly recommend it**!
> **Picking a Chest Freezer**
> Doing some research, I determined that 10-12 cu ft was **probably** big enough for someone of my size (about 5’11”, 185 lbs). But I didn’t want to risk getting something too small, and I’ll never complain about having extra space to stretch out. The one I picked is approximately 15 cubic feet, and I will admit it’s pretty spacious.
> I checked craigslist, but only found one person within 20 mi who had a chest freezer in the 10-18 cu ft range, and they never responded to me so I assume they already sold it. Admittedly, that would’ve been the best way, but there just wasn’t a selection near me. So instead I had to buy something new. So I prepared for my normal large appliance battle:
> I picked out this one from a Lowes near me:
>
> -   [https://www.lowes.com/pd/Whirlpool-15-cu-ft-Chest-Freezer-White/50284169](https://www.lowes.com/pd/Whirlpool-15-cu-ft-Chest-Freezer-White/50284169)
>
> Next, I got onto Ebay, and bought a 10% coupon for Lowes for $5, the ones that are usually given to new home-owners who likely need to buy lots of new stuff, but primarily end up being sold on Ebay.
> Then I purchased a Lowes gift card from Ebay. In my case, I was able to get a gift card with $200 remaining for about $170. In both cases, the seller was able to email me the codes right away to use for an online purchase.
> Finally, I went to the Lowes website from my wife’s ebates account, which gets another couple percent off the purchase. My $400 chest freezer ended up being closer to $320 + tax. I think it said there was free delivery, too, but I was too anxious, and a neighbor with a pickup truck went with me to go pick it up the next day.
> **Accessories to Buy**
> So there’s a couple other things you will want in order to setup your freezer:
>
> -   I used a small tube of [100% Silicone Caulk](https://www.amazon.com/GE-Supreme-Silicone-Water-Ready-caulk/dp/B003YHEBBK) to seal all the seams on the inside of the freezer. I did this before I went to bed (before turning on the freezer), to be sure that it was properly sealed. It’s probably already factory sealed, but it wasn’t terribly expensive or difficult to do for my own peace of mind.
>
> -   I got an [outlet timer](https://www.amazon.com/Century-Plug-Mechanical-Timer-Grounded/dp/B00MVFF59S) to manage the duty cycle of the freezer. The freezer claims to operate at -10F to +10F, which means that if it were to be on all the time I’d end up with a giant block of ice. On the WHM facebook group, someone said that running it only a couple hours per day is enough to keep it at 32F. I set mine up for 2am-5am every day. I’ll be experimenting with more or less time depending on how much ice buildup there is.
>
> -   Although you really should unplug it before getting in, I wanted extra protection in case I forget. So I purchased a [GCFI outlet adapter](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000XU5MEG). The whole point of the GFCI is to detect when there’s a ground fault (which is what happens when you drop a plugged-in extension cord into water) and then cuts the power. US building code requires having built-in GFCI wall outlets within a certain distance of kitchen sinks, bathtubs, etc. After talking to an electrician friend about it, he said that the “Test” button on these outlets is actually creating a ground fault and should trigger the shut-off. I could’ve guessed that was what it does, but I rarely trust something that seems so easy! (UPDATE: I went to plug in my new GFCI adapter and realized that the wall outlet is already GFCI. Whoops.)
>
>
> **Cooling Down the Water**
> After sealing the seams, I filled up the freezer about 80% using a garden hose, unfortunately, the water from the hose was close to 70F so the freezer had a lot of work to do to get it down to freezing. After waiting 8 hours it had only dropped to like 61-62F and the sides of the freezer were really hot. It then occurred to me to do a calculation:
>
>
> So we’re looking at about 28.5 mega-joules to heat all the water. In the US, we have wall outlets that max out at 1,800 W, though the one thing I remembered from thermodynamics was that maximum _theoretical_ refrigerator efficiency is 21.9%. Knowing that the conversion from wall energy into the compressor energy, and transferring it to the water itself was going to be significantly less than that. I assumed about 12%, though I suspect even that is optimistic:
>
>
> So, I was looking at about 1.5 days to get this thing cool, which kind fit my observation. However, given how hot it got from just 8 hours of work, I was worried about burning out the electronics. So I sucked it up and went to the store and got nine, 8lb bags of ice. The first five bags melted pretty quickly which brought the temperature down from about 61 F to 50 F. The rest of it got it near 40 F. At this point, I turned off the freezer and let it take a break for the night. Today I plugged it in for about 4 hours and it’s now down below 40 F, with ice starting to form around the edges. Even if it’s not exactly 32, I’m perfectly content with 35-38 F.
>
>
> Freezer runs 3 hrs/day, keeps it around 36F with a little bit of ice build up around the edges.
> On that note, while my previous posts showed measurements of ~37 F in my bathtub, those were surface temperatures with ice still floating. I tried to stir it up before those measurements, but I suspect the average temperate was a bit higher. I make that conclusion after taking a **real** ice bath at the course in LA, and it was **brutal**. They had a full 3 inches of ice floating on the surface of the pool, so it was definitely ~32 F. And I was caught off-guard by how much shock and discomfort there was even after all my experience. I did the two minutes that was prescribed but I’m not sure I could’ve done a lot more (probably 4-5 min). I even got an afterdrop and started shivering a few minutes after I got out (I can’t even remember the last time I shivered!).
> **Conclusions**
> I’ve only done two soaks in this thing so far, but both have been heavenly. Not only is it brutally cold, it is so without floating ice which I find somewhat distracting. This feels like “pure” cold pressure. I am constantly reminded just how cold ice really is. But that is a good thing, since we learned in the course that part of the cold exercises is to find intense focus and produce a strong stress response from your body.
> Overall, if you are serious about Hoffing, this is a wonderful investment of time and money! Highly recommended!
> P.S. Here’s a joke I learned in the course: What do you call it when multiple people take an ice bath at the same time? Synchronized Wimming!

Like you I have rainwater and bore water supplies so pretty close to cold spring. We used to go for a 15 minute walk to a nearby waterfall pool to CT - beautiful spot in the bush where there is absolutely no cellphone coverage - but wifey saw an eel in the pool one day and that was then end of that \[sigh\].

I was thinking of getting one of these for my hot tub [https://naturalozone.co.nz/products/ozone-generator-for-spa-pool](https://naturalozone.co.nz/products/ozone-generator-for-spa-pool) - maybe one for the CT unit would be good too or just move it from one to the other as needed . . . combining CT with oxygen therapy and keeping the water clean. For algae control in the hot tub I use colloidal silver which I make very cheaply myself - I doubt if a CT tub would have much of an algae problem at those low temps but easy enough to add some - I'd probably just change the water out regularly anyway.

THis one is a coronal disharge ozone generator versus a UV ozone generator. Perhaps using a UV type generator might also fulfill the UV LED function???? Coronal disharge is supposed to be more reliable but I wonder if the UV type might have some added benefit by coding light information into the ozone and water . . . . I guess that's a question for a QED boffin.
