### LieselK — 2019-08-23T23:39:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-is-a-choice-kris-journal.19460/post-276522>

You mentioned in the other thread: "But maybe I simply need to work on having the ability to give time and space while still being committed?"

There's so many factors right? I will terminate relationships that are destructive and abusive, but if the other person is destructive to themselves and I can hold my own boundaries to give time and space and still be there if they choose to come back to me then I can still be committed. The commitment looks different in each situation. Commitment to a current (lost) friendship of mine is that I don't burn the bridge and am willing to receive true friendship if that person ever really wants it again. I can't make that person well or make them want the beauty and intimacy of friendship. It makes me sad that they are in such a crappy place, but it doesn't affect ME like it would if I was low redox/low dope. I'm still me doing what I've always done and ready and willing to engage (in healthy relationships.) And in turn, a friendship of mine that was difficult from the beginning when we were introduced 4 years ago has recently THRIVED and I wouldn't have wanted to miss that for the world. It took a lot of practice along the way of me doing what I need to do for me and taking space when I needed it, but I set the best example for my friend and she is so grateful. People are my jam, gathering people is my jam... we're not all made that way, but I can't and shouldn't deny that part of me. So then a lot depends on how you are made and what your calling is.

As for spouses... I find it hardest when they don't protect their own dopamine status from themselves! But then... his n=1 is not mine and his journey is not mine even though we entangle. The byproduct of our recent decision to move our family is that he has a job that enables him to soak up more sun and he's committed to helping me get well. It's a continual process here.

### kris90 — 2019-08-24T17:47:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-is-a-choice-kris-journal.19460/post-276560>

For sure! I'm having an easy time with friends. They know I'm always here for them, but it's very easy for me to give them space and focus on improving my ownself.

Spouses is a whole different ballgame. I feel the blue light/EMF toxicity from others, so if a spouse chooses not to make wise choices in their own outings, I feel directly impacted as if I was exposed to the same damaging frequencies. I feel my own melanopsin/retinol bond being broken and melatonin being destroyed, and then I become the person I know I am NOT truly. Spouses are supposed to bring out the best in us, or atleast have a neutral affect. We should be able to be the best we can be based on our own decisions and ability to use nature to raise redox and dopamine status, but if a spouse is negatively affecting our ability to do so, to me, it does not feel worth it. Whether they are our soulmate or not. So I'd like to be able to give time and space, but the problem is if I'm being indirectly zapped because of a spouse making unwise choices in their environmental exposures, substance usage and circle of 6, I can't help but feel trapped...

### caroline — 2019-08-25T01:42:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-is-a-choice-kris-journal.19460/post-276584>

I feel so heartbroken for everyone here who is struggling with these issues. I am incredibly lucky to have had an extremely supportive and loving and kind DH. He supported me 150% and I guess, upon looking back, he always put me first - as I did with him.

I can fully understand when Jack says we have to love ourself first - before someone else can.

After my DH passed I was in a black hole for awhile ......but I did climb out of it and got me lots of sun and sand and sea. I think it probably took me about 18 months to become fully functioning and become a whole person again. It is definitely baby steps to move forward.

But, of course, I had jackkruse.com! I took on board everything Jack said and I did eventually blossom.

I can't imagine being with someone who isn't fully supportive. It would be soul destroying.

Our job, as I see it, Is to strive to reach OUR full potential and live our life to the max. Another person, who joins our circle of six has to want all that for themselves and for us too. I could not be with someone who wouldn't strive to be their very best and who doesn't support me to be my very best.

My relationship now is so intense and so full of love and we are committed to being the best we can be for ourselves and each other.

There are fireworks tho!

Marriage is not an easy gig .....but I do think both people have to be on the same page.....at least most of the time.

### kris90 — 2019-09-25T21:10:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-is-a-choice-kris-journal.19460/post-278881>

I had a spiritual awakening and want to share some thoughts:

Stress is not technically real. Our thoughts, fears and worries create the reality we get. All information we read is bullshit. It's based on manmade beliefs. The only information that is accurate is what comes from nature. Research is no longer a necessity in my books. The answers seek us from the Universe not the other way around. We just need a connection.

I moved away from the diet, exercise and supplements paradigm a long time ago but unfortunately I was trapped in a different paradigm with strict rules to live by. There is no toxicity or evil. Just false beliefs that manifest as those negative forces. If we focus on positivity there is no stress.

When you shine light into a dark room, you light up the room. But you cannot shine darkness on light.

I have learned a lot here and am forever grateful for the message to reconnect to nature. I always have and always will. But unfortunately, this paradigm has also created fear, anxiety and obsession. The deeper I go down this path, I begin to realise I am isolating myself. I will end up giving up my family, loved ones, friends, hobbies and other joys of life for the fear that I will lose energy, when the reality is that we are all ONE in this Universe. What you believe is the reality you get. If you think blue light and EMF will destroy you, they will. But if you can allow yourself to oscillate at a higher vibration, you can overcome any perceived "evil". Energy balances stress. Stress is something we create. We are all good. Every decision you make has a positive outcome. In society we choose to create our own negativity.

As I feel myself oscillating at a higher vibration, I began to make some changes and seek joy in life. I went to a concert at night without my blue blocking glasses. I focused on energizing myself with the vibrations of the sound and the lyrics that resonate with me, and the energy and joy of the people around me. I had no issues with sleep, headaches, mood, energy, etc. I chose to become immune to this false stress. Instead I chose to experience joy and a sense of community. I watched the people around me sing and dance and admired their beautiful souls. Normally I would think they are all idiots being under blue light at night drinking beer and eating pizza. But that OLD ME was unhappy and miserable, and the people at the concert were happy and fulfilled.

Our illnesses are created by our fears. If we cannot let go of our fears, we disrupt energy flow, and this alters E=mc2 and this is how disease manifests. The word disease is really just dis-ease and nothing else. If we create a fear of blue light and EMF, then we will become ill when exposed to that environment.

The world is not coming to an end. We are simply losing are connection to the Universe. We think we are alone, but we are really all ONE. Our thoughts and feelings, fears and anxieties are transmitted to the Universe and reflected back at us. If we perceive the world as evil, we will experience a world of evil. If we perceive the world as a beautiful loving place, we will experience beauty and love. Everyone around us and everything we perceive is a direct reflection of what energy we put out. This is why you attract what you emit.

Our species is not dying, we are simply unawakened. This is creating a false reality. Things are not what they seem because of our beliefs. We constantly seek the truth, but only the truth can seek us. We need to give up control and obsession of knowing. Again, we are receivers of information and our life is simply a mirror.

We are all perfect. Every choice we make is right. Life will recalibrate itself no matter what road you choose, like a GPS when you choose to ignore the instructions. We have the freedom to do what we feel. We often feel what we believe and this is why we choose suffering most of the time. This is why the world is ill at large scale.

### kris90 — 2019-09-25T21:11:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-is-a-choice-kris-journal.19460/post-278882>

Do we really need to sleep 8 hours a night? Do we need blackout curtains and complete darkness at night? Or is this a belief we create? Do we live in fear at night of exposure to light? Why do we create this belief? The stars and moon shine every night. There is NO darkness.

Last night I took a piss at 2:30 AM outside. I got up, walked out my bedroom door onto my deck, down the steps and connected with Mother Earth. I stood for a few minutes and looked up at the sky. A beautiful starry night looked down upon me. Energy and information traveled from multiple dimensions to my body and the strengthened magnetic field of the Earth increased my download speed.

The other night I set up camp in my backyard. I sat by the fire until about 11:30 PM, and laid in my tent on the ground under the stars and moonlight all night. I didn't sleep much, but I didn't need to. I love the night time energy. Animals are active at night. They don't always sleep. And sometimes they sleep during the day. They live their life based on intuition and the messages they receive from the Universe. There is no such thing as a paradigm or health protocols. They just live. Here's to the beginning of my life.​

### kris90 — 2019-09-25T21:50:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-is-a-choice-kris-journal.19460/post-278886>

Vermont 2019 planted the seed for me, but my fears quickly took over and created a tarp blocking my reality just like my blue blockers have blocked colors in my life.

Vermont 2019 taught me that community is the #1 aspect for optimal health. Beth Lambert is on a mission to wake the people up to help our children and future generations, but we need to work as a TEAM. It does not matter what diet you think is optimal, supplements or any other belief, it simply matters that we ALL want to achieve success and share our love and support! Jason Prall demonstrated that all supercentenarians embraced their sense of community. Whether they were out all night partying with family and friends at festivals, or connecting to nature through gardening, they knew how to get energy from their environment. They did not read into the news and all the "bad" shit happening. Zach Bush taught us that a cancer cell is the most isolated/lonely type of cell in our body. It is essentially an energy vampire and lacks love and belonging. We need to show all our cells love and re-establish a sense of community at the cellular level.

Zach told us we are all beautiful and perfect. There is NOTHING wrong with ANY of us. None of the speakers in Vermont have their OWN tribes. There is no exclusive access or initiation to become apart of the mission to improve human health and wellness. Everyone is welcome. This is accurate because we are all ONE in this Universe. All of our energy is the same. We need to be careful with our obsession over our beliefs. It's no different than religion. It disrupts the connection we have with one another and the Universe.

I learned this recently when I realised how much connection I needed to nature via sunlight, grounding, and CT to feel my best. As soon as I disconnected and went back to society, I lost energy quickly. My battery was discharged. So I had a choice: fuck everyone and live in isolation like a tree and be connected 100% to nature (like a laptop with no battery that has to be plugged in to the outlet), or learn to hold my charge by seeking love and energy indirectly from nature through people and community. I went with the latter and now I choose the life I want to live. The reason I got so drained from people is because my beliefs were different than 99% of the people. I did not fit in. I was constantly judging others for "poor choices" they make and "low dopamine". Well I learned there is NO SUCH THING as a poor choice. Your life is built by your choices. I don't regret my choices in the past, so why would I choose to judge my own choices of the future or the ones others' choose? Instead, I am choosing to embrace my environment. When kids are running around my house screaming and making messes, I will become a kid and mimic them. When it's calm, quiet and peaceful, I will relax and be still. Life gives you subtle hints and messages. Crazy kids running and screaming are not blue lit toxic. They are teaching us how to remove the stick from our ass as adults and relearn to live and have fun in the present moment.

Worrying about our relationships, finances, blue light, EMF and evil in the world creates the SAME stress. Learning to love and be free eliminates all the stress. This is my new path. Live like a flower. Adapt to the environment. Do not resist. Do not seek to escape. Be aware.

### JanSz — 2019-09-25T22:19:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-is-a-choice-kris-journal.19460/post-278894>

Keep going Kris.
\-----------------------
I watched the people around me sing and dance and admired their beautiful souls. Normally I would think they are all idiots being under blue light at night drinking beer and eating pizza. But that OLD ME was unhappy and miserable, and the people at the concert were happy and fulfilled.
\----------------------
So I had a choice: fuck everyone and live in isolation like a tree and be connected 100% to nature (like a laptop with no battery that has to be plugged in to the outlet), or learn to hold my charge by seeking love and energy indirectly from nature through people and community.

### LieselK — 2019-09-25T23:09:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-is-a-choice-kris-journal.19460/post-278900>

I have been thinking on your post from Aug 24, but could never put my thoughts into words so I never responded. I love what you are sharing and how you explain your thinking. I'm currently enjoying a respite from community at large, but intentionally getting into it in my new place. In the end, if loving others required me to "give up" the things I am doing for myself I would. So much is gained by pouring into other people and loving on them. In moving, I'm learning to hone my skills on my own family and make sure I am actually loving them well first. It feels good and right. I will never regret pouring into them no matter what happens with my own health. There is plenty of room for "me" and "us"... when you're in community that honors and respects one another then we can do it all even if we each do things differently. I have wonderful beautiful friends who gave up time, energy and money to help me move to Florida because they accept that I need to do it. They love me where I am at.

### Cindy Domitrovits — 2019-09-25T23:17:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-is-a-choice-kris-journal.19460/post-278901>

Wow, Im so proud to call you my son, we have both come so far in our journey and everything you say above regarding your awakening just connects us all the more. When you choose to live in fear, you will get sick. There is no fear. Trust in yourself and your intuition and the Universe always delivers. We all have the power to tune into the energy of love and the inner vision of light. We are always pushing to make things happen rather than allowing things to attract naturally. The Universe does not respond well to fear based beliefs and frantic energy, it vibrates at a positive frequency. In order to co-create with it, your energy must align with that frequency. When you are living in a fear based mindset, you go into a comatose state. All these false perceptions deny you of the power you truly have but have forgotten. I'm so happy we are both ditching all the news media and fear based shit that everyone seems to e focusing on. The veil has been lifted and we are living life to the fullest. I now understand why I had to workout like I did for all those years everyday for hours at a time. I could not hold my energy. I'm holding my energy and always rising my vibration to a higher level now. It is so incredible to reach this state of mind and be able to truly enjoy the present moment and always be mindful. Life is so good and we are all LOVE!!!! Thank you Kris90 for all you have taught me.

In moving, I'm learning to hone my skills on my own family and make sure I am actually loving them well first. It feels good and right. I will never regret pouring into them no matter what happens with my own health.

You will see that you are not giving up anything for yourself by giving love to others. We are all one with this universe. Loving another is loving yourself and loving yourself is loving another.

Nothing bad will happen to your health from pouring your love and energy into them. Nobody else makes us ill. Nobody else depletes us of energy. We make ourselves ill and deplete our own energy with negative thoughts and fears. Being one with the universe (i.e. nature: sun, earth, trees, water or people/animals) allows us to tap energy. If we are trapped indoors, we must get creative in our ways to tap energy. We can do this through food, music, talking or touching others, looking at pictures of memories we have made, etc.

Believing that others have toxic energy and that artificial light and EMFs are bad are just negative thoughts that deplete your energy and decharge your human battery. They bring you down to a lower vibration. Energy trumps everything, including circadian biology in my books.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-11-20T18:02:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-is-a-choice-kris-journal.19460/post-282562>

Another study shows why docs like Zach Bush have zero understanding of how sunlight sculpts the microbiome. If he did he would never try to sell any human restore.......he's sell them on vacations to the sun. This is how Vitamin D is created in the body by UVB LIGHT exposure on the skin and NOT by oral supplementation. This ultimately leads to big improvements in gut bacteria populations. Looks like another win for Kruse Longevity Center in stomping out marketers for the public.
Skin Exposure to Narrow Band Ultraviolet (UVB) Light Modulates the Human Intestinal Microbiome
Microbiol., 24 October 2019
Participants who took vitaminD supplements and use the narrowband had no improvement in microbiome diversity.
Participants who used narrowband uvb light only had significant improvements.
Bacteria from several families were enriched in the group that did not take prior vitamin D supplements.
This is the first study to show changes in intestinal microbiome in response to Narrowband-UVB skin exposure.
This evidence suggests the existence of a novel skin-gut axis that could be used to promote intestinal homeostasis and health.
[https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fmicb.2019.02410/full](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fmicb.2019.02410/full)

### kris90 — 2020-01-17T16:38:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-is-a-choice-kris-journal.19460/post-284719>

Had a great time in Mexico and met so many wonderful people! I am glad to be back at home though embracing the cold, ketotic lifestyle I thrive on. I feel better in Canada than I do in Mexico, and my n=1 tells me to stay in my natural habitat versus hopping on a plane to a radically different environment. I had a dehydration effect in Mexico from the strong sun and EMFs. I got nice color because of my solar callus, but my sleep wasn't great and I could not handle carbohydrate electrons as well as I thought I would.

Other news: I'm finally taking advantage of the PPP for performance. After my history of bodybuilding fueling on high carb diets and using anabolic steroids, and learning what I've learned here, I've abandoned that lifestyle long ago. I don't think we should abandon our hobbies and the things that give us joy, but I think it is wise to explore new approaches if we've been hurt by them in the past. I've done just that. I've radically improved my environment in everyway. I've been cold adapted drinking Canadian glacial water and ketotic/fat adapted for easily the past 24-36 months. So it was time to bring back weightlifting into my life, this time utilising the PPP from ketosis + CT. I began putting this pathway to the test back in the fall and the results so far after 3 months:

Packed on 15 lbs of pure muscle.
Increased my squat by 165 lbs.
Increased my deadlift by 180 lbs.
Increased my bench press by 80 lbs.
Increased my overhead press by 60 lbs.

I'm still going strong. These gains are on par with steroid gains on a high carbohydrate diet. My mission is to become a Canadian Sherpa. Superhuman strength, while maintaining a low bodyweight.

> Before I moved to my current location, I was drinking fluoridated city water from Ottawa. My experience? A lot more hunger while fasting. I could not access the PPP.

Then it looks like **you still could do better** (regardless of how good you doing now).
...

may want to get

osmolality**
check.

Dr. Boros is saying that he always check ADH in people that he studies.
**Most of them have ADH=~1**
that is supposed to mean that most (like 90+%) of Americans have
diabetes insipidus

### kris90 — 2020-01-22T17:33:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-is-a-choice-kris-journal.19460/post-284911>

Honestly [@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) I really believe that our own mind creates a lot of the stress. When we think we MUST do something or else we'll get hurt (i.e. unplugging/shutting power off, avoiding EMFs, blue light, etc), we create that reality. When we live in that state chronically, we are inducing a chronic stress/worry. Our thoughts become a reality, the brain cannot differentiate.

I stopped fearing this past year, and am noticing that everything is just fine. The burden of worrying being gone has improved my health more than being overly cautious while living in fear. My environment is great at home. Could it be better? Sure. So could everyone's if we started sleeping on the ground and living in huts again in the middle of nowhere.

Your mindset is your one of your #1 tools for health. It can hurt you or heal you, depending on how you use it. Believing you MUST do more to fix your environment will give you that reality, and you will be trapped in suboptimal until you take dramatic measures to fix it. But you know what follows you in every environment? Emotional trauma. Most people need to fix their INNER environment before their OUTER environment. Once you do that, I believe you can thrive anywhere.

### JanSz — 2020-01-22T18:25:50+0200

[@kris90](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19157/)
Have you thought about the food that we eat? Why some may be better than the other.
Why hydroponically grown food may be grossly missing important nutrients.
That would go for stuff grown on the soil and the animals we eat that first ate that food or the other.

These days farmers are able to grow food almost on concrete slabs, because of a variety of fertilizers.
Way back there was a land where farmers could grow their crops and others where they could not.
That may be described here:
**Agricultural Land Classification**
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Agricultural\_Land\_Classification\[/B\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Agricultural_Land_Classification[/B])

Eventually, it comes down to, that good places for farming had plants and animals living there and dying for a zillion of years.
That type of soil contains
Fulvic acids
Humic acid
They are missing everywhere else.
I was trying to get some comments from @Jackkruse but he is busy.
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...id-and-humic-substances-organic-matter.24603/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/why-dirt-is-good-fulvic-acid-and-humic-substances-organic-matter.24603/)

People benefiting from eating mumijo, mumio, mumiyo, moomiyo, shilajit are actually supplementing with fulvic and humic acids.

I try not to overthink it too much. I do meat orders with a local farm. Everything is grassfed/organic. As for plant food, I'm not really eating any in the winter, and even in the summer now, it won't be more than a serving of something. I think as long as we stick to 90% animal fats/proteins, we'll be OK. Especially if the animals are in a natural environment depleting deuterium via grounding, sunlight and generally connecting to nature.

Given I only eat 1-2 meals a day, there's a lot of fasting, so I know my body is in an optimal state to detox any crap that I shouldn't be consuming.

### kris90 — 2020-01-22T22:03:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-is-a-choice-kris-journal.19460/post-284931>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=284928)
>
> I texted her some live blood slides yesterday.
>
> Maybe I need to bring her to this forum.

I've made my wife well aware of all my findings, and she has taken to it pretty well. But she breaks "rules". Her diet is what most here would consider suboptimal, she drinks a good amount of wine and beer, eats a lot of dark chocolate, won't wear blue blockers, refuses CT etc. But you know what? She's got a DAMN good mindset and that's what's saving her. She does not stress, does not worry or fear and has endless amounts of energy. She celebrates life each and every day. She does not fall victim to paradigms or belief systems and is not a slave to strict rules to live by. We live in an optimal, low EMF environment, and there's no blue light in our home, so I have done my part to protect her and my kids. The rest is on her. But damnit does this woman thrive. If she's tired, she will rest. If she's energetic, she will go and have a good time. No matter how little sleep, she is up early with the kids or cleaning the house. She is a rockstar, and I applaud her for living her life to the fullest. She's never had a health ailment to deal with. So I'm gonna let her do her thing.

BTW, I got called out at the Mexico 2020 Members Event when I opened up a bit about how I am done nagging my family to make changes. I was supposed to "go all in for the people I love". But what does that even mean? Does it mean forcing these rules on people? Nobody understood my context. My family is in optimal health, and we are moving away from the fear/stress of all the bad shit in the world. Could their diet be better? Could they make more effort to block blue light? Sure. But that's on them, not me. If I want to eat 100% carnivore, I will do it. If my family still wants some fruit/veggies, so be it. Humans have freewill. If our choices hurt us, we can make the change and learn. If we refuse to change, then we continue to suffer. I've suffered the most out of anyone in my family. I reached my threshold. It drove me to change. If a family member has not yet been there, I have NO right to take that experience and personal growth away from them.

### JanSz — 2020-01-22T23:38:54+0200

My grandfather spent most of his working life in the NYC metropolitan area. Craftsman, he could make a fine carriage wheels. (Precise work).
His wife and 3 children were living in Poland (supported by him).
He was not able to get a decent job in Poland. Few cross Atlantic trips (to impregnate wife).
Some part of that time was even before there was Poland (before WWI).
\---
My two cousins in Poland have jobs in Poland, (almost all their married life) their wives work in Germany, seeing their husbands every couple of months.
One of their sons moved with his wife to Germany.
The other son is married, he lives with his father, his new wife works on the other side of Poland. Both educated, computer work.
\---
Educated, making "large" money, I was still not able to make a decent living in Communist Poland.
Immigrated (legally) to the USA. (After savings most of my earnings for 8 years, those "large" money were not able to buy a $300 airplane ticket. I had to borrow. (Others were making much less).
It was too long to wait for my girlfriend to join me in the USA.
I went back to Poland just to impregnate her.
Eventually, we all had a rather good life in the USA until my DW died in Oct 2017.

.

I love this Chris! I love how you have figured out what works for you, what makes you happy and how to integrate all this in your own family. We each need to find our own way and at the same time be supportive of those close to us .......our life partner is not a clone of us. They have needs, wants and desires that are separate from ours ....and isn't that how it should be?

Of course we need to have common goals and mutual love and respect. My DH is certainly on board with a healthy lifestyle and he thrives. He also drinks beer and loves cheesecake and eats cookies sometimes. He makes sure we get to the ocean every morning for sunrise and exercise and sometimes he has to remind me to put my blue blockers on.

I eat carnivore ....because it makes me feel so much better. Tony can eat pretty much anything and he is health and happy.

When we were in Europe for 7 weeks we thrived. Every couple of days we slept in a different bed, met new people, enjoyed their homes and there was wifi everywhere.
We slept so well, we tried all kinds of new foods and drinks. we were actively engaged and walked a ton. We saw amazing things that will stay with us the rest of our life.

It seems to me that life is about being actively engaged and enjoying family and friends ....and doing what we love.

People in our life with negative energy have to go.

Chris - your wife sounds like an amazing woman. It is wonderful that you can appreciate all her great qualities without putting her in a box.

Isn't this what we have to do with our children? We lead by example - but they will eventually choose their own path.

### Sue-UK — 2020-01-23T11:31:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-is-a-choice-kris-journal.19460/post-284946>

> [kris90 said:](/goto/post?id=284931)
>
> I've made my wife well aware of all my findings, and she has taken to it pretty well. But she breaks "rules". Her diet is what most here would consider suboptimal, she drinks a good amount of wine and beer, eats a lot of dark chocolate,

Well I'm going to stick my neck out (not for the first time ) and say any pure carnivore diet "rules" may not be suitable permanently for the majority of women. For example, even if silicon is not depleted taking aluminium out of the body, over the longer term a diet mainly based on land based animal muscle meats and animal fat could leave a woman deficient in silicon - because a female gives up silicon to a fetus, and supplies it in breast milk. If she does not replenish her silicon stores, either through food or a silicon rich mineral water, it could affect future pregnancies, or after menopause increase the risk of osteoporosis, particularly at northern latitudes. How much intake is optimal is an n=1, but its in human skin, so any deficiency there is also likely to affect how people handle sunlight, and perhaps even make vitamin D. Whether its my choice of oats, and root veg peel, or beer, wine and dark chocolate, they are all sources of silicon, which in an inner doctor way would be a better option than deficiency.

### kris90 — 2020-01-23T15:00:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-is-a-choice-kris-journal.19460/post-284948>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=284933)
>
> If y'all lived in LA none of your family's health would be close to what it is now though of course.
>
> You can't break the rule of "background" RF, what was making you very sick before the move away from the city.
>
> If there's no BL inside the home, no need to wear blockers.

How much energy do you spend stressing and fearing over it though? That alone is probably really amplifying things. I'm sure it's not a great area and you have a few options. If moving is something that resonates with you, and you feel it is the right thing to do, then make your escape plan. But in the mean time, remain calm and do what you can to feel optimal (i.e. go to the beach for some sun/grounding, do some CT, eat epi-paleo, practice meditation, etc).

I made the move about 4 years ago. Originally the move was to be in a more quiet, rural area and have some privacy and space in my yard. I had no clue about EMFs. It just felt right to me, and I've always wanted a bedroom that goes out to the back deck. After obsessing over EMFs all these years, I realised that my mind was created more stress than the frequencies. Now that I have mastered my mind, I go back out into EMF hellholes from time to time, and I do OK. I don't let fear take over. I practice deep breathing, I enjoy what I am doing and the people I am around. I focus on the positives only. And I'm always OK. I sleep fine, no headaches, and am a lot happier than if I had stayed home by myself in the dark. I went to concerts this past summer without my blue blockers. I had a hell of a good time, and I slept like a baby. In Mexico, the first night I slept OK and that was after 2 stiff drinks. It was just my Mom and I. The rest of the trip I didn't sleep as well. Once I encountered the members the following day, all everyone talked about was how bad the EMF was at the resorts, and pointing out all the cell towers. I wasn't even focused on it, and then bam, my sleep was disrupted. It's because I let that get in my head.

I'm not saying we're all completely immune to EMFs, I'm just trying to bring to light another perspective, and that is your mind and just how powerful it is. Is your woman very ill? How is her health? Does she worry about the EMFs? Sometimes you have to lead by example. Fix you FIRST, and when everyone sees how well you're doing, eventually they will be curious. But humans are stubborn, so you'll need to be patient.

### kris90 — 2020-01-23T15:06:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-is-a-choice-kris-journal.19460/post-284950>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=284940)
>
> I love this Chris! I love how you have figured out what works for you, what makes you happy and how to integrate all this in your own family. We each need to find our own way and at the same time be supportive of those close to us .......our life partner is not a clone of us. They have needs, wants and desires that are separate from ours ....and isn't that how it should be?
>
> Of course we need to have common goals and mutual love and respect. My DH is certainly on board with a healthy lifestyle and he thrives. He also drinks beer and loves cheesecake and eats cookies sometimes. He makes sure we get to the ocean every morning for sunrise and exercise and sometimes he has to remind me to put my blue blockers on.
>
> I eat carnivore ....because it makes me feel so much better. Tony can eat pretty much anything and he is health and happy.
>
> When we were in Europe for 7 weeks we thrived. Every couple of days we slept in a different bed, met new people, enjoyed their homes and there was wifi everywhere.
> We slept so well, we tried all kinds of new foods and drinks. we were actively engaged and walked a ton. We saw amazing things that will stay with us the rest of our life.
>
> It seems to me that life is about being actively engaged and enjoying family and friends ....and doing what we love.
>
> People in our life with negative energy have to go.
>
> Chris - your wife sounds like an amazing woman. It is wonderful that you can appreciate all her great qualities without putting her in a box.
>
> Isn't this what we have to do with our children? We lead by example - but they will eventually choose their own path.

It's wonderful when you can live in harmony with your spouse, but let go of your control over their decisions! As I have mentioned, we have freewill, and we cannot take that away from anyone especially our loved ones! The lifestyle as a whole is more important than the diet, and our experience on this earth is VERY important! If eating some treats makes you happy and you don't stress over it, it probably won't hurt you! But if you live a restricted life, and live in fear of eating the wrong foods, or being exposed to this or that toxin, you end up paying for it because you are telling your body it's "bad". When you stop labelling things as "good" or "bad", you become free, and it feels great!

As for our children, I cannot agree more! I brought up this perspective a bit in Mexico, but nobody resonated with it. I was called out for allowing my 5 year old to make her own decisions. Apparently, her brain has not developed enough to make her own decisions, and I should be forcing her to eat what I want her to eat, and forcing her to go out into the sun. I've tried that before, and it doesn't work. Letting her take the lead works a lot better, and she is quite the intelligent little girl. I love giving her the freedom to make decisions. Sometimes she makes a decision that leads to suffering, and other times, she makes brilliant decisions that lead to reward. Who am I to make decisions for her? I don't care if she's 5 or 16. I am here to hold her hand and guide her, and let her make some mistakes and learn from them. Not make every decision for her.​

All I am saying is don't let it consume your life. Come up with your escape plan, and work toward it. If you're holding back because of other people, you aren't doing yourself any favors. Once you have a plan, you should be more at ease. In the meantime, do what you can to stay healthy, and keep a positive attitude. Enjoy everyday and make the most of your time. Even in suffering we can find pleasure. Ever seen terminally ill patients? They have the best attitudes in the world, and live every day to the fullest. They don't waste anytime complaining, dwelling or fearing. They accept their fight and make the most of their time. You can do the same.

As for the 6th extinction, I completely disagree. I think 2020 is going to be a critical year in history. The energy vibrations are changing on this planet. Humans are beginning to wake up. All the "terrors" we fear and observe are really blessings in disguise to awaken us. It'll be a slow process, but I think we'll make good progress this year and the things are really going to take a turn for the better. I know it.

On this point, I'm going to disagree with you. Talk to anyone with a long standing chronic illness, especially one of the "invisible illnesses" and they've jumped through hoops doing the "emotional work", usually first because doctors can't figure out what's wrong with them physically and due to that, like to tell them it's all in their head. So they go down that rabbit hole of emotional work, usually without results. And all it often takes to start the healing process for these types is a change of environment or tweaking things physically. Often they need to move out of the moldy house. Or if they have chronic pain, they may need to move to the sun. And on it goes.

Often it's NOT about emotional work that needs to be done, but rather, physical changes that need to be made.

### Sue-UK — 2020-01-24T13:32:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-is-a-choice-kris-journal.19460/post-285039>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=284949)
>
> Hi Sue;
> Do you have any thoughts on how (even the best today's organic food) is grown?
> Way back then we had good soils and bad soils.
> These days with technology you can grow food anywhere, including no soil at all.
> The rest of my question is on the post above, [#261](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/optimal-is-a-choice-kris-journal.19460/page-14#post-284913)
> [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...choice-kris-journal.19460/page-14#post-284913](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/optimal-is-a-choice-kris-journal.19460/page-14#post-284913)
>
> What happens to the human body that is low on:
> Fulvic acids
> Humic acid
>
> Why supplementing with it is helpful?
>
> ......................

I remember when those supplements were the rage years ago. These days I'd be more interested to know how much fulvic acid or humic acid there is in the traditional Inuit diet, or the diets of the blue zones of the world.

The interesting thing to me is sprouted seeds - they can be grown in a jar on the window ledge with just sunlight and water. And the nutritional content of the seed compared to the sprouted seeds in different. Either greatly increased amounts, or nutrients that weren't in the original seed or the water. No soil, no added nutrients in the water. Biological transmutation, using light and water, melanin (energy) and possibly silicon or other minerals (mass) in the seed. In the original seed there is enough nutrients (and mass) and potential energy to generate life, but usually not enough to go on and reproduce before dying, because my sprouted seed isn't parasitic, and hasn't formed symbiotic relationships. Its eaten before the plant has completed its purpose (reproduction before death), and that's where I think soil importance comes in.

I see the gut of a human as similar to the root system of the plant. There's the root system nutrient uptake, and the symbiotic relationships between the roots and the bacteria and fungi that are making nutrients the plant needs to take its life cycle full circle. If you look at insectivore and carnivorous plants, they have adapted to bring in nutrients they need because either the root system or the root system bacteria cant provide it either from _natural_ poor soil conditions, or their position outside access to full sunlight means they can't transmute.

It seems to be more about what we are doing to the soil microbes by our agricultural choices, than the soil itself. Mono crops, harvesting a crop rather than a plant decomposing completely, insecticides, pesticides etc. If you translate that into human nutrition, if the human gut is fed a largely processed or a monodiet, with insecticides and pesticide, aluminium etc, the microbiome will lack diversity. If I then deny any "inner doctor" messages as to what to eat to compensate, in the northern latitude where solar panel human photosynthesis is already limited several months of the year, a fulvic acid or humic acid supplement is pissing in the wind. My thought experiment leads me to the idea that if you sprayed humic and fulvic acid over a heavily herbicide/insecticide sprayed mono crop field, would it compensate for all the baddies? I doubt it, the baddies would still hit my microbiome, or be tied up in the meat and fat of the animal fed the crop. So for me its a much wider issue than just humic and fulvic acids. If my problems aren't caused by a lack of fulvic or humic acid supplements, they just become another thing to amuse my logical "left brain" while I continue to mitochondrially unravel.

### JanSz — 2020-01-24T14:28:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-is-a-choice-kris-journal.19460/post-285040>

When I see discussion on humic and fulvic acids, that discussion degrades very quickly to talking about minerals or supplements that can be fabricated by man.

I like these definitions better:
Humic Acid a mineral rich compound created from seaweed that existed millions of years ago.
Humic Acid is an extremely powerful substance that has preserved organic minerals and trace elements from ancient living organic fruits and vegetables as well as ancient seaweed. It’s a unique substance that cannot be chemically reproduced, and it works rapidly in the body to replenish nutrient levels.

Fulvic Acid is another ‘deep earth’ substance that’s extracted in special regions of the world containing these rich organic minerals at about 200-250 feet underground.
\-----------------------

The idea is closer to comparing very good tasty steak in a good (organic) restaurant
and the tough steak I ate in Brazil where each cow had many hectares to roam around.

......

### kris90 — 2020-01-24T19:02:21+0200

> On this point, I'm going to disagree with you. Talk to anyone with a long standing chronic illness, especially one of the "invisible illnesses" and they've jumped through hoops doing the "emotional work", usually first because doctors can't figure out what's wrong with them physically and due to that, like to tell them it's all in their head. So they go down that rabbit hole of emotional work, usually without results. And all it often takes to start the healing process for these types is a change of environment or tweaking things physically. Often they need to move out of the moldy house. I think we could all agree it's both. Some people tackle their illness and stress from more the inner/emotional/spiritual side, and others from the outer environment. I have certainly seen a lot of people here make improvements from the physical side, but I don't see a lot of full reversals. Jack himself I can tell holds a lot of resentment and emotional turmoil inside, and I believe this is negatively impacting his healing.

There are many people who have cured themselves of cancer. Spontaneous remission I believe is what they call it. This is usually done without having to move to the equator or even mitigating blue light or EMF much. It usually has to do with tapping into inner energy and releasing bad energy. So I think tackling the equation from both sides is probably wise, and I think that may be what's missing in this community. We need to focus on mind, body and spirit.

### Sue-UK — 2020-01-24T19:19:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-is-a-choice-kris-journal.19460/post-285068>

> [kris90 said:](/goto/post?id=284950)
>
>
> I brought up this perspective a bit in Mexico, but nobody resonated with it. I was called out for allowing my 5 year old to make her own decisions. Apparently, her brain has not developed enough to make her own decisions, and I should be forcing her to eat what I want her to eat, and forcing her to go out into the sun. I've tried that before, and it doesn't work. Letting her take the lead works a lot better, and she is quite the intelligent little girl. I love giving her the freedom to make decisions. Sometimes she makes a decision that leads to suffering, and other times, she makes brilliant decisions that lead to reward. Who am I to make decisions for her? I don't care if she's 5 or 16. I am here to hold her hand and guide her, and let her make some mistakes and learn from them. Not make every decision for her.​

Stopping a child doing something downright dangerous is one thing, but that covered then experiential learning is important. So I don't think we can teach a child _how_ to make good decisions, if instead we teach them _what_ to think by taking every decision for them.

I've imposed rules with disastrous results. DS1 (from my first marriage and a long gap before my younger 2), had a diet so "perfect" (or so I thought at the time) that I embarrassed him with what went into his lunchbox for school, and who the other kids laughed at and didn't invite to birthday parties etc. Well intentioned on my part (hyperactivity) but it was at a time when it wasn't accepted that food colourings and sugar could cause behavioural issues, and calories was the mantra. A 5 year old might not like the rules, but when the truth about how he felt came out when he became a hugely resentful teenager, it can go the wrong way. Now as an adult DS1 still thinks I know squat about nutrition, and his and his children's diets are nothing like what Jack teaches. Not horrendous, but I suspect that a part of him thinks he is not going to put his kids through what he went through.

I've also seen self initiated "rules" at the extreme - DS2 as a food phobic child, in his case the result of fluoride in toothpaste. There was a limited list of "safe" foods, and he would go hungry before eating anything not on that list. As in going hungry so long that I would give in before he did. When he came out of that when the offending toothpaste was removed, he opened up to new foods very quickly, the most adventurous of my three children (or me at the time for that matter). Including seafood.

DD (older than DS2), at age 6, her friends mother was gobsmacked on inviting her to a McDonalds party and her asking what's a McDonalds...… We ate "real food" but from that point we accepted that occasionally it was going to happen, and when DS2 came out of the fluoride hell hole, the same applied. DS2 and DD had, and have, vastly different needs and tolerances.

I've watched DS2's "inner nutritional coach" at work when it got the chance, and I've seen the consequences of the emotional impact on my eldest child by thinking I knew best when what I didn't know was just as important. Provided its "real food" children are _mainly_ making their choices from, including regular sources of DHA, I wouldn't let anyone call me out about not imposing a rigid set of eating rules for my children (or now my grandchildren when they stay with me). Without fully understanding the context, when it comes to my children and grandchildren, my experience trumps their opinion.

### Inger — 2020-01-25T09:40:29+0200

Religion, in my mind, is something rigid, dogmatism, not willing to see the truth, but instead holding on to old believes.

That too much nnEMF is not good for human biology is a truth, it has nothing to do with religion or dogma.
That we all react to it differently and have different capacity to tolerate it, is also a truth.

It is easier to let go of ones truth to fit into the masses/keeping peace in family/holding up a nice facade.
But.
Someone can be obsessed with nnEMF in a bad way too, and making it worse by fear mongering. Accusing it for things that in reality has other root causes.

Bottom line,
always stay soft and open, examine what is really true for yourself. Do and live that, and do not judge others.
Because everyone has their own path.

### Sue-UK — 2020-01-25T14:42:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-is-a-choice-kris-journal.19460/post-285107>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=285099)
>
> Bottom line,
> always stay soft and open, examine what is really true for yourself. Do and live that, and do not judge others.
> Because everyone has their own path.

I see this in your posts, which is why I enjoy reading them.  I see what you do and maybe think OK I can't do that because of X, but can I get round it by doing Y. And it may draw a comment from you that I'm using porridge for silicon, (whereas you can safely use oysters), but there's never a sneer.

I'm actually feeling healthier for losing the obsession of seeing the sunrise. I'm not worrying if I sleep through it, or if its cloudy … natural early morning light is enough. When I do catch a glorious sunrise its a wow, a moment of grace. Waking up happy anyway (serotonin) rather than needing a dopamine fix from a sunrise. Kris's words " It usually has to do with tapping into inner energy …. " reminds me of Arturo's Solis Herrera MD's book The Human Photosynthesis. If on a physical level, there's an energy source from a form of human photosynthesis using light water and melanin (using mitochondrial uncoupling and thermoregulatory heat as the light source), it shows the potential for surviving and perhaps thriving in a northern winter. My observations from your posts is that you do dips in a rain barrel, not the freezing my arse off in cold water for ages to try and lose weight. I think you also use natural sunlight when you can to generate energy, but my mistake was deliberately losing heat to the environment, in a low energy state, thinking it would help me generate energy. I think you also sleep in a very cold room, but if you are properly "nesting", (something I didn't do last winter), the nesting itself can help provide the heat to generate energy via melanin and water. Your dietary melanin (as opposed to making it endogenously) is possibly from fish head smoothies etc ….I've opted for chaga. Started with it daily and then moved to as and when, I don't want it every day.

The really interesting thing about melanin though is that unlike chlorophyll, the absorption range of melanin is apparently the full electromagnetic spectrum, including radio and gamma rays. Apart from the disease processes Herrera is working with, that has huge potential for mitigating nnemf.

### Inger — 2020-01-25T15:27:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-is-a-choice-kris-journal.19460/post-285109>

That is so true Sue, our mileage might vary  And we all have our owns scars and issues that might have inpact on what is wise to do or not.
I really love the saying, "tread gently... as you move through life". Be soft and gentle. Not hard. Not to your self and not to others. But never stop looking for, and never give up a truth you have found... a precious diamond. No matter what other says. It is a little paradoxical I know..  But if you feel with your heart you will know.

I do "nest"! I always make sure I feel warm and comfortable in bed, but my bedroom is always cold, no heat and window wide open. Love it  I cant sleep if I am cold/freezing.

I also see the dawn and sunrise from my bedroom open window so if I feel really lazy I just lie there and watch the sky..lol
And yes, I believe the important thing is to always get the real light in our eyes in the morning, not stressing too much about going outside seeing the sun in winter storm and gray, if you could just be by an open window inside and watch the sky. It is so much cloudy here anyways in winter. But if the sky is clear I am soo excited like you, to go outside and see the beautiful play of the red orange raising at the horizon   luckily it is so flat here so seeing sunrise is easy almost everywhere.

I do use UV lamps in winter though. It just feels so good so I am going for it. Not excessively, but once in a while.
I feel how it charges all the iron in me..ahh

I would love to try chaga.. but it is a little expensive so I am going to try it later on the road I guess..

I just came up with a significant highly logical theory looking for a Steele video which I couldn't find that shows a tree where the side facing an LED street lamp was dying while the side not facing it appeared normal.

What I did find was this one that shows LED street lamps in between many of the trees but only the side of the tree facing the sun's path has its bark peeling. Remember when I first joined this forum I burned under the sun in just 15 minutes? I was convinced for months nnUVC from our ozone destruction was causing it, and technically it may have been partly responsible as I was sunbathing for hours a day during the summer under 3mm glass and tanning without a problem. It wasn't until Inger's insistence something was still wrong with 'me' (my environment) that I discovered how high wifi radiation actually was and once I got rid of it completely, not just at night but 24/7, I was able to sunbathe raw without any filter under the sun's full spectrum. Before then I was seeing most sunrises every morning in my bikini briefs to "build a solar callus" to no avail. Once I got rid of wifi I pretty much quit seeing all sunrises and have had no problem spending up to two hours near solar noon in UV 8, not a modicum of burn.

I haven't heard Wigington, Trower, Steele, nor Jack propose this before but I'm now thinking microwaves above a given threshold causes **all** life on this planet to be harmed from solar UV. Would also explain why exoskeletal insects are going through population extinctions and apocalypse already.

Before my wifi removal I had a really dark tan from my glass SG and when tried walking around the city without a sun hat my face would still sting after a few minutes because I was being exposed to an average (this was before I learned to only pay attention to peaks) of 3,000 micros in real time at the time.

### Sue-UK — 2020-01-30T11:05:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-is-a-choice-kris-journal.19460/post-285364>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=285338)
>
> Perhaps all that glyphosate in your organic oats is taking a toll.

Fortunately so far the toll hasn't included closing my mind to what nature is capable of.

In the UK there was a lot of fuss about chopping down of trees in the urban areas where they were introducing 5G. I've seen a "5G innovation" paper from a UK university done for the government, co-authored with representatives from the industry which talked of tree height needing to be at least 3m less than the base station height.

"_Having adjacent **trees** and or building at **comparable heights to the mast** can reduce coverage by as much as 70% in that direction, which is not in the interests of the operator, the local planning authorities and more importantly the mobile phone user. This is the source of many of today’s mobile coverage issues for consumers in many rural locations."
_
Far from diddly squat, even the industry is aware of trees blocking 5G.

I know my personal power density and length of RF exposure that either compromises or destroys my Bristol stool chart, do you know yours? Well the background levels I've been exposed to before I started blocking RF had me never with a perfect Bristol for way too many years, and quite often horrible, ignorant me thought it was old age again and normal.

The psychological stress I've been under in the past six months from what I've learned is greater than any other time in my life. Yet my Bristol has been perfect the past six months, except a few days when I intentionally exposed myself to regular background in a couple different environments to see if my Bristol would falter which it did by very significant margins. The effects are immediate, have you seen Pu's live blood slide school wifi study recently?

No matter how rose-colored your glasses are, you cannot deny what is going on is nothing short of genocide and ecocide on a near total scale. Your position sounds to me as "well everyone else should have a right to figure out how to avoid being killed in this concentration camp on their own, who am I to tell people what to do, and if they just stopped stressing about being in an e-camp then they probably wouldn't die anyway." You may not know but it's well proven RF greatly increases apathy and decreases people's ability for compassion and empathy, along with everything else, so I don't judge you or anyone for not sharing what I consider fundamental ethics passion, or "stress" if you prefer to call it.

You may not know but it's well proven RF greatly increases apathy and decreases people's ability for compassion and empathy, along with everything else, so I don't judge you or anyone for not sharing what I consider fundamental ethics passion, or "stress" if you prefer to call it.

For me, your use of the concentration camp and genocide wording shows poor judgement. I think you are doing again what you have done to me a couple of times on this thread - interpreting what someone has said in a way that suits your agenda. Doom and gloom glasses are no more useful than rose coloured ones.

Its interesting that you are relating what you have learned in the past 6 months to the quality of your dumps. Some people here have been learning about RF for a lot longer than 6 months.  Even with that I would suggest that if you knew more about the microbiome, you might still strongly recommend that someone say moves out of the city for the same reasons as now, or hacks their environment, but you could advise someone how to protect microbiome biodiversity so that a trip into a city doesn't wipe someone's biodiversity out. You can't do that if you haven't learned or figured it out for yourself first. Even then you can only share your experience, based on your context, with any hypothesis or theory you might have. You also need to be able to answer why the person you are trying to convince is doing great on the Bristol stool chart, despite chronic exposure in what might be a poor environment RF wise.

Far from not judging, you _are_ judging yourself more empathetic and compassionate and ethical than "anyone" not sharing the self imposed mental prison you seem to have designed for yourself. One of the meter, the use of the whistle when going into urban areas, and now the what it does to my dumps reporting. As for telling people what to do, all any of us can do is tell people _prepared to listen_ what we've learned, or be an example.

For our own children we can make choices about where we live, schools with wifi etc, but when they become adults they make their own decisions. Not having any control over where say a grandchild lives or goes to school does not make someone more apathetic, or decrease someone's compassion or empathy. Even for a spouse - I wouldn't accept being a Stepford wife, being told what to do, and I wouldn't want to be with a partner who would accept that from me. Navigating the dilemmas the issues that people we love bring to the table is not the easy, apathetic route.

Wearing the doom and gloom glasses at a place with the lowest nnemf readings on the planet is not a recipe for longevity, however good anyone's dump is on the Bristol stool chart.

# Thread: Organic food - this question is driving me crazy!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organic-food-this-question-is-driving-me-crazy.19461/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 35
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ScottishEmma — 2017-05-11T20:30:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organic-food-this-question-is-driving-me-crazy.19461/post-213867>

So I've listened to many podcasts with Jack and a few he's mentioned eating food that grows in your light enviroment as it's best suited to your body. I get that. Bang on.

But equally he talks in the book about avoiding non-organic food due to potential issues.

So do I get the raspberries that grow in Scotland that aren't organic or the raspberries grown in Spain that are organic?

It's crazy how I just don't know what's the better option. The lesser of two evils?

Any thoughts welcome!

### Richelle Jones — 2017-05-12T08:39:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organic-food-this-question-is-driving-me-crazy.19461/post-213909>

Its the pesticides, herbicides, fungicides, GMOs and food grown under fake light that is the problem. Just eat organic local food grown in natural light.

### ScottishEmma — 2017-05-12T08:56:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organic-food-this-question-is-driving-me-crazy.19461/post-213911>

> [Richelle Jones said:](/goto/post?id=213909)
>
> Its the pesticides, herbicides, fungicides, GMOs and food grown under fake light that is the problem. Just eat organic local food grown in natural light.

Thanks. Is Spain local to Scotland? Not really. If that's the nearest then you think buying from Spain is the better option?

### Linz — 2017-05-12T10:38:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organic-food-this-question-is-driving-me-crazy.19461/post-213914>

They won't be in season in Scotland yet. Best option is to eat what you can find produced locally and choose something different if it is not produced in the right way. It will help to wash well/ peel non-organic produce if you use it but raspberries don't wash well and may contain quite high levels of crop treatments. GMOs are not grown here but are included in some processed foods (mostly in added soya and maize) and fed to pigs and poultry - and some cattle and sheep.

### ScottishEmma — 2017-05-12T10:43:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organic-food-this-question-is-driving-me-crazy.19461/post-213915>

> [Linz said:](/goto/post?id=213914)
>
> They won't be in season in Scotland yet. Best option is to eat what you can find produced locally and choose something different if it is not produced in the right way. It will help to wash well/ peel non-organic produce if you use it but raspberries don't wash well and may contain quite high levels of crop treatments. GMOs are not grown here but are included in some processed foods (mostly in added soya and maize) and fed to pigs and poultry - and some cattle and sheep.

The season has just started. The only organic fruit grown is in England and is apples. I want to eat more than just apples.

I guess I'll have to forage for local berries but it doesn't leave room for any variety if I'm avoiding non organic produce.

### Sue-UK — 2017-05-12T10:45:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organic-food-this-question-is-driving-me-crazy.19461/post-213916>

Hi Emma

If you see packs of soft fruit that are from another country, double check the label. What it doesn't say could be important .... For example if it says organically grown, it isn't claiming to be organically shipped. I think there would be control measures to stop non native insects arriving ..... Buying organically grown produce from overseas that is usually peeled anyway is one thing, but soft fruit is another......

>
> If you see packs of soft fruit that are from another country, double check the label. What it doesn't say could be important .... For example if it says organically grown, it isn't claiming to be organically shipped. I think there would be control measures to stop non native insects arriving ..... Buying organically grown produce from overseas that is usually peeled anyway is one thing, but soft fruit is another......

I buy my organic produce from Waitrose. It is organically grown so thanks for the heads up.

I'm still none the wiser.

Is the concensus that I should buy the organic produce from Spain to make sure what goes in my mouth is as 'clean' as possible as opposed to buying from my local light growing area?

### Sue-UK — 2017-05-12T12:06:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organic-food-this-question-is-driving-me-crazy.19461/post-213918>

My rule of thumb is to assess how much is likely to have been drawn up through the soil, and how much is likely to have been sprayed. I wouldn't touch either conventionally grown salad leaves which will have been sprayed multiple times, or imported organically grown ones, its not worth paying the organic premium if they are not organically shipped. Its easy to plant a few and grow ourselves, even if its a few pots, same with herbs. We don't harvest the whole plant, just pick off leaves as and when, which stimulates new growth. We might lose some leaves to pests, but a healthy plant will launch some resistance, so there might be a few casualties, but its the response of the plant as a whole. I regularly add organic weeds ... Some things like cabbage I might buy if its not organic, but would remove the outer leaves which would have taken the brunt of any spraying, tomatoes I would buy organic, but only if they are in season and grown in the UK. I don't manage to avoid everything, but some things are much worse than others. Avoiding grain and grain fed meat makes a big difference, the rest is choosing the best I can with what's available, and focusing on my redox to help handle anything I haven't avoided. I've got a book somewhere that went through the worst offenders of potential toxicity, but its quite a few years old, and probably needs updating. There is often organic gardeners selling their surplus at things like car boot sales .... also check out if there is an organic gardeners club near you, or an allotment association where some may be organic growers, and others at least using less intensive methods than commercial sources, and ask if any of them sell their surplus either direct to the public, or at specific venues such as WI markets. Also farm shops may have local organic produce in response to consumer demand.

### Linz — 2017-05-12T13:11:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organic-food-this-question-is-driving-me-crazy.19461/post-213919>

Great advice from Sue. A good redox potential is your best defence.

I prefer UK seasonal to foreign. Rhubarb and gooseberries can be well washed if they are not organic and so can currants, plums and pears. Soft fruit is much more difficult if you can't grow your own apart from wild blackberries. I sometimes buy Waitrose blueberries in summer. Can you grow any of your own raspberries, strawberries or blackcurrants? A lot of apples eaten here too!

### ab70bandit — 2017-05-13T16:30:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organic-food-this-question-is-driving-me-crazy.19461/post-213970>

All else fails, buy frozen organic berries, they are usually frozen at the peak of freshness.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-06-13T00:32:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organic-food-this-question-is-driving-me-crazy.19461/post-216009>

Tonight's lesson: How does inflammation affect mitochondrial redox? When inflammation is raised mitochondrial energy production drops from cytochrome 1-3. This is where food electrons enter. This is why superoxide signaling goe awry i cytochrome 1. Sunlight bypasses cytochrome 1-3 provided UV-A is present. Why is that a key point most everyone misses? UV-A light makes nitric oxide (NO) and it shuts down cytochrome c oxidase and it makes melatonin. Look at the picture below. Red light in sunlight is by far the most abundant frequency making up 42% of sunlight. IR-A light makes massive amounts of ATP with no need for food. So how does inflammtion change things? Keap 1 has four discrete protein domains in which cysteine residues are critically important in stress, sensing of oxidation. This cysteine binding element also is where the NRF2 pathway binds directly to Keap 1. This shows you why cysteine sits in the most critical spot for oxidative sensing in all human cells.

The higher oxidation potential of inflammationmanifests as an extracellular decrease in the ratio of cysteine/cystine and a reduced maount of water made by mitochondria in the cytosol. These things result in an intracellular decrease in the ratio of GSH/GSSG and NAD(P)H/NAD(P). This implies that a metabolic shift in the redox state of the cell's mitochondria by inflammation is more important than ROS made in the mitochondria or on macromolecules. This is why the antioxidant theory of aging is flawed. So lower levels of cysteine and cystine are always seen when water is not being made and both are seen in aging and disease as the first signal from mitochondrion of where disease begins and accelerates the aging process.

Here is some of the complexity I am trying to leave out for the non scientific reading this. The Keap1-Nrf2 pathway plays a crucial role in determining the sensitivity of cells of the ABC transporters. ABC transporters are ATP-binding cassette transporters are members of a protein superfamily that is one of the largest and most ancient families with representatives in all extant phyla from prokaryotes to humans. ABC transporters are transmembrane proteins that utilize the electrons of adenosine triphosphate (ATP) and GTP to fully unfold biological proteins involved in translocation of various substrates across membranes and non-transport-related processes such as translation of RNA and DNA repair. Although most eukaryotic ABC transporters are effluxers, some are not directly involved in transporting substrates.

For example, both the cystic fibrosis transmembrane regulator (CFTR) and the sulfonylurea receptor (SUR) are directly affected by ATP's ability to cause a full open confirmation of its proteins for proper action. This is associated with the regulation of opening and closing of "ion channels demon proteins" using GTP. These proteins are affected by ATP and GTP levels to directly affect the protein conformational folding of the human ABC protein. Here again, we see ATP/GTP being used as an electron withdrawing cardinal absorbent to open the protein conformations of these proteins in membranes. This implies the more ATP we make, the more accurate these transporters will function in cellular signaling. This is why a high fat diet is beneficial for longevity and health. What food guru's forget is 2/3 of ATP in a human is made when that human is out in the sunlight that contains UV-A and IR-A light. Why? UV-A light makes nitric oxide which shuts down tunneling of electrons from cytochrome 1, 2, 3. Food electrons enter mitochondria there. Sunlight makes ATP at cytochrome c oxidase by turning off proximal ECT and using the 42% of IR-A light in sunlight. Sunlight provides double the amount of ATP production that food can provide. We seem to forget this. The more ATP made , the accuracy of cellular signaling, the less chance you age or get ill. This is why sunlight trumps food. Considering quantum cell theory, it really is that simple. Get sun and build your redox first and foremost.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/upload_2017-6-14_5-20-12-png.5034/)

This picture fascinates me. I am looking at the differences and similarities of plant and animal cells. I'm thinking of the human gut as being the portable equivalent of the root system. Just like a root system it forms symbiotic relationships with bacteria etc, and its in the dark. When the light conditions and the environment are right, a plant can take what it needs from the soil and it's root symbionts, and is essentially vegetarian. And some - dare I say it  - have even got humans to grow them in large numbers and look after them ... So in some high quantum yield environments human vegetarians could do well, but I think its about the light and how humans photosynthesise, much less about the chlorophyll given to the human root, because of:

Plants can go dormant for months, so humans couldn't have survived relying on chlorophyll in latitudes that have definite seasons. In plants with not so good (for them) environments, to survive some have adapted to become parasitic, insectivore and even carnivores, and I think there's a human parallel.

>

Conditions in the fields WWI made wounded soldiers prone to infection but physicians noticed that sunlight had a healing effect, so they encouraged the injured to sit in sunlight, with their wounds exposed.

World War I was a catalyst for innovation in medicine. The sheer number of wounded encouraged physicians to look at alternative treatments for diseases and injuries.

Before the war, light, both artificial and natural, was already being used to heal wounds, cure some forms of tuberculosis, and treat rickets, a Vitamin D deficiency stemming from a poor diet and lack of sunshine.

Richard Hobday, in his book “**The Healing Sun**,” notes that during the war, surgeons were confronted with gruesome wounds, some more difficult to treat than anything previously experienced, and that they had to be inventive to cope with the massive numbers of casualties.

“For the first time, surgeons had to deal with injuries caused by high-velocity weapons fired at close range,” Mr. Hobday writes. Conditions in the field made wounded soldiers prone to infection. With no antibiotics and a limited supply of disinfectants, physicians noticed that the sun’s rays had a healing effect, so they encouraged injured soldiers to sit with their wounds exposed to the light.

The war had also led to a spread in poverty and malnutrition, and a corresponding rise in cases of rickets, which causes bones to soften. An avoidable and curable disease, rickets mostly affects children. The experiences of battlefield physicians prompted more doctors to treat rickets with sunlamps.

Around the end of the war, Kurt Huldschinsky, a physician in Berlin, also found that ultraviolet radiation from a vapor lamp, a form of sunlamp, cured rickets. Sunlamps have since been used to treat skin disorders, burns, sleep disorders, neonatal jaundice and even some psychiatric disorders.

In the aftermath of World War I, physicians also took a closer look at the dietary causes of rickets. The surge in the number of children affected gave rise to two popular theories—that rickets was caused by a poor diet, or that it was caused by lack of fresh air and sunlight. The answer was both.

“It was the terrible level of child hunger/starvation in the city in the aftermath of the war that made this research possible,” says Dr. Roberta Bivins, director of the Centre for the History of Medicine at the University of Warwick, in England. A Viennese study to see whether vitamin supplements and dietary changes could successfully treat deficiencies in humans proved that rickets was mostly a disease of nutrition, entirely curable with a cheap vitamin D supplement, she notes.

Pretty interesting info on sunlight & medicine hidden deep in the medical library - found this gem in a book from 1917. A bit dusty but full of great info.

.
