# Thread: Pregnancy/Delivery
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnancy-delivery.19489/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Valsun12 — 2017-05-16T17:47:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnancy-delivery.19489/post-214138>

My thoughts are focused on women's experience of pregnancy/labor and delivery. As a recent new Mom, now I have experienced the programming of what "should" be considered and "process" that women are brainwashed and sold to believe. My concern is primarily set on the high % number of C-sections and our nations high infant mortality rate.

Why are so many women misinformed and not allowed to attempt to birth and are manipulated towards a C-section? (The caveat..Yes I do believe certain situations call all hands on deck, but majority no.)
\---I got lucky(meaning I HAD to help myself) and stepped out of the programming zone for my two deliveries, but felt pressure every step of the way for my decisions--yes many fear tactics felt for not following the "rules"!---My goal was vaginal delivery because of all the important bacteria and benefits to baby...and YEAH, goal accomplished in both deliveries because I was super strong not to cave in to recommendations while also creating a support team in the delivery room!..While following the alternative approach to the these miraculous experiences by seeking birth centers -->I could still feel the strong vibes set by the establishment of medications and ugly recommendations such as...., your "40" weeks are here its time to force baby out", and even also "you need a C-section NOW because at 34 weeks baby is breach" (I Thought the alternatives were suppose to first give you other options before such extreme recommendations!)
However, I said No! lets see if baby can come on their own and flip by using Chiropractic care-->which she did healthy and born at 43 weeks(another paradigm women are forced to believe with bad studies that being post date will always lead to failure! ..But I do remember in this time of waiting and trying other approaches how my internal voice was pulled in so many scary directions!...Women deserve more respect than this when we are bringing life into the world and learning our options.

...At the same time---> What is going on, why are women not producing Oxytocin on their own and the rate of inductions is ridiculously high? Is this due to lack of good sunlight, that women just can't birth as well? ..Yes probably...its a big piece. How can we start creating a system where industry will value and help us be able to have flexible work options (if able, like if you don't have a job as a neurosurgeon )---like being outside more instead of indoors working during pregnancy?..and value the sake of our unborn children to thrive by first having a good birth experience?

### JanSz — 2017-05-16T18:28:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnancy-delivery.19489/post-214143>

I am 77yo man, now.
When my wife got pregnant, she just ended her professional dancer carrier, so she was a fit above average.
Both of my children were delivered "naturally". Both in the hospital.
First son, in USA, then daughter in Poland, 1 1/2 years latter.
In USA she was heavily sedated, all went easy during delivery and afterward.
She went home day or two latter.
She had to be cut to to facilitate delivery.
In Poland, all was natural.
She stretched the birth canal area, took over 2-3 years to settle.
She was in the hospital for two weeks, I had very brief encounter with both of them and then was not able to see them in hospital.
Daughter developed diarrhea and terrible diaper rash, all fault of my wife (they said), (she was on hospital food).
Daughter's hips were somewhat dislocated took a long time to heal.
Since I was not living in Poland, they had to charge me for the use of hospital, less than $10 (ten) of 1975 dollars.
At the time I was making about 6 or $7000/year ($2.25/hr + OT), poor BC/BS insurance, so the over $1000 was harder to come, but no complains.

This is where we are going, unless health care discussions settle. You really do not want something that is free (and worth the price).

My opinion on Health Insurance.
Only very catastrophic should be available.
The rest should be paid by consumer (using cash only).

You would be surprised of the prices when set by market forces.
Also, when bills are settled by using cash only.
Looking at the money helps. It is harder to feel spending when using credit cards.

........................

I fear the masses may not want to think or be responsible for their own health. It's too much work. I can find you countless experts on celebrity divorces, sports stats, and political kabuki shows. You can watch people light up when discussing these things. Turn a conversation to health, you know that thing that's keeping them alive, and the lights go out upstairs. It's fascinating.

# Thread: Involuntary Tummo: My Spine Is Turning Cold Water Warm
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/involuntary-tummo-my-spine-is-turning-cold-water-warm.19490/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AdamF — 2017-05-16T20:49:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/involuntary-tummo-my-spine-is-turning-cold-water-warm.19490/post-214151>

Got in shower at 56 degrees today after doing 53 deg. the last two, and the following happened:

Around five minutes in, I put the shower head up and down my spine and areas with brown fat (upper shoulders, etc...), and the water actually started getting warm. Was able to repeat this several times and then I was able to warm the water up almost everywhere except top of my head. This was not like the water just getting less cold, this water was _warming_ me up.

I thought the water temperature must have gotten warmer since I turned the shower on, but the thermometer showed _the water temperature had actually dropped while this was happening.
_
It was most pronounced in areas of brown fat concentration, so I wonder if I get more brown fat activation by targeting areas other than the BF, itself.

### Scompy — 2017-05-18T07:17:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paradigm-is-awakening.19491/post-214241>

I'm lamenting more and more studies that do not control for real sunlight, and then use supplements processed under who knows what conditions, and then assert conclusions about their study. If I could play God for one day, I'd run my own scientific experiment on all of the science labs around the country to draw my own conclusions about each of them.

"UV-B radiation was provided by 120-cm UV-B fluorescent tubes (UVB-313 EL, Q-lab, Cleveland, OH, USA; 280–390 nm) for 3 h each day from 11:00 to 14:00 h, which coincided with the time of greatest natural UV-B irradiation. The control groups received only visible light, and experimental groups received visible plus UV light. The peak natural dose of solar UV-B in summer at the capture site is 7.4 W m−2 at midday, and the total daily natural dose is 5100 J m−2 ([Beckmann et al., 2014](http://jeb.biologists.org/content/219/1/96#ref-4)). We arranged our lamps so that the peak irradiance was 3.3 W m−2 UV-B at the water surface, which led to a total daily dose of 1188 J m−2. This lower dose of UV-B was chosen to better reflect the average UV-B dose across seasons ([Beckmann et al., 2014](http://jeb.biologists.org/content/219/1/96#ref-4)). We measured sustained swimming performance of 12–15 fish from each treatment after 1 week in treatments, which was sufficiently long to observe effects of UV-B and NAC where they existed (see pilot experiment; [Ghanizadeh Kazerouni et al., 2015](http://jeb.biologists.org/content/219/1/96#ref-22))."

[http://jeb.biologists.org/content/219/1/96](http://jeb.biologists.org/content/219/1/96)

### ScottishEmma — 2017-05-17T19:07:41+0300

### drezy — 2017-05-17T19:47:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weird-ass-question-about-peeing.19493/post-214204>

How would you self evaluate your recent light hygiene and sleep quality lately?

[Tensegrity 5](https://www.jackkruse.com/tensegrity-5-magnetic-sense/) might have a puzzle piece for you. During my weightloss/trimming phase, due mostly to the sun and improved sleep, my son said "Dad why do you need to pee so much?" We read this excerpt:

> Melatonin at night is designed to really clamp down our protein polymers even tighter, by increasing our magnetic sense. It does this by increasing oxygen tensions in our tissues by **increasing electron flow to mitochondria by breaking down our fat at night**. Excess light makes us fat. Certain foods only grown in excessive light periods.
>
> When fat is broken down during sleep in your body, **it turns into water and carbon dioxide. The water is in the form of sweat and urine**. The sweat cools you down to increase your mitochondria’s magnetic sense. Urine is old recycled water that has poor electron carrying capacity. It is less dense than your blood plasma and your CSF. Less dense water is expelled electrically and magnetically by your kidneys. The more dense water is the more electrons it can carry. CSF is more dense by design. This happens by the action of your choroid plexus in your brain. This is why in cold temperatures make you urinate quickly. You are ridding your body of less dense water and structuring more dense water in your CSF naturally. This cycle occurs via water carried in your blood plasma. This is how you structure your own water naturally.

> Melatonin at night is designed to really clamp down our protein polymers even tighter, by increasing our magnetic sense. It does this by increasing oxygen tensions in our tissues by **increasing electron flow to mitochondria by breaking down our fat at night**. Excess light makes us fat. Certain foods only grown in excessive light periods.

The sweat cools you down to increase your mitochondria’s magnetic sense. Urine is old recycled water that has poor electron carrying capacity. It is less dense than your blood plasma and your CSF. Less dense water is expelled electrically and magnetically by your kidneys. The more dense water is the more electrons it can carry. CSF is more dense by design. This happens by the action of your choroid plexus in your brain. This is why in cold temperatures make you urinate quickly. You are ridding your body of less dense water and **structuring more dense water in your CSF** naturally. This cycle occurs via water carried in your blood plasma. This is how you structure your own water naturally.

@[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/)
When I see description "structured water"
I think of EZ water (H3O2).
So, structuring is a process of converting bulk water into EZ.
EZ water is able to exist only in small confined places or as very thin thickness next to any surface.
So, it is easy for me to see discussions about structured water in mitochondria or inside most cells. (They are small enough to hold EZ in their whole volume)
But not so when discussions are about extra cellular spaces.
CSF exist mostly in large extra cellular spaces, so it should be mostly H2O, bulk water, (not H3O2).

Mitochondria makes water.
Most (**or all** ??) intra-cellular water is originated in mitochodria.
So, there is a (uni-directional) flow from (mitochondria) to (inside of cell) to (outside of cell)

So I am confused when Jack said:
**structuring more dense water in your CSF** naturally
it seems that he talks about different structuring than converting bulk water into EZ.
or may be he is saying that thickness of EZ over say surface of brain ventricles is increasing because temperature there have fallen?

Do you have something to add?

......

I use an epsom salt product that has vitamin C in it. A tablespoon seems to do the job. It's my understanding Vitamin C neutralizes the chlorine and fluoride.
For me, it's a noticeable effect for bathwater over my non-vitamin C showers.

there are vitamin C shower filters, berkey shower filters, etc. I've not seen anyone recommend those bathballs that filter tub water.

### Greenie — 2018-01-24T01:17:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/de-fluoridating-bath-water-for-ct.19494/post-233289>

Cool, didn't know Berkey makes a fluoride removal filter for the shower/tub. I have wondered how to modify one to fit onto a faucet.

### Sandra — 2018-01-24T04:54:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/de-fluoridating-bath-water-for-ct.19494/post-233315>

I seem to remember reading a few years ago that Jack said the benefits to CTing far outweighs the presence of chlorine in the water (though I totally agree it would be great to have it out of the water).

I am fairly certain the only way to remove fluoride from water is by reverse osmosis.

I've not really had a practice for years. I've dabbled here and there and never really experienced anything profound.

# Thread: Hello from Texas newbie

### Wayne B — 2017-05-19T00:46:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-texas-newbie.19498/post-214271>

Hello everyone!

I'm a forum newbie having just been introduced to the site and Dr Kruse’s oceans of information in the last few months. I have been hearing about the incredible things going on in the world of all things Paleo, magnetic , sun, CT, etc. with Dr. Kruse. Now, with some of my own issues front and center, I have started in on the webinars, videos, blogs, forum, and am just finishing reading the Epi - Paleo RX. I immediately began a leptin Rx a month ago..Total diet change, lifestyle tuning into Circadians, orange glasses, cutting emf everywhere, and limiting computer exposure to bare minimum..observing natural rhythms, daily exercise, starting CT, etc. Also, I invested in the Magnetico 20 Gauss sleep pad which is amazing and really purges toxins with incredible magnetic field generation. I have the Zyto electro-dermal system with corresponding large plasma tube available as adjunctive therapy. Is this a good addition to the protocol or not? I did a Skype consult with Dr. Kruse a few weeks ago and was completely blown away by his knowledge and experience..Not all of the tests had been done then and so he responded to the earliest April CBC labs which were current then.

My specific story is based on some recent labs which brought me to seeking the optimal health goal after years of ignoring much that is important to maintain health.. A March 2017 Zyto scan showed some major imbalances which led to a couple of CBC labs spaced a month apart (April-May 2017) that showed my leptin at 2.5. Other blood work shows an A/G ratio at .6, and total globulin at 6.4 rising from 6.1 the month before. My wholistic doctor here in the Dallas area became suspicious also of a rising serum protein level from 9.6 to 10.5 in the month between tests as well as the globulin rise.

An electrophoresis test came back with a Gabba M spike of 30.6 % Then the most recent test, a Free Lambda Light chain test came in at 659. – very high and out of range..indicating some high stress amongst plasma cells?..still not sure what we are dealing with but more consultations and tests ahead..

In the meantime continuing full steam ahead on all of the changes that have worked wonders for everyone else working toward optimal. Anything that I’ve overlooked ?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-19T01:24:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-texas-newbie.19498/post-214277>

Welcome........That spike says multiple myeloma has to be ruled out or in. You better learn about the power grid issue tied to Judy Wertheimer's research in Denver in 1977 in Becker and MArino's books fast. That spike says your local environment has something horribly toxic in it. It means your living and working environment needs a serious nnEMF hack. EMF bootcamp is for you.

### Wayne B — 2017-05-22T01:59:08+0300

Thanks, Dr. Kruse and thank you Caroline.
I've begun an intensive survey of all emf sources..I had already switched from the smart meter to an analog one at home a year ago but not at the building which houses my business. I made the meter requests to analog there last week. Also, shielded the wifi router at home with an emf reducing cage and will do the same at work. All wifi and cells are off at night. Staying off of the cell phone and away from any stray emf which I somehow dismissed in the past. Absolutely off of all power tools and computer work now, and am very mindful now of any power supply or blue light emitting display..I am re reading the Becker book, and signed up for the emf bootcamp but am unable to check in with those videos as the ling locks me out..Is there a special access area for the EMF bootcamp for gold members?
Thanks!

I don't want to get too esoteric, however, the AZTEC's did in the calendar of the tonalama predict this time of the conjuction of the 5th and 6th sun (each sun being a 2,650 yr cycle).

I've found a little discipline and ritual can help the interaction with the sun and develop more of an intention with this solar healing. A great start to the day to balance the EMF's I feel; I don't think the Aztecs had this problem with the EMF's.

# Thread: Clearlight Saunas and EMF
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearlight-saunas-and-emf.19515/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rubicon — 2017-05-21T06:38:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearlight-saunas-and-emf.19515/post-214365>

**Summary**
The Clearlight Sauna I purchased new in December 2016 had a high powered WiFi radio and gave off very high levels of RF EMF. The following is a description of how I discovered this, and what I had to do to eliminate it.

**My Sauna Experience**
Last December I purchased a Clearlight Full Spectrum Sanctuary Sauna. This line of saunas is marketed as having low EMF while providing full spectrum IR.

When I first setup the sauna I measured the sauna panels for electric/magnetic fields and everything checked out as very low. This compared to high EMF measurements in my old sauna with ceramic far IR emitters. I was very happy.

A few weeks later I was measuring the smart meter RF in my basement near my sauna. When I walked past my sauna (which was turned off) I detected a spike in RF. I was surprised, but followed the signal and found it was indeed coming from my sauna. The signal was stronger than a cordless phone. I was blown away.

Initially, I thought it was some kind of high-powered bluetooth radio I was detecting. The audio system has bluetooth capability to let you stream audio to the stereo.
It turns out that Clearlight also installs a high power wifi radio in their saunas so that the sauna can be controlled from a wireless device throughout a persons home. I was not told my sauna had this feature, and was not aware of it

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

From that point, it took a few months and about 20 emails to finally get directions from Clearlight on how to disable the radio.

**Image Album**
Here's an album of 22 images showing what I did to detect & disable the radio. In the description for each image is more information & directions.

[http://imgur.com/a/C3PZJ](http://imgur.com/a/C3PZJ)

The process also required completely disabling the audio system to turn off the bluetooth. If you have a Clearlight Sauna and want to disable your radio, the procedure may be similar, but Clearlight tells me that there are several different designs out there that may vary slightly.

**Conclusion**
Throughout this process, I found Clearlight was very pleasant to deal with and they seem to have very good intentions to build the healthiest sauna they can with the features that people want. However, even with that they unknowingly shipped me a super high EMF sauna marketed as low EMF. The problem apparently is that people want too many wireless features and they're added without even considering/appreciating the implications.

Having a meter and testing everything has become a necessity of life.

**Sauna Biohacks**
With the RF EMF eliminated, I tweaked the sauna by lining the walls with aluminum foil to more evenly reflect the IR. I notice the difference and it's great.

You can see the external speakers I now use due to the sauna stereo being completely disabled to turn off the bluetooth. The wires for my external speakers slip under the door. No big deal, but definitely not what I was expecting.

### thrive1992 — 2018-10-05T01:58:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clearlight-saunas-and-emf.19515/post-252378>

How are you going with your FIR sauna protocol?

I am thinking of getting the Sun Stream Evolve Mini as clearlight is out of my budget.

### Walter Rice — 2019-10-25T03:12:14+0300

# Thread: Craters...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craters.19516/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ScottishEmma — 2017-05-21T17:06:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craters.19516/post-214376>

So I've listened to loads of podcast interviews with Jack and in a few he's talking about where to live and how the crater (I forget the name) was a really important consideration.

So I thought I'd google craters in Scotland (that's where I live although we're moving to Spain for the sun) and lo and behold there is a crater site from 1.2 billion years ago.

The crater site is at Lairg but has the name Low Gravity and I'm probably getting confused but is the reason we'd want to live near a crater site because the magnetic force is stronger there? What does gravity have to do with that? Is that of any relevance health-wise?

I thought I might move back to Scotland eventually and even though Lairg is a ridiculously high latitude - way higher than Glasgow - if there were potential health implications to moving to a high magnetic place as I'm assuming this crater area would be then I'd be up for that...

There isn't an actual crater hole by the way! It's been covered over with rock...

### ScottishEmma — 2017-05-22T12:15:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craters.19516/post-214409>

Ok so I understand the gravity and magnetic thing a little better. There's a map available that shows the gravity/magnetic landscape of Scotland and I'd like to buy it.

"Gravity and magnetic anomalies show small departures from the expected values of the earth's gravity and magnetic fields, caused by variations in the density and magnetisation of the rocks within the crust. These anomalies are used to determine concealed geological structure, including the depth and extent of sedimentary basins, depth to magnetic basement and location of concealed igneous bodies. On the broadest scale, they help to convey an understanding of the regional configuration of the Earth's crust and upper mantle."

My question remains, does any of this information tie in to where's best to live? I read the 'Does it really matter where we live?' blog post but I'm maybe not looking in the right place?

Presumably you'd want to live where the magnetic field is stronger?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-22T15:59:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craters.19516/post-214420>

The crater can increase magnetic flux but you can hack it. Based upon what I've found I dont think a high latitude crater is wise. The ones in Mexico are easy accessible and inside the 20's

### ScottishEmma — 2017-05-22T17:29:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craters.19516/post-214424>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=214420)

The change we resist is the growth we miss. Progress comes via the unexpected. Failure enhances learning. Welcome it.

### Lahelada — 2017-05-23T15:08:18+0300

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-23T15:48:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunlight-harmful-when-dehydrated.19519/post-214497>

You have no defects.........your environment contains the defects. Do you allow the environment to continue to harm yoru mitochondria by ignoring the stimulus?

We are designed to work in the environment we are best attuned too. This means once you learn how your mitochondria are tuned you'll know where to go to reverse your issues. Are you committed to change yet? Anyone can dabble in it, but once you've made that commitment, it's very hard to stop you. A committed person never gives up, despite all the obstacles they face, they keep walking towards their destiny not allowing even the slightest discomfort to distract them. That's the difference between interest and commitment. When you're interested in doing something, you do it only when circumstance permits, but when you're committed to something, you accept no excuses, only results. Commitment is the reason why some people succeed and others wonder if they could ever. Personal life commitments are centered around three F's. Be Fixed. Be Focused. Be Faithful. Charisma may get you in the front door, but it's the commitment that closes the back door! The first person we have to commit to, is ourselves. A committed mind never falters under a load. Commitment is the reason you might be living your life a little different in order to carry out your vision. People say "how do you find the time?" Diabetes insipidus
2017 ICD-10-CM Diagnosis Code E23.2

Symptoms and causes

[http://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/diabetes-insipidus/symptoms-causes/dxc-20182410](http://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/diabetes-insipidus/symptoms-causes/dxc-20182410)

\-----------///////////////////////////////////
Anhidrosis
2017 ICD-10-CM Diagnosis Code L74.4

**Anhidrosis is the inability to sweat normally.**

[http://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/anhidrosis/basics/definition/CON-20033498](http://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/anhidrosis/basics/definition/CON-20033498)
\----------
**Risk factors**

Certain factors make anhidrosis more likely, including:

Age. People age 65 and older, infants, and children are more prone to heat stress, which can contribute to anhidrosis.
Certain health problems. Any medical condition that damages your autonomic nerves, such as diabetes, makes sweat gland problems more likely.
Skin disorders. Many diseases that irritate or inflame the skin also affect the sweat glands. They include psoriasis; exfoliative dermatitis, which is marked by severe skin scaling; heat rash; scleroderma, which causes hard, tight skin; and ichthyosis — extremely dry, scaly skin.
Genetic abnormalities. Mutations in certain genes can lead to disorders that affect the sweat glands.

\-----------
**Causes**
Anhidrosis occurs when your sweat glands don't function properly, either as a result of a condition you're born with (congenital condition) or one that affects your nerves or skin. Dehydration also can cause anhidrosis. Sometimes the cause of anhidrosis can't be found.

Causes of anhidrosis include:

Conditions you're born with, such as certain congenital displasias that affect the development of sweat glands
Inherited conditions that affect your metabolic system, such as Fabry's disease
Connective tissue diseases, such as Sjogren's syndrome, which causes dry eyes and mouth
Skin damage, such as from burns or radiation therapy, or diseases that clog your pores (poral occlusion), such as psoriasis
Conditions that cause nerve damage (neuropathy), such as diabetes, alcoholism and Guillain-Barre syndrome
Certain drugs, such as morphine and botulinum toxin type A, and those used to treat psychosis.

\------------------------
**Tests and diagnosis**

Your doctor is likely to suspect anhidrosis based on your signs and symptoms, your medical history, and a physical exam. But you may need certain tests to confirm the diagnosis. These include:

Sweat test. During this test, known as thermoregulatory sweat test, you're coated with a powder that changes color when and where you sweat. You then enter a chamber that causes your body temperature to increase to a level that makes most people perspire. Digital photos document the results, and the whole body surface can be tested at once.
Skin biopsy. In some cases, your doctor might request a biopsy of the area suspected of anhidrosis. For this test, skin cells and sometimes sweat glands are removed for examination under a microscope.

\-------------------
**Treatments and drugs**
\-------------------------------
****Prevention****
**By Mayo Clinic Staff**

**Anhidrosis often can't be prevented, but serious heat-related illnesses can. To stay safe:**

-
    **

    -   **Wear loose, light clothing** when it's warm.

    -   **Stay indoors** on hot days.

    -   **Monitor your activity level closely** so you don't overdo.

    -   **Learn the signs of heat-related illness** and how to treat them.

    **

..

Hi Sean,

Always nice to meet another Sean - and you are from the UK too! Which area are you from?

Hopefully I can add something to this post that I think is missing in your search for an answer to your problems.

**1\. I think your Frequency of exposure to AM and PM sun need to continue a lot longer before you can really measure any change.**

I live in the UK too. We had a semi-decent weekend with UV of around 6 at it's highest. That was in London, not sure where you are, but north of London had some areas being overcast all afternoon - thus reducing UV index to maybe half that.

Prior to this, we didn't have a UV index of 6 until over a month ago in April. Which means:

-   You have had arguably 2 weekends in the last 2 months of UV exposure that is semi-decent
-   If you are not working, then there were some weekdays that were semi-decent... maybe a UV 5, here and there.
-   Overall, you have probably had max. 10 days of UV index above 5. That is being generous.

**1\. Get out in the Sun more frequently, but not necessarily for long periods until you can take it**
Continue to get sun exposure, on a daily basis, as we move toward the summer. BUT, move OUT of the sun when you get this uncomfortable hot sensation. It CAN be harmful if you can't ABSORB it properly. This needs to be BUILT. Check this thread here:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/all-things-sunscreen.19417/#post-214276](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/all-things-sunscreen.19417/#post-214276)

Your redox is clearly low, and your ability to assimilate light is not as good as others on this forum. It will take TIME. Be grateful for a hot summer and get out in it every day. By winter, you should have built up a better tolerance. This cannot be done in 10 days during April and May.

**2\. Your exposure to EMF needs to be reduced.**

The others are right, the forum is good to learn, but the goal is the less the better as... Forum = EMF and Blue light.

These are both destroying your redox potential and dopamine, and thus your ability to assimilate light and build your redox potential and Dopamine levels.

It's really a double edged sword.
**
3\. Read these books **in the AM sun and PM sun (while you can tolerate it) and then move into a candle lit or IR light lit room to finish reading before bed:****

-   **The Body Electric by Robert O'Becker -** skip straight to the chapter called "Maxwell's Silver Hammer" and read this first. This will open your eyes to why you are not healing and the impact of your non-native Electromagnetic Field.

[https://www.amazon.co.uk/gp/product/0688069711/ref=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o07\_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.co.uk/gp/product/0688069711/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o07_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1)

**4\. Final comment****

-   Eating lots of DHA laden foods following a Keto Leptin Rx with periods fasting up to and around the 24 hour mark
-   Showering under the coldest tap water every day for 10 minutes

### JanSz — 2017-05-25T19:21:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunlight-harmful-when-dehydrated.19519/post-214684>

> [SatoriHeart said:](/goto/post?id=214664)
>
> Inability to metabolize fatty acids + low absorption of oral Vitamin D = lowered ability to leverage sunlight's photoelectric effect in skin = even lower Vitamin D status = disruption of immune pathways
>
> Tell me more about your experience with fats. I have a feeling there is a big clue there. Remember, **PPAR and RXR** work in concert to coordinate lipid metabolism, adipocyte differentiation, thermogenesis, ubiquitination, and _gluconeogenesis_. There a many potential reasons this cycle could be disrupted ... but rarely irresolvable.

PPAR and RXR
\---------------------
Peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Peroxisome\_proliferator-activated\_receptor](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Peroxisome_proliferator-activated_receptor)
\--------------------

Retinoid X receptor
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Retinoid\_X\_receptor](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Retinoid_X_receptor)
\------------------

Peroxisome
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Peroxisome](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Peroxisome)
They are involved in catabolism of very long chain fatty acids, and number of other.
\-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
One major reason to watch fatty acids (in detail).

### seanb4 — 2017-05-27T10:27:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunlight-harmful-when-dehydrated.19519/post-214836>

> [SatoriHeart said:](/goto/post?id=214824)
>
> Don't get stuck on the nutrigenomics bandwagon. Amy Yasko will get richer and you'll run in circles for a few years listening to Ben Lynch webinars, wondering which dose of Methyl-Folate is the best thing for your MTHFR mutation.
>
> All kidding aside (forgive me).... According to 23andMe and other genomic sequencing services I have used, I also have homozygous SNPs in COMT, MAOB, and MAOA. I've read many warnings linking my SNPs to schizophrenia and panic disorder, and it's all garbage. Why? **Because methylation is environmentally controlled.
> **
> Why, with the SNPs listed above, do I have markedly low dopamine and a propensity (in the past) for alcohol abuse? You would have thought that COMT and MAOB mutations would come to my rescue. Sadly, they did not because my environment and lifestyle didn't activate them. I enjoyed proper dopamine / serotonin balance in youth, but antibiotics and Epstein-Barr (i.e. mononucleosis) caused a major train wreck... and with the addition of alcohol ... threw dopaminergic signaling off a cliff whilst tearing up the microbiome and depleting precious serotonin-producing flora. The rest, as they say, is history.
>
> With neurotransmitters, taking precursors is all but useless. It's like asking someone to do your pushups for you. Right amounts of protein and good metabolism -- entirely different story. But until metabolism is online, you might need a nudge in the right direction. Deprenyl also increases norepinephrine (mildly) and can be potentially useful for POTS. In my case, if taken first thing in the morning, MAO would kick in towards dinner time without anxiety. We are talking the minimal effective dose --- 1mg liquid.
>
> But that's my N=1. Ultimately, neurotransmitters will be skewed as long as the microbiome and hormone cascade are in the gutter. And that will almost certainly be the case if you have high inflammation, poor metabolism of fatty acids, and no photoelectric inputs. Healing is impossible without proper Vitamin A, D, and T3 status --- and they will forever elude you if you don't harness sufficient sunlight and have adequate cholesterol.

This makes sense. I played around with different methylation protocols and usually came out worse for it. I think the main player is the enviroment as like you said, I didn't have these issues before I got sick, however I think they are a secondary nuisance.

> [SatoriHeart said:](/goto/post?id=214824)
>
> I'm not sold on the alpha-adrenergic theory of POTS. You are running on empty. You don't even have building blocks for proper glucocorticoid production! Of course there is going to be POTS. Whether or not there is a pathogenic component to your parasympathetic dysregulation remains to be seen ... I have been hitting heavy with Monolaurin and increasing sun exposure, and my symptoms are abating (in a big way). But again, that's my N=1.
>
> I have used Centrophenoxine, on and off, for stimulation of acetylcholine. I was not able to get a consistent effect with Alpha GPC or CDP Choline, but Centrophenoxine would consistently produce effects. It's a DMAE derivative and is located in a different position in the acetylcholine synthesis chain. It also has the added benefit, with chronic use, to remove lipofuscin from the brain -- though clinical proof is sparse.
>
> Stimulating acetylcholine when the vagus nerve is inflamed or "misfiring" may produce paradoxical effects. It can be calming and then, because of what may be going on with the glial and mast cells, it could not be active enough. Inflammation is unpredictable that way.

Very glad to hear your having success with monolaurin + sun.
I think (Centrophenoxine) it is worth a try. I dont think the alpha adrenergic and your theory are mutually exclusive. I know that studies have shown lots of POTS patients with antibodies against aA1, and people have responded to drugs that help. This could be due to not having the building blocks for proper glucocorticoid production in the first place, and aA1 problems. I know my POTS symptoms improved significantly with mirtazapine. I can only assume this is through increase aA1...

> [SatoriHeart said:](/goto/post?id=214824)
>
> My personal opinion is that a virus does not necessarily need to be in lytic phase for there to be low-level nerve excitation. Though it may not be actively replicating and moving outside the nerve ganglia, it can still trigger responses from glial / mast cells, causing unnecessary "irritation".
>
> I am not entirely convinced inducing lytic state is a wise choice for an immune-compromised individual. I have not yet returned to Sodium Butyrate, but I am still getting results with Monolaurin and sun exposure. And we do know there is some [lytic activation from UV light](http://www.newworldencyclopedia.org/entry/Lytic_cycle), and with antiviral support, this could be just as effective as inducing it biochemically.
>
> Antivirals can't kill a virus in its dormant phase. Again, it either has to leave the ganglia in order for PAMP-detection to kick in or a drug like Ganciclovir may penetrate the ganglia membrane without lytic activation. The latter intuitively seems ineffective.
>
> In any case, I do believe sunlight is your best medicine. And you may be able to stimulate some sweat production by using a crutch like Centrophenoxine for a while, but beware of dopamine antagonism with rising acetylcholine levels.
>
> Given your reaction to L-Glutamine (i.e. neuronal exitotoxicity), I would say you most certainly have a leaky gut along with high systemic IL-6 and TNF-alpha levels. And that is disrupting your metabolism of proteins and fats along and causing a cascade of problems with both sex and glucocorticoid production. I'd be willing to bet your Vitamin D and cortisol are both flatlining.

Yes quite possibly. I have been taking a tub of water outside and constantly applying a coating of water on me. This makes the heat much more bearable.

Yes that makes sense. If the viruses are still in the cells they will no doubt be causing havoc.

Thanks Satori, as usual you have given me a lot to thick about.

Just want to update this thread. Thanks to some great advice from @[Sue-UK](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9658/) my tolerance to the sun light has greatly improved!

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-05T09:09:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunlight-harmful-when-dehydrated.19519/post-217985>

> [Penny said:](/goto/post?id=217967)
>
> Curious - why not coconut oil? Also, soaking your feet in magnesium chloride is kinda like sea water - as long as you ground at the same time - I get huge energy hits from mag chloride and sea salt actually - bipedalism blog was amazing - the gland at the bottom of the feet being different than all the others - that's when I went nuts walking the beach and mag chloride after the shower -

I didn't note where it was posted but I wrote down something Jack said "Remember human brains and membranes ARE fat and generate/store electron charge. Sulfated proteins attach to these saturated fats and produce voltage! BOOM!" So my aim is to get sunshine on the saturated fat from the ghee, hopefully interacting with the sulfated proteins in my skin. Coconuts and olives don't grow in the UK but what also rules the oils out for me is that they have SPF. I'd use lard before I used either of those, but ghee appeals to me more.  Its called boiled butter and other names in Europe so I suspect the ancients would have used it. I think its the saturated fat and yellow pigment that make it useful in a lower available sunlight latitude.

Mag chloride and sea salt didn't do anything noticeable for me, but maybe that's because it doesn't do much if the skin is already dry. I wouldn't have said I had dry skin before, but perhaps it was sub clinical IYKWIM. Also my hacks are now aimed at getting my mitochondria to respond to the natural environment where I am, not try to fake somewhere I'm not, because that hasn't worked so far. The ocean on holiday is amazing, faking it at home does nothing. So I've switched to a bit of amplification of what is versus trying to artificially recreate something that isn't. So I'm keeping my feet planted on the earth I'm living on, and using the gut as the portable human equivalent of a plant's root system. Which means no supplements that don't include the nutrients I am after in the context of the whole plant, and the plant has to be capable of growing or being harvested from the shoreline in the UK. I've had more success with MB on the skin, but I think of it as a modern version of woad, which the ancients here used.

### JanSz — 2017-07-05T16:05:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunlight-harmful-when-dehydrated.19519/post-218007>

In 1945-1970 my parents had a farm.
It was very small farm, few cows, no silo.
No sign of silo anywhere I would go.
Their and all other farmers were producing
cheese and butter that would spoil readily.
It was west side of Poland. The land there was freshly taken from Germany after WWII.
So, it was relatively "high tech" farm, specially the commercial fertilizers.
Crops were grown every year on all arable land.
Way back in the old place (current Ukraine), life on a farm was even simpler.
Cow manure or fermented urine as the only fertilizer. (Not sure about urine).
Every year a third of land was left to rest.

.......
Progress

Crohn's, IBS, sick gut in return.
....

> butyric acid (C4:0)
> should also be placed.
> I am trying to figure this one out, but proliferation of Crohn's, IBS and other gut problems may correlate with homogenized milk or butter additives that results withem being poor source of butyric acid.
> Butyric acid smell is the smell of rotten butter or rotten farmers cheese.
> They do not rot anymore.

I think to behave well, membranes must be able to respond to the environment they face. Like squirrels [https://www.scientificamerican.com/article/understanding-hibernation/](https://www.scientificamerican.com/article/understanding-hibernation/).

Butter does go off in heat, which is why I think boiled butter/ghee became popular as a way of preserving it. I make my own ghee, and if I use it as a sun cream, it reacts with the proteins etc in my skin and the residue that gets on my tan through does cause a rotten butter smell after a while. I'm in and out of water a lot so it doesn't affect me, and the tan through just gets washed anyway.

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-05T17:02:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunlight-harmful-when-dehydrated.19519/post-218014>

>

I put a block of bog standard non salted butter in a very small saucepan on the stove on the lowest heat, and leave it for up to 8 hours, then strain it to remove the non-fat bits that have separated and risen to the top. Half a teaspoon will do my solar panels, warmed if its cold, cold if the weather is hot. The butter packet doesn't say its from grass fed, and its not organic, but in the context of shit from a shovel, it doesn't seem to matter. My n=1 is that sufficient natural light can work with minimal amounts on my skin surface. Its less about the amounts of individual components of the ghee, more about the non linear effects of light's interactions with it on the skin surface. It all goes back to light .... and later that will change to light levels dropping and the then dominant effects of cold. I might then switch to butter.

### Sue-UK — 2017-07-07T15:55:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunlight-harmful-when-dehydrated.19519/post-218167>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=218015)
>
> Indya\_Gage
> thinks that Alzheimer and dementia patients benefit from butyric acid.
>
> But possibly they have those problems due to chronic low content of butyric acid in food.
> That low content is due to farming practices.
>
> [https://www.paleohacks.com/ghee/butter-vs-ghee-1940](https://www.paleohacks.com/ghee/butter-vs-ghee-1940)
>
> Since we are beginning to treat Alzheimer and dementia patients with ghee, even here in the West, because of the butyric acid in ghee which feeds nerve and brain tissue, and reverses the damaging affects of the disease,

My Mum and maternal grandmother both had dementia, so [http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0304394016300775](http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0304394016300775) _Butyrate, neuroepigenetics and the gut microbiome: Can a high fiber diet improve brain health?_ interested me. Not for the high fibre bit necessarily but the gut surface microbiome. I think of the gut as the human portable root system. If a plant can't get what it needs from the environment or soil, or form symbiotic relationships, it either adapts or dies off. Some adapt by becoming insectivore, carnivore, or parasitic. So I sort of think of it that humans do something similar, depending on the environment they face. In some latitudes, a diet that includes a fair bit of plants could provide the raw materials for the gut microbiome to make it, perhaps even exclusively, (unless something else has screwed/is screwing up the microbiome). In other latitudes we can get it from plant or animal sources, depending on seasonal availability. So at my latitude I do a bit of both. I crave ghee much more from late autumn to spring. Because of my risk factor from my maternal side, I also would only take antibiotics as a very last resort, and I deliberately drink well water rather than "purer" sources, and avoid chlorinated water as much as possible, to help diversify and protect my microbiome. I think considering the gut as a "root system" surface linked to the other surfaces and the environment is a safer long term strategy than worrying about farming practices, or getting on the supplement bandwagon again on the off chance it might stop me developing AD.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-07T16:56:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunlight-harmful-when-dehydrated.19519/post-218174>

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=217985)
>
> I didn't note where it was posted but I wrote down something Jack said "Remember human brains and membranes ARE fat and generate/store electron charge. Sulfated proteins attach to these saturated fats and produce voltage! BOOM!" So my aim is to get sunshine on the saturated fat from the ghee, hopefully interacting with the sulfated proteins in my skin. Coconuts and olives don't grow in the UK but what also rules the oils out for me is that they have SPF. I'd use lard before I used either of those, but ghee appeals to me more.  Its called boiled butter and other names in Europe so I suspect the ancients would have used it. I think its the saturated fat and yellow pigment that make it useful in a lower available sunlight latitude.
>
> Mag chloride and sea salt didn't do anything noticeable for me, but maybe that's because it doesn't do much if the skin is already dry. I wouldn't have said I had dry skin before, but perhaps it was sub clinical IYKWIM. Also my hacks are now aimed at getting my mitochondria to respond to the natural environment where I am, not try to fake somewhere I'm not, because that hasn't worked so far. The ocean on holiday is amazing, faking it at home does nothing. So I've switched to a bit of amplification of what is versus trying to artificially recreate something that isn't. So I'm keeping my feet planted on the earth I'm living on, and using the gut as the portable human equivalent of a plant's root system. Which means no supplements that don't include the nutrients I am after in the context of the whole plant, and the plant has to be capable of growing or being harvested from the shoreline in the UK. I've had more success with MB on the skin, but I think of it as a modern version of woad, which the ancients here used.

Why did it work: [http://circ.ahajournals.org/content/136/1/83?download=true](http://circ.ahajournals.org/content/136/1/83?download=true)

# Thread: Hockey Player/Coach/Optimization Student

### Mason Baptista — 2017-05-22T22:11:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hockey-player-coach-optimization-student.19520/post-214439>

My name is Mason Baptista and I'm a pro hockey player at the ECHL level. I'm also a hockey skills development coach and work in the field of movement biomechanics. I see the journey to optimization like a rubix cube and I understand that there are many parts to create a truly optimal human. The rubix cube metaphor works well in explaining how people can be deceived by getting a couple sides of the optimization game and crediting that with the way to optimal health like only doing the ketosis diet or only doing the CT.

Moving forward I have joined this group to further expand my knowledge and dive deep into the root of optimization at the molecular level. I have started reading the books laid out by Kruse on his podcasts, implemented environmental corrections to mediate my own environment and location and now I'm ready to learn more. I have recently come under scrutiny by peers and others I share this knowledge too because I don't have the degrees or medical schooling of doctors and clinicians. I may simple have a business degree but I can understand the difference between a toxic environment and optimal one where many scrutize me for saying that there isn't anything going on at a molecular level when you watch tv or sit at a computer screen for hours on end. Also I find many hockey players and other athletes saying I'm too extreme because they know of other elite athletes that have made it to the top level while living in these toxic environments. I plan to prove to them that you can make it to the top level while mediating and optimizing your environment. So the best place to start I believe is with myself and I have attached to the bottom my baseline lab results from the beginning of this year. I hope for some input and thought on where I should be moving towards and what I should avoid. I will have recent lab results up shortly as I will be doing biannual blood draws. Suggestions and criticism are all encouraged and I believe starting this type of a conversation is the first step into addressing the overwhelming issues in sports today from a molecular standpoint.

Lab results
Total cholesterol 204
LDL 112
HDL 83
Triglycerides 46
Total to HDL ratio 2.5
Non HDL Cholesterol 121

Apo B 90
Lp(a) 22

hs-CRP .7

Hemoglobin A1c 5.4

ALT/SGPT 27
ALP 86

eGfr 121

MCHC 33.4
RDW 13.4

### JanSz — 2017-05-22T22:59:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hockey-player-coach-optimization-student.19520/post-214441>

-   Male

-   Jan 9, 1990 (Age: 27)

\----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
The ECHL is a mid-level professional ice hockey league based in Princeton, New Jersey, with teams scattered across the United States and one franchise in Canada.
\----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
We are creatures of light.
We need sunlight to get:
energy
circadian Rhythm
vitamins A & D
\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Vitamin D (precursor) 25
What is this, details, name, laboratory range, units
off hand looks (very) low
\============================

Goal:
Vitamin D (25-Hydroxyvitamin D)(250nmol/L=100.16ng/mL)
3/4 of above is still ok
max 2x

Do not use vitamin D supplementation.
Preferably use sun to the whole body (including armpits, genitals and anus)
In Princeton NJ or any where on higher latitudes you will be missing on natural sunlight.
The remaining balance support using (UVa and UVb) lights.
Like this:
Sperti Fiji SUN Table Top Tanning Lamp
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B001ID8P78/ref=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o01\_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B001ID8P78/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o01_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1)
Depending on time of the year, 15 - 30 minutes/day
Think of always keeping skin conditioned to heavy sunlight. (Avoid sun-burn).

Think of your pineal gland, it is sensitive to blue light and it manages Circadian Rhythm.
Major are cortisol and melatonin levels and timing.

\-------------
Time spend indoor, under artificial lighting, in time of the day that does not match sunlight, is hurtful to our health.

There are beginning efforts to alter artificial lighting to match human needs.
Those efforts are good but they will newer mach sunlight.
Sunlight is unpolarized light.
artificial light is polarized.
Frequencies do not mach.
IRa is missed mostly due to high energy requirements.
Sunlight have some frequencies missing, Fraunhofer lines.

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738)
......................................

Work on your vitamin D.
Figure out your circadian rhythm status.

Sleep Balance Profile Home Test Kit (Dried Urine: Diurnal Melatonin (MT6s x 4), Free Cortisol (Cx4))

[https://www.amazon.com/Sleep-Balance-Profile-Dried-Urine/dp/B00ZAJY5SO/ref=sr\_1\_1\_a\_it?ie=UTF8&qid=1495482803&sr=8-1&keywords=zrt melatonin cortisol](https://www.amazon.com/Sleep-Balance-Profile-Dried-Urine/dp/B00ZAJY5SO/ref=sr_1_1_a_it?ie=UTF8&qid=1495482803&sr=8-1&keywords=zrt+melatonin+cortisol)

$239.99
\-------------------------
Above, seems to be expensive,
for the amount of information you get in return.

For little extra you may get all of above and really great status of the rest of your
Steroid Hormone Profile.

If you would attempt to do it,
pre-discuss any hormonal supplementation you may already use.
(DHEA, pregnenolone, progesterone, other) since they may alter results and will make it hard to evaluate results.

[https://dutchtest.com/resource/dutch-complete-sample-report/](https://dutchtest.com/resource/dutch-complete-sample-report/)

I'm in Fort Wayne and spend around 30 mins to an hour outside now with the sun out more. I use the dminder app to track the inclination of the sun.

I take zero supplementation whether it be vitamins or proteins. I stopped probably a couple years ago. Tomorrow I'm going to post in my optimal journal an in depth analysis of my environment and routine every day to give everyone a clear indication of what my environment is like. I use uva light anytime I'm inside while the sun is up and red light once the sun sets. I'll look into those supplementation options you mentioned and I'll see if I can do that test. Right now I have the budget for my next baseline test to compare to the results I posted. I'll hopefully be able to swing that test in the near future. Any thoughts on the 23andme test? Would like to get a deep look at my mitochondrial makeup.

Thanks again and look forward to working with you and everyone on this forum!

### JanSz — 2017-05-23T20:18:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hockey-player-coach-optimization-student.19520/post-214516>

> [Mason Baptista said:](/goto/post?id=214514)
>
> I'm in Fort Wayne and spend around 30 mins to an hour outside now with the sun out more. I use the dminder app to track the inclination of the sun.

I think of dminder at minimum as a distraction, but more important as a tool that reduces your sun exposure.
Just measure your vit d from time to time, after a while you will get idea about how you are responding.
Practically, with hardened skin, you can't get too much sun.
It is important to keep skin always hardened to sunlight.

Best sunlight is at two hours before and two after solar noon.
Unless one is at equator when sunlight practically have unvarying UVa & UVb content.
\---
Solar noon.
During summer saving time, clock is moved up one hr, so at that time solar noon is at 1PM.
Good sun exposure then is at 11AM-3PM.
\---

For maintenance of circadian rhythm it is good idea to look (indirectly) at sun or bright light right after getting up, (for few minutes).

### JanSz — 2017-05-25T00:37:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hockey-player-coach-optimization-student.19520/post-214610>

> [Mason Baptista said:](/goto/post?id=214604)
>
> After that lab I started on stricktly reverse osmosis water and the nnemf was bad but since then I have been working on the sun exposure.

Spring water may be just fine.

Speaking of water, there is number of descriptions that may be misleading or not clear.

Water have four states.
ice
liquid
vapor
4th state
\-------------
First three are commonly known water, H2O,
4th state is H3O2
\--------------
To describe that 4th state numerous descriptions are used.
EZ, EazyWater, Exclusion Zone
Structured water
\--------------
People often are (inappropriately) mixing names, structured water with drinking water.
That is sometime done for commercial reasons.
This are two different states of water.

4th state of water is able to exist only in highly confined spaces,
or as (very thin) amount next to surface.
\----------------

We are made of cells. They are (usually) very small.
Cells have membranes at its boundaries.
Cells elements are located inside cells and also have boundaries.
There is a volume inside cells and outside cells. Intra-cellular, extra-cellular.
Cells and their components are small enough to carry 4th phase water.
One of cells components, mitochondria, is producing water as a by product of its energy production.
(I think)(to be confirmed) that all cellular water is 4th phase and originate from mitochondria.
If above is true:
Water that we drink does not end inside cells.

Some people (even when drinking reasonable amounts of water)(latent heat=???) are dehydrated,
that have not much to do with the amount of water one drinks,
to repair that, cell health must be repaired.

........
Most of extracellular water originates in water we drink or as a content of food we eat.
Extracellular water when next to surface can (self organize)(with no exchange of energy??) or (latent heat=??) and be a 4th phase.
As a %% of total volume that is usually very small amount
.
.

Globulin 2.8-3.2
A/G ratio 1.6-1.5

Creatinine .83 -.8
eGfr 121-122
Bun 22-21

Sodium 140-140
Potassium 4.6-4.5
Chloride 104-104
CO2 31-29
Calcium 9.8-10.4

Vitamin D (precursor) 25-36
ALP 86-64

White blood cell count 5.8-6.4
Hematocrit 44-47.2
RBC 4.91-5.24
Hemoglobin 14.7-16
MCV 89.7-90.1
MCH 29.9-30.5
MCHC 33.4-33.9
RDW 13.4-12.2

So my LDL went really high with the triglycerides as well any suggestions on why? I eat pretty health fats but I do fry a fair amount of food so could that be producing the bad type of fat? Couple other flucations like the HScrp, bun ratioand the calcium which I artibuate to the surgery I had two weeks ago plus the flight I had just after that. Did get my vitamin d to go up slightly while going through the winter for the most part. First results are from January and second are from last week. Any suggestions would be great!

In addition, is it a genetic factor that the mole or skin tag may develop into melanoma-->how does the mitochondria convert it, if at all?

### ScottishEmma — 2017-06-01T16:04:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freckles-kids-adults.19522/post-215258>

I'm interested in this question.

I had freckles all over my face as a child. Hated them! Now I wish I still had them... I am covered in moles.

### labellavita — 2017-07-09T00:17:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freckles-kids-adults.19522/post-218264>

I had freckles on my face as a child but they faded as I grew up. I have a lot of "freckles" (moles) on my shoulders and some on my arms and less on my legs.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-09T06:03:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freckles-kids-adults.19522/post-218274>

Freckles are to cows and mice and dogs coats. Now go re read John Ott's book more carefully. The light the animal senses via the eye is capable of changing the coat. Adaptations to spatially varying selective pressures, such as climates, is particularly important in human populations. This is where freckles come from and why they need to be embraced. They are a remnant to your coupled past at the equator to absorb more UV light. Because the human species first arose in equatorial Africa in the East African rift, the ability to tolerate high temperatures and a strong UV light environment was likely under strong long-term selection in ancestral human populations. As humans spread out of equatorial Africa to regions at higher latitudes, cold temperatures likely became important selective forces and likely were the stimulus to islands of melanin and a back ground of hypo pigmentation that increased our ability to absorb more light when it was more sparse. That light variation created SNP's that sculpted energy flux in the skin to create freckles by changing how tyrosine and phenylalanine would be used at higher latitudes. SNP's are more enduring than any other part of the nuclear genome because they are fundamentally supported by energy flux fromt he more important part of the genome........the mitochondrion.

**HOW MUCH SUN TO GET SPHINXing?** [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...fect-redox-potential.17411/page-3#post-213969](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/how-do-mthfr-snps-effect-redox-potential.17411/page-3#post-213969)

### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-09T06:04:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freckles-kids-adults.19522/post-218275>

> [labellavita said:](/goto/post?id=218264)
>
> I had freckles on my face as a child but they faded as I grew up. I have a lot of "freckles" (moles) on my shoulders and some on my arms and less on my legs.

and if they did do you really think this is not something of a clue you missed? Could that mean something big? It is huge in my mind.

### labellavita — 2017-07-09T16:19:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freckles-kids-adults.19522/post-218296>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=218275)

# Thread: DHA and Acne
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-and-acne.19524/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Charlotte89 — 2017-05-24T00:26:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-and-acne.19524/post-214533>

Hello,
In the past whenever I have tried taking fish oil it has given me painful cystic acne. I have tried several high quality brands (including Eskimo 3 and Green Pastures.) And I stopped because of the acne.

After reading the work of Jack Kruse, and being impressed with the amount of nutritional density, over the last few weeks I have seriously increased the amount of seafood I eat.
I hoped that because I was eating the whole food as nature intended it would not give me acne.

However the severe painful acne has come back (mostly near my mouth).

Therefore it cannot be a rancidity issue as the seafood has always been fresh.

I am not knocking seafood.

I realize this is a very strange reaction to a substance that is normally very good for the human body.

Can anybody explain why this is happening and what it indicates is going on in my body?

I have always wondered, but no professional I’ve asked has ever been able to explain why.

Thank you.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-24T03:35:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-and-acne.19524/post-214539>

I don't recommend any pills for DHA just seafood because DHA is a PUFA is exquisitely sensitive to temp, light, and mechanical disruption leading to instability and become rancid.

Acne is a sure sign of a Vitamin A and D issue and circadian mismatch. Also tells me your environment is BAD.

### Charlotte89 — 2017-05-24T21:10:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-and-acne.19524/post-214602>

Hi Jack. Thank you for replying.

I understand that normal acne is caused by Vitamin A and D issues and circadian mismatch and I accept that - but this feel very different to normal acne.

Maybe I should clarify this is not what I would consider normal acne, it is cystic acne - very inflamed 1/2 inch to inch painful cysts which I have only ever got in my life if I have fish oil - another seafood link.

I haven't taken fish oil in years (I stopped because the acne was so bad and and I wouldn't ever again take it again because your work woke me up to how much better it is to get it from seafood).

However in the last few weeks I massively increased my seafood consumption, and the cystic acne is back. Literally nothing else in my life has changed.

Could this be indicative of a seafood allergy/intolerance? (I have no other symptoms)

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-25T06:17:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-and-acne.19524/post-214627>

It does not matter what kind it is......all acne is inflammation and all inflammation is a circadian mismatch linked to poor mitochondrial function.

It has Nada to do with seafood and all to do with the light your choosing to live under. Your skin eye and brain are speaking to you........are you listening?

### JanSz — 2017-05-25T15:01:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-and-acne.19524/post-214645>

Food is very much down the list.
Sunlight = #1

....
If you are not supplementing vit D, you may want to test its (unadulterated) level.
Aim at least at 3/4 of this level or up to twice that:
Vitamin D (25-Hydroxyvitamin D)(250nmol/L=100.16ng/mL)

Also this test will give you decent idea of your Circadian rhythm status.

[https://www.amazon.com/Sleep-Balance-Profile-Dried-Urine/dp/B00ZAJY5SO/ref=sr\_1\_1\_a\_it?ie=UTF8&qid=1495713606&sr=8-1&keywords=zrt melatonin cortisol](https://www.amazon.com/Sleep-Balance-Profile-Dried-Urine/dp/B00ZAJY5SO/ref=sr_1_1_a_it?ie=UTF8&qid=1495713606&sr=8-1&keywords=zrt+melatonin+cortisol)

Sleep Balance Profile Home Test Kit (Dried Urine: Diurnal Melatonin (MT6s x 4), Free Cortisol (Cx4)

### emmao — 2017-05-25T16:16:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-and-acne.19524/post-214649>

I also struggled with very bad acne. Though it wasn't the 1/2 inch cysts, it was pretty horrendous.
Recipe for clear skin:
3 oysters a day
Orange goggles at night
Coconut oil/tea tree oil on skin.
Acne is very stressful. The stress of one zit can easily multiply it. Sit in the sun (no sunscreen), put your toes in the grass, and just relax.

### Charlotte89 — 2017-05-30T02:33:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-and-acne.19524/post-215057>

Thank you everyone, (especially Jack).
Going to continue with the seafood.

### Butters — 2017-05-30T11:46:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-and-acne.19524/post-215078>

What about just Bad skin? Im having a lot of blackheads since my puberty. Only upper body area and face. Same Problem without inflammation?

### ScottishEmma — 2017-05-30T15:15:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-and-acne.19524/post-215089>

My god, I can relate. I have only just got rid of my acne after twenty three years. Yes, 23 years!! And like you whenever I took fish oils it made my acne go cystic and horrendous!!

I have also, like you, been upping my fish intake to the mega level and I'm pleased to say that it has not had a negative impact on my skin. Like I said, it's really been the last few months that it's gone (only other two times were during pregnancy) and I really put it down to the fact I've not been on my laptop and have been getting as much sunlight as possible.

I guarantee that was the bloody cure all along. All through my teenage years ad twenties I used to shut the curtains when I was inside!!! I rarely got light at all. Coupled with artificial suns and plenty of mobile phone use I'm not surprised it's only just gone.

You have my condolences. I can honestly say acne ruined my life. I would avoid going out or looking people in the eye and it damaged my confidence no end.

The good news is that your skin heals quickly when you get to the root of the issue. Also, try to not think it's the fish and instead focus on light and see if that makes a difference...

Wishing you luck!

### Antonis — 2018-08-17T14:30:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-and-acne.19524/post-248677>

That's why me and my brothers had acne in teenaged years. But we had pill therapy instead.

### Michael CULLEN — 2018-08-17T15:08:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-and-acne.19524/post-248678>

Neem will clear up any acne problems.

### Butters — 2018-08-17T22:26:25+0300

# Thread: How much DHA per day?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-dha-per-day.19527/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AdamF — 2017-05-24T05:35:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-dha-per-day.19527/post-214547>

Other than "as much as possible", what's the RDA in Epi-Paleo land?

Thank you,

Adam

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-24T05:54:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-dha-per-day.19527/post-214549>

How addicted to technology and man made light. How bad is your environment? what is your haplotype and SNP trend? How many fluorescents are you under? What is your ASI and D3 and salivary mealtonin or your BUN creat levels?

Then.......maybe you can make an educated guess. Until you know the variables do not look for answers because the people that give you a number are giving you a guess.

A guess is not worth the risk. Mitochondriac test. They do not guess.

### AdamF — 2017-05-24T06:57:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-dha-per-day.19527/post-214552>

Just read that and saw how isomorphic your teachings are to the Einstein quote about problems not being solved at the same level of thinking that created them.

Your response just changed my paradigm for all of this; thank you.

Man made light: Low
Environment: Have power line running through back-yard. That is the largest hazard; do not use WiFi in house, no Smart Meters, etc...cell phone use is less than 1 hour per week without airplane mode and phone is always covered by one of the few legitimate EMF shielding devices on the market.

Haplotype: Unknown
SNP's: Homozygous MTHFR
Fluroescents: None
ASI: Unknown
D3: Unknown
Salivary Melatonin: Unknown
BUN/Creat.: Unknown

### JanSz — 2017-05-24T15:38:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-dha-per-day.19527/post-214570>

How much DHA per day?
\===============================================

> [AdamF said:](/goto/post?id=214547)
>
> Other than "as much as possible", what's the RDA in Epi-Paleo land?
>
> Thank you,
>
> Adam

For me, answer is universal.
Just enough,
not too much, not to little.

As for RDA=Required daily allowance.

That makes me think of long half life, so it doesn't have to be every day.

I like to test, more better.

DHA is one of many fatty acids.
DHA is one of many Omega3 fatty acids.
Omega3 category is very strong, little goes long way.
Get too much of O3 and it will suppress Omega6.
We need them all in just right amount.

Numerous people posted here their fatty acids analysis.
Consider getting it done and post it here.
So far, one girl posted hers and had low DHA. Others are high.

DHA is a part of fish oil/krill oil scam.
To the point that there are laboratories that always recommend eating seafood or supplementing with those oils. They do not recommend upper range on DHA or EPA.
So, when testing one have to be careful.

I use this test that can be had at LabCorp or Mayo Clinic.

Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-199717](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-199717)

..........
