# Thread: En'vision'ing the future - Katrina's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/envisioning-the-future-katrinas-journal.19528/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Katrina — 2017-05-24T07:03:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/envisioning-the-future-katrinas-journal.19528/post-214553>

Hello...
It was suggested that I write this journal of my history and my journey with my issue. My issues are both vision and hearing related, but I want to find answers for my eyesight more that I do my hearing loss.

I shall start with my family history. My parents by and large have had a very healthy history. It has only been since they have been older that they have encountered problems. My mother had back issues back in her 30s/40s in which she had surgery for, my father I can't think of anything he has had untl in the pst 10 years, high blood pressure, gout, and he was a smoker for a long time in his life and so now he is dealing with heart issues an breathing issues. My mother did have cancer in her either ovaries or uterus, can't remember which at the moment. Neither had hearing issues until they were older and both wear glasses to see far I believe. I don't know a whole lot about my grandparents health history. I do know that my grandfather on my mother's side had cataracts. My faternal grandparents died at 72 and 83, my maternal grandparents at 59 and 88. My mother's sister died at 29 of lung cancer. All of my dad's siblings are still alive, except an older brother tho was killed by a drunk driver when he was in his 40's I believe. I was fairly young so I don't remember for sure.

My own history is largely healthy. My mother had rubella when she carried me, when she was I think like 3-4 months pregnant. I had the usual childhood diseases like chickenpox, lots of colds but nothing major. I had my tonsils out at about 6 years old. I was diagnosed with hearing loss at least in one ear when I was 5. I was fitted with an aid then. I have always had low blood pressure and I have most of my life struggled with weight gain and loss. I have only been hospitilized for the tonsil removal, exploratory surgery of the ear and and the birth of my three kids.

When I was 34 I was diagnosed with borderline glaucoma but it wasn't a definite diagnosis as they weren't entirely sure I had it, and they wanted to observe me over a few months to see how my eye pressures were. They did put me on medication, which I wasn't good about taking. When they did finally diagnose me with having glaucoma it was a few years later. I have gotten better about taking my medication but not perfect. I started loosing sight about 6 years ago. I have some left in my left eye and the right eye is largely blind, except some peripherial vision.

I live in San Diego. I have lived here for nearly 30 years now. I work as a fitness professional teaching group exercise classes. I teach both land classes and water so sometimes I am indoors and other times I am outdoors but under a roof. I am a lover of sunshine so when I can I like to be in it. I don't get out in nature as much as I'd like as it's fairly far away, I am not a beach/ocean fan but I will go once in a while. Would love to move some place where there is less city and I am planning to within a year. I used to love going barefooted, haven't done that outside in ages. I never wear shoes when I am at home, only when i go out.

As I said I struggle with weight and I have been on many diets, eating plans, etc. None of them have every been dr recommended but I have tried many on my own. I can loose weight but it get tired of trying so hard and it taking so long and I can't seem to get rid of the areas that are my tough areas, I know you're supposed to love yourself how you are, but back in the 80's it was all about how you looked and it was important. I am the only one in my family that seems to struggle with this. I do like to each healthy, and I do keep fruits and veggies in the house for myself and kids. I am just not fond of cooking. But currently, after reading Jack's book I am trying the Epi Paleo diet to the best of my abilities. I primarily only drink water, however I will drink tea when it's cold.

Sleep. Well I have been sleeping well mostly lately but just in the past two weeks I have had a few nights when my brain has decided it must think about stuff. But when I do sleep I sleep well, I dream and stay asleep for many hours. I used to enjoy getting up early but in the past few months I have really been having a hard time getting up, even if I get to bed at a decent time. I am at the end of menopause, I think. I have tried after reading you book to get up and view the sunrise but like I said it's been difficult lately.

I have never heard of blue blockers, until the person who responded to my initial post mentioned it. I spend a good amount of time at the computer, probably over 6 hours a day. I don't do that with my phone at all. I use a tablet for reading, largely because I can change the background color and font size so that I can see it better.

So what I am looking for, hoping to find on this site, is help to find ways or a way to reverse or at least stop the progression of my glaucoma. I have been told there's no cure. I have also been told the same about my deafness. And I have also been told the body can heal itself...a bit contradictory eh? I understand both of my conditions are neurological. My hearing loss is nerve damage as well as my eyesight. I do believe they both were cuased byt the ruebella. I would prefer not to have to have surgery to fix the pressures and even that is not a guarantee. I really need some hope as I don't know how I will communicate if I loose both hearing and eyesight. I already struggle with hearing words and conversations. I am willing to try anything to help myself.

I hope this is a decent opening entry. Thank you in advance for any suggestions you may give.

### caroline — 2017-05-24T08:55:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/envisioning-the-future-katrinas-journal.19528/post-214554>

welcome again Katrina.

If you can manage it - become a gold member and then you will have access to the monthly webinars and the follow up live Q&A when you can ask Jack anything.

Are you following Emma Sabin? She has vision issues......

### JanSz — 2017-05-24T18:10:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/envisioning-the-future-katrinas-journal.19528/post-214590>

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/introduction-and-my-health-question.19356/#post-212471](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/introduction-and-my-health-question.19356/#post-212471)

### Katrina — 2017-06-11T22:13:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/envisioning-the-future-katrinas-journal.19528/post-215842>

Update:
I have been reading through some of the posts on Facebook on Dr. Kruse's page. I have read some of the blogs. This is what I am understanding that I might could do to help myself.

I do know that they don't really know what causes Glaucoma. I have looked to see how far back it goes in history, apparently Hippocrates was aware of it. so having said that I began to think, really think about when exactly I was first made aware of it and what exactly was happening in my life. And one thing came up that I had not considered before and it was through reading the posts and blogs that made me consider this. Roughly about the time I was first diagnosed was when we got a computer in our home and I started using it more, largely at night when the kids were in bed and I had time to surf and chat with people online. And it was about this time, I believe, that the light bulbs started moving towards the one we are required to use today., flurescent and such. I am trying to recall if we ourfitted our house with it that early.

So based on the reading about the mitochondria and how the death of it can cause many problems in many areas. I began to wonder if anyone had considered that to be a probability of what causes Glaucoma. After reading what I have so far I think it's plausable, something worth considering. So in that case from what I understand getting enough morning sunlight could possibly help my situation. My question then would be how much time each day and how long should it be nefore I might see results.

I also am getting that I would need to change my diet to help with the healing, but I am a bit unsure on that. I am also thinking that I need to get the EMF
s to be much less then they are in my home now. I think someone mentioned a blue light shield...where do you find these things?

Another thought and I am sure people have studied this but I could not find much in the way of answers, could glaucoma be attributed to inflammation? but from what? they say it's pressure in the eyes but that is so vague to me. I have been told it's nerve damage, which brings me back to the idea that it involves the mitochondria.

Other types of therapy have been mentioned in my search for healing Energy medicine, sound therapy, color therapy,n Also wondering if more Vitamin B will help specifically Retinal?

thank you for listening! Any comments are appreciated.
Katrina
